title: Retraction Notice: News Recommendation Systems
authors: Mahalakshmi A, Dharani P, DivyaPriya S, Gayathri M
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: News Data is structured as well as unstructured formatted data, which carries attributes like source, date, location, author, headline text, and detailed data. To extract features from the textual data, which deals with all of the language and text-related aspects. News articles are basic structured as well as unstructured and text-formatted data, which leads it to become the point of interest to the researchers. Various research like classification of news articles according to domain depending on the region or the area of interest of people using various machine learning techniques, sentiment analysis over the news articles using the emotional dictionary, and text summarization on the news data so as to reduce the bulkiness of data, Special Character Recognition.

title: Retraction Notice: Hash Tag-Based Sentiment Analysis Using A Deep Learning Approach
authors: R. Venkatesan, M. Sangeetha, S. Nivedha, M. Selvakumar, K.S. Chandru, P. Parthasarathi
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Social commerce uses a variety of social networking sites like Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, Pinterest, and business Whatsapp as a platform to promote products and services. Since advertising, a product, or service in s-comm will reach as high as a possible number of users on networking sites. Ads will spread virally by sharing among friends and friends of friends. So that wider reachability is possible and shortly will get the customer for our business. The success of an s-comm campaign is measured by the degree to which consumers interact with the sellers through review comments, likes, and shares between friends. Customer behavior analysis is needed for s-comm marketing because social commerce can significantly boost economic growth. The sentiment analysis of customer recommendations was investigated using a dataset of women's s-comm apparel hashtags and reviews. So for such reviews are experimented with different machine learning algorithms (Decision tree, SVM, Random Forest, KNN, Naïve Bayes and LSTM). These models handled the common English reviews and compared the metrics (Accuracy, Sensitivity and Specificity) with one another. Instead of working with individual model, this paper introduces a novel hybrid approach that integrates CNN and Ada Boost LSTM algorithms to provide better results in sentiment analysis when compared to other machine learning algorithms mentioned above. While considering the hash tag with common review comments the hybrid system is out performed. The comparison analysis is done for performance matrices namely accuracy of 98%, specificity of 98.1%, and sensitivity of 98.1%. In the s-commerce transaction industry, this hybrid approach will help to create a deeper understanding of consumer sentiment analysis and psychologically attract clients.

title: A Logistic Regression Pipeline for Chiang Mai Air Quality Risk using Scaling, SMOTE, and Splines
authors: Sane Yu, Nina Bencheva
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Accuracy of air quality risk prediction in Chiang Mai is a significant challenge. There are acute <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> pollution in Chiang Mai, Thailand in every year from February to May. The interpretable model like standard logistic regression is beneficial for operational public health sectors, but the model performance is weakened by complex real-world data and extreme class imbalance. This study investigates how pipeline design specifically features scaling, nonlinear spline transformations, and the Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE) can materially improve logistic regression for the prediction of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{PM}_{2.5}$</tex> in Chiang Mai. This study uses a public dataset of 29,078 hourly meteorological and pollution records for Chiang Mai with a severe class imbalance (approximately 3 percent high risk). This study evaluates four distinct pipelines against a deep learning benchmark. Results demonstrate that standard logistic regression fails to detect high risk events, yielding zero recall. However, the introduction of SMOTE successfully restores operational utility, increasing recall to over 0.81. The combined pipeline, integrating both SMOTE and cubic spline feature engineering, provides the best overall discrimination among all interpretable models. It achieves a Receiver Operating Characteristic Area Under the Curve (ROC AUC) of 0.823, balancing the need for high sensitivity in disaster warning systems with the requirement for model transparency. While the deep learning benchmark sets a higher performance ceiling, the results demonstrate that for highly imbalanced data, an engineered logistic regression pipeline offers the optimal balance of safety and auditability for municipal environmental monitoring.

title: Effective English Communication for Food and Beverage Staff to Achieve Guest Satisfaction
authors: Aye Mon, Premchit Khurana
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: English communication is vital in the hospitality industry, especially for food and beverage (F&B) staff interacting with international guests. This study examines the challenges of English communication among food and beverage (F&B) staff in Thailand, its impact on guest satisfaction, and strategies for improvement. Data was collected through interviews as well as secondary research. The study highlights how language barriers, limited vocabulary, and cultural differences affect service quality and presents recommendations for enhancing staff English proficiency for better guest experiences.

title: Enhancing Research Administration Services in Universities: Research Grant Disbursement Online Tracking System
authors: Shisa Singkhamfu
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Higher education institutions encounter intricate and swiftly evolving obstacles, especially regarding research administration, a fundamental task influencing university rankings and academic reputation. This paper delineates a case study from the College of Arts, Media, and Technology (CAMT) of Chiang Mai University (CMU) regarding the development of an “Research Grant Disbursement Online Tracking System”. The research examines the integration of Lean Management concepts with Knowledge Management (KM) principles to transform tacit information into explicit and accessible knowledge. By delineating explicit six-step workflows, the system mitigates “administrative friction” and operational waste. Results indicate significant organizational impact in terms of time savings and improved user satisfaction.

title: Ethical AIGC Pipelines for Film and Animation Production
authors: Wai Yie Leong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content (AIGC) has rapidly reshaped film and animation production by enabling automated asset creation, accelerated ideation, and complex multimodal storytelling. Despite these advancements, the integration of AIGC technologies introduces substantial ethical risks, including biased character representation, culturally insensitive outputs, unclear authorship, dataset opacity, copyright infringement, and weakened creative labour protection. This paper develops a comprehensive ethical framework for AIGC pipelines tailored to cinematic and animation workflows. Through an extensive review of current generative models-diffusion systems, multimodal transformers, neural animation generators, and large language models-the study identifies recurring ethical deficiencies that undermine responsible deployment. A technical methodology is proposed, featuring transparent dataset provenance tracking, embedded fairness auditing, cultural-sensitivity validators, copyright-risk scoring, and human-in-the-loop approval mechanisms as integral components of the AIGC pipeline. Experimental simulations across storyboard generation, character design, motion synthesis, and dialogue creation demonstrate that the Ethical AIGC Pipeline (E-AIGCP) significantly reduces bias propagation, minimizes copyright exposure, and enhances human-AI creative alignment. Results indicate a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 2 \%}$</tex> reduction in high-risk outputs and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 1 \%}$</tex> improvement in perceived creative integrity. The study provides a structured, technically rigorous blueprint for studios and regulators seeking to implement responsible AIGC systems in film and animation production.

title: Evolutionary Algorithm for Determining the Data Transmission Route Between Sensor Nodes
authors: Yaroslav Pyrih, Yuliia Pyrih, Taras Andrukhiv
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: A genetic algorithm is presented, the purpose of which is to minimise the weight of the route between sensor nodes. The total weight of the edges, which reflects the distance or delay value when transmitting data between nodes, is used as the optimisation criterion. The proposed approach involves representing the route as an individual and using genetic operators to form new generations. The fitness of individuals is assessed based on a function inversely proportional to the total weight of the route, which allows one to approach the optimal path with minimal costs. To study the operation of the presented algorithm, simulation modelling was carried out using the developed software product. Based on the results obtained, it is shown that the author's algorithm allowed determining the shortest route in terms of both distance and delay value, compared to the greedy one.

title: The Improved Models for Calculation of Human Body SAR in the Case of Sitting Position
authors: Mykhaylo Andriychuk, Taras Nazarovets
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The calculation of Specific Absorption Rate (SAR) is carried out in the framework of the advanced modeling based on the improved model of linear antenna, which takes into account the seated posture of human, and the FDTD approach, based on the calculation of the internal characteristics of the EM field. The numerical data for both the approaches are proposed and issues related to the effectiveness and correctness of method are discussed. The simulation data confirm proximity of results obtained by both the methods.

title: Retraction Notice: A collaborative filtering proposal in view of client conduct and client profile on online interpersonal organization
authors: Priyanka Sampat Bomare, Sudhir G. Shikalpure
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: The target of this work is to help peoples to identify other peoples depending on user's items of interest, hobbies and entertainments for example hobbies, sports, books, movies, etc. Users will get acquainted with different peoples having same interest in some aspects. It is very difficult to find peoples having similar interest. With the proposed system user will receives suggestions of different peoples whose user profile and behavior matches with user's interest. The item or thing Rating and Friendship Model are utilized as a survey framework for the clients of the informal organization. This model surveys the client profiles and client conduct furthermore gathers audits gave by the clients to the thing set by the clients. Collaborative Filtering and Tanimoto Coefficient algorithm and LogLikelyhood Similarity algorithm is then carried out to provide suggestions. The CF calculation used to anticipate the relationship between clients in light of the client rating on things and the client's profile. The Tanimoto Coefficient and LogLikelyhood Similarity calculation figures the comparability between clients through finding the closest neighbors for every client in the interpersonal organization. In trial examination, information set “DouBan” will be utilized and exhibits the execution of the enhanced method with a site. Client can give the remark and can likewise do its assessment investigation.

title: Cebuano Speech Tutor Using Hybrid TDNN-HMM Phoneme and Prosodic Feature Recognition
authors: Christian Gabriel Ibaoc, Antonio Luiz Veloso, Nigel Kristoffer Valero, Shawne Michael Tumalad, Ronald Pascual
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Speech processing technology is a growing technology that relies on the effective use of available speech data. In the Philippines, most speech processing, from recognition to synthesis, is focused on either Tagalog or English, with Cebuano being often neglected. This study focuses on the Cebuano language and the prospect of developing a computer-assisted speech tutor using a TDNN-HMM hybrid speech model-based phoneme recognition, to teach language learners up to Level A1 CEFR in the Cebuano language. The model was evaluated using adapted WER and SER metrics as well as expert validation, and user improvement was evaluated through pre-tests, post-tests, model-based data, and expert validation. The speech model was calculated to have 2.64 percent WER and 8.00 percent SER, though expert assessments, on average, determined it to have a 17.82 percent error rate. Meanwhile, users of the website were found to have improved both their vocabulary and pronunciation, with an average score of 6.43 to 12.78 out of 15 from pre-test to post-test, and an increasing trend line of correct syllables per day with a strong coefficient of determination. User improvement was validated by an expert with 20 samples of final audio recordings. Recommendations for future research are discussed.

title: Blind Radio Map Construction via Topology Guided Manifold Learning_supp1-3680610.pdf
authors: Edith Ngai
publish: N/A
abstract: Constructing radio maps traditionally requires extensive site surveys with precise location labels, resulting in costly and time-consuming calibration. Conventional approaches derive labels from inertial measurement units (IMUs), but are constrained by device-level access permissions and the need for pre-installed IMU hardware. In this paper, we present a calibration-free radio map construction method that relies solely on Channel State Information (CSI) measurements, thereby obviating the need for location labels. Our key insight is to embed CSI data into a two-dimensional geographic space using a neural network, without any label information. The primary challenge is to preserve the real-world topology in the embedded space. To address this issue, we propose a novel Topology-Guided Manifold Learning (TGML) approach that learns a low-dimensional embedding through self-supervision based on its mapping to a topological map in the geographic space. Specifically, we introduce an embedding network that learns locally smoothed proximity, thus creating a compact low-dimensional representation in the latent space. We further employ a regularized transport method with a differentiable Sinkhorn distance to establish an optimal mapping between the latent and geographic spaces. We use the resulting mapping deviation to supervise the training of the embedding network, enabling continuous refinement through self-supervision. Experiments in an office environment show that TGML achieves an average localization error of 2.37m and a relative beam estimation error of 6.68%, outperforming state-of-the-art methods.

title: Photonic Structuration of the Airy-Bessel Beam for High-Performance Fiber-Optic Refractive Index Sensing
authors: Bodem Indraja, Arijit datta, Sreenivasulu tupakula, Swagata Samanta, Saswat kumar ram
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The emergence of structured light as a controllable photonic entity has transformed the manipulation of electromagnetic fields, facilitating advanced light-matter interactions that critically enhance the operational efficiency of diverse fiber-optic sensors. Within this emergent framework, the Airy-Bessel beam, characterized by its non-diffracting propagation and self-healing dynamics, stands as a paradigmatically superior candidate for fiber-based refractive-index measurements. In this investigation, we employed the Airy-Bessel excitation within a decladded multimode fiber to transcend the inherent constraints of conventional Gaussian illumination, whose efficacy is typically curtailed by shallow evanescent-field penetration and diminished mode-medium interaction. By tailoring the excitation conditions, higher-order guided modes are efficiently stimulated, thereby amplifying the evanescent-wave contribution and enabling a superior refractive index sensitivity. The proposed sensor architecture was validated using full-vector beam propagation method (BPM) numerical modeling performed in OptiBPM (v13.1.3, Optiwave Inc.), allowing a comprehensive analysis of Airy-Bessel beam evolution within the sensing structure. The sensor demonstrates absolute sensitivities of 1502.96 dB/RIU and 1878.72 dB/RIU for sensing lengths of 20 cm and 25 cm, respectively, with corresponding detection resolutions of 6.65×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> RIU and 5.32×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−6</sup> RIU. These results affirm a notable advancement over Gaussian beam configurations and highlight that structured-beam excitation enables scalable sensing without the need for geometric alterations such as tapering, side-polishing, U-bending, surface-functionalized designs, etc. Therefore, the proposed sensing method furnishes a potent pathway for futuristic fiber-optic sensing across chemical, biomedical, and environmental domains where ultralow refractive index detection is imperative.

title: Lightweight Edge Intelligence: Adapting 1.5B LLM via LoRA for Log Analysis in Campus Networks
authors: Zhenpeng Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The practical adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) in cybersecurity remains constrained by computational cost, deployment complexity, and increasingly strict data-privacy requirements. These challenges are particularly acute in resource-limited environments such as vocational colleges and similar resource-constrained institutional environments, where cloud-based security analytics may be undesirable or infeasible, while traditional rule-based intrusion detection systems often struggle with noisy alerts and limited semantic understanding of network events. To address this gap, I propose Edge-SecGPT, an on-premise framework for lightweight log screening under constrained campus deployment conditions. Built on the Qwen2.5-1.5B backbone, Edge-SecGPT adopts parameter-efficient adaptation through Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) together with 4-bit NF4 quantization. Rather than targeting maximal model scale, the framework is designed for deployment on resource-constrained single-GPU edge-oriented hardware and operates as a task-restricted binary log-screening module for privacy-conscious campus-network analysis. To improve methodological rigor, I evaluate the framework using a stricter temporal file-holdout protocol, where one earlier log window is used for training, a subsequent window is used for validation, and a later unseen temporal holdout window is reserved for final evaluation. Across 32,762 desensitized campus log records, the revised study frames Edge-SecGPT as a localized lightweight LoRA-based log-screening framework together with a controlled serialization study under the stricter temporal file-holdout protocol and an additional same-protocol conventional baseline. On the later unseen temporal holdout, the conventional logistic-regression baseline achieved 77.96% precision, 98.48% recall, and 87.03% F1-score, with an estimated 826.5 false alarms/day. Under the same temporal split, the TinyLlama-1.1B LoRA baseline achieved 94.37% precision, 100.00% recall, and 97.10% F1-score, while the raw-JSON Qwen2.5-1.5B configuration achieved the strongest empirical performance among the compared configurations under the present single-site temporal-holdout protocol and current label-construction regime (94.68% precision, 100.00% recall, and 97.27% F1-score). The corresponding structured configuration achieved 93.81% precision, 99.18% recall, and 96.42% F1-score. These results suggest that serialization choice materially affects deployment behavior in the present within-site setting, while broader attack-semantic generalization remains to be validated.

title: Compact Silicon-Photonics Waveguide- Confined Receiving and Transmitting Antenna with Signal Remodulation for Indoor Optical Wireless Communication
authors: Yin-He Jian, Mikołaj Wolny, Chi-Wai Chow, Eduward Tangdiongga
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We propose and demonstrate a compact silicon photonics (SiPh)-based waveguide (WG)-confined receiving antenna (RA) and transmitting antenna (TA) integrated with an enhanced direct intensity remodulation scheme. In a narrow-beam-steered system, the RA exhibits only 5.3-dB power variation over a 12° angular range, supporting data rates > 28.12 Gbps, while in an ultra-narrow-beam-steered system, a 6-dB field-of-view (FoV) of 6.4° enables data rates > 70 Gbps. The combined RA-TA architecture supports bidirectional channels without additional UL lasers. The proposed signal interference cancellation (SIC) algorithm further enhances UL performance. Experimental results show signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) improvements up to 2.37 dB and potential UL throughput gains up to 45% for higher DL modulation depths.

title: Modality-Mix Learning: Promoting Multimodal Learning Through Multilabel Objective_supp1-3677806.pdf
authors: Nannan Lu
publish: N/A
abstract: Multimodal fusion provides a comprehensive way to understand the world by integrating data from different sources. However, some studies believe that due to the optimization imbalance, partial modalities cannot be fully learned during multimodal learning. They attempt to achieve the balance between different modalities by controlling their learning process but ignore the function of the learning objective as an essential factor. The uniform objective for all modalities leads to the network being unable to sufficiently exploit discriminative information from different modalities. Therefore, we propose a new multimodal learning method, namely, modality-mix learning (MM learning), aiming to promote the sufficient learning of each modality via the designed multilabel objective. MM learning generates modality-mixed samples by combining modalities of different samples with varied labels, transforming the single label of a sample into a probability vector representing multilabel information. These modality-mixed samples are then fed into the network, which is trained to recognize the varying proportions of multilabel information. In addition, we introduce a bilevel learning scheme, where the network is first trained using standard learning to capture general features and select samples with strong prediction, followed by MM learning on these selected samples to further optimize the subexploration modality. MM learning forces different objective information to be learned from different modalities, avoiding the insufficient learning of modalities caused by the uniform learning objective. The experimental results show that the method can significantly boost different fusion strategies and methods in diversified multimodal datasets and improve the robustness of multimodal networks as well.

title: Joint Optimization in Pinching-Antenna-Assisted NOMA-MEC Systems via DRL
authors: Yujie Ma, Wenqi Huang, Zhiguo Ding, Daniel K. C. So, Vimal Bhatia
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: This work investigates the integration of pinching antennas into latency-sensitive mobile edge computing (MEC) systems, where a hybrid non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) strategy is employed for task offloading to minimize energy consumption. An energy minimization problem is formulated to jointly optimize the pinching antenna position, transmit power, and time allocation. Due to the non-convexity caused by highly coupled optimization variables, an entropy-based deep reinforcement learning (DRL) approach is developed. Simulation results show that the proposed soft actor-critic (SAC) framework achieves near-optimal performance under dynamic channel conditions while maintaining stability and generalization. Meanwhile, with the integration of pinching antennas, hybrid NOMA outperforms other strategies in latency-critical scenarios.

title: Design of a Low-Profile Wideband Horizontally Polarized Omnidirectional-like Loop Antenna with Characteristic Mode Analysis
authors: Dazhi Piao, Juan Wang, Meiwei Zhang, Jiaojiao Song
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A low-profile wideband horizontally polarized (HP) omnidirectional-like loop antenna is proposed based on characteristic mode analysis (CMA). By analyzing the current distribution on a metal loop using CMA, the mode J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub> is observed to exhibit a HP radiation pattern. To effectively excite mode J<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sub>, nonperiodic and nonuniform slots are introduced into the loop structure to achieve capacitive loading, which enhances both the modal significance and the modal bandwidth. In order to further extend the bandwidth, a dual-loop structure is formulated by loading a parasitic loop. The proposed single-loop and dual-loop antennas are fabricated, and the measurement results show that -10 dB impedance bandwidths of 67.5% (2.75-5.55 GHz) and 88.7% (2.7-7.0 GHz) can be realized, respectively. The physical dimensions of the antennas are π × 28<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>× 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (0.26λ°)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.007λ°) for the single-loop and π × 30<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>× 0.8 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (π × (0.27λ°)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> × 0.007λ°) for the dual-loop design, where λ₀ denotes the free space wavelength at the lowest operating frequency. Furthermore, the relative bandwidth-to-area ratios of the two antennas reach 318 and 387%/λ°², respectively, which are significantly higher than those of the previously reported HP omnidirectional antennas.

title: A DQN-Guided Genetic Algorithm With Local Intensification for Agile Earth Observation Satellite Scheduling
authors: Jiang Zhu, Qingpei Hu, Jian Wu, Yonghao Du, Xiaolu Liu, Yanjie Song, Witold Pedrycz, Lining Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: The agile earth observation satellite scheduling problem with time-dependent transition constraints is a complex combinatorial optimization problem that has been proven NP-hard. To address this challenge, a Deep Q-network guided genetic algorithm with a local intensification strategy (DQN-LIGA) is proposed. First, we adopt a hybrid initialization method that combines greedy and random strategies to generate a high-quality and diverse initial population. Next, multiple crossover–mutation operator pairs are designed. A Deep Q-network is then employed as a policy model to adaptively select operator combinations based on individual and evolutionary states. Moreover, a simulated physical oscillation strategy (SPOS) is proposed to periodically perturb elite individuals, which is designed to refine solutions and strengthen local intensification throughout the evolutionary process. Finally, extensive experiments are conducted on 20 benchmark instances under both China and World scenarios. The results demonstrate that DQN-LIGA consistently achieves higher observation profits compared to state-of-the-art algorithms, and the integration of SPOS further improves solution quality, especially under complex scheduling conditions.

title: CVLS-ST: A Cross-View Long-Short Framework for Traffic Risk Prediction in Intelligent Urban Transportation Systems
authors: Yachao Yuan, Xingyu Chen, Zixiang Peng, Muting Li, Zhen Yu, Yingwen Wu, Thar Baker
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: As a cornerstone of smart city consumer ecosystems, accurate traffic accident risk prediction is vital for proactive safety services in intelligent transportation systems. However, current solutions often fail to integrate heterogeneous relational features (e.g., historical risk correlations) or effectively balance long-term periodic patterns with transient dynamics. To address these issues, we introduce a Cross-View Long-Short Spatial-Temporal Framework (CVLS-ST) for traffic risk prediction, which innovatively decouples long- and short-term dependencies to achieve more precise urban traffic risk prediction. Specifically, we design a cross-view attention graph network to effectively capture complex and diverse interactions across multiple relational graphs. Besides, we develop a Mamba block that incorporates state-space modeling into LSTM’s gating architecture to effectively capture global spatial semantics and temporal dependencies. Moreover, we introduce a state-inherited strategy to jointly and accurately model the long-term trends and short-term dynamics of traffic risk. Extensive experiments on two real-world datasets show that the F1-score of CVLS-ST outperforms the state-of-the-art by 20.37% on CHI and 5.83% on NYC, validating its effectiveness in complex urban environments.

title: iSeg: An Iterative Refinement-based Framework for Training-free Segmentation
authors: Lin Sun, Jiale Cao, Jin Xie, Fahad Shahbaz Khan, Yanwei Pang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Stable Diffusion has demonstrated strong image synthesis ability to given text descriptions, suggesting it to contain strong semantic clue for grouping objects. The researchers have explored employing Stable Diffusion for training-free segmentation. Most existing approaches refine cross-attention map by self-attention map once, demonstrating that self-attention map contains useful semantic information to improve segmentation. To fully utilize self-attention map, we present a deep experimental analysis on iteratively refining cross-attention map with self-attention map, and propose an effective iterative refinement framework for training-free segmentation, named iSeg. Our iSeg introduces an entropy-reduced self-attention module that utilizes a gradient descent scheme to reduce the entropy of self-attention map, thereby suppressing the weak responses corresponding to irrelevant global information. Leveraging the entropy-reduced self-attention module, our iSeg stably improves cross-attention map with iterative refinement. Further, we design a category-enhanced cross-attention module to generate accurate cross-attention map, providing a better initial input for iterative refinement. Extensive experiments across different datasets and diverse segmentation tasks (weakly-supervised semantic segmentation, open-vocabulary semantic segmentation, unsupervised segmentation, and mask generation on synthetic dataset) reveal the merits of proposed contributions, leading to promising performance. For unsupervised semantic segmentation on Cityscapes, our iSeg achieves an absolute gain of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.8\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> in terms of mIoU compared to the best existing training-free approach in literature. Moreover, our proposed iSeg can support segmentation with different kinds of images and interactions, and also be used as a post-processing, or in different frameworks, to improve training-free segmentation. The project is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://linsun449.github.io/iSeg</uri>.

title: MaliVD: Source Code Vulnerability Localization via Attention-Based Multi-Modal Learning
authors: Enze Dai, Shuo Wang, Xi Xiao, Qing Li, Sheng Wen, Tianqing Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Source code vulnerabilities represent a critical threat to software security, potentially leading to severe consequences such as data breaches and system failures. Traditional static analysis tools, while widely used, suffer from high false positive rates and struggle to adapt to the increasing complexity of modern software. Deep learning-based approaches hold promise for automated vulnerability detection, but they face challenges including limited dataset quality, inadequate feature extraction, and lack of precise vulnerability localization capabilities. To address these limitations, we propose MaliVD, a novel vulnerability detection method in source code that leverages a multi-modal attention mechanism. MaliVD not only identifies vulnerability types but also pinpoints the specific lines of code where vulnerabilities are triggered. The model extracts sequential, tree-based, and graph-based features from source code and employs specialized neural networks to learn these diverse representations. By strategically focusing on Points of Interest within the code, MaliVD effectively prioritizes potentially vulnerable code regions, enhancing both detection accuracy and localization precision. Experimental results show that when compared with eight advanced vulnerability detection models across three large datasets, MaliVD demonstrates superior vulnerability detection and localization capabilities, maintaining high F1 scores and localization precision. Particularly on the ReliVul dataset, the F1 score is improved by 21.82%, and the Top-5 localization accuracy is 18% higher than other methods, with lower false positives across six mainstream vulnerability types, validating MaliVD’s practical application value in real-world environments.

title: Retraction Notice: Spatio Temporal Feature-based Weapon and Violence Detection from Video Surveillance using Deep Learning
authors: Anitha G, Siddharth Shanker M, Durgam Muni Soma Sukrith, Loakeshvaran S K, Venkatraman M, Sadam Mohamed Usman

abstract: The increasing incidence of violence in public spaces has made developing efficient and accurate weapon detection systems necessary. A Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model built on the MobileNet structure has been proposed for the detection of violence and weapons. It examines each frame of a video stream to look for indications of violence or the presence of weapons. The proposed system is trained on a dataset of simulated violent incidents. The dataset includes fights, gunfights, knife attacks, and diverse environmental and lighting conditions. The system is excellent for use in surveillance and security systems and 93.86% accuracy is achieved. It can be implemented on embedded systems or mobile devices due to its high efficiency and minimal computing demands. The proposed method can increase public safety by notably increasing the efficiency of security and surveillance systems.

title: Hybrid Anomaly-Based Intrusion Detection System for SDN-Enabled IoT Networks
authors: Olha Shpur, Taras Havryliv, Andriy Luntovskyy
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments has increased the scale and complexity of modern network infrastructures, making them vulnerable to diverse distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks, including high-volume floods and low-intensity stealthy behaviors. Traditional signature-based intrusion detection approaches are often ineffective in such environments due to traffic heterogeneity, dynamic attack patterns, and limited computational capabilities of IoT devices. This paper presents a hybrid anomaly-based intrusion detection system for Software-Defined Networking (SDN)–enabled IoT networks that combines three complementary detection mechanisms: entropy-based traffic source distribution analysis, protocol-level heuristic inspection of TCP SYN traffic, and a machine learning–based behavioral detector using the Isolation Forest algorithm. All detection components are deployed within the SDN controller, enabling centralized traffic visibility and real-time telemetry processing without introducing computational overhead on resource-constrained IoT devices. A majority-voting scheme is employed to aggregate partial decisions from individual detectors and improve robustness against diverse attack characteristics. The proposed system is implemented using the Ryu SDN controller and evaluated in a cloud-based experimental environment under multiple traffic scenarios, including normal IoT operation, SYN flood and UDP flood attacks, botnet-like distributed traffic, and low-intensity stealth attacks. Experimental results show that the hybrid approach effectively detects volumetric and certain low-rate attack patterns while maintaining stable behavior during normal traffic conditions. The study highlights the benefits of combining heterogeneous detection methods within an SDN architecture and discusses limitations related to distributed botnet traffic and threshold selection.

title: Enhancement of User Query Processing in Distributed Databases
authors: Olena Hordiichuk-Bublivska, Oksana Urikova, Andrii Masiuk, Oleksandr Androsiuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper examines the problem of optimizing data processing in recommender systems operating within distributed PostgreSQL databases. It presents a systematic multi-layer SQL optimization framework that integrates indexing, materialized views, execution-level monitoring, and physical resource analysis. Experiments were conducted in a distributed PostgreSQL environment using key performance metrics, including execution time, CPU utilization, disk I/O operations, and system throughput. The experimental results demonstrate substantial performance improvements: query execution time decreased by 98%, CPU utilization was reduced by more than 50% (from 93.5% to 46%), and disk read operations were completely eliminated. System throughput increased from 9.26 million to 529.65 million rows per second. The proposed approach provides both theoretical and practical insights into the construction of high-performance distributed recommender systems, especially in cloud-based architectures. The results underscore the importance of multi-layer optimization and provide a foundation for future work involving stream analytics, automated ETL/ELT pipelines, and multi-cloud performance evaluation.

title: A Canvas Framework for Participatory Design of Cultural Products Using Generative AI
authors: Pruet Putjorn, Pattayaporn Unroj, Pollavat Prapattong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper introduces the “HHAI Canvas,” a novel participatory design tool for co-creating culturally grounded products using Generative AI (GenAI). The framework supports community engagement in the design of physical handicraft products by linking cultural identity (“Heart”), artisan skill (“Hand”), and AI-driven ideation (“AI Harmony”). The canvas guides local participants to articulate key product insights through storytelling, sketching, and coevaluation, enabling GenAI to serve as a tool for cultural expression rather than a replacement for human creativity. We demonstrate the framework's application in a Chiang Saen community workshop in Northern Thailand, where artisans applied the canvas to create new textile, food, and craft product ideas. The study utilized the Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model to generate high-fidelity visualizations while maintaining subject consistency. The resulting designs preserved cultural essence while gaining appeal for contemporary markets. The study positions the canvas as a scalable boundary object for community-based innovation, showing how local knowledge and AI can be harmonized to support sustainable design and cultural continuity.

title: Wearable Antenna Design for Medical WBAN Applications Supporting Next-Generation Wireless Systems (5G/6G)
authors: Ahmed Kudhair Daraj, Mahmood Farhan Mosleh, Raed Abd Alhameed, Ghalib Najm Radad, Inas Fadhil Jaleel
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative wearable antenna design for fifth and sixth generation wireless communications. The proposed antenna is designed for Wireless Body Area Networks (WBAN) and telemetry devices specific to biomedical applications. The principal challenge facing researchers is the flexible configuration and dimensions of antennas in relation to efficiency. The suggested antenna is made up of a single element with a unique rectangular shape. It is built on a Rogers RT/duroid substrate that is 0.29 mm thick and lossy. The dimensions of the proposed antennas are 10 mm x 10 mm for both the ground and substrate. The patch measures 4.12 mm by 3.13 mm. The proposed design features curves on either side of the antenna. Two circles in the antenna corner serve as pattern ratio balances that ascertain the optimal dimensions and positioning. Three slots are optimized to attain the requisite bands for 5G and 6G functionality. Three frequency bands have been designated: the initial band at 28 GHz for 5G applications and the subsequent bands at 68 GHz and 80 GHz for 6G applications. Furthermore, the ideal design optimizes both gain and bandwidth, achieving a VSWR of 1.006 and a gain of 9.75 dBi. The innovative design addresses the primary issue of varying antenna parameters due to human motion.

title: Retraction Notice: Smart Access Control System For Covid Safety Smart Access Control System during Covid
authors: S G Rahul, D Subitha, S Velmurugan, R Chitra, Avinash Reddy, Grande Nishanth

abstract: During the period of prevailing unsettled COVID pandemic, the countries and states started to plan reopening during which necessitates the non-contact temperature evaluation gadgets as a part of a preliminary look at access points to identify the humans with elevated body temperatures. Despite the utilization of these devices, temperature assessment restricted the impact on lowering the spread of COVID-19. Non-contact temperature measuring devices are used to measure the temperature of any person. Detection of a high temperature is one huge manner to pick out a person who might also have COVID-19 contamination. In this project, a room environment is created in which certain precautions are taken. A laser diode and receiver are used to detect the entrance of a person, and the system also detects the body temperature of the entering person. If the temperature is less than a threshold temperature entry for the person is permitted or else the entry is denied. This system also has a feature where it permits only a pre-determined number of persons inside the room. It also facilities to view the allowed temperature, the number of people to be allowed in the room and the number of people present actively using a Bluetooth App. This system aimed to be useful to combat the spread of COVID infections.

title: Joint Degradation-Aware Arbitrary-Scale Super-Resolution for Variable-Rate Extreme Image Compression
authors: Xinning Chai, Zhengxue Cheng, Xin Li, Rong Xie, Li Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Broadcasting
abstract: Recent diffusion-based extreme image compression methods have demonstrated remarkable performance at ultra-low bitrates (0.1<0.1 bpp). However, most approaches require training separate diffusion models for each target bitrate, resulting in substantial computational overhead and hindering practical deployment. Meanwhile, recent studies have shown that joint super-resolution can serve as an effective approach for enhancing low-bitrate reconstruction. However, when moving toward ultra-low-bitrate regimes, these methods struggle due to severe information loss, and their reliance on fixed super-resolution scales prevents flexible adaptation across diverse bitrates. To address these limitations, we propose ASSR-EIC, a novel image compression framework that leverages arbitrary-scale super-resolution (ASSR) to support variable-rate extreme image compression (EIC). An arbitrary-scale downsampling module is introduced at the encoder side to provide controllable rate reduction, while a diffusion-based, joint degradation-aware ASSR decoder enables rate-adaptive reconstruction within a single model. We exploit the compression- and rescaling-aware diffusion prior to guide the reconstruction, yielding high-fidelity and high-realism restoration across diverse compression and rescaling settings. Specifically, we design a global compression–rescaling adaptor that offers holistic guidance for rate adaptation, and a local compression–rescaling modulator that dynamically balances generative and fidelity-oriented behaviors to achieve fine-grained, bitrate-adaptive detail restoration. To further enhance reconstruction quality, we introduce a dual semantic-enhanced design. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ASSR-EIC delivers state-of-the-art performance in extreme image compression while simultaneously supporting flexible bitrate control and adaptive rate-dependent reconstruction.

title: IPM-Net: An Interpretable Partial-level Matching Network for Few-shot Fine-grained Classification in Optical Remote Sensing Images
authors: Kaiwen Zhu, Song Qiu, Jingyi Cao, Yanan You, Yuanyuan Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep learning has enabled AI-driven fine-grained object classification in optical remote sensing imagery with remarkable precision. However, insufficient training samples critically hinder their performance in data-scarce scenarios such as military operations and natural disaster response, significantly diminishing the practical utility of deep learning-based approaches. Consequently, few-shot fine-grained classification methods have garnered substantial attention due to their superior ability in knowledge learning and transfer under limited data conditions. Current approaches prioritize discriminative feature extraction and enhancement, but lack mathematical definition of fine-grained features and an explainable analysis of how these features influence decision-making processes. This deficiency prevents models from explicitly identifying critical features for knowledge transfer. In response to this limitation, we introduce the Interpretable Partial-level Matching Network (IPM-Net), the main goal is to match the query target’s partials in the support set, thus, building and visualizing the decision-making process. This network consists of three modules: a Partial-level Game-theoretic Interpretable Module(PGI), Cross-pose Semantic Alignment Module(CSA), and Refine-and-Rediscover Module(RaR). Experiments on a variety of fine-grained datasets—both remote sensing and natural images—confirm IPM-Net’s superiority in accuracy and interpretability. The source code is publicly available on GitHub: https://github.com/Silver2kw/IPM-Net.

title: Embedded Vibration Monitoring and Robust Closed-Loop Current Demodulation Using an Eight-State Square-Wave Scheme in Spun QWP-Based Reflective FOCS
authors: Jundong Tian, Shunyang Liu, Wei Li, Li Xia
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Spun quarter-wave plates (QWPs) can be co-fabricated and packaged with spun fiber sensing coils, which reduces fusion splices and improves device consistency. Their distributed birefringence and extended length also increase sensitivity to mechanical vibration. In reflective all-fiber optic current sensors (FOCS), vibration can change the interferometric visibility. This change acts as a time-varying equivalent closed-loop gain and can affect AC current demodulation. We develop an analytical polarization transfer model for a spun QWP and describe vibration as a time-varying perturbation of the dimensionless rotation parameter. We then propose an eight-state square-wave modulation with synchronous three-channel demodulation. Within one modulation period, it yields a current error signal, a half-wave-voltage drift observable, and a visibility observable. The visibility-related channel can be used to track the vibration-induced waveform. It can also be used to normalize the current error so that the effective loop gain is less sensitive to visibility changes. Tests from 0 to 30 Hz indicate that, under these low-frequency conditions, the sensing-coil phase disturbance is small in the reflective configuration. The dominant effect is visibility variation associated with the spun QWP. With the proposed normalization, the mean standard deviation of the extracted AC-current peaks and valleys decreases from 0.27 A to 0.094 A at 50 Hz and from 0.28 A to 0.089 A at 100 Hz. The method does not require additional components and can improve demodulation robustness in vibratory environments.

title: DPMVectorNet: End-to-End Ship Detection, Heading, and Velocity Estimation via Dual-Point Motion Vectors in Satellite Imagery
authors: Xiunan Li, Peng Chen, Haitian Zhu, Lei Huang, Wentao An, Ling Zhu, Gang Zheng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Ship motion analysis using remote sensing imagery is essential for maritime surveillance, traffic monitoring, and ocean management. Existing optical detection methods, however, have primarily concentrated on identifying static ship targets through horizontal or rotated bounding boxes, and therefore cannot effectively estimate dynamic attributes such as heading and velocity. In this work, we develop DPMVectorNet (Dual-Point Motion Vector Network), an end-to-end learning framework designed for the joint estimation of ship position, heading, and speed from medium-resolution multi-temporal satellite imagery. Specifically, the framework is evaluated on multi-temporal ASTER VNIR data (15 m spatial resolution), in which temporally adjacent observations are combined to construct RGB composite images, which are then processed by a convolutional neural network to capture and fuse multi-scale spatial features. Ship motion is represented through the temporal matching of two bow keypoints, from which the movement direction is derived, while the velocity is computed from their displacement and the corresponding imaging interval. Experiments conducted on multi-temporal ASTER data show that DPMVectorNet achieves an F1-score above 90% for ship detection, with a mean heading error below 2°, and a velocity deviation within 1 m/s when compared with Automatic Identification System (AIS) records. These results demonstrate that the proposed framework enables accurate ship detection and reliable heading and velocity estimation from medium-resolution imagery by effectively exploiting multi-temporal information.

title: FedSecureFormer: A Fast, Federated and Secure Transformer Framework for Lightweight Intrusion Detection in Connected and Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Devika Sathyan, Vishnu Hari, Pratik Narang, Tejasvi Alladi, F. Richard Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The era of Connected and Autonomous Vehicles (CAVs) reflects a significant milestone in transportation, improving safety, efficiency, and intelligent navigation. However, the growing reliance on real-time communication, constant connectivity, and autonomous decision-making raises serious cybersecurity concerns, particularly in environments with limited resources. This highlights the need for security solutions that are efficient, lightweight, and suitable for deployment in real-world vehicular environments. In this work, we introduce FedSecureFormer, a lightweight transformer-based model with 1.7 million parameters, significantly smaller than most encoder-only transformer architectures. The model is designed for efficient and accurate cyber attack detection, achieving 93.69% classification accuracy across 19 attack types and improving performance on 10 attack classes, outperforming several state-of-the-art (SOTA) methods. To assess its practical viability, we implemented the model within a Federated Learning (FL) setup using the FedAvg aggregation strategy. We also incorporated differential privacy to enhance data protection. For testing its generalization to unseen attacks, we used a histogram-guided GAN with LSTM and attention modules to generate unseen data, achieving 88% detection accuracy. Notably, the model achieved an inference time of 3.7775 milliseconds per vehicle on the Jetson Nano, approximately 100× faster than SOTA models. These results position FedSecureFormer as a fast, scalable, and privacy-preserving solution for securing future intelligent transport systems.

title: High Input Impedance Chopper-stabilized Analog Front-end Based on Variable-capacitor Calibration for Bio-consumer Electronics
authors: Feng Yan, Jingjing Liu, Weijie Ge, Kangkang Sun, Xinying Su, Junhao He
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Analog front-end (AFE) circuits for bio-consumer electronics require ultra-high input impedance to ensure robust bio-potential acquisition under interference. This paper presents a chopping-stabilized AFE featuring an automatic input-impedance enhancement technique based on a positive feedback loop (PFL) with a continuously tunable variable-capacitor. An oscillation-detection-based calibration loop dynamically adjusts the PFL to compensate front-end parasitic capacitance, driving the system from an unstable condition to an optimal stable boundary with maximized input impedance while avoiding over-compensation. To improve calibration robustness, a frequency ripple suppression scheme is introduced, enabling precise control of the variable-capacitor independent of input frequency variations. The proposed variable-capacitance technique provides femtofarad-level resolution without relying on discrete capacitor arrays, achieving wide tuning range and high linearity. Fabricated in a 0.18 μm CMOS process, the AFE achieves an input impedance of 46.3 GΩ at 50 Hz, representing an 83× improvement over conventional approaches. The prototype occupies 0.71 mm², achieves a mid-band gain of 40 dB, an input-referred noise of 0.93 μV<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</sub>, and a power consumption of 3.48 μW. Experimental EEG and ECG measurements using comfortable dry electrodes validate the effectiveness of the proposed AFE for bio-consumer electronic applications.

title: Zero-Shot Low-Light Enhancement via Coupled Retinex-Scattering Frequency Flows
authors: Ayan Sar, Chiranjit Dutta, Tanupriya Choudhury
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: The letter presents a new zero-shot low-light image enhancement framework, Retinex-Scattering Frequency Flows (RSFF), which is based on signal processing theory instead of black-box deep learning. RSFF combines a partial differential equation (PDE)-based Retinex decomposition with a multi-scale wavelet-scattering frequency flow to cross-scale propagation of priors in illumination. In contrast to data-intensive supervised networks or computationally expensive diffusion models, RSFF trains physically interpretable operators in a per-image and zero-shot way without paired training examples. We give guarantees of energy conservation and stability of deformation in the scattering domain through theory. Numerous experiments on four benchmarks show that RSFF sets state-of-the-art zero-shot performances at 21.8 dB PSNR on the LOL dataset, outperforming the state-of-the-art zero-shot algorithms by 2.9 dB and matching the state-of-the-art supervised performance, yet RSFF is <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$160 \times$</tex-math></inline-formula> faster than current diffusion-based methods.

title: GOF-Based DMI Integrated Solution to Seamless Navigation for Autonomous Vehicles During GNSS Outages
authors: Wenhui Yang, Yong Li, Chris Rizos
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Autonomous vehicles rely on the integration of inertial navigation system (INS) and global navigation satellite system (GNSS) for most transportation applications. However, GNSS signals are often degraded in challenging environments such as urban canyons and tunnels, etc. To achieve seamless navigation with continuity and safety, it is necessary to deploy an auxiliary or backup navigation system that does not rely on GNSS. This paper proposes a Dual Micro-electro-mechanical INS (DMI) architecture comprising both rotating and fixed inertial measurement units (IMUs), and utilizes vehicle chassis information to aid the INS during GNSS outages. In terms of multi-sensor fusion, traditional optimal multi-sensor fusion takes the Centralized Kalman Filter (CKF) as a global optimal criterion. The Decentralized Kalman Filter (DKF), such as the well-known Federated Kalman Filter (FKF), aims to achieve the same accuracy as a CKF, while reducing the computational load. This paper innovatively applies an improved decentralized filtering method - Global Optimal Filter (GOF), and develops a GOF-based DMI integrated solution. This solution optimizes state estimation by fully utilizing multi-source data through spatial projection and orthogonalization. The field test results have demonstrated the GOF-based DMI integrated solution has significant advantages in most evaluation metrics. Specifically, it achieves accuracy improvements of 29.1% in position, 30% in velocity, 22.2% in attitude, and 58.3% in heading over the CKF-based DMI integrated solution. Compared to the FKF-based DMI integrated solution, the accuracy improvements are even more substantial: 70.4% in position, 36.3% in velocity, 70.5% in horizontal attitude, and 72.5% in heading. In addition, the introduction of extended KITTI evaluation metrics also indicates that the GOF-based DMI integration solution has significant advantages compared to the other two solutions.

title: AdaSch: Load-Adaptive Scheduling for Accelerating Real-Time Edge Video Analysis With Diverse Inference Modes
authors: Zheyi Chen, Jia Wen, Junjie Zhang, Lexi Xu, Jie Li, Geyong Min
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Inference with spatio-temporal filtering plays a vital role in accelerating emerging edge video analysis. However, most existing solutions rely on a single inference mode, limiting their ability to cope with the heterogeneity and dynamic nature of edge systems. While combining multiple inference modes can enhance the real-time performance of video analysis, achieving efficient load scheduling across these modes remains challenging due to diverse device configurations and varying task requests. To address this important challenge, we propose AdaSch, a novel load-adaptive scheduling framework designed to accelerate real-time edge video analysis using diverse inference modes. First, we design a new inference mode selection mechanism based on Region-of-Interest (RoI) features in different scenarios of video analysis. Next, we develop a Model Predictive Control (MPC)-based RoI scheduling method, which predicts continuous RoI sequences and obtains the optimal scheduling using a linear programming solver. Finally, we design a probabilistic sparse self-attention mechanism to capture server-side load patterns, enabling inference scheduling with optimal load balancing by solving a relaxed convex optimization problem. Extensive experiments on the real-world testbed and video datasets demonstrate the superiority of AdaSch. Compared to state-of-the-art methods, AdaSch improves accuracy and Service Level Objective (SLO) satisfaction rate by approximately 19.20% and 42.94%, respectively. Moreover, AdaSch exhibits excellent adaptiveness across various scenarios while maintaining minimal overheads.

title: Design of a Physics-Informed Neural Network for PCF-Based Multi-Analyte Biosensors
authors: Kawsar Ahmed, Md Mamun Ali, Md. Shohidullah, Francis M. Bui, Li Chen
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: The integration of deep learning (DL) and photonic crystal fiber (PCF)-based multi-analyte detection biosensors is increasingly used to improve measurement specificity in many crucial applications and to enhance detection sensitivity to the micro-range. For the first time in this paper, we introduce the Physics-Informed Neural Network (PINN) for four different multi-analyte biosensors, enabling detection and analysis with a single model. The model was trained on various datasets and input features (IF) for each biosensor. The proposed PINN model shows the lowest mean square error (MSE) of 0.0022(±0.0002) and the mean absolute error (MAE) of 0.0122(±0.0010). The performance R2 of the PINN model for four different biosensors is 0.9238(±0.0113), 0.9788(±0.0031), 0.9560(±0.0112), and 0.9883(±0.0025), respectively. A noticeable trend is that larger datasets lead to better model performance. The proposed PINN model is competent and flexible for designing and optimizing four different applied multi-analyte biosensors and can be used to effectively design and optimize other multi-analyte biosensors.

title: Reduced On-Resistance and Jitter of GaAs PCSS with Low Optical Energy in Quasi-Linear Mode
authors: Xule Li, Song Li, Jinhong Wei, Fanzheng Zeng, Chenglin Jia, Quan Sun, Lin Penghao, Baoliang Qian
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters
abstract: In this letter, the effect of the trigger optical pulse on the switching performances of a gallium arsenide photoconductive semiconductor switch (GaAs PCSS) under quasi-linear mode is analyzed, both numerically and experimentally. It is found that, under identical optical pulse conditions, the PCSS operates in a unique quasi-linear mode under side incidence, with a 30% reduction in on-state resistance and an 83% reduction in time jitter compared to conventional front incidence. The positive effect of the quasi-linear mode on switching performance is attributed to the uniform illumination and absorption achieved by the device, resulting in a more uniform distribution of photogenerated carriers along the current filament channel.

title: CarbonWise CX: An Agentic AI Framework for Carbon-Aware Customer Support Analytics Using RAG and LLM-Based Code Generation
authors: B.M. Beena, Thotapalli Sri Surya Manideep, Sneha Saragadam, Sailesh Rathi, Ramaswamy Ramesh, Vijay Holimath
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Organizations accumulate sufficient amounts of structured and unstructured customer support data, but current systems like SQL queries and fixed dashboards still generate actionable insights on a delay, increasing operational expenses, and inefficient use of cloud energies. This project aims at developing a cloud-native, agentic AI-driven analytics system that can be used to improve the decision-making processes of enterprises without violating the principles of green cloud computing. The proposed system is called CarbonWise CX, which incorporates Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to perform qualitative reasoning, the dynamic generation of Python code, which entails the use of LLM when making quantitative decisions, and cloud-deployed versions that are scalable. The queries in natural language are handled via, agentic decision layer that automatically identifies which analytics workflow to choose. One of such innovations is the carbon-conscious cloud routing, the dynamic choice of the execution areas according to the current carbon intensity, with maintained latency and SLA limitations. The system with a deployed infrastructure on AWS EC2 will exhibit a 34.6 percent reduction in the number of carbon emissions per query in the operation using a modular architecture and on multi-region workloads simulated in the BAR. Also, 8,469 actual customer support records were analyzed resulting in 62.3 percent less manual tasks and 41.8 percent faster query response time than traditional workflows. The results of CarbonWise CX yield to achieve a better SLA compliance (29.5% of the significant improvement), to optimize cloud resource usage (36.7% of the significant improvement), and to minimize the work of the analyst (they were decreased significantly). The architecture shows how agentic AI and green cloud computing can provide intelligent and scalable, as well as environmentally friendly customer experience analytics. The article corresponds to the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption and Production), SDG 13 (Climate Action).

title: Recent Advancements in Course Recommendation Systems in the Era of Large Language Models (2022–2025)
authors: Satyam Mittal, Ishita Malhotra
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Course Recommendation Systems (CRS) help learners choose courses aligned with goals, yet face cold-start, limited interpretability, prerequisite feasibility, and fairness challenges. This review synthesizes peer-reviewed course recommendation research from 2022–2025 and reports improvements strictly within each study (baseline→best) to avoid invalid cross-paper pooling across datasets and protocols. The surveyed methods span session/set recommendation, temporal and knowledge graphs (KGs) augmented sequence models, and fairness-aware multi-graph learning, alongside CRS-adjacent large language models (LLMs)-based retrieval and re-ranking patterns discussed in recent education recommendation pipelines. We further refine the taxonomy with peer-reviewed evidence on prerequisite-aware recommendation, data-quality-aware KGs, and interpretable CRS grounded in catalog text or graph paths. Lastly, we summarize reported deployment considerations (retrieval→re-ranking, indexing, summarization, quantization) and identify open challenges in offline–online alignment, feasibility/data-quality reporting, and privacy-preserving, auditable deployments.

title: FlockCounter: Automated Counting for Large-Scale Bird Flocks
authors: Tianjiang Hu, Weiming Yan, Zijie Sun
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Automated bird counting is crucial for ecological assessment, yet current methods often lack accuracy with large-scale flocks. This paper proposes and develops FlockCounter, a novel approach for end-to-end flock counting that relies solely on synthetic training data. Our method consists of three stages. Labeled synthetic data of large-scale flocks is automatically generated by using a collective behavioral model with only a limited set of real-world bird flock trajectories. Each annotated image is sequentially enhanced into the visual realism via style transfer. Finally, we train a customized deep learning network exclusively on synthetic data to achieve accurate bird flock counting. As demonstrated in the testing results, FlockCounter achieves state-of-the-art cross-domain performance on real-world datasets, attaining 91.3% accuracy on ground-based images and 92.3% on aerial-view images, substantially outperforming existing methods. This work provides a scalable, annotation-free solution for accurate large-scale bird flock counting, supporting efficient ecological monitoring and conservation efforts.

title: Dual-Attention Weighting for Multi-Agent Collaborative Perception
authors: Wei Li, Lin Ma, Run Zhang, Longteng Huang, Hao Huang, Danyang Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Multi-agent collaborative perception has shown significant potential in improving environmental awareness, especially in complex and dynamic environments where it enables a more precise and comprehensive understanding. However, existing collaborative perception systems still face several challenges, particularly during feature fusion, where issues such as information redundancy and noise interference arise. To address these challenges, this paper introduces the Dual-Attention Weighting Collaborative Perception (DAW-CoPe) framework. The framework integrates dilated convolutional techniques within the Multi-Scale Spatial Attention Module (MSAM), allowing for the capture of multi-dimensional object features across different scales and the effective integration of richer contextual information, thereby enhancing the model’s perceptual capabilities. Furthermore, the incorporation of local convolutions strengthens the model’s ability to extract fine-grained features, improving its capacity to capture local details during feature fusion and reducing the risk of losing important information. To further enhance perception accuracy, we introduce a dual-attention weighting (DAW) mechanism: a same-level, learnable gating module that jointly reweights channel semantics and multi-scale spatial cues, thereby emphasizing informative channels and critical spatial regions. By suppressing redundant and noisy signals, this mechanism delivers substantial gains in performance and robustness across perception tasks. Experimental results demonstrate that DAW-CoPe outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods on three public datasets, significantly boosting the accuracy, reliability, and adaptability of collaborative perception systems. The code associated with this research will be made publicly available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/HIT-K/DAW-CoPe</uri>

title: Evolutionary Stackelberg Routing for Social Cost Reduction in Traffic Networks
authors: Andrea Bertolini, Lorenzo Castelli, Raffaele Pesenti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: An evolutionary Stackelberg framework for a two-population routing game is proposed, in which a boundedly rational central controller manages a fleet of vehicles sharing the network with selfish drivers. The controller assigns routes to minimize social cost by managing her fleet via the E-Aloof strategy, a low-information, adaptive rule that requires only the observation of the instantaneous traffic distribution and no memory of past states; E-Aloof is an evolutionary extension of the greedy, static Aloof strategy. The drivers of the remaining vehicles behave selfishly and myopically, adapting routes according to the Stackelberg Replicator Dynamic by comparing their travel costs with those of randomly sampled vehicles. The work formulates the problem as a nonatomic routing game on a two-parallel-link network. It establishes the existence of asymptotically stable equilibria under standard assumptions of continuously differentiable, nondecreasing, convex cost functions. The conditions under which centralization improves performance are derived, showing that while Aloof is beneficial when at least half of the vehicles are centralized, E-Aloof requires fewer centralized vehicles depending on the cost functions. For linear costs, conditions are found under which the equilibria coincide with those of a full-information Stackelberg game.

title: EAP-LSTM: A Bi-LSTM-based Deep Learning Framework for Quantitatively Predicting Enhancer Activity in Drosophila and Human Cell Lines_supp1-3620986.pdf
authors: Hao Wu
publish: N/A
abstract: Enhancer activity plays a critical role in gene regulation, influencing various biological processes such as development and disease progression. Accurate prediction of enhancer activity is essential for understanding the mechanisms underlying gene regulation and enhancer function. This study introduces a novel deep learning framework, EAP-LSTM (Enhancer Activity Prediction based on Bi-LSTM), to quantitatively predict enhancer activity across different species and cell lines. The model integrates multiple feature modules, including Word2Vec-based representations of DNA sequences, reverse complement k-mer, mismatch k-mer features, and epigenomic data. Evaluated on six cell lines, including five human cell lines (A549, HCT116, HepG2, K562, and MCF-7) and one Drosophila cell line (S2), EAP-LSTM consistently outperforms state-of-the-art models, such as DeepSTARR and HEAP, in all datasets. For example, on the K562 dataset, EAP-LSTM achieves a Pearson correlation coefficient (PCC) of 0.7944, outperforming DeepSTARR and HEAP by 13.65% and 2.73%, respectively. In addition, EAP-LSTM demonstrates strong performance in small-sample learning scenarios, showing clear improvements compared with baseline models. Furthermore, the study investigates the role of transcription factor binding sites (TFBSs) within enhancer regions, identifying critical motifs associated with enhancer activity. These findings not only improve enhancer prediction accuracy but also provide valuable insights into the molecular mechanisms underlying enhancer function.

title: AquaFed: Ascending Quantized Federated Learning on Heterogeneous Devices
authors: Juntao Hu, Hong Huang, Kuan Liu, Huimin Lu, Bingyi Liu, Dapeng Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables privacy-preserving collaboration across distributed edge devices but faces severe challenges in cross-device settings due to stringent memory and computation constraints. While quantization offers a natural remedy, existing techniques primarily target inference acceleration and fail to improve training efficiency. Consequently, current quantized FL frameworks only mitigate communication overhead while suffering from memory inefficiency during on-device training. Moreover, they exhibit Heterogeneity-induced optimization instability, as devices with varying bit-widths jointly participate in federated learning, introducing inconsistent quantization noise that hinders convergence and degrades final accuracy. In this work, we propose AquaFed, a memory-efficient Ascending quantized Federated learning framework tailored for heterogeneous edge environments. We first introduce Memory-Efficient Quantization (MEQ), which quantizes both weights and activation caches during training to drastically reduce peak memory usage and memory I/O. To address instability arising from heterogeneous precision, we theoretically establish the Quantization Noise Equivalence (QNE) Hypothesis: Quantization error exhibits a behavior analogous to the stochastic noise induced by a large learning rate in quantized federated learning. Guided by QNE, AquaFed adopts an ascending quantization schedule, allowing low-bit devices to lead early coarse-grained learning, while high-bit devices refine the model in later stages for stable convergence. Extensive experiments on vision and language benchmarks demonstrate that AquaFed consistently delivers superior accuracy, generalization, and memory efficiency compared to state-of-the-art methods. On CIFAR-10 with ResNet-18, AquaFed achieves a 20% accuracy improvement and 39% memory saving over the best existing framework.
