abstract: This paper discusses the advantages of using passive antenna arrays in the ground segment of a communications system for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). The focus of this paper was on matching methods for rectangular patch elements in antenna arrays. It was noted that there are no universal methods that would simultaneously ensure antenna array matching over a wide frequency band without changing its polarization characteristics. This paper presents the design of a 4x4 right-handed circular polarization (RHCP) antenna array, comprised of passive director patch elements for the 4.9-6.1 GHz operating frequency range. Thanks to the developed power supply circuit of the radiating elements, a stable VSWR value of less than 1.5 was achieved over the entire operating frequency range. The antenna array gain reaches 19.5 dB at high frequencies with an average half-power beamwidth (HPBW) of 13 degrees.

title: A Knowledge-Driven Framework for Cross-Border-Logistics Innovation: A Case Study from Southeast Asia
authors: Krittawaya Thongkoo, Sixian Lu, Kannika Daungcharone, Khanita Tumphasuwan
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Cross-border logistics in Southeast Asia continues to face inefficiencies arising from fragmented regulations, limited infrastructure, and heterogeneous information systems. This study proposes a knowledge-driven innovation framework that integrates digital platforms with structured knowledge processes to support cross-border logistics operations. Using a Thailand-based logistics provider as a case study, a web-based logistics platform was developed to connect Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) and Transportation Management System (TMS) modules with IoTenabled tracking and multi-channel notifications, thereby enhancing real-time information flow and operational visibility. The platform supports automated reporting, standardized supplier data integration, and real-time stakeholder communication. Based on system prototyping, stakeholder surveys, and performance benchmarking, the evaluation indicates an observed reduction of approximately 15 % in data processing time, reporting accuracy of 98 %, and a 20 % increase in user satisfaction. The findings suggest that embedding knowledge-sharing and reuse mechanisms within digital logistics platforms may support more consistent coordination and organizational learning in cross-border transport contexts. This case provides empirical insight into how logistics providers can align knowledge management with digital innovation to enhance transparency and operational resilience in dynamic regional trade environments.

title: Adaptive Reinforcement Learning–Based Test Selection for Improving Web Application Testing Accuracy
authors: Anna Kovalova
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents an approach for improving the accuracy of automated testing of web applications through adaptive test scenario prioritization using reinforcement learning. The proposed model enables an agent to learn from past test execution data and dynamically adjust its prioritization strategy to detect defects earlier under limited testing budgets. An experimental study was conducted on three open-source web applications using real test suites and several baseline selection strategies, including random choice, change-based prioritization, and coverage-based ordering. Quantitative evaluation, based on the proportion of defects detected within a fixed subset of tests, the average rate of early fault identification, and mutation testing performance, demonstrates that the proposed reinforcement learning method achieves significantly higher accuracy and faster defect localization compared to traditional approaches. These improvements were further validated using the Wilcoxon statistical test. The results highlight the potential of reinforcement learning for building adaptive test prioritization systems in modern web applications and for integrating such methods into CI/CD pipelines.

title: Advancing Afghanistan Mineral Stone Classification Using Hybrid CNNs with Attention and Texture Learning
authors: Iqbal Samsoor, Siam Yamsaengsung, Mohammad Shuaib Zarinkhail
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This research developed an automated deeplearning system that classifies Afghan mineral stones from images. We created a novel, high-quality dataset of 5,840 images covering 42 mineral types, collected with formal permission from the Afghan Ministry of Mines and Petroleum. Our model is a hybrid that combines two pretrained backbones (ResNet50 and EfficientNetB3) and is enhanced with dual attention modules (channel and self-attention) plus a dedicated texture feature extractor to emphasize color, structure, and subtle surface textures that are key for distinguishing similarlooking stones. Both backbones were fine-tuned by training only their last two residual blocks to adapt to the dataset while keeping training efficient and reducing overfitting. On the held-out test set, the best model achieved 99.39% accuracy. The proposed system can help make mineral identification more accessible and efficient for the mining sector in Afghanistan and other resource-rich regions.

title: Deep Learning towards Intrusion Detection in IoT
authors: Olena Semenova, Andrii Semenov, Olha Voitsekhovska, Andrii Dzhus, Volodymyr Martyniuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the Internet of Things has transformed digital ecosystems by interconnecting a vast number of devices. However, this connectivity also introduces significant security risks, particularly vulnerability to sophisticated cyber threats. This study investigates the application of deep learning methodologies for the development of intelligent Intrusion Detection Systems in IoT environments. Three neural network architectures – Feedforward Neural Networks, Convolutional Neural Networks and Recurrent Neural Networks were implemented and compared in MATLAB to evaluate their effectiveness in detecting abnormal patterns and unauthorized access. Furthermore, a genetic algorithm optimization procedure was applied in MATLAB to enhance a model performance by fine-tuning network parameters. The study also outlines potential directions for future research in securing IoT infrastructures.

title: Retraction Notice: Prediction of Air Pollution by using Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: Jayant Kumar Singh, Amit Kumar Goel
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: controlling and defensive the higher air greatness has gotten one in everything about first imperative occasions in different creating and metropolitan districts at the present. The greatness of air is adversely contacting collectible to the different styles of tainting influenced through the transportation, power, powers consumptions, and so forth. In our country population is a big problem as day by day population is increasing, so the rapid increasing in population and economic upswing is leading environment problems in city like air pollution, water pollution etc. In some of air pollution and air pollution is direct impact on human body. As we know that major pollutants are arising from Nitrogen Oxide, Carbon Monoxide & Particulate matter (PM), SO2 etc. Carbon Monoxide is arising due to the deficient Oxidization of propellant like as petroleum, gas, etc. nitrogen oxide (NO) is arising due to the ignition of thermal fuel;Sulphur Dioxide(So2) is major spread in air, So2 is a gas which is present more pollutants in air, it’s affect more in human body. the predominance of air is overstated by multi-dimensional impacts containing spot, time and vague boundaries. The goal of this improvement is to take a gander at the AI basically based ways for air quality expectation. In this paper we will predict of air pollution by using machine learning algorithm.

title: Deep Learning-based Segmentation for Assessment of Kidney Tumour Ablation Therapy in CT Images
authors: Maryam Rastegarpoor, Derek W. Cool, Aaron Fenster
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Kidney tumor ablation is a minimally invasive treatment for Renal Cell Carcinoma (RCC). Manual segmentation of the kidney ablation zone (KAZ) is time-consuming, skill-dependent, and variable, making accurate assessment of treatment efficacy challenging. We propose a deep learning-based workflow for KAZ segmentation in CT images by using residual connections, multi-scale fusion, and a proposed channel-aware block. It involved predicting on 2D slices sampled radially, followed by reconstructing and evaluating the ablated volume within the kidney. Using the segmented KAZ produced by our Channel-Aware-ResUNet++ (CAResUNet++) model, we identified the margin within the kidney, which is critical for assessing ablation success. The deep learning model was trained and evaluated on a local dataset from the academic health network (London, Canada), including annotated KAZ from 76 patients' CT images. Quantitative analysis demonstrated that the proposed pipeline achieved promising performance metrics, including 85±08% DSC, 5.21±2.94mm Hausdorff distance, and 1.82±1.01mm Mean Absolute boundary Distance (MAD) for the whole KAZ. Analysis of the predicted margin within the kidney resulted in a mean MAD and Mean Signed boundary Distance of 1.38mm and 0.52mm, respectively, indicating its robustness, reliability, and applicability in clinical settings

title: Joint Optimization of Computation Offloading and Resource Allocation in Heterogeneous UAV-Assisted Edge Computing: A Game-Theoretical Approach_supp1-3680337.pdf
authors: Guangming Cui
publish: N/A
abstract: In UAV-assisted edge computing systems, existing research inadequately addresses multi-resource heterogeneity, encompassing computational resource differences and energy consumption coefficient variations among edge servers. This heterogeneity causes critical load imbalance: energy-efficient servers attract excessive users despite limited capacity, while high-capacity servers with higher energy costs remain underutilized, degrading performance for latency-sensitive applications. To address this challenge, this paper formulates the joint offloading and resource allocation as an NP-hard optimization minimizing user costs, reducing resource competition, and maximizing server utility. We propose SOTNRA (Server Offloading Target Node selection and Resource Allocation), a hierarchical framework based on a two-layer game structure combining potential and Stackelberg games. The first stage employs a potential game-based server selection algorithm (PGSSA) considering load balancing constraints with proven convergence. The second stage uses a momentum-based gradient ascent algorithm (MBGA) for optimal pricing and offloading ratios. Extensive experiments demonstrate superior performance in reducing user costs while maintaining load balancing compared to single-dimensional heterogeneity approaches.

title: The future of electric mobility between technology, business acceptance and policy making: lessons learned from the INCIT-EV project
authors: Alfredo Felix Cota, Guido Perboli, Paolo Guglielmi, Antonio Miguel Munoz, Gregorio Fernandez Aznar, Irene Maria Torres Alfonso, Gilney Damm, Hussein Al-Haj Hassan, Juan De Blas Pombo, Pedro Salazar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: INCIT-EV was an European Project financed under the Horizon2020 programme, grant agreement number 875683, that involved over 30 partners, grouped in national consortia (Spain, France, Netherlands, Estonia and Italy) and developed 7 case studies aimed at experimenting with new charging solutions for Electric Vehicles (EV) and business models capable of fostering the adoption of EVs in the European Union (EU). The project successfully ended on 30/6/2024, and the present work is aimed at illustrating the results of the research activities and experimental tests carried out, with an in-depth outlook on the future of EV charging and EV adoption based on the information gathered and the experiences of this project regarding the technology development, the economics of the EV market and the importance of the policy making activities by the governance authorities and technical committees.

title: ADODN: A Dropout-Based Occlusion-Aware Deep Network for Facial Landmark Detection
authors: Muhammad Sadiq, Junhao Wu, Yu Geng, Mohammad Sultan Mahmud, Amar Khelloufi, Hua Zheng, Yunsheng Zhang, Junwei Liang
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper introduces a dropout-based occlusion-adaptive deep network (ADODN) for robust facial landmark detection under challenging conditions, including occlusions, extreme poses, and illumination variations. While convolutional neural networks have achieved high accuracy in Facial Landmark Detection (FLD), their performance often degrades under partial occlusions due to overreliance on a few highly discriminative facial regions. ADODN addresses this limitation through three complementary modules: (1) a geometry-aware module that captures spatial relationships and structural priors among facial components; (2) an attentive dropout module that stochastically alternates drop masks and importance maps to encourage balanced feature learning from both dominant and subtle facial cues; and (3) a low-rank learning module that regularizes the regression representation by exploiting inter-feature correlations to recover occlusion-missing information. Unlike deterministic reweighting schemes, the attentive dropout mechanism improves robustness by randomly suppressing prominent responses during training, which mitigates feature over-dependence and promotes holistic structural inference.The resulting framework remains end-to-end and does not require auxiliary classifiers or multi-stage training. Extensive evaluations on challenging benchmarks (300W, COFW) show that ADODN achieves competitive and consistent performance, especially under occlusion-heavy settings. For example, ADODN attains 2.80 NRMSE on the 300W Common set and 5.81 on the Challenging set, improving upon recent baselines including ODN, AODN, and RHT-R. We also report parameter efficiency relative to prior ODN-style designs, supporting efficient training and inference.

title: UAV-Assisted Shadow Removal Framework for Accurate Pavement Crack Segmentation under Complex Illumination Conditions
authors: Yu Tian, Bin Lv, Yingchao Zhang, Jianzhu Wang, Ziyi Zhang, Benhao Xie, Jianqing Wu
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Timely and accurate detection of pavement cracks is crucial for ensuring road safety and prolonging service life. However, real-world pavement cracks often contain complex shadow occlusions from trees and vehicles, reducing detection accuracy. While existing methods perform well on standardized datasets, they frequently neglect shadow interference, limiting their effectiveness in practical applications. To overcome this challenge, this study proposes a novel UAV-assisted dual-stage shadow crack segmentation framework named DSCSNet. It initially employs a shadow removal module to preprocess images, effectively mitigating the impact of environmental factors such as shadows and lighting variations. Subsequently, a crack detection module performs pixel-level classification to accurately distinguish cracks from background. Additionally, this study also introduces a dedicated crack dataset that incorporates shadow occlusions to improve model generalization. Extensive experiments demonstrate that DSCSNet achieves superior robustness and accuracy under shadow interference, outperforming state-of-the-art methods and exhibiting strong potential for real-world pavement inspection applications.

title: Generation Expansion Planning Framework Integrating Battery Energy Storage Systems into the Screening Curve Method
authors: Matheus T. De Sousa, Eugênio L. F. Fortaleza, Yan T. De Oliveira, José Oniram De A. Limaverde Filho, Matheus B. S. De Castro, William H. C. Sánchez, Thiago F. De Souza
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Generation expansion planning is crucial to ensure a long-term, least-cost electricity supply. The screening curve method is a widely used tool for this purpose, valued for its straightforward approach. At the same time, battery energy storage systems have become increasingly important for peak shaving, supporting the integration of renewable energy, and reducing dependence on thermal units. Despite these advantages, current formulations of the screening curve method do not account for the role of energy storage systems, limiting their utility as a decision-support tool for modern low-carbon systems. This study proposes a new approach that incorporates battery operation into the screening curve framework for identifying the least-cost generation mix. The method employs a root-finding peak shaving strategy and utilizes a genetic algorithm for battery optimal sizing, while maintaining analytical simplicity. Two case studies for the Brazilian system, with and without renewable intermittent sources, are used to evaluate the approach, with total cost as the performance criterion. Results indicate that battery integration consistently reduces total system costs by up to 0.68% annually, primarily driven by an average 20% reduction in thermal start-up events. Furthermore, mathematical validation against mixed-integer linear programming benchmarks demonstrates that the proposed heuristic achieves an optimality gap within 0.37%. These findings validate the proposed approach as an effective decision-support framework, offering policymakers and system operators a transparent means to assess the economic synergy between storage and generation.

title: A Hybrid Blockchain Approach to Secure and Transparent Casino Betting and Payouts
authors: Jaehyun Kim, Murat Kantarcioglu
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper proposes a blockchain-based decentralized casino system that ensures fairness and transparency through smart contract automation. The system is implemented using Solidity and features core functionalities including secure bet submission, on-chain random number generation, and automatic reward distribution. By recording all game outcomes and transactions immutably on the blockchain, the system prevents manipulation and ensures verifiability. Experimental results demonstrate reliable performance, with transaction costs ranging from 34,826 to 45,553 gas and execution times under two seconds. To address reviewer concerns about methodological rigor and local-testbed interpretability, we additionally report measurements over N=11,000 betting operations and summarize controlled-environment gas micro-benchmarks (e.g., placeBet() 148,532 gas and fulfillRandomWords() 121,884 gas) together with controlled-environment end-to-end settlement latency statistics (mean 23.84,ms, 95This approach highlights the potential of blockchain to establish trust in digital gambling environments through autonomous and tamper-resistant mechanisms.

title: Multi-Modal 3D Semantic Segmentation with Capsule-Weighted Knowledge Distillation
authors: Hafsa Benallal, Nadine Abdallah Saab, Jamal Riffi, Hamid Tairi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate 3D semantic segmentation of outdoor environments is a fundamental requirement for autonomous systems, yet it remains challenging owing to the heterogeneous nature of sensor data. LiDAR sensors provide precise geometric information but lack semantic richness, whereas RGB cameras capture detailed appearance cues but suffer from unreliable depth estimation. Existing multi-modal fusion approaches typically apply uniform fusion strategies across all spatial regions, implicitly assuming equal feature reliability, which limits their effectiveness in complex scenes. We address this limitation by proposing a confidence-aware fusion strategy that uses capsule network activations to weight the knowledge transfer between 2D and 3D representations. Our key observation is that capsule vector magnitudes naturally encode feature presence confidence, which we leverage to guide adaptive fusion without requiring additional networks or supervision. The framework processes RGB images through a capsule-based encoder and LiDAR point clouds through a sparse point-voxel encoder. A four-stage fusion module then combines these modalities: first extracting capsule confidence scores, then weighting 2D features accordingly, projecting them to 3D space, and finally performing confidence-guided knowledge distillation from the 3D domain to enhance 2D feature learning. Confidence-aware fusion consistently outperforms uniform baselines across all evaluated categories. Experiments on SemanticKITTI confirm this hypothesis, achieving 66.8% mIoU on the test set and 70.6% on the validation set, with a 2.0% improvement over baselines while maintaining computational efficiency with only 21.4M parameters. These results validate confidence-aware fusion as a simple yet effective approach for multi-modal 3D semantic segmentation in autonomous driving applications.

title: Retraction Notice: Road Sign Recognition System for Autonomous Vehicle using Raspberry Pi
authors: K. Vinothini, S. Jayanthy
publish: 2019 5th International Conference on Advanced Computing & Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Road sign recognition is one of the important tasks of intelligent transportation systems (ITS). The project aims at implementation of road sign detection and control of an autonomous vehicle using Haar Cascade Classifier algorithm. In this proposed work, the system automatically detects the road signs, controls the vehicle and command certain actions. The system consists of Raspberry Pi 3 processor and web camera which automatically captures the video data and converts them into number of frames which are processed by the proposed algorithm in OpenCV to detect the road sign and control the vehicle. Based on the detected sign, the vehicle is controlled by two DC motors interfaced with Raspberry Pi. The experimental results for Peak Signal to Noise Ratio (PSNR) and Minimum Mean Square Error indicate the proposed system gives more accurate results with higher PSNR value compared to Hough Transformation. The performance metrics of the algorithm implemented in ARM processor is much better compared to the results obtained using MATLAB software.

title: Retraction Notice: A Review of Machine Learning Techniques for Breast Cancer Diagnosis in Medical Applications
authors: Praveen Tumuluru, Ch. Prasana Lakshmi, T. Sahaja, R. Prazna
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: During their life, among 8% of ladies are determined to have Breast cancer (BC), after Lung Cancer. Breast cancer is the second predominant cause for the increasing fatal rate. Breast cancer is exposed by the transformation of qualities, consistent agony, changes in the size, colour (redness), and the skin surface of the breast. Classification of breast cancer leads the pathologists to discover a specific and target prognostic, by binary classification (normal / abnormal). This work associates five of the most prevalent machine learning methods utilized for breast cancer exposure and analysis, explicitly Support Vector Machine (SVM) for breast cancer exposure and analysis, explicitly S.V.M, k-Nearest neighbours, logistic regression, decision tree, and random forest. The Wisconsin dataset is utilized to estimate and compare the accuracy of five different ML methods with the evaluation metric viz. accuracy. The best performance is obtained by decision tree and random forest with the highest accuracy.

title: IEEE Awards
authors: Eric Hellstrom
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Presents the recipients of IEEE awards for 2025.

title: Improved Classification Accuracy of Convolutional Neural Networks with Connected Layers for Electroencephalogram Data
authors: Viet Quoc Huynh, Tuan Van Huynh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Objective: Advanced deep-learning techniques have been employed to enhance the field of brain-computer interfaces through the processing of electroencephalogram (EEG) signals. Recent studies have shown application of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with manually extracted EEG features to be a promising approach to improve classification accuracy. However, these models require complex input features to generate output decisions. This study aimed to improve EEG recognition by developing novel CNN architectures based on branch CNNs: the step CNN (the base model) and the input- and layer-additive CNNs, which incorporate connected layers. The models exhibit exceptional performance across established WAY-EEG-GAL benchmark datasets. Additionally, the proposed method was evaluated and optimized through EEG dataset experiments, and surpassed other state-of-the-art techniques. Using connected layers in the models increased the effectiveness in recognizing EEG signals during grab-and-lift classification.

title: Algorithm Design and Regret Analysis for Aggregative Optimization in Open Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Yuxuan Liu, Maojiao Ye, Lei Ding, Qing-Long Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: This paper studies the problem of aggregative optimization in open multi-agent systems (OMAS), where agents are allowed to join and leave the system in a free manner during the decision-making process. A multi-aggregator communication mechanism is proposed to facilitate information exchange among agents, in which the aggregators are responsible for collecting information from agents and exchanging them with neighboring aggregators. Based on the multi-aggregator communication mechanism, a novel semi-distributed algorithm is designed. Dynamic regret is taken as a metric to evaluate the performance of the proposed algorithm. It is analytically shown that the dynamic regret can be bounded by the sum of agents' individual regrets, each of which grows sub-linearly with its active period length under locally diminishing step-sizes and a slowly changing environment. Moreover, to quantify the impact of agents' joining and leaving on algorithm performance, the regret is analyzed in the case of agent replacement, wherein some of the agents are replaced by new ones though the number of agents remains constant over time. Additionally, the algorithm is simplified in some special cases, resulting in a tighter regret bound. Finally, two numerical examples on target surrounding problems and price based energy management are given to verify the effectiveness of the proposed methods.

title: Hybrid GNN-Centric Architectures for AI-Native 6G Wireless Networks: A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Mostafa Rahmani Ghourtani, Sajedeh Norouzi, Jinxuan Chen, Hamed Ahmadi, Torsten Braun, Kaushik Chowdhury, Alister Burr
publish: IEEE Communications Surveys & Tutorials
abstract: The growing complexity, scale, and heterogeneity of 6G wireless systems call for a shift toward AI-native architectures that are not only data-driven but also topology-aware, adaptive, and distributed. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs), with their native support for graph-structured data, are well-suited for modeling the irregular and dynamic relationships inherent in wireless communication systems. However, standalone GNNs may be insufficient to address key 6G challenges such as continual learning, data scarcity, and dynamic adaptation. This survey, therefore, explores the emerging synergy between GNNs and complementary AI paradigms, including deep reinforcement learning (DRL), federated learning (FL), meta-learning, generative models, mixture-of-experts (MoE), and world models, enabling hybrid AI–GNN architectures for intelligent control, predictive adaptation, and scalable optimization across the wireless stack. We systematically review how these GNN-centric AI models can support intelligent functionality in next-generation network architectures such as O-RAN, as well as core 6G domains, including edge computing for wireless systems, advanced MIMO, traffic prediction, and digital twins. The survey also highlights key challenges in hybrid AI–GNN adoption, particularly scalability, generalization across dynamic topologies, interpretability, and symbolic reasoning, and discusses emerging strategies such as graph causality learning, dynamic GNNs, and neurosymbolic integration to address them. By consolidating recent advances and outlining open research directions, this work positions hybrid AI–GNN architectures as a promising approach toward enabling intelligent, energy-efficient, and context-aware wireless systems for 6G and beyond.

title: Hierarchical Gradient Preserved Network for High Resolution Hyperspectral Fusion Imaging_supp1-3680385.pdf
authors: Meiling Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: High resolution hyperspectral fusion imaging (HRHFI) is an important topic in the remote sensing related tasks, which integrates advantages of hyperspectral images (HSIs) and multipectral images (MSIs) to generate the high resolution HSIs (HR-HSIs). Traditional methods fail to maintain multi-scale change information and directional information of HSIs and MSIs during the HRHFI process, limiting the performance. To solve this challenge, a novel hierarchical gradient preserved network (HGPN) is proposed for HRHFI. Concretely, we begin by establishing the foundational observation model, which serves to make an initial estimate of HR-HSI so that the basic observation loss is thus constructed. Subsequently, we integrate both multi-scale spectral gradient preservation loss and multi-scale spatial gradient preservation loss into the basic observation loss, which results in the overall loss function of the proposed HGPN method. Further, an optimization strategy based on alternating directions is devised to solve the overall loss and derive the corresponding solutions, which finalizes the imaging fusion mapping. Different from traditional approaches, the proposed HGPN method can achieve hierarchical fusion, which retains multi-scale gradient information of input HSI/MSI for HRHFI. Extensive experiments have been implemented to verify that the proposed HGPN method achieves more competitive performance than several state-of-the-art approaches in the HRHFI task.

title: Personalized Vehicular Health Diagnosis via Large Language Model
authors: Yushen Chen, Guan Qiang, Ximing Xie, Boyu Wang, Donghong Cai, Muhammad Diyan, Tianlong Liu, Fang Fang
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: In the past decade, vehicle on-board diagnostics (OBD) systems have significantly improved by leveraging numerous electronic control units (ECUs) to monitor a wide array of real-time sensor data and report faults. However, traditional OBD systems often confuse drivers with generic warning lights that provide no context on issue severity. Recently, large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated advanced capabilities in knowledge acquisition and human-centered interaction, offering a promising solution for personalizing warning contents within existing OBD systems. In this paper, we propose an LLM-based OBD (LLM-OBD) system, an intelligent agent designed to deliver not only precise diagnostic information but also personalized guidance when a vehicle fault occurs. Moreover, the proposed LLM-OBD system can be used in coverage-challenged scenarios, such as highway, maritime routes and disaster situations, where terrestrial infrastructure is unavailable or unreliable. In these cases, non-terrestrial networks (NTN) can be leveraged to maintain reliable diagnostic capabilities. Specifically, LLM-OBD deploys a lightweight LLM on the vehicle to parse complex sensor data and driver personalization profiles into a concise query with negligible transmission overhead. Upon receiving the query through the satellite links, a responding LLM hosted on a central server generates accurate warning messages tailored to individual drivers, which are then relayed to the vehicle via the NTN connection. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed LLM-OBD system reliably delivers personalized guidance to diverse drivers.

title: Continuous-Space Multi-Agent Path Finding via Enhanced Prioritized Search with Ackermann Kinematic Constraints
authors: Tianyuan Zhang, Lin Zhang, Qiyu Cai, Shoukun Wang, Junzheng Wang
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Multi-Agent Path Finding (MAPF) in complex environments remains challenging due to high computational complexity, frequent conflicts, and realistic motion constraints. Most existing methods focus on discrete spaces or idealized omnidirectional models, often neglecting or partially considering nonholonomic constraints, which limits their applicability to real-world robotic systems. This letter proposes a hierarchical continuous-space MAPF framework explicitly designed for Ackermann-steered robots, balancing computational efficiency, global coordination, and motion feasibility. In the path search layer, a spatiotemporal hybrid A* algorithm with an adaptive dynamic weighting factor improves the trade-off between computational cost and path quality, while a homotopy-group clustering mechanism provides structured agent grouping for conflict resolution. In the conflict resolution layer, a partial-order priority reconstruction and flexible priority-based dynamic adjustment strategy effectively reduce search space and conflict density. The trajectory optimization layer integrates a decentralized sequential quadratic programming method to ensure trajectory feasibility and smoothness. Comprehensive experiments, including ablation, comparative, and scalability studies, demonstrate that the proposed method achieves lower runtime, reduced execution cost, and better coordination than existing MAPF approaches, while maintaining strong scalability in high-density environments.

title: Toward Functional Testing of Autonomous Ships: A Structured Survey With a Hybrid Virtual-Real Implementation Framework
authors: Jialun Liu, Zhilin Dong, Shijie Li, Zhouhua Peng, Xinjue Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) proposed the concept of a maritime autonomous surface ship (MASS), which could integrate perception, decision-making, and control functions to implement autonomous navigation in complicated environments. It is recognized as the fundamental component of the future new generation of shipping systems. The development of MASS requires the use of specific test tools and use cases for the evaluation of the safety, reliability, and functional performance of its navigation system. At present, the lack of standardized, modular, and serial testing paradigms and methods for MASS impedes the development and improvement of related products and applications. Therefore, it is essential to develop a practical and applicable testing framework. This paper presents a functional analysis of autonomous navigation systems and provides a comprehensive overview of current research status, potential solutions, and future challenges. Moreover, this paper also takes a step towards facilitating the functional testing of autonomous surface vehicles by proposing a hybrid virtual-real framework consisting of scenario generation, virtual simulation, model-scaled physical experiment, validation and evaluation. The research opportunities and future aspects are also addressed. This work may serve as a reference for academic and industrial researchers to investigate new methods and to develop prototype systems for future autonomous surface ships.

title: Modelling Different Penetration Rates of Automated Eco-Driving Electric Vehicles in an Urban Area
authors: Jakub Krol, Bani Anvari, Craig Rafter, James Fleming, Xingda Yan, Roberto Lot
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Eco-driving strategies have proven to be effective in providing energy savings for the vehicle that is utilising them. This paper explores the underinvestigated impact of eco-driving vehicles on other network participants adhering to conventional driving styles. An eco-driving strategy designed for an electric vehicle that trades off energy savings with naturalistic driving without relying on vehicle-to-infrastructure or vehicle-to-vehicle communication is extended using Gaussian Process Regression for real-time predictive speed optimization. It enables the assessment of its network effects in two scenarios: 1) a platoon and 2) an urban network simulation with mixed-mode traffic and varying demand levels. Initial validation involves an eco-driving vehicle responding to a platoon leader, influencing following vehicles governed by the Intelligent Driver Model (IDM). Further analysis introduces eco-vehicles into an IDM-governed network under different traffic conditions. Energy savings of up to 21% were achieved in cars following a vehicle that prioritises energy savings and, of up to 13% if they followed a vehicle that attempts to balance energy savings and conventional driving style. In the urban scenario, positive effects on other users are observed in high density traffic even if only a small number of eco-vehicles are present in the network. The largest energy savings achieved in conventional vehicles were 5.1% and were obtained for high-density traffic and a network consisting of 25% of eco-vehicles.

title: Stabilization of G-Stochastic Complex Networks via Asynchronous Hierarchical-Triggered Intermittent Control
authors: Junqing Ma, Junhao Hu, Jingwen Xue, Sen Li, Wenxue Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: For providing a more accurate description of probabilistic and model uncertainties, this paper studies the stabilization of G-stochastic complex networks (SCNs-G), where the normality assumption on noise is removed by accounting for non-Gaussian G-noise. Unlike prior intermittent event-triggered control (IE-TC), a novel asynchronous hierarchical-triggered intermittent controller (AHTIC) is designed, where the asynchronous self-triggered law determines the switching between intermittent control (IC) intervals and rest intervals, while the asynchronous dynamic event-triggered mechanism (E-TM) governs the sampling rule within the control intervals. The hierarchical-triggered feature enables the proposed AHTIC strategy to respond adaptively to unanticipated events while improving energy efficiency. Note that the sublinear expectation framework that the SCNs-G stems from, results in the existing IE-TC stabilization results inapplicable. Subsequently, a novel G-Lyapunov functional with assisting functions is constructed, and a new analytical technique is introduced to tackle the challenges arising from sublinear expectations. Specifically, the stability analysis relies on the estimation of sublinear expectations for several integrals relating to sampling errors, dynamic terms, and system states, which in turn enables the derivation of less conservative stability conditions. By means of Lyapunov functional method, and G-stochastic analysis techniques, two types of exponential stabilization criteria, including mean-square and quasi-sure exponential stability, for the control system are developed. Finally, the effectiveness of theoretical results is validated by numerical simulations on autonomous land vehicle networks.

title: Comparing Independent and Centralized Multi-Agent Learning for District Energy Systems
authors: Dmytro Voitekh, Anatolii Tymoshenko, Volodymyr Pavlenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) offers promising coordination strategies for district-scale energy management, but explicit coordination methods like Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MADDPG) come with significant computational overhead. Prior work has primarily evaluated small-scale deployments (9-17 buildings), leaving the scalability-performance tradeoff uncharacterized at realistic district scales. We address this gap by comparing Independent Learners using Soft Actor-Critic (IL-SAC) against centralized MADDPG across four scales (10, 20, 50, 100 buildings) using CityLearn's Texas Travis County dataset. We find that independent learners capture 85-90% of coordination performance while requiring only 1/6 the training time. At 10 buildings, IL-SAC achieves 18.3% peak demand reduction versus MADDPG's 21.1%. As districts scale to 100 buildings, this performance gap narrows to under 2 percentage points while computational costs increase 6-fold, making independent learning the practical choice for large-scale deployments.

title: Methodology for Evaluating the Occurrence, Multiplication, and Spread of Errors in Artificial Intelligence Models
authors: Yuriy Danyk, Valery Shestakov, Roman Shyrshov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents the results of a study regarding the problem of error occurrence in artificial intelligence (AI) models, which leads to the generation of incorrect, false, non-existent, or contradictory information. The research proceeds from the hypothesis that errors are intrinsic to the nature of AI model training. The authors analyze typical scenarios and models through which errors ensue and spread. Analogies are drawn: the multiplication and scaling of errors relative to the number of AI interactions is compared to Moore's Law regarding the doubling of transistors in integrated circuits over certain time intervals, while their dissemination is compared to the spread of a virus through a population. Methodologies for evaluating the rate of error accumulation and scaling in AI models are formalized using the Lyapunov exponent. To ensure the safe deployment of AI systems in critical infrastructure, the authors have developed a comprehensive methodology for evaluating the occurrence, multiplication, and spread of errors in AI models.

title: Full-Scale 3D Models for Training in Rhinoscopic Interventions
authors: Andrii Sokoltsov, Nataliia Shushliapina, Oleg Avrunin, Yana Nosova, Oleksandr Avrunin, Ihor Kandaurov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper presents the development and implementation of full-scale 3D-printed models for training in rhinoscopic and endoscopic sinus surgery. Based on patient-specific CT data, anatomically accurate nasal cavity models were created using segmentation and additive manufacturing. These models provide realistic simulation of surgical procedures, enhancing spatial orientation and technical skills without patient risk. The study highlights the advantages of 3D printing in surgical education, including reproducibility, safety, and cost efficiency, as well as the potential for remote learning. Experimental results confirm the high educational value and applicability of 3D models for training otorhinolaryngologists and improving clinical outcomes.

title: BFX: An Interpretable Statistical Tool for Anomaly-Oriented Examination of BGP Features
authors: Marian Kyryk, Stanislav Maruniak, Leonid Uryvsky
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Research on Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) anomalies frequently introduces new update-derived features, yet demonstrating their behavior around incidents often relies on ad hoc scripts that hinder comparison. In this study we propose BGP Feature Examiner (BFX), an interpretable statistical tool that examines BGP feature behavior over time spans containing an anomaly using precomputed snapshots. It provides raw and normalized time-series views and concise, test-based summaries of variability, distributional change, separability, and change-point evidence, presented with compact charts. The design goal is a unified, pre-model assessment step that helps authors and operators demonstrate and compare feature behavior consistently before training detection models, while allowing additional measures to be added when needed.

title: Quantum compressed sensing tomographic reconstruction algorithm
authors: Arim Ryou, Kiwoong Kim, Kyungtaek Jun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering
abstract: Computed tomography (CT) is a non-destructive technique for observing internal images and has proven highly valuable in medical diagnostics. Recent advances in quantum computing have begun to influence tomographic reconstruction techniques. The quantum tomographic reconstruction algorithm is less affected by artifacts or noise than classical algorithms by using the square function of the difference between pixels obtained by projecting CT images in quantum superposition states and pixels obtained from experimental data. In particular, by using quantum linear systems, a fast quadratic unconstrained binary optimization (QUBO) model formulation for quantum tomographic reconstruction is possible. In this paper, we formulate the QUBO model for quantum compressed sensing tomographic reconstruction, which is a linear combination of the QUBO model for quantum tomographic reconstruction and the QUBO model for total variation in quantum superposition-state CT images. In our experiments, we used sinograms obtained by using the Radon transform of Shepp-Logan images and body CT images. We evaluate the performance of the new algorithm by reconstructing CT images using a hybrid solver with the QUBO model computed from each sinogram. The new algorithm was able to obtain a solution within 5 projection images for 30 × 30 image samples and within 6 projection images for 60 × 60 image samples, reconstructing error-free CT images. We anticipate that quantum compressed sensing tomographic reconstruction algorithms could significantly reduce the total radiation dose when quantum computing performance advances.

title: Characterization of Patterned Self-Assembled Electrets for Vibrational Energy Harvesters
authors: Yuya Tanaka, Ryu Kumagai, Ayato Jingu, Suzaku Kimura, Syuuma Kawamura, Yuji Takata, Takaaki Suzuki, Daisuke Yamane
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Electret-based vibrational energy harvesters (E-VEHs) are promising power sources for wireless sensor nodes. However, conventional E-VEHs require a dedicated charging process for the dielectric, which complicates high-throughput manufacturing. Our precious work introduces the use of self-assembled electrets (SAEs), which are polar organic semiconductors exhibiting spontaneous orientation polarization (SOP), and demonstrates an SAE-based VEH that does not require a charging process. For this device, the effect of the surface potential of the patterned SAE (p-SAE) on the output current must be understood; however, the relationship between them remains unclear. In this study, the surface potential and output current of p-SAEs with various structures are precisely evaluated using a Kelvin probe. A linear relationship between the surface potential and output current is observed in the modeled p-SAE VEHs, indicating that the output current can be enhanced merely by increasing the average film thickness of the SAE. Furthermore, we find that the surface potential of p-SAEs originates from the SOP and can be controlled by changing the average film thickness, density, and occupancy rate of the pattern. These findings suggest that p-SAEs are suitable for the mass commercialization of E-VEHs, as they eliminate the need for a dedicated charging process and enable the deposition of SAEs after the movable electrode is fabricated.

title: Disturbance-Observer-Based Sliding Mode Controller for Physical Human-Robot Interaction in Multi-Joint Exoskeleton Robots
authors: Mostafa Mogharabi, Iman Kardan, Ali Moradi, Alireza Akbarzadeh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: A robust and stable physical Human-Robot Interaction (pHRI) controller is crucial for robotic systems, particularly for rehabilitation devices that enable continuous human-robot interaction. A key challenge is to achieve a precise interaction torque that enables the robot to provide active assistance or remain transparent to the user. This paper proposes a novel direct force controller, termed as Integral Sliding Mode Controller with a Disturbance Observer Based on the Interaction torque model (ISMCDOBI), for pHRI control of multi-joint exoskeleton robots. A spring-based contact model is explicitly integrated into the robot dynamics to account for human–robot interaction effects and to compensate for human motion. Unlike SEA-based or position/velocity-based methods that indirectly regulate interaction forces, the proposed approach operates directly in the force/torque domain. An integral sliding mode controller is combined with a disturbance observer that employs a nominal interaction model to account for uncertainties and disturbances. Gravity, friction, and human motion compensators are also incorporated to reduce control effort and improve the tracking of the desired interaction torque. The effectiveness of the proposed framework is validated through realistic MATLAB–OpenSim co-simulation on a coupled human–exoskeleton biomechanical model. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed interaction controller by maintaining interaction torques less than 0.42 N·m and 0.52 N·m in transparent mode for the hip and knee. Also, a one-way ANOVA analysis depicts the superiority of the proposed controller over two common torque control methods in various modes.

title: Efficiency-aware Federated Learning for Image Classification via Model Adaptation
authors: Yiheng Lu, Ziyu Guan, Maoguo Gong, Wei Zhao, Zhuping Hu, Fenlong Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Massive client training and bandwidth occupation are two main efficiency issues in Federated Learning (FL) for image classification. In this paper, we propose a model adaptation technique to alleviate the efficiency concerns of the FL. The model adaptation technique incorporates two key components: a Spectrum-Tempered Filter Pruning Framework (STFPF) and an Adaptive Accuracy-Based Selection Mechanism (AABSM), which are designed to effectively reduce client-side training costs and minimize bandwidth consumption, respectively. STFPF employs spectral analysis of the weight matrices to dynamically determine the optimal pruning rate. Specifically, the framework analyzes the eigenvalue distribution of each layer’s weight matrix, enabling the identification and removal of filters based on the magnitude of their corresponding eigenvalues. This spectral-based pruning mechanism allows STFPF to effectively reduce the scale of local models, thereby significantly decreasing computational costs throughout the FL process. Moreover, AABSM intelligently selects a subset of clients for model aggregation based on their individual model performance metrics. This performance-driven selection approach significantly reduces the consumption of required communication time between the central server and local clients, thereby effectively conserving bandwidth resources throughout the FL process. We extensively evaluate our model adaptation technique on three prominent architectures with AlexNet, VGG-16, and ResNet-50, using the FashionMNIST, CIFAR-10, and CIFAR-100 datasets, respectively. For AlexNet, out approach can reduce the computation cost obviously while with tiny accuracy sacrifice. For VGG-16 and ResNet-50, our approach not only achieves superior accuracy in the central model compared to baseline methods but also yields significant reductions in computational complexity (FLOPs), model parameters, and bandwidth consumption throughout the complete federated learning cycle.

title: Full W-Band HDI Antenna-in-Package Array Based on a Coplanar Magnetoelectric Dipole
authors: Pei Gan, Yuxiang Zheng, Yunyan Zhou, Gang Song, Xinyu Zhang, Chenglin Yang, WenWen Zhang, Jun Li, Qidong Wang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: A full W-band antenna-in-package (AiP) array implemented in a six-layer high-density interconnect (HDI) package is presented. The radiator is a coplanar magnetoelectric (ME) dipole in which the electric dipole current and the equivalent magnetic current are synchronously excited on the same layer by a TM10-mode coupling patch. Unlike previously reported slot-based via-free ME dipoles using a resonant λ0/2 ground slot, the proposed magnetic source is a localized subwavelength gap, which suppresses vertical phase detuning and avoids electrically long-slot behavior at the upper W-band. A dual-layer artificial magnetic conductor (AMC) is further embedded as both an in-phase reflection boundary and a functional copper-density compensation structure for multilayer fabrication. Based on the proposed element, an 8×8 array is designed and fabricated. Measurements show an impedance bandwidth of 75–110 GHz (37.8%), a peak realized gain of 22.7 dBi, and stable symmetric broadside radiation across the full W-band. The proposed architecture is well suited for compact W-band HDI-AiP systems.

title: Retraction Notice: Implication Centered Learning Mechanism for Exploring Analysis of Variance by Means of Linear Regression in Artificial Neural Networks
authors: Akey Sungheetha, Rajesh Sharma R., Jemal Nuradis

abstract: The resolution of this learning is to explore the client-provision supplier rapport in the insurance production by means of artificial neural networks and linear regression. Data Analysis visualization is suggested to alleviate calculation difficulties in terms of knowledge discovery. Also, a category of currency data is presented to addresses the practical application by combining F ratio and p value to compute currency data. The experimental results based on One way Analysis of Variance (ANOVA) is used to find the F ratio and p value where the F ratio is used in N way ANOVA to find the significance of party and chamber currency through artificial neural network. This shows that N way ANOVA produce good performance in the case of computing the currency data set and computes faster than the evolution strategy for the computing system.

title: Retraction Notice: A Reflection of Driver Drowsiness Detection Techniques
authors: Rehna Baby Joseph, R. Satheesh Kumar, Chidambaranathan, M.B Lakshmi
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Creating savvy frameworks to stop vehicle mishaps are extremely powerful in limiting catastrophes cost. One among the elements which assume a pivotal part in accidents is that the human mistakes including driving exhaustion relying on new brilliant strategies. A spread of tiredness recognition techniques subsists which screen tiredness of a person who drives the vehicle also, warn the chauffeur in the event that they are not focusing on driving. The applicable highlights are regularly removed from outward appearances like mouth opening state, eye conclusion, and upper portion of the body movements for deriving degree of sluggishness. Natural state of the drivers’ body, likewise as motor conduct, is investigated for driver sleepiness discovery. This analysis grants a complete examination of the winning techniques for driver laziness identification and presents an inside and out investigation of broadly utilized grouping procedures during this respect. This particular survey, we group the predominant strategies into three classifications: social, transport, and biological boundaries-based strategies

title: Improving the Dynamics of a Lifting Mechanism Based on Fuzzy Control Approaches
authors: Yaroslav Paranchuk, Roman Paranchuk, Oleksiy Kuznyetsov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The feasibility of using artificial intelligence approaches to improve the dynamics of lifting mechanisms of various production and technological processes has been substantiated. A fuzzy speed controller for the control system of the lifting mechanism of an excavator bucket has been designed. A simulation model of the electromechanical system of the lifting mechanism of an excavator bucket has been designed in Matlab/Simulink. A set of simulation studies of the dynamics of the bucket movement in different operating modes of the lifting mechanism and various setting and disturbances has been carried out. The study has been carried out using the proposed fuzzy speed controller in comparison with a traditional precise proportional controller in the structure of the automatic control system for this mechanism. A comparative analysis of the obtained results demonstrated a decrease in the amplitude of speed fluctuations and dynamic torque in different operating modes of the lifting mechanism when using a fuzzy controller.

title: Retraction Notice: An Overview of the Challenges and Applications towards Web Mining
authors: Ayyavaraiah Monelli, Shoban Babu Sriramoju
publish: 2018 2nd International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC), 2018 2nd International Conference on
abstract: Data mining has pulled in a fantastic arrangement of consideration inside the data business and in the public arena in general as of late, because of the wide accessibility of huge sums data and likewise the nearby at hand might want for moving such data into supportive data and knowledge. The data and knowledge picked up will be utilized for applications beginning from market research, fraud detection, and client retention, to production management and changed investigations. This paper is to inspect the part of data mining for data extraction in web page, structure and utilizations mining in current web models, and likewise the frameworks the strategy for separating designs from data. This paper furthermore blessing data processing natives, from that data processing question dialects will be composed. Issues concerning an approach to incorporate a data mining framework with a database or data distribution center are specified. title: The Impact of Stress on Cognitive Performance in University Students: EEG-Based Study
authors: Hnin Ei Hlaing, Wanus Srimaharaj
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study explores the correlation between academic stress and cognitive performance among university students during exams using the publicly available EEG (electroencephalogram) data. The dataset includes recordings of 26 University Students (19 females, 7 males; mean age <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.23 \pm$</tex> 1.14). EEG signals were analyzed from Alpha, Beta, and Theta frequency bands, while cognitive performance is determined using Theta/Alpha Ratio (TAR) and Theta/Beta Ratio (TBR). Stress levels were categorized as No, Low, Moderate, and High based on perceived stress scores. Spearman's rank correlation was applied to assess associations between stress and EEG-derived cognitive performance of students. The results indicated weak negative correlations between stress and both TAR (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\rho=-0.063, p=0.760$</tex>) and TBR <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\rho=-0.078, p=0.705)$</tex>. Students with low stress levels show stable TAR and TBR values, which indicate consistent cognitive performance. In contrast, those with moderate stress showed a tendency toward the higher mean TAR and TBR values, suggesting optimal engagement and mental effort. University students under high stress and no stress demonstrated a significantly wider range of values and large in EEG patterns, which reflected increased brain activity variability and cognitive strain. The results demonstrate that stress primarily influences cognitive performance through non-linear processes, leading to destabilized brainwave patterns and affecting cognitive performance. The results demonstrate the importance of monitoring academic stress and related cognitive factors to support and maintain optimal learning performance.

title: Retraction Notice: Advances in Vision based Lane Detection Algorithm Based on Reliable Lane Markings
authors: P. Priyadharshini, Pulugurtha Niketha, K. Saantha Lakshmi, S. Sharmila, R. Divya
publish: 2019 5th International Conference on Advanced Computing & Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: This paper advances in a vision based driver assistance system for effective lane detection Technique. First method involves image processing technique initially to convert the Red, green and blue (RGB) into grayscale images takes place by removing the effects of shades and immersion data of the images by maintaining the brightness of the images. Then to remove the sound available in the images and the obtained video is allowed to pass through the Gaussian filter in-order to remove sound from the images. The edges recognition is an important function to identify and detect the sharp break in an image by canny edge detection algorithm. Then Hough transform technique is applied to obtain the image pixels in the form of long straight line in the Hough space. So we can detect the lane if the vehicle tends to deviate from the lane in a complex situation and take corrective measures to change the lane.

title: Designing an Adaptive Color Morphology Operator for Exudates and Microaneurysms Detection in Color Fundus Images
authors: Milad Rostamian, Motahare Shekari
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is a leading cause of preventable blindness, making automated lesion detection critical for early screening. However, traditional unsupervised methods often fail due to image variability and ignore critical color information, while modern deep learning models require vast annotated datasets. This paper introduces a fully rule-based, unsupervised framework requiring no training or labeled data that integrates the detection of exudates and microaneurysms to overcome these challenges. The core of our method is a new Adaptive Color Morphology operator that leverages the perceptually uniform CIE L*a*b* color space for robust exudate segmentation using fixed a priori parameters shared across datasets. By first identifying and removing exudate regions, our pipeline improves the specificity of the subsequent multi-scale microaneurysm detection stage by reducing inter-lesion confusion. Evaluated on public datasets, our method achieves a competitive unsupervised exudate detection accuracy of 0.93 on DIARETDB1 (DDB1) and microaneurysm detection F-Scores of 0.460 on e-Ophtha and 0.291 on the challenging Retinopathy Online Challenge dataset. Our results demonstrate that this transparent, color-aware pipeline is an interpretable and practical alternative to data-dependent models for automated diabetic retinopathy screening. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/milad67/Adaptive-Color-Morphology-DR.

title: A Hybrid Model for Enhancing 3D Medical Image Segmentation with Self-Attention Volumetric Transformer and Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Ankit Srivastava, Santosh Rajak, Munesh Chandra, Ashim Saha, Sanjoy Chakraborty
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In modern healthcare, accurate diagnosis, treatment planning, and surgical guidance are affected mainly by precise segmentation of 3D medical images. Clinicians can more precisely identify complex anatomical structures and detect abnormalities in 3D medical images than in 2D images. Most segmentation work is based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Vision Transformers (ViTs). CNNs are good at capturing local image details, but struggle to represent broader spatial contexts, whereas ViTs excel at finding long-range dependencies within an image but require substantial processing power. This paper presents a hybrid segmentation approach that combines the strengths of both paradigms. This study employs a Volumetric Transformer Encoder (VN), which shows strong performance in capturing the non-local dependencies across 3D space, and a CNN-based decoder reconstructs fine-grained segmentation maps, showing strong spatial fidelity. The proposed model also uses hierarchical attention and skip connections, which support multiscale learning and maintain semantic consistency. The proposed model’s performance has been evaluated using the BraTS 2020 benchmark dataset, and the results show its superiority over the state-of-the-art existing models based on a higher average Dice score and a lower Hausdorff Distance.

title: AI-Assisted Semiotic Analysis of the Cultural Absence of the Zulfikar Sword in Chinese Islamic Visual Culture
authors: Qiling Huang, Rui Sun
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The Zulfikar sword is a highly standardized sacred weapon symbol in Shia visual culture, forming a stable iconographic lineage across the Middle East and the Irano-Ottoman world. However, it is notably absent from Chinese Islamic visual traditions, resulting in a phenomenon of "cultural absence." While existing studies interpret this absence through religious history and iconography, they lack verifiable visual-structural explanations. This study proposes a cross-cultural semiotic framework by constructing a triple-layered symbolic structure—semantic core, visual grammar, and cultural vehicle—and introduces the Cross-Cultural Symbolic Structure Adaptability Framework (CSSAF). An integrated visual corpus of Zulfikar representations, Chinese Islamic artefacts, and decorative patterns was assembled. Using pretrained visual models, high-dimensional features were extracted and analyzed through dimensionality reduction, clustering, and discriminant methods to examine their relationships within latent space. A structurally and semantically constrained diffusion model was further employed to test the symbol ’ s potential integration into Chinese visual grammar. Results reveal that the Zulfikar symbol forms a stable, isolated cluster, lacking transitional continuity with Chinese Islamic decorative systems. Generative experiments show that attempted visual fusion results in superficial combinations rather than intrinsic integration. These findings demonstrate that the symbol ’ s absence arises from structural incompatibility between its strong semantic directionality and the decorative, de-subjectivised visual grammar of Chinese Islamic art. This study provides a quantifiable framework for examining cultural absence and offers a methodological reference for AI-assisted cross-cultural symbol analysis and generation.
