title: HarassGuard: Detecting Harassment Behaviors in Social Virtual Reality with Vision-Language Models_supp1-3680723.pdf
authors: Junhee Lee
publish: N/A
abstract: Social Virtual Reality (VR) platforms provide immersive social experiences but also expose users to serious risks of online harassment. Existing safety measures are largely reactive, while proactive solutions that detect harassment behavior during an incident often depend on sensitive biometric data, raising privacy concerns. In this paper, we present HarassGuard, a vision-language model (VLM) based system that detects physical harassment in social VR using only visual input. We construct an IRB-approved harassment vision dataset, apply prompt engineering, and fine-tune VLMs to detect harassment behavior by considering contextual information in social VR. Experimental results demonstrate that HarassGuard achieves competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines (i.e., LSTM/CNN, Transformer), reaching an accuracy of up to 88.09% in binary classification and 68.85% in multi-class classification. Notably, HarassGuard matches these baselines while using significantly fewer fine-tuning samples (200 vs. 1,115), offering unique advantages in contextual reasoning and privacy-preserving detection.

title: SkyFind: A Large-Scale Benchmark Unveiling Referring Expression Comprehension for UAV
authors: Kunyu Wang, Guanbo Wu, Xueyang Fu, Xingbo Wang, Kean Liu, Xin Lu, Chengjie Ge, Wei Zhai, Zheng-Jun Zha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAV) are increasingly deployed to assist humans in diverse tasks, where understanding human intentions is critical to effective collaboration. Referring expression comprehension (REC) links language to visual targets, allowing UAV to recognize human-intended targets of interest, thereby supporting subsequent actions. However, existing REC research is almost exclusively confined to ground-based scenarios, leaving aerial scenarios largely unexplored. In this paper, we formally define UAV-based REC as a new research problem and highlight its unique challenges, including abundant background interference, small target size, and complex referring relations. To enable systematic study, we introduce SkyFind, a large-scale dataset with one million high-quality target-expression pairs, providing a solid foundation. In addition, we propose AerialREC, a baseline framework that reduces background interference in UAV imagery by searching for a potential target region before localization. We establish benchmark results on SkyFind using ten representative REC methods and validate the effectiveness of the AerialREC framework.

title: Reinforcement Learning-Based Parameter Optimization for Multi-Material Cloth Simulation
authors: Roman Chekhmestruk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This research presents a reinforcement learning framework for automated optimization of cloth physics parameters across heterogeneous material systems in real-time garment simulations. Unlike traditional approaches requiring extensive manual parameter tuning, our methodology employs multi-objective DDPG agents with domain-specific reward engineering to automatically discover optimal material dependent physics configurations. The framework incorporates cross-material knowledge transfer mechanisms and validates against laboratory-measured fabric properties for cotton, silk, denim, and synthetic materials, achieving significant reduction in optimization overhead (p < 0.001) with measurable accuracy improvements.

title: Methods for Over-the-Air Detection and Digital Processing of Wireless Analog Video Signals Using a Microcontroller with Built-in ADC
authors: Oleksandr Malyi, Serhii Harachuk, Oleksandr Shchukin
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper discusses methods for detecting and digitally processing wireless analog video signals in the radio spectrum using the ESP32 microcontroller, which features a built-in analog-to-digital converter (ADC). A software-based approach to real-time filtering of a composite video blanking and sync (CVBS) is proposed, enabling the recognition of the video signal structure without the need for dedicated hardware decoders. The temporal characteristics of PAL and NTSC standards, the specifics of vertical and horizontal synchronization, and statistical analysis methods ensuring reliable sync pulse detection in noisy channels are examined. It is demonstrated that applying a Kalman filter can enhance reception stability and mitigate interference effects. The obtained results demonstrate the feasibility of implementing intelligent analog video signal processing directly on microcontrollers in FPV, monitoring, and robotics systems.

title: Context-Aware Computational Model for Adaptive Big Data Processing Pipelines
authors: Marian Pisotskyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents a context-aware computational model for adaptive Big Data processing pipelines. The model integrates a multi-layer representation of data, system, user, and environmental context into a unified semantic vector used to guide runtime adaptation decisions. The pipeline is treated as a reconfigurable DAG whose topology, operator layout, execution mode, and resource allocation may be modified according to contextual deviations. The experimental results indicate that under progressively higher stream velocities, the latency gap between the static and adaptive configurations grows substantially. The adaptive pipeline maintains a stable trend across all tested rates, delivering a consistent 40–45% reduction and surpassing 50% in the most demanding scenario.

title: Method of Resource-Optimised Selective Video Encryption using U-Net Semantic Segmentation
authors: Oleksandr Kurlan, Volodymyr Strukov, Borys Teliatnyk, Oleksandr Kolodiazhnyi, Ihor Mazhara, Serhii Nemynushchyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: In today's digital world, video stands as one of the most abundant and valuable forms of data. Sources ranging from surveillance cameras and mobile devices to drones and online platforms generate massive volumes of visual information daily, much of which inherently contains sensitive or personal content. While traditional protection strategies, such as the full encryption of video streams, offer a high level of security, they come with significant practical limitations: they demand substantial computational power, introduce unacceptable transmission delays, and impede essential real-time processing. The challenge of providing video information selection within environments characterized by severely limited computing resources is particularly critical for applications like video surveillance and medical record management, where confidentiality is required only for specific, sensitive regions within the image frame. Our primary objective is to develop a software solution that can dynamically identify and encrypt only the crucial video segments using a combination of semantic segmentation and machine learning techniques. The proposed system utilizes a U-Net neural network architecture to accurately delineate the specific image areas that require cryptographic protection, thereby enabling precise, targeted encryption. Experimental validation, conducted using the Stanford Background dataset, confirms the high efficacy of this selective approach. The resulting methods are ideally suited for integration into resource-limited systems that demand both high performance and robust security.

title: AI-Integrated Gamified Learning Media for Enhancing Chinese Pronunciation: A Design and Implementation Study
authors: Zhengyang Hu, Krittawaya Thongkoo, Kannika Daungcharone
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study investigates the design and implementation of an AI-integrated gamified learning system developed to enhance Chinese pronunciation skills among lower secondary school students. The system combines Quizizz-based gamification with AI-generated voice dubbing from ElevenLabs to deliver authentic pronunciation models and interactive practice opportunities. A quasi-experimental design was employed with 40 Grade 9 students assigned to experimental and control groups. Data were collected through pre- and post-Chinese pronunciation assessments, as well as a learner satisfaction Survey. The results show a clear and statistically significant improvement in the experimental group's pronunciation performance (pre-test: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$M=7.75$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S D=5.53$</tex>; posttest: <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{M}=10.20$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SD}=4.63; \mathrm{p}<.05$</tex>), while the control group demonstrated no meaningful changes. Students also expressed very high satisfaction with the AI-supported gamified activities (overall mean <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$=4.72$</tex>), particularly noting that the approach made practice more engaging, enjoyable, and effective. These outcomes suggest that bringing together AI voice synthesis and gamified learning can genuinely support more accurate pronunciation, sustain learner interest, and strengthen motivation in the classroom. The study highlights the practical value of AI-enhanced digital learning tools and points to their potential for broader use in pronunciation instruction and other areas of language learning where repeated, guided practice is essential.

title: Spectral And Photovoltaic Analysis Of InAgS Quantum Dot-Based Ternary Solar Cells: A Comparison With Standard P3HT:C70 Systems
authors: Khrystyna Ivaniuk, Oleh Klymkevych, Serhii Melnykov, Anna Pidluzhna, Ihor Semkiv
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This study experimentally investigates the photovoltaic performance and optical properties of a ternary heterostructure incorporating newly synthesized indium silver sulfide (InAgS) quantum dots (QDs): ITO/ PEDOT:PSS/ P3HT:C70:InAgS QDs/ BCP/ Al. We analyze the spectral response of this hybrid system in comparison to the well-established P3HT:C70 binary structure described extensively in scientific literature. Our analysis reveals that the incorporation of InAgS QDs induces a distinct spectral evolution, extending the photo-response beyond the conventional limits of the P3HT:C70 blend. We report on the photovoltaic parameters of the synthesized ternary device, which exhibited a short-circuit current of 31.2 nA, an open-circuit voltage of 0.37 V, fill factor (FF) of 22% and a power conversion efficiency (PCE) of 6.9×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−7</sup> %.

title: On the Competition of Modes in a Magnetron with Significant Role of Space Charge
authors: Igor Bondarenko, Oleksandr Hnatenko, Alexander Gritsunov, Olexiy Pashchenko, Hennadii Bendeberia, Mykhaylo Kopot
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper investigates the excitation and competition of various oscillation modes in a typical 2.45 GHz, 1 kW magnetron, commonly used in household and industrial microwave ovens. A distinctive feature of these magnetrons is the significant influence of space-charge forces on spoke formation and the mutual transformation of modes during "mode hopping." Under certain conditions – such as a low-quality high-frequency shielding system – this process leads to spectral broadening of the output signal, which can degrade the electromagnetic environment around microwave devices and potentially interfere with Wi-Fi communication systems operating in adjacent frequency bands. The transition between the operating (π-mode) oscillation and the adjacent high-voltage mode is successfully simulated. A further transition to the next mode is not observed; instead, spoke disintegration occurs. During the transient process, a significant, though relatively short-term, spectral broadening is observed. The overall influence of space charge in relatively low-power magnetrons introduces pronounced nonlinearity in the electron cloud, enhancing intermodulation components during the transitions and causing anode voltage hysteresis when switching between modes.

title: The Impact of Employee Compensation, Benefits, and Reward Programs on Retention and Productivity in the Hotel Industry in Thailand
authors: Thandar Oo, Premchit Khurana
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study examines the effects of compensation, benefits, and reward programs on employee retention and productivity in Thailand's hotel industry. A mixed-methods approach was employed, combining semi-structured interviews with hotel staff and owners from selected hotels in Thailand, analyzed using thematic analysis, as well as comprehensive literature review. The research also identifies key factors contributing to effective retention strategies. Findings indicate that fair compensation, personalized rewards, positive work environments, and career development opportunities significantly enhance employee satisfaction and loyalty. The study also identifies challenges such as staff retention during off-peak tourism seasons and balancing budget constraints with employee needs. The results highlight the importance of aligning compensation strategies with employee expectations, while implementing holistic reward systems and integrating modern talent management practices to reduce turnover.

title: Probabilistic inversion of airborne electromagnetic data using conditional variational autoencoder: Yellowstone case study
authors: Chenyu Zhou, Sihong Wu, Jiajia Sun, Jiefu Chen, Yueqin Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Airborne electromagnetic (AEM) inversion is inherently ill-posed, resulting in challenges for reliable subsurface interpretation. While Bayesian methods offer principled uncertainty quantification, their high computational cost limits scalability. We propose a conditional variational autoencoder (CVAE) framework for fast, probabilistic AEM inversion. By introducing a latent variable and conditioning on both AEM data and transceiver height, the CVAE learns the mapping relationship from latent space to posterior resistivity distributions. Synthetic tests demonstrate the CVAE’s noise robustness and its ability to reconstruct the model posterior. A comparison with McMC-based inversion is also discussed to highlight methodological and computational differences. Applied to AEM data acquired from Yellowstone region, the CVAE effectively recovers key hydrothermal features and provides quantified uncertainty estimates. This approach achieves orders-of-magnitude speed-up over traditional Bayesian methods, enabling large-scale, uncertainty-informed subsurface resistivity imaging.

title: Global Token Dictionary Augmented Patch Transformer for Hyperspectral Image Reconstruction
authors: Yubao Sun, Hongkang Luan, Zhenyu Zhang, Le Sun, Qingshan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Reconstructing hyperspectral images from RGB images is a cost-effective and convenient method to obtain fine spatial-spectral information. The reconstruction methods based on transformers is currently very competitive. They mainly take each pixel as the token to calculate the correlation within the spatial window, or take each spectral band as the token to calculate the correlation along the entire spectral dimension. Although they have improved the reconstruction quality, they still have limitations in learning global contextual information while preserving local spatial-spectral features. To address these issues, we propose a novel global token dictionary augmented patch Transformer (GAP-Trans) network to learn the inverse reconstruction mapping. The core innovation of GAP-Trans is to use multi-scale local patches as the basic unit to calculate spectral-wise mixed-attention within local patches and between local patches and global contextual information generated by the Global Token Dictionary Generation Module. Specifically, GAP Trans is configured with dual-branch to calculate the cross-attention between the global token dictionary and local patches, as well as the self-attention within local patches. The Fast Fourier Fusion operator is then adopted to fuse the output of the dual-branch in the frequency domain. This mechanism enables interaction between global and local information in an effective way, promoting cross-scale spatial and spectral feature fusion. Furthermore, we construct the encoder-decoder network in terms of GAP-Trans to obtain the final reconstruction. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed network significantly outperforms baseline methods in reconstruction accuracy.

title: Instantaneous State InSAR: Estimation and Prediction for Near Real-Time Displacement Monitoring
authors: Yuqing Wang, Wietske S. Brouwer, Freek J. Van Leijen, Ramon F. Hanssen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Urban resilience and decision-making rely on continuous monitoring of key safety indicators. The increasing availability of interferometric synthetic aperture radar (InSAR) observations offers a valuable opportunity for near real-time stability monitoring, particularly in the built environment. Traditional InSAR time series methods use batch processing of all available data at a particular moment in time to estimate static and global displacement parameters, describing the motion of the effective scatterer over the entire evaluated time period. This batch approach limits the agility of the method to adapt to a changing temporal behavior, early anomaly detection, computational efficiency, and the systematic inclusion of newly acquired SAR data. Here we introduce a new method to capture complex dynamic behavior of a scatterer by estimating its instantaneous state instead of using a time-invariant parametric description. The instantaneous state (IS) estimation and prediction model uses single new SAR acquisitions to provide time updates and measurement updates using a Kalman-filter methodology. It imposes smoothness constraints on the displacement signal by modeling the velocity as an exponentially correlated, mean-reverting Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process, thereby enhancing the practicality of the method, and employs the normalized median amplitude dispersion as a proxy for phase quality. The results demonstrate that IS-InSAR matches the estimation quality of batch methods in non-dynamic circumstances while more effectively capturing dynamic behavior. Updating instantaneous parameters with single observations enables near real-time monitoring, and the explicit specification of smoothness parameters facilitates implicit phase unwrapping.

title: Retraction Notice: Integration between wireless sensor and cloud
authors: Leena Mhatre, Neha Rai
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: As the efficient of internet of Things (IoT) is rising and is alter for remote observation of the surrounding parameters and various stuffs with the utilization of sensors that acquaint for wireless sensing of real time data and transfer them into the specified kind and facilitate to forward the perceived data across the network cloud via Internet Connection. Here the project work deals with The IoT ThingSpeak internet service that would be a generous open API service that act as variety for the vary of sensors to look at the perceived data at cloud level and composite a special feature of porting the perceived data using a channel ID and browse API key that is assigned by services and prepared to trace data value at fastidious sample at express intervals. This project to boot uses associate degree Arduino UNO board, Raspberry Pi 3 Module that helps to technique and transfer the detected data to the ThingSpeak Cloud.

title: Retraction Notice: Indiscriminate assortment navigation methods for secure statistics transmission in wireless sensor networks
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, Tapas Kumar, Y. Suresh Babu
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: In WSN different security dangers are experienced, so different security giving calculations are accessible. Traded off hub and dissent of administration are the two key assaults in remote sensor systems. Here, we contemplate directing systems that dodge dark gaps shaped by these assaults. We contend that current multi-way steering methodologies are defenseless against such assaults, primarily because of their deterministic nature. So once an enemy gains the steering calculation, it can figure the same courses know not source, and consequently imperil all data sent over these courses. We create instrument that produces randomized multipath courses. Under our configuration, the courses taken by the shares of various parcels change after some time. So regardless of the possibility that the directing calculation gets to be known not foe, the foe still can't pinpoint the courses crossed by every parcel. Other than haphazardness, the courses produced by our components are likewise profoundly dispersive and vitality effective, making them entirely equipped for bypassing dark openings at low vitality cost. Here we propose four calculations those are absolutely arbitrary proliferation, non-dreary irregular engendering, direct irregular spread and streamlined irregular spread and we contrast the four diverse calculations with study the execution. Graphical results are utilized to check the viability of our configuration.

title: Meta-Path Propagation Profiles with Machine Learning for Accurate Drug-Disease Prediction
authors: Piyanut Tangmanussukum, Apichat Suratanee, Kitiporn Plaimas
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Computational prediction of drug-disease associations is a critical task in drug discovery, often relying on machine learning (ML) models with extensive feature sets. However, large feature sets derived from drug and disease similarities can significantly increase computational costs and training time. To address this challenge, we propose the use of meta-path propagation profiles combined with machine learning for accurate drug-disease prediction. This approach is designed to reduce the need for an excessive number of features while maintaining high prediction accuracy. First, we construct six meta-path profiles for the drug-GO-disease heterogeneous network and then apply a propagation algorithm to uncover potential drug-disease interactions. The predicted drug-disease relationship vectors are then used as input features for ML models. Instead of directly combining all paths into a single profile, we compare different integration strategies and select the best-performing combination. This method effectively reduces the dimensionality of the features required for model training. After training, the results demonstrate that integrating propagation algorithms across multiple paths improves both efficiency and predictive performance. Overall, our framework provides a scalable and effective solution for drug-disease interaction prediction and prepares the way for faster and more efficient drug discovery applications.

title: Verifiable Multi-user Dynamic Searchable Symmetric Encryption with Forward and Backward Privacy Feasible for Cloud Storage
authors: Liqing Chen, Yeting Wu, Hao Zhang, Jian Weng, Zhiquan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: With the evolution of cloud storage, data outsourcing has become a common trend. While cloud storage provides convenient services, its privacy and security issues have also attracted widespread attention. Dynamic searchable symmetric encryption (DSSE) provides an efficacious means of addressing data privacy issues by allowing data owners to directly perform retrieval or update operations on data while it is stored in the cloud. However, traditional DSSE cannot meet the needs of multi-user scenarios in practical applications. In addition, DSSE implements forward privacy and backward that is resistant to file-injection attack and support validation of search results. Therefore, we present verifiable multi-user dynamic searchable symmetric encryption with forward and backward privacy feasible for cloud storage (VM-DSSE-FB). The scheme constructs a DSSE framework that satisfies both forward and backward privacy by fusing symmetric encryption with homomorphic addition and bitmap indexing techniques. Meanwhile, we set up an encrypted update queue for each user to adapt to the requirements of multi-user scenarios. In addition, with the help of bitmap indexing and logic AND operations, we realize the accurate multi-keyword query function. In particular, the cloud server returns the search results with a workload proof to realize the effective verification of the integrity and correctness of the search results. Security analysis proves that VM-DSSE-FB approach achieves adaptive security. The performance analysis shows that compared with existing similar schemes, VM-DSSE-FB achieves more functionality while improving efficiency and is more suitable for cloud storage services.

title: Low Common-Mode Noise Driver Design With Parasitic Parameter Extraction of Current-Mode Logic Driver
authors: Zhanxi Pang, Hanzhi Ma, Jianquan Lou, Haiwen Lu, Alpesh Bhobe, Er-Ping Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: The common-mode (CM) noise generated by serializer/deserializer systems causes electromagnetic (EM) interference by radiating EM energy into the surrounding environment. This article focuses on the CM noise challenge in current-mode logic (CML) driver fabricated in TSMC N65 process. The generation of CM noise in the CML driver is analyzed by applying an equivalent circuit model with parasitic capacitors at the 20-Gb/s data rate. Based on the equivalent circuit analysis, an accurate CM noise analysis method is proposed. A low-CM-noise driver design based on cascaded CML drivers is proposed to mitigate the output CM noise. The proposed driver reduces CM noise by 20.5 dB for non-return-to-zero (NRZ) signaling compared with the traditional CML driver.

title: Retraction Notice: Information Measure Computation and its Impact in MI COCO Dataset
authors: D.K. Sharma
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Classification of images is an essential task that seeks to interpret up an entire picture overall. By attaching it to a particular label, the purpose is to identify the image. Image Classification usually refers to images where only a certain individual considers and is examined. On the other hand, object identification requires either classification and implementation tasks and is used to examine more practical instances in which an image may well have several objects. This study investigates the information measure to classify images that use deep learning and machine learning methods. As a computer language, Python is used since it comes with the Creators additionally. The input data primarily focuses on the group images in which four different styles of images have been included in this article. Shannon's information measure was chosen as the training phase's best choice as it generated a high accuracy rate. In terms of the accuracy of the classification tasks in percentage, findings are discussed. One of them about 90.67 percent, so the same goes for another form of where second up to 90 percent and beyond is the average result.

title: UAV-RIS-Assisted Covert ISCC System for Near-Field Low-Latency Scenarios: System Design and Performance Analysis
authors: Wei Zhao, Shuai Hao, Ao Song, Jiaxing Yang, Xinye Li, Zhijuan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: Multifunctional integration in near-field systems challenges the timeliness and covertness of integrated sensing, communication, and computing (ISCC), particularly in ultra-reliable low-latency scenarios. We investigate an unmanned aerial vehicle-reconfigurable intelligent surface (UAV-RIS)-assisted covert ISCC system exploiting near-field spherical wave propagation. Unlike far-field approaches, our framework achieves precise distance-dependent beam focusing, shielding signals from angle-aligned wardens while performing sensing and offloading. We derive a closed-form expression for the covert age of information to quantify timeliness. We aim to maximize the covert ISCC rate constrained by covertness, block error probability, and UAV-RIS settings. The resulting non-convex, high-dimensional problem challenges traditional optimization in dynamic environments. To address this, we propose a quantum multi-agent meta-reinforcement learning algorithm with meta-training and local adaptation phases. This algorithm achieves faster convergence and higher robustness than conventional baselines. Simulations validate its effectiveness in balancing sensing accuracy, covertness, and timeliness.

title: Automatic Depression Level Prediction Based on Facial Video With Emotional Cues Using a Hybrid Machine Learning Framework
authors: Mi Li, Yingyu Chen, Junlong Gao, Zongan Dai, Wenjing Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Depression is a kind of mood disorder. Nevertheless, there is a lack of research on emotional information in the identification of depression. This study intends to explore the role of emotions in the identification of depression by analyzing the performance in facial videos induced by different emotional stimuli. It also aims to address the deficiency of emotional representation in existing video datasets. In order to study the influence of emotions on the prediction of depression levels, we initially collected facial videos from 80 depressed patients and 80 normal subjects while they were performing tasks of watching calm, sad, and joyful video clips, and then constructed an emotional facial video (eFV) dataset with emotional cues. A hybrid framework integrating deep learning and ensemble learning was established for predicting depression levels. This framework utilizes attention-based 3-D convolutional neural network (3-DCNN) to automatically learn and extract the spatio-temporal features of facial videos, assigns significance to different emotions via the emotion-cross-attention mechanism, and finally employs the ensemble learning method (XGBoost) for depression level prediction. Comparative experiments were carried out on the eFV dataset and the widely-used AVEC2013 and AVEC2014 public facial video datasets. The results show that facial videos with emotional cues significantly improve the performance of depression recognition. The evaluation errors (MAE/RMSE) of our proposed model on AVEC2013 and AVEC2014 facial videos are 7.21/9.25 and 6.57/8.36, respectively. However, the evaluation errors on facial videos with sad and happy emotions are 5.06/6.35 and 5.17/6.58, respectively. Furthermore, the fusion of sad and happy emotions achieved the lowest evaluation error  of 4.45/5.75. This highlights the critical role of emotional cues in depression recognition.

title: Tunnel-Guided Assist-as-Needed Control for Hand Pre-Shaping: Development and Validation in Healthy Participants
authors: Mianzhi Zhou, Peter Walker Ferguson, Guanyu Chen, Jacob Rosen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Rehabilitating hand function after stroke is challenging, particularly for individuals with spasticity. Grasp preshaping— an anticipatory component of motor control—is often undertrained in conventional therapy. This work introduces a synergy-based assist-as-needed (AAN) framework for preshaping using the Opposable Thumb Hand Exoskeleton for Rehabilitation (OTHER Hand). Desired motion is expressed in a synergy-dependent representation rather than time, with Gaussian Mixture Models and Regression (GMM/GMR) generating smooth, task-specific reference trajectories. Virtual corrective and tangential forces derived from joint errors and velocities adapt motor output to encourage active effort. A pilot study with five healthy participants tested the controller during both unconstrained movement and a simplified impairment condition created via constant feedforward torque. Although not a velocity-dependent model of spasticity, this perturbation provided a controlled proxy for evaluating adaptive assistance. AAN increased user engagement by 57.6% relative to passive position control and improved efficiency—defined as the ratio of user effort to trajectory error—by 10.1%-22.5% higher than for either force feedback or position control across both constraint levels. These results indicate that synergy-based minimal assistance can enhance engagement and performance, supporting its potential for clinical translation.

title: Evaluation of Metallic Interference in Electromagnetic Tracker for Handheld Robotics
authors: Poonnapa Chaichudchaval, Robert A. MacLachlan, Cameron N. Riviere
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Electromagnetic trackers (EMTs) are position sensors that enable surgical navigation and robotic guidance by measuring the six degree of freedom motion of a magnetic sensor with respect to a fixed source field. Metallic interference is a major limitation for EMTs in clinical and industrial applications. We systematically examined this problem using our In-Loop Electromagnetic Tracker (ILEMT) with dual-frequency capability (300 Hz and 10 kHz) and compared results with commercial pulsed DC modulation. We tested six metals in rod, tube, and sheet forms across multiple positions and orientations, including both ferromagnetic and non-ferromagnetic materials with varying conductivities. Low-frequency modulations (300 Hz and pulsed DC) produced nearly identical interference despite different tracker hardware, validating ILEMT’s low-frequency channel as equivalent to pulsed DC systems. The effect of low-frequency modulation varied dramatically from 260× reduction to 5.5× increase in interference depending on metal type, shape, and orientation. Contrary to simple models, large metal sheets showed unexpected behavior where material properties had minimal effect and ferromagnetic metals sometimes produced less interference at low frequencies. Shape and orientation significantly affected interference, with interference decreasing with distance following power laws with exponents ranging from -3 to -6 rather than the -6 predicted by dipole theory. These findings provide practical guidance for EMT workspace configuration and demonstrate the potential of dual-frequency systems for adaptive interference mitigation.

title: Implantable Multiband Antennas for Future Wireless Brain Health Devices: Review on Design Approaches and Recent Developments [Bioelectromagnetics]
authors: N. Pournoori, T. Björninen, L. Sydänheimo, L. Ukkonen, Asimina Kiourti
publish: IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine
abstract: Efforts to promote patients’ quality of life through advanced healthcare technology are increasingly focusing on implantable wireless biomedical telemetry systems, particularly on the development of compact, multifunctional implantable antennas. These antennas enable continuous medical monitoring and support various functions such as data transmission, wireless power transfer (WPT), and control signals across multiple frequency bands. This article reviews the challenges and recent advances in miniature multiband antennas for brain implants, with a comparative analysis of three planar inverted-F antennas (PIFAs) designs, dual band, triple band, and quad band, as case studies evaluated under the same metrics as the surveyed literature.

title: Frame-Wise Optimized Range Gate Pull-Off Jamming Strategy via Action Masking Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yujie Zhang, Weibo Huo, Xu Zhou, Cui Zhang, Yulin Huang, Jifang Pei, Yin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Range gate pull-off (RGPO) jamming is a critical electronic attack (EA) technique used to deceive radar tracking systems. However, traditional RGPO strategies often rely on fixed pull-off patterns, which are susceptible to recognition and suppression by modern adaptive radar counter-countermeasures. To address this limitation, this paper proposes a frame- wise optimized pull-off (FOP) jamming strategy based on action masking reinforcement learning, named MaskFOP. The proposed method decomposes the jamming process into discrete frames and dynamically optimizes the pull-off increments in real time. Specifically, a sequential state representation method is proposed to capture temporal dynamics by leveraging historical pull-off information. An action masking mechanism is designed to constrain the action selection space, ensuring physical feasibility and temporal consistency. Furthermore, a weighted reward function is formulated to balance the pull-off success rate and pull-off distance. Experimental results demonstrate that MaskFOP significantly outperforms benchmark strategies in terms of both deception success rate and cumulative pull-off distance. The proposed framework provides a robust and adaptive solution for modern electronic warfare scenarios.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine

title: Preisach-Deep Learning Hybrid Approach for Hysteresis-Nonlinear Dynamic Identification of a 2-DoF Force Feedback Mechanism Using MR Fluid Actuators
authors: Minh Ky Nguyen, Thanh Danh Le
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The main objective of this paper is to propose the innovative mathematical model for identifying the hysteresis dynamic behavior of a 2-DoF force feedback mechanism based on magnetorheological (MR) fluid. Indeed, the configuration of this mechanism includes two MR brakes, which are mounted orthogonally together thanks to the Cardan coupling. Each MR brake provides an independent resistance torque transmitted to the control handle. Due to exhibiting both strong hysteresis characteristics and nonlinear dynamic behavior, the construction of the identification model is designed by combining the Preisach model with the deep learning (DL) model in series, where the former is used to estimate the hysteresis effects meanwhile the influences of nonlinear dynamics are captured by the latter. Inspired by data-driven modelling, the Preisach weighting function is estimated by using the least squares method, whereas the DL is trained by the metaheuristic optimization method. Then, the effectiveness of the proposed model is evaluated and compared with that of a pure data-driven DL model. The experimental results demonstrate the capability of the proposed model to identify the complex dynamic behavior of the 2-DoF force feedback mechanism.

title: Blinded by the Beam: A Unified Real-Time Defense Against Laser-Based Attacks on Navigational Perception of Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Abhijeet Solanki, Wesam Al Amiri, Syed Rafay Hasan, Terry N. Guo
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Autonomous vehicles (AVs) rely on multi-sensor perception, particularly camera and LiDAR systems, for critical tasks such as object detection and scene understanding. However, recent studies have revealed that camera-based perception is especially vulnerable to laser-based adversarial attacks, including the Adversarial Laser Beam (AdvLB) and Adversarial Laser Spot (AdvLS). These attacks are impractical for real-time deployment as they are originally designed with iterative and dynamic configurations. In this work, we introduce and analyze a static-parameter attack strategy targeting the camera, which eliminates the need for iterative tuning while consistently degrading perception accuracy. This simplicity enables real-time feasibility, posing a practical threat to deployed AV systems. We characterize the visual artifacts introduced by such attacks using first-order statistical metrics, image filtering techniques, and machine learning-based anomaly detection. Our results show that even static laser interference can impair object recognition and disrupt camera-LiDAR sensor fusion. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that basic filters such as sharpening and Laplacian, combined with Hue, Saturation, and Value (HSV)-based contour extraction, effectively detect AdvLB artifacts. In contrast, the more subtle AdvLS perturbations require geometry-aware, adaptive detection methods. To address both, we propose and implement a lightweight, unified camera-side detection framework that integrates statistical and geometric cues; our experiments and threat model are limited to the camera modality. The system achieves over 90% detection accuracy with near real-time performance on the Jetson Orin Nano device.

title: RV-DroneEye: Unity-based framework for a Synthetic dataset for robust UAV recognition
authors: Andro Aprila Adiputra, Sehwa Ko, Thi-Thu-Huong Le, JunYoung Son, Howon Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The rapid proliferation of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has created an urgent need for robust detection & identification systems to ensure airspace security and safety. However, the scarcity of large-scale, diverse, and accurately annotated real-world datasets hinders the development of effective UAV detection & identification algorithms. To address this challenge, we present the RV-DroneEye (Realistic Virtual DroneEye) Dataset, a comprehensive framework for generating synthetic datasets utilizing the Unity 3D simulation engine to enhance UAV detection training. Additionally, we leverage photorealistic augmentation using Flux.1 diffusion-based model as a base, diverse environmental conditions, and physically accurate flight dynamics to generate large-scale annotated datasets that capture the visual complexity of real-world UAV detection scenarios. Then we evaluate our dataset’s generalization on various benchmarks and train multiple object-detection models. The RV-DroneEye dataset includes diverse UAV models, environments (urban, forest, lake), weather, and lighting conditions.We assess its effectiveness by training state-of-the-art object detection models and evaluating them on real-world test sets, including a multiclass task for more than 20 UAV types. Results show that models trained on RV-DroneEye achieve comparable or better UAV pattern generalization than those using limited real-world data, with marked gains in challenging scenarios such as low-light conditions, extreme angles, and cluttered backgrounds.

title: A Lightweight Framework With Pruning and Knowledge Distillation for Ship Detection in SAR Images
authors: Xu Xu, Wei Zhang, Gyanendra Kumar, Bayan Alabdullah, Jing Yang, Por Lip Yee
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Lightweight synthetic aperture radar (SAR) ship detection is critical for real-time maritime monitoring on resource-constrained platforms. However, speckle noise and sea clutter often obscure small and low-contrast vessels, making accurate detection difficult under strict computational budgets. To address this, we propose a compact detection framework that integrates layerwise amplitude-based pruning with dual-stream knowledge distillation, enabling substantial model compression while maintaining detection performance. In addition, we adopt efficiency-oriented building blocks to improve feature utilization and introduce a localization-aware regression objective to strengthen bounding box fitting on low intersection over union (IoU) samples. Experiments on three public SAR ship detection benchmarks demonstrate that the proposed pruned and distilled model achieves higher precision and mean average precision (mAP) than recent peer methods, while running above 200 frames per second (FPS) with only 7.4 M parameters and 41.9 G floating point operations (FLOPs). These results indicate that pruning-guided distillation provides an effective and practical path toward deployable SAR ship detection.

title: A Double-Stage Detail Enhancement Framework for Small Object Detection in High Spatial Resolution Remote Sensing Images
authors: Yubo Wang, Jinhui Li, Wei Zhao, Yetao Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Small objects in remote sensing imagery are difficult to detect due to their limited spatial extent and severe background interference. To address this challenge, a Double-Stage Detail Enhancement Framework (DSDEF) is proposed to improve detection performance through two successive stages. In the first stage, remote sensing imagery is refined by a Detail-Aware Reconstruction Module (DARM), which leverages an improved diffusion-based mechanism to restore structural integrity and edge sharpness of small targets. In the second stage, a detection network built upon the DINO architecture is enhanced with custom-designed modules for attention refinement and text-guided feature modeling. Specifically, a Selective Channel Enhancement Unit (SCEU) and a Text-Semantic Perception Module (TSPM) are introduced to improve discriminative capability and attention precision in cluttered scenes. Furthermore, a Semantic-guided RankTop Initialization (SRTI) strategy leverages textual priors to guide decoder queries, enabling more accurate localization of small objects. Experimental evaluations demonstrate that the proposed DSDEF significantly improves the detection of extremely small targets. Specifically, DSDEF achieves 79.5% mean average precision (mAP) on the DIOR dataset and 95.3% on NWPU VHR-10, demonstrating its effectiveness and robustness. The code is publicly available at: https://github.com/wyb711/DSDEF.

title: Rapid Multipoint Gas Sensing Using a Silicon-Photonic-Integrated Widely-Tunable Narrow-Linewidth Laser by FMCW Interferometry
authors: Xiutao Lou, Yunfei Meng, Ning Xu, Yongkang Dong
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We present a spectroscopic method using a silicon-photonic-integrated laser by frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) interferometry to realize rapid multipoint gas sensing. The employed hybrid integrated external cavity laser has a wide tuning range of 147 GHz, a narrow linewidth of 180 kHz and a high sweeping speed of 294 THz/s (∼2700 nm/s), enabling sub-millisecond multipoint spectroscopic gas sensing. In the proof-of-concept experimental demonstration, three methane sensing nodes are deployed along a 30-m optical fiber, achieving a measurement sensitivity of 6 ppm with a time resolution of 0.5 ms and a positioning spatial resolution of 0.7 mm. Having the advantage of high spatiotemporal resolution, our proposed method promotes a novel way of developing spectroscopic gas sensors for challenging safety monitoring applications where spatially resolved chemical analysis with fast response is required.

title: Large Language Model-based Smart Agents for Attack-Defense Game on Cyber-Physical Power Systems
authors: Bingrui Wang, Yuan Zhou, Shouxiang Wang, Bowen Xu, Ruilong Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid
abstract: With the deep integration of information technology, cyber-physical power systems (CPPSs) face increasingly complex attack-defense challenges. Traditional game theory-based methods struggle with sophisticated strategy reasoning and decision interpretability. This paper proposes an innovative CPPS attack-defense game paradigm incorporating large language model (LLM)-based smart agents to address these limitations. First, we define a game model integrating Bayesian Nash Equilibrium and betweenness centrality to characterize the strategy stability and key node prioritization. Second, we design smart agent architectures with memory, toolset, planning, and action execution modules to enable adaptive strategy formulation in complex environments. Then, we give a systematic elaboration of the dynamic game processes, including vulnerability scanning, attack, detection, response, and post-analysis phases. Experiments on IEEE 14 and IEEE 33 bus systems demonstrate that the proposed method significantly enhances defense success rate, convergence efficiency, and strategy diversity, validating the potential of LLMs in improving CPPS security through the intelligent attack-defense game.

title: Validation of Real-Time Text (RTT) over IMS Functionalities A Case Study in a Telecommunications Laboratory Environment to Ensure Software Quality
authors: André José França, Gaspar Henrique Alves Mota
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 14th International Conference on Software and Information Engineering
abstract: None

title: JNTD-DS: A Benchmark Dataset for Just Noticeable frame rate-based Temporal Difference in Perceptual Video Coding
authors: Sanaz Nami, Farhad Pakdaman, Sahab Taali, Mahmoud Reza Hashemi, Shervin Shirmohammadi, Moncef Gabbouj
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Multimedia Systems Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Real-time hybrid DAS/F-DMAS block-wise beamforming in imaging systems with 2-D matrix arrays and embedded microbeamformers
authors: Piero Tortoli, Valentino Meacci, Lorenzo Castrignano, Giulia Matrone, Enrico Boni, Alessandro Ramalli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Ultrasonics
abstract: High-quality 3-D imaging can be obtained by means of densely populated 2-D matrix array probes that embed a microbeamformer (μBF). Delay and Sum (DAS) beamforming is achieved by compensating for fine delays in the μBF, and for coarse delays in the associated scanner. Modifying the operation of the μBF integrated into the probe is really challenging, and this has discouraged the possible adoption of different beamforming algorithms. This experimental study evaluates whether the contrast and resolution benefits of the Filtered-Delay Multiply and Sum (F-DMAS) beamforming algorithm can be achieved while retaining DAS processing in the embedded μBF, and performing F-DMAS exclusively at the scanner level. Experimental tests were performed using an open scanner connected to a commercial probe equipped with a 3072-element array and an embedded DAS μBF that reduces the channel count to 192. For this project, a simplified F-DMAS algorithm was implemented directly on the scanner. Phantom images as well as phantom and in vivo bi-plane movies show, for the first time, the feasibility of such a 3-D hybrid DAS/F-DMAS operation in real-time. Thanks to an acquisition modality purposely developed for this project, it was possible to compare the “hybrid” images with those obtained through conventional DAS and F-DMAS, respectively. It was thus found that, compared to DAS, the proposed method improves both resolution (e.g., about 20% narrower point spread functions) and contrast (e.g., up to +11.4 dB contrast ratio), while, compared to full F-DMAS, image quality changes to a limited extent.

title:
Updating Arc-Flash Analysis for Offshore Platform to IEEE Std 1584
                    <sup>TM</sup>
                    -2018
authors: Alok Gupta, Raghu Veeraraghavan, Walter Gonzalez, Chris Chaney
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: This paper presents a case study on revising an offshore platform's arc-flash analysis to align with IEEE 1584-2018<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TM</sup> and NFPA 70E 2024. The initial arc-flash study for the platform electrical generation, distribution, and utilization system was performed several years ago based on 2002 version of the standard. The paper discusses the challenges in data gathering and model revision, provides guidance for the update process, and compares the original and new study outcomes. Recommendations are provided on the approach for updating the model and use of simulation tools to prepare for future expansion. Explanation of differences in results between the existing study and the updated study is included. The findings offer insights into the effort involved in updating the study and potential differences in results for ANSI design electrical systems on offshore platforms. Mitigation strategies and comprehensive review of protection and coordination studies to reduce incident energy are not a major focus of this paper, but are briefly mentioned for context.

title: Role of Hydrogen on the ITO/Ag Contact Resistance for Low-Temperature Screen-Printing Metallization of Pero/Si Tandems
authors: Jannik N. Kleesiek, Maximilian Günther, Alexander Steigert, Darja Erfurt, Stefan Janke, Marlene Härtel, Rutger Schlatmann, Bernd Stannowski, Angelika Harter
publish: IEEE Journal of Photovoltaics
abstract: The upscaling of perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells is an increasingly important topic in research to bridge the gap between laboratory scale performance and industrial application. This requires adapting well-established technologies to enable the processing of upscaled tandem cell manufacturing steps, including the transparent conductive oxide (TCO) front electrode by magnetron sputtering and the metal contact by screen printing. For state-of-the-art silicon heterojunction (SHJ) solar cells, both the TCO and the metal contact are annealed at temperatures around 200 °C. Perovskite films in contrast are known to degrade at such high temperatures, which makes the processing of the front contact for perovskite based solar cells more challenging. In this work, we demonstrate an optimized screen-printing process that, with the right choice of sputtering process parameters, enables a low TCO/Ag contact resistivity of around 1 mΩcm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at a maximum processing temperature of 135 °C and therefore suitable for (most) perovskite absorbers. The interaction between the TCO and the silver paste is analyzed in detail with respect to achieving low contact resistivity between the two layers. Our results demonstrate that controlling the hydrogen content during the sputtering process is critical: higher hydrogen levels result in a clear increase in the specific contact resistivity. Finally, we show a screen-printed tandem device that further confirms the process feasibility.

title: SPCS-RSNet: Shape Perception and Context Synergy for Lightweight Remote Sensing Object Detection
authors: Guoqing Li, Ming Zhang, Fei Fan, Dahua Yu, Kehong Liu, Xiaoli Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: To address the challenges of high computational complexity and large model parameters in remote sensing object detection on edge devices, this paper proposes a Shape Perception and Context Synergy for Lightweight Remote Sensing Object Detection (SPCS-RSNet). First, we introduced a Feature Coordination Spatial Adaptation (FCSA) module, which integrated dual-branch dynamic convolution and coordinate-aware attention to expand the receptive field while improving the network's ability to perceive object shape and location. Second, we designed a Lightweight Cross-layer Feature Pyramid Network (LCFPN) that adopted an asymmetric bidirectional topology and adaptive weighting strategy, significantly reducing the parameter load during multi-scale fusion. Furthermore, we proposed a Lightweight Spatial-Channel Attention Downsampling (SCADown) module, which combined group convolution and single-head self-attention to preserve critical spatial-channel information during feature compression. Finally, we constructed a Multi-scale Synergistic Detection Head (MSSD Head), which aggregated multi-scale contextual information through a group-parallel-fusion mechanism to improve detection accuracy while reducing computational redundancy. Experimental results on datasets such as DIOR, NWPU-VHR-10, and HRRSD showed that the proposed method achieved detection performance superior to current mainstream methods while maintaining a low parameter quantity of 2.36 M and a floating-point operation volume of 6.1 G. Additionally, its successful deployment on the NVIDIA Jetson Xavier NX platform further validated the practical value of this method in resource-constrained real-time remote sensing detection scenarios.

title: Defense Surface Enhancement Control for Capturing a Superior Evader in Reach-Avoid Games With Incomplete Information
authors: Lulu Zhang, Libin Zhang, Lixiu Yao, Yunze Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: In multi-agent reach-avoid games, the incomplete information and kinematic superiority of the evader exacerbate the challenge for pursuers to achieve successful capture within a finite time. Given the evader's advantages in information and kinematics, we propose a cooperative distributed control strategy for pursuers in the reachability-based control framework. The pursuers are required to achieve the capture condition while maintaining the defense surface. First, we extend the analysis of the geometric properties of the reach-avoid game based on Apollonius circle, and provide the linearized feasible sets of pursuers, simplifying the original nonlinear constraints to linear ones. We then construct conservative feasible sets of pursuers over a time horizon to account for all possible behaviors of the evader. Leveraging this construction, we develop a distributed model predictive control based strategy with adaptive weighting matrices, which simultaneously achieves capture and enhances the maintenance of defense surface. Finally, simulation results demonstrate that the proposed strategy can achieve high capture performance and connectivity maintenance capability across diverse cases, and validate the effectiveness for capturing a superior evader with incomplete information.

title: FFGSM: A Novel Fuzzy Logic-Based FGSM Attack for Deep Neural Networks Vulnerability Assessment
authors: Oumaima Liouane, Seifeddine Messaoud
publish: IEEE Transactions on Fuzzy Systems
abstract: Adversarial examples remain a major obstacle to the reliable deployment of deep neural networks, yet widely used one-step attacks such as FGSM rely on a fixed perturbation budget that ignores how vulnerability varies across inputs and architectural paradigms. In this paper, we propose Fuzzy-FGSM (FFGSM), a single-step, interpretable adversarial attack that adaptively allocates the $\ell \_\infty$ perturbation budget on a per-sample basis. FFGSM couples the FGSM sign direction with a lightweight Mamdani fuzzy inference system that maps two human-interpretable indicators-the input-gradient norm (GN), capturing local sensitivity, and the model confidence (CN), capturing prediction certainty-into a bounded, sample-specific perturbation magnitude $\hat{\epsilon }\in [0,\epsilon \_{\max }]$. We evaluate FFGSM under $\epsilon \_{\max }=8/255$ across three datasets (CIFAR-10, STL-10, and Tiny-ImageNet) and four architectures (ResNet-18, ResNet-50, VGG-16, and DenseNet-121). Our results demonstrate that FFGSM consistently outperforms transfer-based and black-box baselines, achieving peak Attack Success Rates (ASR) of up to $85.53\%$ on DenseNet-121/Tiny-ImageNet, while remaining surprisingly close to the strong multi-step AutoAttack ensemble of about $80.2\%$ vs. $81.7\%$ ASR on CIFAR-10/ResNet18, and $64.5\%$ vs. $66.8\%$ on STL-10/ResNet50. Notably, FFGSM remains remarkably competitive with the strong multi-step Auto-Attack ensemble, often trailing by only a 1–$4\%$ margin despite being a single-step method. Furthermore, cross-model transferability analysis reveals that FFGSM effectively exploits the “transferability gap,” showing that residual-based models generate more universal adversarial directions than sequential models like VGG-16. Runtime measurements confirm that the fuzzy controller adds only a negligible overhead (approx. $5.4\%$–$7.7\%$), preserving the simplicity and real-time efficiency of single-step generation.

title: Joint Power Allocation and Performance Analysis for NOMA-Based Communication-Radar Co-Existence Systems
authors: Surjeet Kumar, B. R. Manoj, Manav R. Bhatnagar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The co-existence of communication and radar systems has emerged as a promising approach to address spectrum scarcity in 5G and beyond networks. This paper investigates a power-domain non-orthogonal multiple access (PD-NOMA)-based joint communication-radar framework that utilizes the same resource block to enhance spectral efficiency, while simultaneously introducing mutual cross-interference challenges.We propose three co-existence strategies: a communication-centric approach that maximizes the individual user rates in a two-userNOMAscenario, a radar-centric approach that maximizes the radar signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR), and a joint strategy that balances both radar and communication objectives. The joint success probability (JSP), defined as the probability of simultaneously satisfying both radar and communication constraints, is derived together with a high-SINR approximation that offers deeper analytical insights into system performance. The JSP formulation is further extended to account for correlated log-normal shadowing in the canonical two-user setting and, separately, to enable scalability to a multi-user downlink NOMA system. Furthermore, radar target detection under communication interference is formulated as a binary hypothesis testing problem. Two optimal detection thresholds are derived: the Neyman-Pearson (NP) threshold for a fixed false-alarm probability and the minimum-error threshold, which minimizes the total probability of detection error. A closed-form relationship between the two thresholds is established, revealing a scaling dependence on the effective radar SINR. The analysis assumes coordinated communication-radar co-existence, while uncoordinated operation is examined as a benchmark to quantify performance degradation due to unknown communication interference. Moreover, the optimal power allocation factor (PAF) that maximizes the JSP is obtained. The simulation results validate the theoretical analysis and demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed framework in achieving a reliable communication-radar spectrum sharing solution to overcome the spectrum congestion problem.

title: ESHIC: Efficient Learning-Based Scalable HDR Compression with Hybrid Structural-Tonal Prior Modeling
authors: Yang Chen, Liquan Shen, Zhaoyi Tian, Xiangyu Hu, Feifeng Wang, Shiwei Wang, Zihao Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: To ensure backward compatibility with legacy Standard Dynamic Range (SDR) devices which require an SDR bitstream, dynamic range scalable transmission strategies face a critical challenge: how to transmit a minimal-sized High Dynamic Range (HDR) enhancement bitstream on top of the decoded SDR bitstream while still enabling high-fidelity HDR reconstruction? To address this problem, the strong correlation between SDR and HDR images (distinct tonal properties but shared structural information) is leveraged to improve inter-layer reference modeling in our novel learning-based framework, termed Efficient Scalable HDR Image Compression (ESHIC). ESHIC introduces two key innovations: (1) An Inter-layer Shared Knowledge Embedding (ISKE) module that employs hybrid structural attention to embed shared structural knowledge across SDR and HDR domains in the enhancement layer to help eliminate multi-scale structural redundancy; (2) An Inter-layer Context Refinement (ICR) module that extracts compact HDR tonal priors from HDR images to help predict high-precision inter-layer context which enables more accurate entropy modeling. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ESHIC achieves a 39.06% bit-rate reduction over the traditional scalable codec SHVC, and 16.63% bit-rate savings compared with the learning-based method EPIC-HDR, in terms of the HDR-VDP3 metric.

title: Interval Type-2 Fuzzy-Based Dynamic Event-Triggered Distributed Control for Multiarea Power Systems Against Hybrid Attacks
authors: Xiehuan Li, Guangdeng Zong, Xiang Liu, Xudong Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: This article investigates the dynamic event-triggered distributed load frequency control (LFC) problem for interval type-2 (IT2) fuzzy-based multiarea power systems under hybrid malicious attacks, i.e., denial-of-service (DoS) attacks and deception attacks. Based on the acknowledgment (ACK) character technique, a new dynamic event-triggered mechanism (DETM) is proposed to further save network resources. Considering the uncertain nonlinearity in turbine and governor dynamics, a novel stochastic switching IT2 fuzzy power system model is built under hybrid attacks and DETMs. To achieve LFC, a distributed fuzzy controller is designed to incorporate the neighborhood information of power systems. Then, a novel piecewise Lyapunov functional is developed to make the closed-loop system mean-square exponentially stable with <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$H_{\infty }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> disturbance attenuation performance. With the developed three-step estimation method, the boundary information of membership function (MF) product terms is acquired and introduced into the stability analysis to reduce the conservativeness of stability conditions. Finally, three-area power systems are given to show the effectiveness of the distributed security control algorithm.

title: A Cyclic Shift Chirp Modulation Scheme for Analog Message with Simplified PPM Demodulation
authors: Chanapat Kaew-in, Nisukan Chatchaiphat, Paramote Wardkein, Peeramed Chodkaveekityada
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: In this work, a cyclic shift chirp modulation scheme with an analog message signal is presented. The demodulation process is carried out by converting the cyclic shift chirp into pulse position modulation (PPM), followed by three demodulation approaches: (i) direct LPF filtering of the PPM pulses, (ii) inversion of the PPM pulses prior to LPF filtering, and (iii) integration driven by the PPM pulses. The recovered signal in these cases corresponds to the differentiated form of the original message signal. However, when the message signal is preintegrated before PPM modulation, the subsequent demodulation via integration directly yields the original message signal, eliminating the need for an additional integration stage at the receiver. This results in a simplified and less complex receiver compared to conventional PPM schemes without input pre-integration. Analytical and experimental results are in close agreement, validating the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Implementation of Accuracy Improvement Methods for Sensory Data Measurements in Embedded Systems
authors: Ivan Tsmots, Vasyl Teslyuk, Vasyl Rabyk, Yurii Opotyak
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Methods for improving sensor measurement data in embedded systems based on the Kalman filter and incremental PCA algorithms (GHA, CCIPCA) are proposed. The main advantages and limitations of each are given. The Kalman filter and the incremental method of GHA PCA are implemented in C to filter noise from sensor measurements. Examples of data filtering with noise using Kalman filter algorithms and GHA PCA, based on modeling of sensor input data, are considered. MSE was used to evaluate the accuracy of noise filtering. A study was conducted to analyze the accuracy of recovering input data with noise using the GHA PCA method, with a focus on the number of principal components. A comparison is given in terms of the accuracy of filtering input data using the Kalman filter and the GHA PCA method.

title: Expert System for Coin Condition Assessment Using Interval Type-2 Fuzzy Sets
authors: Artem Kharchenko, Natalia Pasichnyk, Renat Rizhniak
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Type-2 fuzzy logic is often used in expert systems to model complex types of uncertainty, particularly those arising from discrepancies in expert opinions and the ambiguity of linguistic terms. This paper explores the application of interval type-2 fuzzy sets to formalize uncertainties during the numismatic grading process. In this work, a fuzzy inference system based on interval type-2 fuzzy sets was developed to automate the determination of coin condition. The key elements of the system are described, including 5 input linguistic variables and 1 output variable. Terms and membership functions for each linguistic variable have been defined. To account for coin assessment involving both negative and positive factors, a model for the rule base was also developed. Dependencies between the input and output parameters of the model have been modeled and are presented. The proposed model can be integrated into decision support systems to increase objectivity in the numismatic market and for the automated classification of large collections.

title: Retraction Notice: A Model based on Embedded Artificial Intelligence for Retail Industry
authors: Kiran Nair, Khalid K. Moenardy, Santosh Rupa Jaladi, Yudi Supiyanto, Kymbat Suleimenova, Yuriy Popov

abstract: The application of fashion technology systems and Artificial Intelligence (AI) implanted in Industrial Automation (IA), and Artificial Intelligence consciousness is a huge registration field for modern retail industrial application. title: Unhealthy OCT Image Detection Using Machine Learning Based on Line Profile Features
authors: Onwara Potchongruk, Chanakan Chanpipattanachai, Yada Empraneat, Pakinee Aimmanee
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) provides high-resolution retinal cross-sections for early detection of vision-threatening diseases. This study investigates the effectiveness of intensity line profile features extracted in vertical, horizontal, and combined directions for classifying unhealthy OCT images using conventional machine learning. Eleven statistical and textural features were extracted from each directional profile and evaluated using several machine learning classifiers. Experiments conducted on 1,200 OCT images demonstrate that the best performance is achieved by combining vertical and horizontal line profiles, reaching an accuracy of 93.5% with the Support Vector Machine classifier. ANOVA feature importance analysis supports the finding that the most discriminative descriptors come from both horizontal and vertical line profiles. Our proposed method is particularly suitable for large-scale or resource-limited clinical screening.

title: Development of a Natural Language Processing Model for Mental Health Risk Assessment
authors: Ajchara Phu-ang, Sawit Asawamethapant
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study aims to develop a Natural Language Processing (NLP) model combined with machine learning techniques for classifying and assessing mental health conditions from social media texts. The scope encompasses both general populations (Normal) and individuals at risk of mental health issues such as Depression, Anxiety, Stress, Suicidal Tendencies, Bipolar Disorder, and Personality Disorder. The research process involves extracting statistical and linguistic features, including TF-IDF and Term Frequency, along with tokenization, stemming, and stop-word removal, to optimize input quality. Machine learning algorithms, including Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and Multi-Layer Perceptron are applied to train and evaluate the best performing model based on Precision, Recall, and F1-Score. In addition, the study explores the use of a Large Language Model, Typhoon, as a translation layer to convert Thai texts into English before analysis, making mental health screening more accessible and applicable across both languages.

title: Investigating Psychosocial Risk Factors in the Workplace: A Systematic Review
authors: Jetawat Yimyeamsuk, Pittawat Ueasangkomsate
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study aims to investigate psychosocial risk factors in the workplace. This study employed a systematic literature review methodology; 29 studies published between 2014 and 2025 were selected from the Scopus database and analyzed through content analysis following the PRISMA 2020 guidelines. The findings revealed four themes: high workload, digital strain, work- life imbalance and toxic workplace environment. Workload and digital strain were associated with excessive job demands and constant connectivity, while work–life imbalance and toxic environments reflected cultural and managerial deficiencies that undermine employee autonomy and social support. The findings indicate that psychosocial risks arise primarily from organizational structures and work design rather than individual weakness. This study provides a conceptual foundation for understanding psychosocial risk factors as systemic organizational conditions and suggests future research in underrepresented contexts beyond Europe, particularly in developing economies and hybrid work systems, to capture evolving patterns of workplace risks.

title: Cloud Resource Allocation Framework using Machine Learning
authors: Maryyam Maryyam, Ahmed Shaharyar Khwaja, Anju S. Pillai, Alagan Anpalagan
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Cloud computing enables scalable and on-demand resource provisioning. However, inefficient cloud resource allocation either leads to overprovisioning that wastes resources, or underprovisioning that degrades performance. This study proposes a machine learning-aided resource allocation framework to optimize cloud resource allocation by predicting workload demands. Historical workload data is used to train predictive models, including XGBoost, Random Forest, and Support Vector Regression. Feature engineering techniques such as lag features and time-series transformations are applied, and hyperparameter tuning is performed using Grid Search Cross-Validation. Results show that the XGBoost achieves the highest accuracy. The bestperforming model is used for workload prediction and resource allocation in two modes: static provisioning, which maintains a fixed resource allocation, and dynamic provisioning, which adjusts resources in real-time. Additionally, load-balancing techniques ensure efficient task distribution across virtual machines. Experimental results show a reduction in overprovisioning while maintaining system performance, improving resource utilization.
