title: Optimal I-PD and I-TD Controllers Design for Vehicle Cruise Control System Using Whale Optimization Algorithm
authors: Chaiyo Thammarat, Kittisak Lurang, Thiwa Jitwang, Sarot Hlangnamthip, Auttarat Nawikavatan, Deacha Puangdownreong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: PID and TID controllers have been proposed as the primary controllers in automatic control systems. However, a major drawback of both PID and TID controllers emerges when they encounter a sudden change in the input signal–the control signal exhibits a phenomenon known as the set-point kick. This behavior poses significant challenges for the practical implementation of these controllers. To address this issue, I-PD and I-TD controllers can be employed. This paper presents the optimal design of I-PD and I-TD controllers for the vehicle cruise control system (VCCS), utilizing the whale optimization algorithm (WOA). The results demonstrate that both I-PD and I-TD controllers optimized by the WOA effectively mitigate the set-point kick phenomenon occurred in the PID and TID controllers. Moreover, the I-TD controller exhibits a faster response time and reduced oscillation compared to the I-PD controller.

title: Interactive Word-Cloud-Based Learning Approach to Enhance Chinese Vocabulary Learning in Primary Students
authors: Qi Wang, Kannika Daungcharone, Krittawaya Thongkoo
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study evaluated the impact of a word cloud technology-based instructional intervention on Chinese vocabulary acquisition and learning motivation among second-grade students in a Thai primary school. Grounded in Cognitive Load Theory (CLT) and Self-Determination Theory (SDT), the research employed a pre-test and post-test quasi-experimental design, with the experimental group using interactive word cloud learning activities and the control group receiving traditional rote memorization and flashcard instruction. The study covered the Youth Chinese Test (YCT) Level 1 curriculum over a period of five weeks. Statistical analyses employed one-way ANOVA to evaluate students' learning achievement and one-way MANOVA to evaluate students' motivation to learn. The word cloud activities were designed to reduce extraneous cognitive load through visual signaling, while supporting autonomy and competence by allowing students to choose their own organizational themes and layouts. Key findings indicate that, in terms of vocabulary achievement, the experimental group's post-test was significantly higher than that of the control group, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{(1,58)}=26.232, p<.001$</tex>. In addition, the analysis revealed a highly significant multivariate effect, with a very large partial eta-squared (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\eta^{2}=.545$</tex>). Follow-ups revealed significant effects on intrinsic motivation, self-efficacy, self-determination, and academic motivation. Word clouds, as a low-cost, high-leverage pedagogical tool, help manage cognitive load and satisfy fundamental psychological needs, including autonomy, competence, and relatedness, offering a scalable solution for resource-constrained educational settings. The results add to the growing body of research on technology-enhanced language learning and have useful implications for teachers and curriculum designers who work with students who speak more than one language.

title: Sustainability and Monetization Approaches in EU Digital Innovation Hubs and EU Excellence Hubs
authors: Viktoria Dimitrova, Albena Mihovska, Yana Tsankova, Elitsa Gieva, Agata Manolova, Vladimir Poulkov, Didoe Prevedorou
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper examines how sustainability is defined and operationalized differently across two distinct EU innovation support models: Digital Innovation Hubs (EDIHs) and European Excellence Hubs (EIHs), with particular attention to how monetization strategies enable and constrain sustainability outcomes in each model. As the European innovation ecosystem transitions from centralized to distributed support systems, understanding sustainability and the dimensions encompasses (financial, operational, environmental, and social), alongside the monetization mechanisms that fund these operations, becomes critical for long-term viability. Through comparative analysis, this study investigates how structural differences between EDIHs (focused on digital transformation and SME support with market-oriented revenue logic) and EIHs (oriented toward research-innovation networks with public-goods-oriented funding logic) necessitate divergent sustainability and monetization strategies across short-term (1-3 years) and long-term (3+ years) horizons. Sustainability and monetization definitions for each hub type are developed, and the strategic approaches are mapped across different institutional contexts (public, private, mixed). Finally, mechanisms, including diversified revenue streams, asset development, and stakeholder engagement that ensure organizational viability within their respective ecosystems are identified. The research aligns these findings with the European Green Deal, Smart Specialization Strategy, and Sustainable Development Goals, proposing integrated sustainability and monetization frameworks that can enhance transferability of best practices across European regions and institutional contexts.

title: Retraction Notice: A Machine Learning Approach for Adversarial Attack Detection and Mitigation in Cybersecurity
authors: Sohini Chowdhury, Rupali Gill, Hemant Singh Pokhariya, Anurag Shrivastava
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: This study examines the viability of machine learning approaches in recognizing and relieving antagonistic assaults in cybersecurity. Leveraging a different dataset, including kind and malicious organized activity tests, four noticeable calculations were assessed: SVM, Random Forest, CNN, and LSTM. Results illustrate CNN and Random Forest beating, accomplishing precision values of 98.3% and 97.6% individually. Precision, review, and Fl-score values for CNN are 97.9%, 98.6%, and 98.2%; for Asymmetrical Forest, they are 96.3 %, 98.2 %, and 97.2 % respectively. These calculations show vigorous execution in precisely recognizing and moderating antagonistic assaults, underscoring their utility in reinforcing cybersecurity resistance. Besides, comparison with related work within the field highlights the predominant execution of our approach.

title: Quasi Colored Noise Models And Their Amplitude Distributions
authors: Andrii Kuzyk, Myron Nykolyshyn
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Proposed work presents a method for synthesizing colored noise signals in the time domain using the inverse Fast Fourier Transform (IFFT) applied to a vector of spectral samples that represent idealized colored noise with limited frequency band. The model allows generation of white, pink, blue and quasi-colored noise with adjustable spectral non-uniformity coefficient, resulting in realistic stochastic waveforms. Time-domain noise realizations and probability density histograms were analyzed. The method can be implemented in mathematical software environments (SMath Studio, Mathcad) and programming languages such as Python. Analytical expressions for the main statistical parameters are presented. Numerical simulations are performed to verify the theoretical results. Histograms of amplitude distributions and power spectra are obtained and analyzed. The influence of model parameters on the shape of amplitude distributions is demonstrated.

title: Design and Development of the Food Expiration Reminder Application Based on the Theory of Planned Behavior as an Innovative Solution to Reduce Food Waste
authors: Alesya Loventya Andisuwa, Happy Ivana, Siencia Deva Winata, Devanny Gumulya, Sheena Liman
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Food waste is a global issue with severe environmental, economic, and social impacts. The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) estimates that one third of global food production, approximately 1.3 billion tons, is wasted each year, while in Indonesia household food waste reaches 23 to 48 million tons annually. Young adults living independently often struggle to manage household food efficiently, especially packaged products that are easily forgotten and left to expire. To address this issue, this study developed Expire Me, a mobile application designed to help users organize and monitor packaged food by tracking expiration dates, providing reminders, and suggesting recipes based on available ingredients. The research applies the Theory of Planned Behavior (TPB) to examine the psychological factors influencing food management and integrates the Design Thinking process and User Experience (UX) principles to create an intuitive, user-centered design that encourages sustainable consumption. Data were collected through interviews, questionnaires, user testing, and analysis of existing applications. The findings indicate that personalized reminders, visual tracking, and contextual recipe suggestions enhance user awareness and perceived control, promoting responsible food use in line with Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 12.3, which aims to halve global food waste by 2030.

title: A Conceptual ARP-Based Framework for DHCP IP Address Recovery in ICMP-Restricted Environments
authors: Mayoon Yaibuates, Thanawut Thanavanich, Maykin Warasart, Krittika Kantawong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: IP address recovery is a critical function of DHCP servers, particularly in dynamic and security-sensitive network environments. Existing implementations rely primarily on ICMP Echo Requests to determine whether an assigned IP address remains active. However, the widespread adoption of host-based firewalls, security appliances, and constrained IoT devices has resulted in a growing number of systems that either block or silently drop ICMP packets. Therefore, ICMP-based probing increasingly produces false positives, leading to incorrect assumptions about IP occupancy, potential IP conflicts, and misidentification of legitimate devices as malicious nodes. This work proposes an ARP-based IP Address occupancy verification mechanism that eliminates ICMP dependency by leveraging Layer 2 ARP probing, which is inherently more reliable in local broadcast domains and significantly less prone to filtering.

title: ILSMPA: An Iterated Local Search Marine Predators Algorithm for AUV Magnetic Calibration
authors: Fanzong Zeng, Zefei Zhu, Xueting Zhang, Zhenlong Xu, Hao Li, Xuanlin Liu, Chenhang Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Magnetometer-equipped autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) are widely used in magnetic anomaly detection, where measurement accuracy is critical for reliable data interpretation. Magnetic correction algorithms play a vital role in improving magnetometer data quality. While swarm intelligence optimization algorithms have emerged as effective tools for parameter calibration due to their robustness to suboptimal data collection environments, they often suffer from premature convergence to local optima. To address this limitation, we propose an Iterated Local Search Marine Predators Algorithm (ILSMPA) that enhances optimization performance through two key innovations: Halton sequence-based parameter initialization to improve search space coverage, and a memory-driven local search mechanism to refine the Marine Predators Algorithm (MPA) process. These modifications accelerate convergence while preventing local optima entrapment. Furthermore, we derive a simplified magnetic compensation model for AUV platforms by analyzing dominant error sources. The algorithm’s performance was rigorously validated through controlled pool tests followed by progressive field trials in lake and marine environments. Comparative results demonstrate ILSMPA’s superiority over standard MPA: 13.53nT reduction in root mean square error (RMSE) for pool test data, 77.13nT decrease in maximum magnetic field fluctuation amplitude (F), and significantly faster convergence (15-25% improvement in iteration speed). These findings confirm that ILSMPA offers superior effectiveness and reliability for AUV magnetic compensation applications.

title: ChargeX: Exploring State and Rate Attacks in Electric Vehicle Charging Systems
authors: Ce Zhou, Qiben Yan, Zhiyuan Yu, Eshan Dixit, Ning Zhang, Huacheng Zeng, Alireza Safdari Ghanhdari
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) have become one of the promising solutions to the ever-evolving environmental and energy crisis. The key to the wide adoption of EVs is a pervasive charging infrastructure, composed of both the private/home chargers and the public/commercial charging stations. The security of EV charging, however, has not been thoroughly investigated. This paper investigates the communication mechanisms between the chargers and EVs, and exposes the lack of protection on the authenticity in the SAE J1772 charging control protocol. To showcase our discoveries, we propose a new class of attacks, ChargeX, which aims to manipulate the charging states or charging rates of EV chargers with the goal of disrupting the charging schedules, causing denial of service (DoS), or degrading the battery performance. ChargeX inserts a hardware attack circuit to strategically modify the charging control signals. We design and implement multiple attack systems, and evaluate the attacks on a public charging station and two home chargers using a simulated vehicle load in the lab environment. Extensive experiments on different types of chargers demonstrate the effectiveness and generalization of ChargeX. Specifically, we demonstrate that ChargeX can force a Tesla’s charging state to switch from “stand by” to “charging”, potentially leading to overcharging. Additionally, ChargeX can transition any charging state to an error state, effectively launching a DoS attack on Tesla. If deployed, ChargeX may significantly demolish people’s trust in the EV charging infrastructure.

title: Manufacturers' Vertical Integration Strategies in Chain-to-Chain Competition with Downstream Risks and Upstream Innovation
authors: Haojia He, Xingzheng Ai, Jing Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: This study models two competing supply chains, each consisting of a supplier, a manufacturer, and a retailer, operating under downstream demand uncertainty and upstream innovation. Manufacturers can choose forward integration, backward integration, or non-integration. The results show that intense competition between integrated chains can expand the overall market, but high innovation efficiency may disadvantage a unilateral backward-integrated manufacturer. Three distinct vertical structures emerge in equilibrium. Notably, non-integration arises under low innovation efficiency, intense competition, and stable demand—an outcome that departs from prior research. Extended analyses show that non-integration remains optimal both when suppliers are non-strategic and when bilateral integration willingness is considered. In addition, examining the accuracy of asymmetric signals confirms the robustness of the baseline results. These findings clarify why manufacturers adopt diverse integration strategies and why some firms deliberately remain non-integrated to preserve competitive advantage.

title: SemanticAction: a Semantic-Driven and Behavior-Aware Password Framework for Adaptive VR Authentication under Observation Attacks
authors: Tingjie Wan, Yalin Deng, Chunlin Wang, Jian Pan, Zixuan Guo, Xubo Yang, BoYu Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: As immersive Virtual Reality (VR) applications become increasingly widespread, ensuring secure and usable authentication is critical. Traditional knowledge-based methods (e.g., passwords, PINs) suffer from memorability issues and are highly vulnerable to observation attacks such as Man-in-the-Room (MITR). Meanwhile, biometric and behavioral approaches raise concerns regarding practicality, privacy, and cross-platform deployment. We present SemanticAction, a semantic-driven, behavior-augmented authentication framework that integrates knowledge-based passwords with gesture-based behavioral biometrics. Passwords are encoded as scene anchored directions executed through intuitive hand gestures, enabling semantic meaning to guide user interactions. To counter observation attacks, SemanticAction employs randomized scene prompts and decoy scenes, while a dual-constraint verification mechanism adapts to both cold-start/few-shot conditions. Two user studies provide initial evidence that SemanticAction can mitigate MITR attacks while alleviating memorability challenges, maintaining favorable usability and security performance even with limited behavioral data. This work offers early insights and practical design considerations for behavior-aware authentication in VR.

title: Retraction Notice: A Graphic Model, Simulators and Formal Evaluation of Protocols For Wireless Communication
authors: Anandakumar H., Arulmurugan R.
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: The fundamental purpose of this article is to develop the performance evaluation of wireless network protocols centered on simulation composed of a formal performance evaluation. This model is critically referred to as the evaluation of protocols that define wireless networks, including the evaluation of their capabilities to tolerate the problems that arise because of the uncertainties defining wireless networks. These issues also define the topology of wireless networks concerning the protocols of internal algorithms. Overall purpose simulation applications do not utilize actual world models of wireless network, including the findings of simulation experiments which differ over simulators that have scant connections to fieldwork experiments. Moreover, the proposed model can produce more exhaustive and robust measures of evaluation, for simulation, it is flawed to communicate a network detail that is overgeneralized. In this article, we have analyzed a graphical specification model that provides the evaluation of topology effects in the performance evaluation through visualizing spatial features of wireless network and critical performance measures. Distinct to the graphical visualization application, the recommended model integrates a simulation tool that used a crucial Castalia simulator and probabilistic framework that uses a prism.

title: GVF-MPC Depth Control Framework Motivated by Restoring-Moment Mechanism Analysis for Bionic Robotic Fish
authors: Jiarong Han, Xiangqing Yuan, Bo Yin, Yu Liu, Suli Zou, Zhihong Deng, Zhongjing Ma
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Longitudinal oscillations are commonly observed in depth control of low-speed underwater platforms such as bio-inspired robotic fish. To investigate this issue, this paper conducts a quantitative analysis of the vertical-plane dynamics. It reveals the speed-dependent coupling between the depth and pitch channels as the swimming speed decreases, and clarifies the underdamped mechanism induced by restoring-moment dominance. A critical speed condition for the onset of low-speed oscillations is also derived. Based on the dynamic analysis, a hierarchical GVF–MPC depth control framework is proposed, which structurally decouples geometric guidance from dynamic execution. The guidance layer employs a guiding vector field (GVF) to generate naturally smooth attitude references from geometric information, with reduced sensitivity to model degradation and pitch disturbances. The inner loop applies model predictive control (MPC) to track the guidance commands while explicitly accounting for dynamic constraints, balancing convergence speed and control-input smoothness to improve stability and safety under low-speed conditions. Simulation and experiments are conducted to evaluate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed method. Simulations consider sinusoidal and V-shaped depth path and compare the proposed approach with LOS, PID, and SMC based controllers. Experiments are performed in still water and under simulated Sea-State 1 disturbances to assess steady state accuracy, disturbance recovery, and overall robustness.

title: FPGA IMPLEMENTATION of NLMS and VSLMS ALGORITHMS for UNDERWATER ACOUSTICS APPLICATIONS
authors: Emre Karakaya, Oktay Aytar
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study investigates the Normalized Least Mean Squares (NLMS) and Variable Step-Size Least Mean Squares (VSLMS) algorithms to mitigate noise effects in underwater acoustic applications, with a particular focus on ping-based measurement and detection scenarios. A real-time implementation workflow was developed on a Xilinx XC7A100T FPGA. The algorithm was implemented using a 16-bit fixed-point representation under a semi-parallel hardware architecture. System performance was evaluated using 300 kHz center-frequency ping reflected from a metal surface in a controlled pool environment. Based on a parameter sweep, NLMS improved the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) from 8.85 dB at the input to 29.36 dB, while VSLMS increased it to 28.57 dB. Hardware resource utilization was measured as 2.51% LUT and 4.04% FF for NLMS, and 2.91% LUT and 4.70% FF for VSLMS. The results indicate that NLMS exhibits faster convergence, whereas VSLMS provides competitive performance under high-noise conditions.

title: Federated Digital Twin with Spatio-Temporal Learning and Post-Quantum Security for Robust IIoT Anomaly Detection
authors: Muhammad Usama Tanveer, Syed Rizwan Hassan, Samir Brahim Belhaouari
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: The Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) is increasingly vulnerable to sophisticated cyber-attacks, including zero-day and device-specific threats, which pose severe operational and security risks. We present a Federated Digital Twin for Spatio-Temporal Anomaly Detection approach that is tailored to offer a real-time, scalable and privacy-preserving security for IIoT settings. The framework integrates digital twin modeling to represent the physical network, spatio-temporal deep learning for capturing complex dependencies across devices and time, federated learning to enable collaborative model training without exposing sensitive data, and post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) security to ensure robust protection against emerging quantum-era threats. We evaluate the framework on the RT-IoT2022 dataset, demonstrating an overall detection accuracy of 99.89%, precision of 99.91%, recall of 99.87%, and an AUC of 0.998. The proposed method surpasses contemporary intrusion detection systems, accurately detects zero-day attacks, and reduces the overhead of communication by 65% in comparison with centralized training. Ablation studies demonstrate the importance of multi-view graph learning, cross-graph attention, and federated aggregation for obtaining good performance. Additionally, the framework offers transparent descriptions of the anomalies, so that operators can name and take response action against malicious operations. Its federated architecture ensures sensitive IIoT data remains on local devices, enhancing privacy and compliance with data protection regulations. The integration of PQC mechanisms future-proofs the system against quantum-era attacks, making it resilient to next-generation threats. Overall, the proposed framework offers a holistic, robust, and scalable solution for securing industrial networks and supports deployment across diverse IIoT infrastructures with minimal operational overhead.

title: FUSION Dialogue System for Fuzzy Rule Matching
authors: Naeemeh Adel, Keeley Crockett
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Dialogue systems frequently misinterpret user input containing vague, hedged, or perception-based expressions such as "somewhat warm" or "not bright". Traditional sentence similarity measures rely on crisp semantic categories and struggle with the inherent fuzziness of natural language, leading to incorrect rule matching and dialogue breakdown. This paper presents a study comprising of two question-and-answer dialogue systems that integrate fuzzy semantic similarity measures based on Interval Type-2 fuzzy sets to address this limitation. The first system embeds a fuzzy similarity algorithm into a café customer-feedback scenario and was evaluated with 32 participants generating 288 responses. The second system incorporates algorithmic enhancements including a fuzzy negation operator and a cross-category influence factor, evaluated in a remote working context with 35 participants producing 630 utterances. Comparative evaluation against a WordNet-based baseline demonstrated that the fuzzy approach achieved substantially higher rule-matching accuracy (87.14% versus 69.05%), with particularly strong performance in subjective categories such as Temperature (96.88% versus 68.75%), Worth (90.63% versus 62.50%), and Speed (90.63% versus 62.50%). The study provides a complete implementation methodology including a five-phase linguistic preprocessing pipeline and an empirically validated fuzzy dictionary covering nine human perceptual categories. Results confirm that fuzzy semantic similarity offers a robust foundation for interpreting perception-driven language in dialogue systems.

title: Semantic Image Synthesis via Diffusion Models
authors: Wengang Zhou, Weilun Wang, Jianmin Bao, Dongdong Chen, Dong Chen, Lu Yuan, Houqiang Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models (DDPMs) have achieved remarkable success in various image generation tasks compared with Generative Adversarial Nets (GANs). Recent work on semantic image synthesis mainly follows the de facto GAN-based approaches, which may lead to unsatisfactory quality or diversity of generated images. In this paper, we propose a novel framework based on DDPM for semantic image synthesis. Unlike previous conditional diffusion model directly feeds the semantic layout and noisy image as input to a U-Net structure, which may not fully leverage the information in the input semantic mask, our framework processes semantic layout and noisy image differently. It feeds noisy image to the encoder of the U-Net structure while the semantic layout to the decoder by multi-layer spatially-adaptive normalization operators. To further improve the generation quality and semantic interpretability in semantic image synthesis, we introduce the classifier-free guidance sampling strategy, which acknowledge the scores of an unconditional model for sampling process. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed method, achieving state-of-the-art performance in terms of fidelity (FID) and diversity (LPIPS).

title: A Novel Adaptive TSCH-MAC Protocol for Industrial IoT: Enhancing Efficiency via Topology, Trust, and Data Diversity
authors: Niayesh Gharaei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: In Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) environments, efficient energy usage, data reliability, and system resilience are crucial for sustained performance. This paper introduces a novel adaptive Time-Slotted Channel Hopping (TSCH)-based Medium Access Control (MAC) protocol designed to optimize the active time of industrial node devices based on three key contextual factors: node position, failure history, and data diversity. Unlike traditional TSCH implementations with static scheduling, the proposed protocol dynamically allocates time slots by evaluating the contextual importance of each node. Nodes located in critical positions (such as those near base stations or serving as relay nodes) are prioritized for longer active durations, while nodes with frequent failure histories are assigned shorter time slots to minimize the propagation of unreliable data. Additionally, the protocol promotes nodes that provide unique or diverse data types, enhancing overall system insight while minimizing redundancy. Although TSCH is not natively supported in standard Zigbee implementations, our protocol can be adapted to Zigbee-like networks through integration with IEEE 802.15.4e-compliant stacks, offering a more flexible and intelligent approach to channel access in IIoT applications. While traditional Zigbee networks typically rely on CSMA/CA or static TDMA scheduling, our adaptive TSCH-based approach improves energy efficiency, reduces communication overhead, and ensures that the most valuable and reliable data is transmitted. By intelligently managing time slot allocation, the protocol balances network longevity with effective data prioritization. This approach not only enhances the performance of IEEE 802.15.4-based IIoT networks but also paves the way for smarter, more resilient industrial systems.

title: Autonomous Robotic Liver Ultrasound via Vision-Guided Initialization and Imitation Learning
authors: Ji Ma, Yue Zhao, Zhuang Liu, Yi Shen, Jianxing Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Liver disease is a leading cause of mortality worldwide. Ultrasound (US) examination is a primary modality for monitoring and assessing the risk of liver disease. Robotic ultrasound system (RUSS) has emerged as a promising approach to reduce dependence on sonographers and to improve the standardization and reproducibility of US examination. To advance liver US examination, this study proposes a fully autonomous two-stage methodology for robotic liver US examination, integrating vision-guided initial positioning and imitation learning (IL). In the initial positioning stage, the probe autonomously establishes initial contact with patients using RGB-D images and determines the transition timing to the IL stage based on real-time US images. In the IL stage, scanning skills–represented by robot trajectories in Cartesian space and corresponding US image state trajectories–are learned and reproduced using an enhanced IL method, Adaptive Ensemble Transfer Bayesian Interaction Primitive (AET-BIP). Compared with its original formulation, AET-BIP reduces fluctuations in scanning phase velocity, thereby improving stability. To evaluate repeatability, the robot’s end-of-scan poses were measured under varying initial conditions in the IL stage, yielding position and orientation repeatability errors generally below 2.50 mm and 0.020 rad, respectively. Comprehensive experiments incorporating both stages validate the feasibility and adaptability of the proposed methodology, highlighting its potential for standardized and reliable clinical liver US examination.

title: Energy-Efficient Task Orchestration in the Edge-Cloud Continuum Using Deep Reinforcement and Federated Learning for Sustainable IOT
authors: Achyut Shankar, Shahid Mumtaz, Joel J. P. C. Rodrigues, P. Karthikeyan, Velliangiri Sarveshwaran
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Efficient orchestration in the edge–cloud continuum is essential for reducing energy consumption and meeting latency requirements in large-scale IoT systems. This article presents a hybrid deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and federated learning (FL) framework that dynamically allocates computation across IoT, edge, fog, and cloud layers. The DRL agent learns energy-efficient scheduling strategies through a latency-aware reward design, while FL enables decentralized model training without exposing raw data. Experimental evaluation demonstrates up to 31.6% lower energy consumption and 28.4% latency reduction compared to existing heuristics. Results also show rapid learning convergence within 200 episodes, indicating strong adaptability under changing network and workload conditions. These findings confirm the effectiveness of the proposed framework in improving energy efficiency, latency performance, and scalability for next-generation IoT deployments.

title: Robust Adversarial Patch for Object Detection Using Self-Similarity for Multiscale Attacks
authors: Yang Li, Tingrui Wang, Mingxin Fu, Xin Zhou, Quan Pan, Zhunga Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Research on adversarial examples for deep learning models has gained significant attention, particularly in object detection, where dynamic relative positioning between the object and the detector poses unique challenges. Existing attacks remain limited in handling real-world scenarios, especially regarding distance variations that induce blurring, reducing patch effectiveness. Additionally, as the target carrying the adversarial patch moves closer, incomplete patch capture may further weaken its impact. To address these issues, we propose a systematic approach to designing robust adversarial patches for object detectors. Our method leverages self-similarity to ensure attack effectiveness across scales and incorporates a blur simulation module to enhance resilience against distance-induced distortions. Furthermore, we introduce two key geometric optimization metrics, Local Patch Shift (LPS) and Embedded Boundary Gap (EBG), to analyze the impact of local perturbations on global patch optimization. Experimental results demonstrate that our adversarial patch achieves a high attack success rate on several object detection models in digital environments. In addition, we perform evaluations in simulated and physical environments, validating the robustness of the patch under varying lighting, motion, and detection angles. The findings highlight the effectiveness of our approach in both simulated and real-world settings, providing valuable insights into improving adversarial patch design for practical applications.

title: Enhanced Privacy-Preserving Protocol Based on Novel Triangular Matrix Blinding Technique for Matrix Inversion Computation
authors: Kaidi Xu, Jia Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: As a fundamental operation, Matrix Inversion Computation (MIC) plays an important role in various applications. Nevertheless, it is difficult for resource-constrained devices to independently compute large-scale MIC. A good choice of the user is to leverage the cloud for large-scale MIC computation under the condition of privacy protection. Recently, some privacy-preserving protocols for large-scale MIC have been proposed. In the paper, we show these existing protocols perform well in protecting dense matrices but fail to sufficiently safeguard the privacy of sparse matrices. For the applications based on sparse matrices, these protocols suffer from security vulnerabilities, which may leak privacy information through the zero elements or linear relationships in the blinded matrices. To circumvent such vulnerabilities, we firstly design a novel triangular matrix blinding technique that can well protect the sparse matrices. This blinding technique, however, has an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$O(n^{3})$</tex-math></inline-formula> computational complexity, unable to be applied to secure outsourcing MIC directly. Then, we further construct an optimization algorithm that transforms the matrix multiplications involved in the designed triangular matrix blinding technique into matrix-chain multiplications and additions, thereby reducing the computational complexity from <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$O(n^{3})$</tex-math></inline-formula> to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$O(n^{2})$</tex-math></inline-formula>. Based on above blinding technique and optimization algorithm, we finally propose an enhanced privacy-preserving protocol for large-scale MIC. Theoretical analysis shows that the proposed protocol satisfies privacy, correctness, verifiability, and high-efficiency. Experimental results demonstrate the performance advantage of the enhanced privacy-preserving protocol.

title: FedVSR: Towards Model-Agnostic Federated Learning in Video Super-Resolution

title: Retraction Notice: Hyperspectral Information in Urban Areas Using Big Data and Machine Learning
authors: Vivek C, Gowtham A, Dharaneesh K S, Sajina Therese P
publish: 2023 9th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: With the advancement of remote detecting the utilization of hyper otherworldly pictures is turning out to be increasingly inescapable. The precise characterization of ground highlights through hyper ghostly pictures is a significant exploration content and has drawn in broad consideration. Numerous strategies have accomplished great order and bring about the arrangement of hyper spectral pictures. The electromagnetic range of an observational scene can be covered by two or three hundred absurd collections produced by hyper spectral information in differentiating a single acquiring. The generated 3D hyper spectral data square contains a substantial quantity of geographical and urban information. The speed and volume of the hyper-spectral photo collection create a data age issue, especially for distant recognition applications. The project is organized to first provide an in-depth analysis of agent studies to provide details into fundamental urban research attempts putting an emphasis on frameworks or plans, information management, and considerable learning using big data, AI, and assessment using hyper spectral and multispectral data.

title: Retraction Notice: A Review on Power Generation Using Vertical Windmill
authors: Suhan. M, Chitharthan Shanmugam, Vimal. A, Vijay Ram. G, Janani. B, Amarnath. A. S
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The realm of windmill technology encompasses a rich tapestry of research, innovation, and interdisciplinary explorations. This review compiles insights from 28 journals, classified into diverse categories, to offer a comprehensive overview of the current state of windmill-related research. From the development of Levitating Friction-less Vertical Windmills that optimize performance in low-wind regions to the resurrection of Persian windmills through 3D modeling, these journals traverse the boundaries of engineering, heritage science, and material science. Notably, the study on Acetylation Modification of Windmill Palm Fiber underscores the versatility of natural materials in composite reinforcement. Similarly, windmill-shaped nanostructures illuminate the potential for optical applications at the nanoscale. The investigation into various windmill brake state models reveals essential insights for wind turbine simulations, enhancing their precision and reliability. Photonic crystals with windmill-like defects showcase novel phenomena at the intersection of photonics and windmill architecture. For practical applications, a new and reusable foundation solution introduces sustainable alternatives for windmill installations, while a low-cost tilting blade vertical axis windmill offers hope for rural irrigation. As we delve into history, windmill architecture's historical evolution highlights its integral role in our journey toward sustainable energy solutions. A portable windmill and seafloor geomorphology research add a contemporary and geological dimension to windmill studies. Finally, the analytical approach to Windmill Chiral Cell Structures and the integration of solar and wind power generation systems represents cutting-edge solutions and novel perspectives in wind energy. This review underscores the dynamic an

title: A Low-Profile Dual-Polarized Dual-Band Antenna With High Isolation For Drone Application
authors: Md Shakiul Jafor, Md Nur Alam, Elias A. Alwan
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a dual-polarized, dual-band aperture-in-aperture (AIA) antenna for small unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) that combines GNSS/GPS L1-band (viz. 1.568–1.576 GHz) and Ka-band drone-to-satellite links (viz. 29.3–29.70 GHz) in a single, ultra-low-profile package. The radiator fits in a 119.12 mm × 119.12 mm footprint with an overall height of 0.51 mm corresponding to 0.625 λ0 × 0.439 λ0 ×0.0026 λ0 at the lowest frequency band (L1). The design employs nested apertures, allowing the Ka-band radiator to occupy the center of the L1-band window without blocking it. Concurrent operation is achieved using orthogonal feeds and a via fence-based passive decoupling structure. Additionally, a novel feed via section is introduced at each port of an L-band antenna to enhance intra-band isolation between the Horizontal-polarization (H-pol) and Vertical-polarization (V-pol). Full-wave simulations show S11 < −10 dB across 1.568–1.576 GHz (GNSS L1) and 29.3–29.70 GHz (center frequency at 29.5 GHz). Inter-band isolation exceeds 40 dB, while intra-band port isolation is around 20 dB. The L1 radiator exhibits stable broadside patterns with cross-polarization below −20 dB and a realized gain of around 4.9 dBi. The Ka-band aperture yields a realized gain of 12.4 dBi with dual linear (H/V) polarization for link diversity, where co- and cross-polarization discrimination is better than 20 dB near boresight. The measured results are in close agreement with the simulation results. The AIA approach reduces volume, weight, and cabling compared to separate antennas and simplifies integration on compact airframes.

title: Lightweight Asymmetric Multimodal Fusion for Cross-Subject Emotion Recognition
authors: Lin Zhou, Zaijun Wang, Haixin Li, Sizhuang Lei, Xiaojun Qin
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Addressing the dual challenges of resource constraints and modality-specific noise in wearable affective computing, existing symmetric fusion paradigms often overlook reliability disparities across modalities, which can induce multimodal negative transfer. To address this issue, we propose a Physiologically Anchored Multimodal Framework (PAMF), a lightweight asymmetric architecture for robust cross-subject emotion recognition at the edge. Departing from reliability-neutral interaction, PAMF establishes a directional dependency topology in which high-confidence electroencephalography (EEG) representations act as semantic anchors to selectively retrieve useful evidence from the visual branch, thereby suppressing low-quality visual cues during fusion. Furthermore, we introduce an instance-adaptive Gaussian temporal bias to model the asynchronous latency of neuro-muscular responses and employ an orthogonal subspace projection strategy to decouple Valence and Arousal representations within a shared compact backbone, thereby alleviating gradient conflicts in multi-task learning. Rigorous Leave-One-Subject-Out validation on the MAHNOB-HCI and DEAP benchmarks demonstrates that the model achieves a Valence Accuracy of 73.9% and an Arousal Accuracy of 66.8% with only 1.5M parameters and <50 MFLOPs per inference. As further illustrated by the UAR–efficiency analysis, PAMF offers a favorable trade-off between robustness, compactness, and deployment efficiency for next-generation mobile affective brain-computer interfaces.

title: Masked Generative Models for Real-Time Network Traffic Forecasting
authors: Lei Deng, Xiao-Yang Liu, Wenhan Xu, Jingwei Li, Danny H. K. Tsang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Network traffic forecasting is crucial for dynamic resource allocation and network management. However, real-time network traffic forecasting in real-world scenarios is challenging due to the limitation of incomplete data. In this paper, we propose a masked generative model (MGM) for real-time network traffic forecasting from incomplete data. Firstly, we formulate the forecasting task as a low-tubal-rank tensor completion problem and verify that generative models can produce low-tubal-rank tensors through low-dimensional latent variables. Secondly, we propose MGM, which adapts masked autoencoders to robustly learn latent variables from incomplete traffic data. The variables are then mapped to complete low-tubal-rank traffic tensors through pretrained generative models for real-time forecasting. We also establish a performance guarantee that quantifies the error bound of the proposed approach. Finally, experiments on real-world datasets demonstrate that our approach achieves accurate network traffic forecasting within 100 ms, with a normalized root mean squared error (NRMSE) below 0.1.

title: Examining Nostalgia Marketing Strategy in Maximising Brand Values - A Case in Vietnam
authors: Trang Trieu Hong Phuc, Nguyen Vu Thanh Ngoc, Le Nguyen Hong Anh, Huynh Anh Gia Bao, Phan Nguyen Khanh Ngoc, Le Thi My Danh, Nguyen Khanh Thu, Trong-Hop Do
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Knorr, Kido and Phe La illustrate how Vietnamese brands have revitalised shared cultural memories through nostalgic imagery to enhance consumer trust and Brand Value perceptions. Building on this context, the present study examines how Nostalgia Marketing transforms emotional memories into measurable Brand Value, specifically Brand Awareness, Brand Reputation, and Brand Loyalty. Using a quantitative approach, data were collected from 267 consumers via an online survey and analysed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM). The findings indicate that atmosphere nostalgia and family nostalgia exert significant and broad effects across all Brand Value dimensions, while interpersonal nostalgia selectively enhances Brand Reputation. These results suggest that nostalgic marketing does not operate as a uniform emotional appeal but functions through differentiated perceptual and relational pathways. The study contributes to Nostalgia Marketing literature by conceptualising nostalgia as a multidimensional construct that strengthens consumer-brand connections in digital contexts. Practically, the findings offer insights for marketers seeking to design effective nostalgia-based strategies in emerging markets.

title: Robust Autonomous Driving via Vulnerability-Aware Co-Optimization Framework Against Safety-Critical Scenarios
authors: Guangyao Bai, Jie Li, Yucheng Shi, Lei Shi, Yufei Gao, Shuqin Zhang, Wei Wang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: As autonomous driving technology matures, evaluating and improving the robustness of autonomous vehicles is vital. While realistic and challenging long-tail traffic scenarios are crucial for training, their inherent rarity leaves even well-trained systems vulnerable. Although large-scale simulated scenario generation has been explored, it often suffers from a simulation-to-reality distribution gap, and existing methods lack a unified strategy to leverage real-world data to enhance robustness and safety. Complementary to prior work handling safety-critical scenarios at the policy level, we introduce a novel vulnerability-aware co-optimization framework designed to improve autonomous systems in the presence of collision-prone driving scenarios. Our framework establishes a closed-loop process between scenario generation and policy optimization, alternately exposing robustness vulnerabilities and refining policies over time. Specifically, we decompose real-world scenario generation into three tractable sub-tasks: pairwise collision checking, autonomous vehicle on-policy rollout estimation, and surrounding vehicle motion prior sampling. Simultaneously, we iteratively optimize driving policies within these scenarios while enforcing kinematic feasibility and cost constraints. Extensive experiments demonstrate the feasibility and generalizability of our proposed framework in exploring robustness vulnerabilities of autonomous driving systems.

title: Chaos Suppression Based on Fractional-Order Model in a Doubly-Fed Induction Generator
authors: Yuchen Zhang, Yanling Lyu, Zhen Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Chaotic oscillations in doubly-fed induction generator (DFIG) systems, caused by strong nonlinear coupling and parameter uncertainties, pose significant risks to power system stability. Although increasing evidence indicates that electromechanical systems exhibit fractional-order characteristics, most existing chaos suppression methods are developed within an integer-order framework, leading to inherent modeling mismatch and limited decoupling accuracy in fractional-order systems. Based on Caputo’s fractional-order calculus, fractional-order operators are incorporated into the traditional interactor algorithm, thereby enabling the development of a fractional-order interactor. This framework enables the construction of a fractional-order pseudolinear model and a corresponding fractional-order internal model, thereby facilitating effective system decoupling. On this basis, a fractional-order internal model control strategy is developed for chaos suppression. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated through numerical simulations and hardware-in-the-loop experiments on a fractional-order DFIG system under different fractional orders. The proposed control strategy can be readily extended to the decoupling and stabilization of other fractional-order nonlinear systems.

title: Anatomy-Guided Self-Supervised Distillation Learning for Medical Image Analysis
authors: Huihui Yu, Qun Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: 3D medical imaging modalities, including CT and MRI, provide high-resolution views essential for precision medicine. However, the increasing volume and complexity of 3D medical images challenge manual analysis, particularly in classification and segmentation tasks. Although deep learning has shown considerable promise, it struggles to characterize small-scale, low-contrast anatomical structures, generalize across imaging domains, and mitigate annotation scarcity. Self-supervised learning (SSL) has emerged as an effective and annotation-efficient solution, yet existing methods, largely adapted from natural images, often fail to capture the anatomical heterogeneity and complex semantic dependencies inherent in 3D medical data. To address these limitations, we propose AG-SSD (Anatomy-Guided Self-Supervised Distillation), a framework that explicitly incorporates anatomical priors into SSL. AG-SSD comprises three complementary modules: (i) Cross-View Anatomical Consistency (CVAC), which generates multi-scale, anatomically consistent positive pairs via overlap-aware cropping; (ii) Edge-Aware Adaptive Masking (EAAM), which prioritizes anatomy-sensitive, high-edge regions to enhance local feature learning and robust global representation; and (iii) Cross-View Attention Alignment (CVAA), which leverages attention-based fusion to achieve semantic compensation and alignment across views, mitigating semantic drift to stabilize distillation. These modules are optimized using a unified objective that combines intraview patch distillation, inter-view [CLS] token distillation, and masked patch reconstruction. Extensive experiments on CT and MRI datasets demonstrate that AG-SSD consistently outperforms state-of-the-art SSL methods in both classification and segmentation under annotation-scarce scenarios, highlighting its potential as a scalable, label-efficient paradigm for 3D medical image analysis and clinical applications.

title: How Harassment Shapes Self-Perception and Well-Being in Social VR: Evidence from a Controlled Lab Study
authors: Jonathan Tschanter, Christian Merz, Carolin Wienrich, Marc Erich Latoschik
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Social Virtual Reality (SVR) allows users to meet and build relationships through embodied avatars and real-time interaction in virtual spaces. While embodiment can strengthen social connections and presence, it can also intensify negative encounters, making SVR particularly vulnerable to harassment. Despite frequent reports of verbal, visual, and “physical” violations in SVR, little is known about how harassment reshapes users' self-perception, including their sense of embodiment, self-identification, closeness, and avatar customization preferences. We conducted a controlled experiment with 52 participants who experienced either a neutral or a harassment condition in a scenario modeled after real SVR incidents. Participants perceived the harassing peer as significantly more negative, annoying, and disturbing than the neutral peer. Contrary to prior reports, harassment did not significantly affect well-being measures, including emotional state, self-esteem, and physiological arousal, within this controlled scenario. However, participants reported stronger bodily change, attributed more of their own attitudes and emotions to their avatars, and increased interpersonal distance when personal space was invaded. Self-reported coping strategies included ignoring, stepping back, using humor, and retaliating. Notably, avatar customization preferences shifted across conditions. Participants in the neutral condition favored personalized avatars, whereas those in the harassment condition more frequently preferred anonymity in public spaces. Together, these findings demonstrate that harassment in SVR not only exploits embodiment but also reshapes self-perception. We further contribute methodological insights into how harassment can be ethically and reproducibly studied in controlled SVR–like experiments.

title: KArAt-KidneyNet: Proactive Threat Hunting and Anomaly Detection Framework for Consumer Wearable Healthcare Electronics in Kidney Disease Diagnosis
authors: Jing Yang, Hongjin Wang, Yupeng Chen, Theyazn H. H. Aldhyani, Xu Yan, Lip Yee Por, Jiaxing Lin, Jiawen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Consumer wearable healthcare electronics, including kidney-monitoring wearables and point-of-care diagnostic devices, generate continuous multimodal physiological and imaging data. Their increasing integration within connected health systems also expands the attack surface, creating vulnerabilities that may affect diagnostic integrity, model reliability, and patient safety. This paper presents KArAt-KidneyNet, a unified framework for kidney disease diagnosis and system-state monitoring that combines Kolmogorov–Arnold Transformer (KArAt) attention, Soft Actor–Critic (SAC)–based adaptive defense, and privacy-preserving federated learning. The proposed KArAt attention module replaces softmax attention with a set of learnable basis functions inspired by the Kolmogorov–Arnold representation concept, enabling structured feature decomposition and interpretable attention maps for ultrasound imaging. The SAC component provides adaptive parameter adjustment for anomaly-aware defensive actions based on system state indicators derived from detection modules. Federated learning with differential privacy is used to train the diagnostic model over decentralized healthcare datasets without sharing raw patient data. Additional protective layers include post-quantum cryp-tographic signatures resistant to emerging quantum computing threats and blockchain-based logging to ensure authenticity and traceability of diagnostic outputs. Evaluation across multi-center kidney ultrasound datasets and simulated adversarial/anomalous operating conditions shows that the framework achieves 96.8% diagnostic accuracy, 94.0% robustness under attack, and 94.2% anomaly detection rate, with inference latency of 412.3 ms on edge-class hardware (Jetson Xavier), meeting real-time clinical screening requirements. These results indicate that KArAt-KidneyNet can support secure and interpretable kidney disease classification in distributed consumer healthcare environments.

title: Retraction Notice: A Novel Approach for Disease Identification in Grape Leaf Using Machine Learning Algorithm
authors: S Rangeetha, B Sruthi, R Suvedha, U Suwetha
publish: 2022 Sixth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Plant diseases cause substantial crop damage and economic losses. Early detection of plant diseases increases the yield and standard of the crop significantly. Misdiagnosis of the disease and its effects leads incorrect usage of insecticides. In the proposed work, various diseases affecting grape leaves are detected using K-Means Clustering algorithm, GLCM and MultiSVM algorithms. The affected area is also calculated so that preventive measures can be taken. Once, the disease has been identified, the motor connected to the pesticide is automatically turned on.

title: From Visual Similarity to Physical Interpretability: Multi-Task Learning for Material Understanding in Immersive Environments
authors: Omar Ghozlani, Nicole Christoff, Krasimir Tonchev, Nikolay Neshov, Agata Manolova
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: In this study, contrast training and synthetic rendering are used to estimate material properties from visual data. We extend the architecture of the MatSim framework for single-shot material recognition by adding a two-head design that predicts metallicity, a physically meaningful property, and jointly learns discriminative embeddings. Baseline metallicity values calculated from material mixtures were added to the dataset to allow for observation of a regression head in addition to the descriptor head. Our experiments show that the embedding space is reshaped in interpretable ways by the additional regression objective. The model maintains between-class separability and within-class compactness with moderate weight loss <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\lambda=0.1)$</tex>. With stronger weighting (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda=0.5$</tex>), metallic and non-metallic categories become more distinguishable, although some overlap persists between subclasses. These results demonstrate that metallicity observation improves physical interpretability while preserving competitive recognition performance.

title: Retraction Notice: Design of Rlc Based Switching Noise Reduction Model for Low Noise Amplifier
authors: S Suvitha, J.M. Mathana
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The RLC (Resistor, Inductor and Capacitor) based switching noise reduction model with Low Noise Amplifier (LNA) has been proposed for reducing the noise of analog RF ICs. The Scrounging Impedance (R, L & C) centered noise diminishing circuit utilized in the mixed signal circuit. The recommended design of the LNA circuit is centered on the mixed similar-gate and shunt response topological aspect of wireless communication application. The duty flight calculation alongside the Particle swarm optimization (PSO) calculation is applied to develop starting answers for recreated strengthening (SA) conspire and these underlying arrangements are utilized to break down the whole pursuit space into various tempering districts. Further, each toughening district is abused to look for an ideal arrangement utilizing a reenacted strengthening plan while keeping up a high level of decent variety. In this work, the active decoupling inductor and capacitor model is incorporated into the noise amplifier for the efficient noise and power analysis. The main objective of this work is to reduce the noise and power of the RF LNA. The proposed optimization of RF LNA is simulated by using Agilent ADS tool and the result ensures the increased performance in comparison with existing work.

title: Retraction Notice: An Improved Early Detection Method of Autism Spectrum Anarchy using Euclidean Method
authors: V. Deepak, S. John Justin Thangaraj, M. Rajesh Khanna
publish: 2020 Fourth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Autistic Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is one of the mental disorders that affect the acquisition of linguistic, communication, cognitive, and social skills and abilities. Regardless of being determined to have ASD, a few people display extraordinary educational, non-scholastic, and aesthetic capacities, in such cases representing a moving assignment for researchers to give answers. In the most recent couple of years, ASD has been examined by social and computational insight researchers using trend-s etting innovations, for example, AI to improve analytic planning, exactness, and quality. AI procedures, for example, uphold vector machines, choice trees, strategic relapses, and others, these types of strategies have been applied to various and particular datasets identified with a chemical imbalance to develop prescient models. These models guarantee to improve the capacity of clinicians to give strong determinations and visualizations of ASD. Nonetheless, examines concerning the utilization of AI in ASD determination and treatment experiences theoretical, execution, and information issues, for example, how symptomatic codes are utilized, the sort of highlight choice utilized, the assessment estimates picked, and class irregular characteristics in information among others. A more genuine case in ongoing examinations is their advancement of another strategy for ASD analyses dependent on AI. This undertaking of our own fundamentally examinations these ongoing analytical investigations on chemical imbalance, articulating the issues in these examinations as well as suggesting ways forward that improve AI use in ASD concerning conceptualization, execution, and information. Future investigations concerning AI in mental imbalance research are incredibly profited by such a proposition.

title: Retraction Notice: Mammographic Cancer Identification By Using DCNN (Deep Convolutional Neural Network)- A review
authors: Timmana Hari Krishna, C. Rajabhushnam
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: It is recently observed that, most of the women are suffered from the bosom cancer disease all over the world. bosom cancer has become one of the major health challenges. Also it is an extremely difficult and time consuming task for the pathologists to analyse the complicated structure of bosom cancer tumor. During diagnosis, the examination of histopathological images plays an important role. For the feature extraction of these histopathological images, the conventional feature removal method extracts only small level features. Therefore it is necessary to design a proper feature extraction method to extract important features from these histopathological pictures. To extract the high level features automatically from an image, deep learning methods are used. Therefore, in this dissertation work, we used a DCNN (Deep Convolutional Neural Network) to extract the different features of histopathological images. In the first step, we have developed two architectures namely Inception-V3 and Inception-ResNet V2. These two architectures are designed to solve the multi-class and binary issues of classification of histopathological images of bosom cancer. The imbalance problem of histopathological images is present in a different subclasses, where it can be removed by rotating the image in clockwise - anticlockwise, up - down, left - right and in different degrees and for this purpose Ductal Carcinoma is considered as a baseline. The experimental results of this dissertation work shows that the categorization of histopathological pictures of bosom cancer are more superior to the existing methods. However, the Inception-ResNet V2 network executes unverified examination of the histopathological pictures for bosom cancer correctly, and it also correctly extracts the different features from the images. These extracted features ar

title: Conversion of Tobacco Leaves into Hydrochar Using Induction Heating
authors: Wittaya Chunet, Alisa Thammarit, Samroeng Santalunai, Jariya Pakprom, Chanchai Thongsopa, Nuchanart Santalunai, Samran Santalunai
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study presents the design and evaluation of a hydrothermal carbonization (HTC) system using induction heating for converting tobacco leaves into hydrochar. Nine coil configurations were simulated, and it was found that a 15×10 mm coil, combined with an appropriate coil-reactor wall gap, provided the optimum balance between electromagnetic field intensity and spatial uniformity, thereby promoting efficient eddy current heating at the vessel wall. Experiments conducted at an electrical power of 5 kW with residence times of 120 and 180 min yielded temperature and pressure profiles that increased steadily before stabilizing at approximately 140°C and 4.1 bar and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$153{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex> and 6.2 bar, respectively. The resulting products exhibited a darker color (indicating a higher degree of carbonization), while the pelletized hydrochar showed increased hardness and brittleness. At the same time, hydrophobicity and shape stability were markedly improved in the 180 min condition. Based on these results, it can be concluded that the induction heating system can control the severity of the HTC process in a uniform and tunable manner, producing hydrochar with quality and physical properties suitable for subsequent downstream applications.

title: Retraction Notice: Fraud Detection in Credit Card Transactions Using SVM and Random Forest Algorithms
authors: S K Saddam Hussain, E Sai Charan Reddy, K Gangadhar Akshay, T Akanksha
publish: 2021 Fifth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: This project's primary objective is to detect credit card fraud in the real world. Recent growth has resulted in a significant increase in the number of credit card transactions. The objective is to obtain goods from an account without paying for them or using unapproved funds. It is critical for all banks that issue credit cards to reduce the cost of implementing an effective fraud detection system. One of the most difficult challenges is that neither the card nor the cardholder is needed to complete the transaction during a credit card transaction. Thus, the seller cannot verify whether the customer who is making an acquisition is an authentic cardholder or not. The accuracy of detecting fraud is improvised with this system proposed using random forest algorithm, decision tree, and support vector machine algorithms. A random forest algorithm is a classification process for observing the data set and optimizing the accuracy of the resultant data. The techniques' performance is judged based on precision, sensitivity, & accuracy. Some of the data provided are processed to identify fraud detection and provide visualization for the graphic model.

title: Retraction Notice: A Sensor-Based Data Analytics for Patient Monitoring in Connected Healthcare Applications
authors: S Shanthi, S Mithun, P Kavi Prakash, K Maharajan, C Nandha Kishore
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Nowadays, Remote sensor networks have been broadly utilized in medical services applications, like emergency clinic and home patient checking. Compared to wired organizations, Remote clinical sensor networks are more vulnerable to spying, modification, manipulation, and playback attacks. To get remote clinical sensor organizations, a lot of work has been done. The current methods can protect patient information during transmission, but they can't preclude the director of the patient data set from uncovering sensitive patient information during an attack. In this research, we offer a practical method for preventing internal attacks by storing patient data on multiple information servers. The main goal of this work is to properly disseminate patient information across several information servers while using the Paillier and elgamal cryptosystems to conduct measurement investigations on the data without jeopardizing the patients' safety. Remote clinical sensor networks definitely lead to an improved knowledge of the nature of care without affecting patients' comfort. However, there are numerous potential security risks to the patient's sensitive physiological information transferred over public networks and stored in back-end frameworks. Snooping creates a security risk to the privacy of patient information.

title: A Model-Based Design Approach for CFD Hardware Acceleration: Comparative Analysis of FPGA, CPU, and GPU Performance in Channel Flow
authors: Emine Elif Yigit, Ramazan Yeniceri, Ibrahim Ozkol
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This paper presents a model-based FPGA (a type of reconfigurable digital integrated circuit) accelerator for well-known classical channel flow problem CFD simulation and compares its performance with CPU and GPU implementations. A 100-core pipelined solver for Couette–Poiseuille flow is developed using a Model-Based Design approach in MATLAB/Simulink environment, where the high-level algorithm is developed and automatically translated into HDL code (the source code of the accelerator) via MATLAB HDL Coder. The design achieves a 6.34× speed-up over the GPU on a 350 × 350 grid with 177,824 iterations. The model-based workflow significantly reduces development time by enabling high-level algorithm design, automatic HDL generation, and early verification. Operating at 106 MHz, the FPGA solution offers high data processing capacity and low power consumption, making it an ideal choice for real-time, onboard CFD applications in energy-constrained aerospace systems. Experimental results demonstrate higher performance per watt and consistent timing compared to CPU and GPU alternatives. The results highlight the scalability and versatility of model-based FPGA acceleration for CFD and pave the way for future applications involving unstructured grids and more complex flow scenarios.

title: Hierarchical Model Predictive Control for Multi-Time-Scale Energy Management in V2B-Integrated Smart Buildings
authors: Mohamed S. Taha, Yasser A.-R. I. Mohamed
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Today’s power grid is no longer a one-way system in which electricity flows from utilities to users. Instead, it is becoming an interactive network in which buildings, renewable generation, batteries, and electric vehicles (EVs) all play active roles. In particular, electric vehicles with bidirectional Vehicle-to- Building (V2B) capability introduce new flexibility but also make energy management much more complex, thereby limiting the effectiveness of traditional control strategies. To address this, this paper proposes a hierarchical multi-time-scale Model Predictive Control (MPC) framework that separates long-term scheduling decisions from short-term operational control. This structure allows the controller to plan economically over a longer horizon while still responding quickly to short-term disturbances, improving computational efficiency without reducing reliability. The proposed method uses predictive models of EV fleet availability and state-of-charge and accounts for battery degradation costs, enabling the optimization of building thermal dynamics, on-site generation, and EV storage. Simulation and validation studies on a realistic commercial building model, including seasonal, Monte Carlo, and adversarial forecast experiments, show that the proposed approach remains competitive with the full single-layer MPC benchmark in operating cost while preserving EV feasibility, improves comfort relative to the rule-based baseline, and maintains robust performance under forecast uncertainty, albeit with weaker peak-import reduction than the full sing-lelayer benchmark under the submitted 24-hour/4-hour configuration. These results suggest that V2B can be practically integrated into building energy management systems and that hierarchical MPC provides an effective, scalable, and robust way to operate future smart buildings within increasingly flexible power grids.

title: A Fault Diagnosis Method for Wind Turbine Gearboxes Considering Wind Energy Characteristics
authors: Liu Yang, Yang Xiaohui, Cheng Xian, Qiao Haiqiang, Ge Guowei
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The vibration signals of wind turbine gearboxes are characterized by non-stationarity, weak periodicity, and frequent sudden mutations, posing significant challenges to fault diagnosis. To improve diagnostic accuracy, this paper proposes a fault diagnosis method for wind turbine gearboxes based on the Snake Bird Optimization Algorithm (SBOA)-optimized Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and an improved Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (BiGRU). First, SBOA is employed to adaptively optimize the number of modes and the penalty factor in VMD, enhancing the effectiveness of signal decomposition. Second, an improved BiGRU model is constructed, featuring a three-channel BiGRU architecture, along with the introduction of periodic skip connections, a temporal attention mechanism, and a time-decay loss function to strengthen the extraction and fusion of temporal features. Finally, a Softmax classifier is used to achieve precise identification of fault types. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an average accuracy of 99.40% in diagnosing five types of faults, significantly outperforming comparative methods. Ablation studies further validate the effectiveness of each improved module. The proposed method provides reliable technical support for the intelligent operation and maintenance of wind turbine gearboxes.

title: MIGHTY: Hermite Spline-based Efficient Trajectory Planning
authors: Kota Kondo, Yuwei Wu, Vijay Kumar, Jonathan P. How
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Hard-constraint trajectory planners often rely on commercial solvers and demand substantial computational resources. Existing soft-constraint methods achieve faster computation, but either (1) decouple spatial and temporal optimization or (2) restrict the search space. To overcome these limitations, we introduce MIGHTY, a Hermite spline-based planner that performs spatiotemporal optimization while fully leveraging the continuous search space of a spline. In simulation, MIGHTY achieves a 9.3% reduction in computation time and a 13.1% reduction in travel time over state-of-the-art baselines, with a 100% success rate. In hardware, MIGHTY completes multiple high-speed flights up to 6.7 m/s in a cluttered static environment and long-duration flights with dynamically added obstacles.

title: SHORT COURSES
