title: SCOTT-Gait: A Spiking Cross-attention Temporal Triple-loss Network for Occlusion-compensated Gait Recognition_supp1-3680495.pdf
authors: Chip Hong Chang
publish: N/A
abstract: Occlusions significantly degrade gait recognition performance, particularly when only anonymized representations like binary silhouettes are provided instead of original videos due to privacy concerns. Existing methods either rely on computationally expensive silhouette reconstruction or extract occlusion-invariant features, which often underperform under severe occlusions. To address these limitations, we propose SCOTT-Gait – a Spiking Neural Network (SNN) that integrates spatial and temporal occlusion compensation mechanisms directly into the recognition model for accurate gait recognition under occlusions. Spatial compensation is achieved through a deformable spiking-based convolution, which infers missing structural information by modeling the co-movement between visible and occluded regions within a binary silhouette image. These adaptively sampled, geometrically correlated samples are processed by the SNN backbone augmented with a Temporal Cross-Attention (TCA) module, which is designed to address temporal compensation by enhancing the network’s ability to aggregate temporal features. A Cycle-Consistency Triplet Loss (CCTL) is introduced to further align features across the walking period to ensure that similar poses are grouped closer together in the feature space, even if they are from different points of the walking cycle. Extensive experiments on both synthetic and real-world occluded gait datasets demonstrate that our method maintains competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art CNN-based approaches, while remaining computationally efficient - a remarkable milestone as the first vision-based SNN gait recognition with occlusion. In particular, it has the most robust performance when trained on naturally, mildly occluded samples and evaluated on severely occluded synthetic samples.

title: A Wind-Field Adaptive UAV Channel Model Considering Random Body Flipping and Position Drifting Effects
authors: Chaoyu Zhao, Yu Liu, Lingqiang Kong, Lingzhong Han, Ji Bian, Jie Huang
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are susceptible to random body flipping (roll, pitch, yaw) and position drift induced by wind and atmospheric turbulence—especially in harsh environments. Accurate UAV channel modeling faces challenges arising from capturing the realistic impact of such environmental disturbances on UAV orientation dynamics. This paper proposes a novel three-dimensional (3D) cluster-based channel model for sixth-generation (6 G) UAV communication, explicitly incorporating the impact of wind-induced UAV body random flipping and drifting. The model integrates a stochastic wind speed model with the UAV dynamic model and control model, enabling accurate simulation of UAV attitude angle variations and position drift. Based on the proposed model, typical channel characteristics are derived and analyzed, and the model's accuracy is verified using measurement data. Results demonstrate that UAV flipping and drifting decrease the temporal correlations of the channel, with higher wind speeds leading to more significant correlation degradation.

title: A Reliable Resource Scheduling Method With Knowledge Transfer for Edge-Cloud Collaboration-Enabled Industrial Internet of Things
authors: Wei Zhang, Jianchang Liu, Honghai Wang, Yuanchao Liu, Shubin Tan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: With the rapid development of industrial Internet of Things (IIoT), the edge-cloud collaboration architecture combining the powerful computing ability of cloud computing with the low latency of edge computing plays an increasingly important role in providing the computing resources and reducing the latency for IIoT. However, under this architecture, existing methods in scheduling the resources for IIoT often focus on latency and energy consumption but ignore some other important factors especially the reliable factor, thereby making it difficult for them to adapt to real-world IIoT scenarios. To this end, we propose a reliable resource scheduling method with knowledge transfer for edge-cloud collaboration-enabled IIoT. Specifically, we first model the resource scheduling as a many-objective optimization problem, considering these optimized objectives: latency, energy consumption, load balance, resource utilization, and trust measure between tasks and servers. Then, we develop a knowledge transfer accelerated clustering evolutionary algorithm (KTCEA) for many-objective optimization to solve the model, where the knowledge transfer aims at accelerating the evolution and the clustering makes the population converge from various directions. Under the collaboration of knowledge transfer and clustering, KTCEA can utilize the small population size to effectively search the objective space, and thus have the high real-time performance. Extensive experiment results on a benchmark test suite and the constructed model demonstrate that KTCEA is highly competitive compared with some advanced methods and our method can efficiently achieve the resource scheduling for edge-cloud collaboration-enabled IIoT, respectively.

title: FMSA: Few-shot Multimodal Sentiment Analysis for Social Media via Integrated Prompt Learning and Vision-Language Models
authors: Xianxun Zhu, Heyang Feng, Erik Cambria, Xiaohan Yu, Jose Santamaria, Xuhui Fan, Rui Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Multimodal sentiment analysis (MSA) on social media is increasingly critical for understanding complex emotional expressions, yet it faces significant challenges in data-scarce environments where annotated multimodal content is limited. Here, we present FMSA, a novel framework that integrates prompt-based learning with advanced vision-language models to enable robust few-shot MSA. Our approach leverages an instruction-aware Query Transformer (Q-Former) to dynamically extract and align visual features with task-specific textual prompts, enhancing cross-modal fusion. We introduce a distributed consistency sampling strategy to construct representative few-shot datasets, ensuring statistical diversity under constrained conditions. Evaluated across six benchmark social media datasets-including MVSA-S, MVSA-M, and Twitter-Depression-FMSA outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving an accuracy of 63.47% and an F1 score of 57.34% on MVSA-S with full data, and 61.25% accuracy with 56.06% F1 in few-shot settings using just 1% of the data. By fine-tuning lightweight components while preserving pretrained model robustness, FMSA mitigates overfitting and delivers generalizable performance. We also release a curated few-shot dataset as a community resource. This framework advances MSA by offering an efficient, scalable solution for interpreting multimodal emotions in low-resource scenarios.

title: Ordinal-Based Speech Emotion Recognition Leveraging Consecutive Trends: A Curriculum-Driven Preference Learning Approach
authors: Abinay Reddy Naini, Carlos Busso
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: In the domain of human-computer interaction, accurately ranking speech samples by their expressive content is crucial for enhancing emotional understanding and response mechanisms. Traditional methods, relying on absolute attribute scores annotated by multiple raters, face challenges in label reliability for training preference learning frameworks. This paper introduces a novel preference learning approach that leverages the inherent trends in raters' annotations of consecutive samples, circumventing the inconsistencies associated with absolute scoring. By focusing on annotation trends within evaluation sessions, our method significantly reduces the ambiguity in preference labels, providing a more robust foundation for preference learning. Our approach uses curriculum learning (CL) to further refine this approach. Common methods to create relative labels from absolute scores require setting fixed margins to establish preferences. We can avoid fixed thresholds by using CL, where strict margins are used early in training and progressively relaxed. CL's structured progression inherently aligns with the sequential nature of our preference learning model, enhancing the model's ability to discern subtle emotional cues within speech samples. Comparative analysis across diverse corpora, including the MSP-Podcast, IEMOCAP, and BIIC-Podcast corpora, reveals that this combination of trend-based preference learning and curriculum learning not only yields superior performance within individual datasets but also demonstrates significant cross-corpus generalizability. This cross-corpus efficacy underscores the robustness and adaptability of our methodology, marking a substantial advancement in the field of emotion recognition and human-computer interaction.

title: Authorship, Authenticity, and Academic Integrity in the Age of Generative AI: Evidence from Essay Corpora
authors: Saber Alavi, Mohammad Pakbaz
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study empirically investigates distinctions between AI-generated and human-authored essays in an EFL context, focusing on authorship, authenticity, and academic integrity. A balanced corpus of 300 essays (150 human, 150 AI) was compiled and analyzed using computational stylometry and readability measures. Results show that AI-generated texts consistently exhibit higher lexical sophistication and syntactic complexity but with lower variance across features, while human essays show greater variability reflecting individual differences. A multivariate statistical model (Discriminant Function Analysis) trained on a 70% hold-out subset of the data achieved 92.2% accuracy in classifying authorship on a held-out test set, with readability and syntactic features as the strongest discriminators. In contrast, teacher evaluations reached only 65% accuracy, revealing biases: polished AI essays were often misidentified as human, while weaker human essays were mistaken for AI. A complementary student survey linked human authorship to cognitive effort and revision strategies. Findings highlight a duality: AI excels in producing formally competent texts, but human writing retains “cognitive imprints” of individual voice and effort. The study concludes that safeguarding academic integrity requires shifting from purely output-based detection toward pedagogical and policy frameworks that prioritize writing processes.

title: Semantic Compression for 3D Content: A Unified Conceptual Framework and Survey of Advanced Solutions
authors: Ivaylo Bozhilov
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The increasing use of 3D content in applications such as virtual reality, gaming, and telepresence highlights the need for compact representations that are interpretable and accessible for user interaction. This paper presents a unified approach to representing 3D scenes and avatars through intermediary descriptions that capture both large-scale contextual information and fine-grained object- or avatar-level details, supporting efficient storage, transmission, and manipulation. Building on this representation framework, a range of existing techniques are analyzed and compared in terms of their interpretability, efficiency, and compactness. Particular emphasis is placed on representations that align with human understanding and enable direct interpretation and modification of 3D content without full reconstruction, a role most effectively fulfilled by text-based intermediary formats. The analysis identifies promising directions for future research and emphasizes the need for robust evaluation metrics.

title: Retraction Notice: Next Generation Wireless Communication Challenges and Issues
authors: Anandakumar H., Arulmurugan R.
publish: 2019 Third International conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: The scaled commercial positioning of the 5<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> generation wireless network has forced organizations and researchers to consider launching the upcoming generation of mobile network systems. In that regard, this paper proposes to critically evaluate the 6<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> generation wireless communication, potential problems and novel technologies in the future. The article begins with the evaluation of the future of the 6<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> generation and its vision with respect to the preferences of users. To fulfill the research objective, it is vital to analyze the technical necessities and the key challenges that ensures that the 6G mission is achieved, which involves the ultimate throughput, connectivity, self-aggregating network fabrics, new theories, non-technical issues and greater energy effectiveness. The evaluation of the future mobile communication involves novel technologies such as new spectrum, visible light network and terahertz wireless network. Additional technologies featured include the sparse theories, new channeled code, extensive antenna, flexible spectrum utility and the artificial intelligence-based wireless networking tactile. Through the exploration of the 6<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> generation vision, the challenges and requirements, it is possible to outline the general model of the 6G, which possibly provides a guide to enhance research on the 6<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">th</sup> generation network.

title: Enhancing Chinese Vocabulary Acquisition Through Digital Game-Based Learning for Primary Students
authors: Junli Chen, Kannika Daungcharone, Krittawaya Thongkoo
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study investigates the application of digital educational games in Chinese vocabulary learning among Grade 6 students at Anuban Chiangmai School. Traditional Chinese language teaching often relies on teacher-centered instruction and rote memorization, which can lead to insufficient motivation, limited memory retention, and difficulty applying vocabulary in real-world contexts. With the development of information technology, game-based learning has been shown to enhance learners' interest and achievement. This research selects three platforms: Easy Chinese, Wordwall, and Kahoot!. A quasi-experimental design has been adopted, with approximately 60 students divided into an experimental group and a control group. The experimental group integrated digital games into classroom activities, while the control group followed traditional instruction. Research instruments include pre-test and post-test assessments, as well as a motivation questionnaire, which focuses on students' learning motivation and vocabulary acquisition. After conducting the study, the results revealed that students in the experimental group who learned through digital educational games showed higher vocabulary achievement and stronger motivation than those in the control group. The integration of Easy Chinese, Wordwall, and Kahoot! effectively improved students' vocabulary retention and their ability to apply words in context. Furthermore, game-based learning encouraged greater participation, enjoyment, and self-efficacy, creating a more engaging learning environment for Chinese vocabulary acquisition.

title: Retraction Notice: Improving Design Quality of Software Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: C. Lakshmi Prabha, N. Shivakumar
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Design patterns commonly pre-owned in Object-Software oriented Development. It provides Information helping to overcome recurrent design challenges (problems). Recognition of patterns can be described as identification and representation of information based on knowledge obtained from statistical data derived from patterns. Detection of design patterns provides the advantages of an extensive investigation of object-oriented software applications. When using various pattern detection techniques depending on static research issues to identify patterns it detects similar pattern structures. For this, we seek to identify patterns in design of Software by using classification-based methods and information metrics. The JHotDraw, QuickUML, and Junit of object-oriented software are used to detect software problems in the patterns of designing a Software. The datasets are manual creations which are designed utilizing software criteria for classifying learners. It is carried out in two steps, namely the development of a statistics-oriented dataset and the identification of trends in software trends in software design. Tests are carried out by Usage of 3 open source computer programs to test the process, then the tests are examined.

title: Retraction Notice: Dynamic Brain Segmentation and Intelligent Deep Neural Networks
authors: Sonali Vyas, Karthikeyan Kaliyaperumal, Bhopendra Singh, Ghassan K. Ali, R. Aarthi, Ahmed Gawish
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: In recent development research, brain tumour examination is generally essential for diagnosing brain disorders. To distinguish the cancer cell, different strategies are utilized. The proposed novel strategy called a Deep Learning Convolution Neural Systems (DCNN) calculation is done that’s utilized to discover the participation work of dental. The metric is defined for assurance of shape by utilizing properties, which is irregular in the brain. By and large, the exactness of 96% is obtained utilizing the proposed DCNN strategy. The proposed division strategy’s affectability, specificity, and accuracy values are found to be 96.7%, 95.6% and 98.4% separately. The closeness of the proposed strategy relative to Genuine Positive values at the ROC bend demonstrates the execution as higher. Exploratory comes about demonstrate proposed framework viability compared with other location strategies.

title: Towards Safe and Robust Autonomous Vehicle Platooning: A Self-Organizing Cooperative Decision-making Framework
authors: Chengkai Xu, Zihao Deng, Jiaqi Liu, Aijing Kong, Yu Tang, Chao Huang, Peng Hang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In hybrid traffic environments where human-driven vehicles (HDVs) and autonomous vehicles (AVs) coexist, achieving safe and robust decision-making for AV platooning remains a complex challenge. Existing platooning systems often struggle with dynamic formation management and adaptability, especially under complex and dynamic mixed-traffic conditions. To enhance autonomous vehicle platooning within these hybrid environments, this paper presents TriCoD, a twin-world safety-enhanced Data-Model-Knowledge Triple-Driven Cooperative Decision-making Framework. This framework integrates deep reinforcement learning (DRL) with model-driven approaches, enabling dynamic formation dissolution and reconfiguration through a safety-prioritized twin-world deduction mechanism. The DRL component augments traditional model-driven methods, enhancing both safety and operational efficiency, especially under emergency conditions. Additionally, an adaptive switching mechanism allows the system to seamlessly switch between data-driven and model-driven strategies based on real-time traffic demands, thus optimizing decision-making ability and adaptability. Simulation experiments and hardware-in-the-loop tests demonstrate that the proposed framework significantly improves safety, robustness, and flexibility.

title: DynaPURLS: Dynamic Refinement of Part-Aware Representations for Skeleton-Based Zero-Shot Action Recognition
authors: Jingmin Zhu, Anqi Zhu, James Bailey, Jun Liu, Hossein Rahmani, Mohammed Bennamoun, Farid Boussaid, Qiuhong Ke
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: Zero-shot skeleton-based action recognition (ZS-SAR) is fundamentally constrained by prevailing approaches that rely on aligning skeleton features with static, class-level semantics. This coarse-grained alignment fails to bridge the domain shift between seen, unseen classes, thereby impeding the effective transfer of fine-grained visual knowledge. To address these limitations, we introduce DynaPURLS, a unified framework that establishes robust, multi-scale visual-semantic correspondences, dynamically refines them at inference time to enhance generalization. Our framework leverages a large language model to generate hierarchical textual descriptions that encompass both global movements, local body-part dynamics. Concurrently, an adaptive partitioning module produces fine-grained visual representations by semantically grouping skeleton joints. To fortify this fine-grained alignment against the train-test domain shift, DynaPURLS incorporates a dynamic refinement module. During inference, this module adapts textual features to the incoming visual stream via a lightweight learnable projection. This refinement process is stabilized by a confidence-aware, class-balanced memory bank, which mitigates error propagation from noisy pseudo-labels. Extensive experiments on three large-scale benchmark datasets, including NTU RGB+D 60/120, PKU-MMD, demonstrate that DynaPURLS significantly outperforms prior art, setting new state-of-the-art records. The source code is made publicly available at https://github.com/Alchemist0754/DynaPURLS

title: Joint Design of Transmit Beamforming and Receive Filters for MIMO ISAC Systems Under Low-Altitude Dynamic Scenarios
authors: Zhiwei Wang, Jingjing Wang, Guangyu Wu, Pengfei Ren, Ziyang Wang, Junhui Qian
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: This paper investigates the joint transmit beamforming and receive filters design for multiple-input multiple-output integrated sensing and communication (MIMO ISAC) systems serving multiple users and low-altitude dynamic targets. We aim to minimize multi-user interference subject to total and per-user power budgets, sensing signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio (SINR) thresholds, and two-dimensional Cramér–Rao lower bound (CRLB) constraints for target estimation. To tackle the highly nonconvex CRLB constraints, we reformulate them into tractable semidefinite programming (SDP) forms. An alternating optimization framework is then proposed, in which receive filters are updated via closed-form minimum variance distortionless response (MVDR) solutions, while transmit beamforming is optimized using an alternating direction penalty method (ADPM). Theoretical analysis establishes the computational complexity and guarantees convergence to a stationary solution. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm outperforms the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM) benchmark, achieving faster convergence and lower objective values.

title: Evolutionary Zero-Shot Proxy in Various Evaluation Scenarios: A Symbolic Learning Perspective_supp1-3680503.pdf
authors: Lianbo Ma
publish: N/A
abstract: Neural architecture search (NAS) faces a significant challenge due to the high computational cost of architecture evaluation. Zero-shot (ZS) proxies have been proposed as an efficient alternative to reduce evaluation overhead; however, their limited rank correlation often leads to sub-optimal architecture selection. To alleviate this issue, recent studies attempt to combine multiple ZS proxies, yet most existing approaches rely on heuristic aggregation guided by domain knowledge and remain restricted to a single evaluation scenario, which limits both effectiveness and interpretability. In this paper, we propose a novel perspective that treats ZS proxies as architectural features and formulates their combination as an evolutionary symbolic learning problem. Specifically, we leverage a genetic programming based symbolic learning framework to automatically evolve interpretable proxy combinations, enabling flexible exploration of symbolic structures without manual design. The evolved symbolic models capture complex and non-linear relationships between ZS proxies and architectural performance while maintaining strong interpretability. Furthermore, we systematically model three evaluation scenarios, including pointwise, pairwise, and listwise settings, and design scenario-specific fitness functions to guide evolutionary search toward different ranking objectives. Extensive experiments across 19 tasks using 13 representative ZS proxies are conducted to evaluate the proposed framework. The results demonstrate that the evolved symbolic models consistently achieve improved rank correlation and provide valuable insights into proxy interaction patterns under different evaluation scenarios, highlighting the effectiveness and generalizability of the proposed approach for NAS.

title: IEEE Circuits and Systems Society Information
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology

title: A 0.95-ppm/°C Voltage Reference with Second-Order Core-Injection Sub-Ranging Compensation
authors: Wenzhao Lv, Pengxu Ren, Dan Ye, Chao Jiang, Zhuojun Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This article presents a second-order core-injection sub-ranging voltage reference with an ultra-low temperature coefficient (TC) over a wide temperature range for Internet-of-Things (IoT) applications. Based on a shunt-PTAT second-order temperature-compensated voltage reference, a resistor-ratio based core-injection sub-ranging is applied in the voltage reference for lower TC. The proposed voltage reference was designed and fabricated in a standard 65-nm CMOS technology. The proposed second-order core-injection sub-ranging voltage reference achieves an average TC of 0.95 ppm/°C (σ = 0.16 ppm/°C) after trimming from −60 °C to 85 °C, and the worst-case TC is 1.17 ppm/°C. It consumes 29 μA at 27 °C and occupies 0.09 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Projection-Constrained Adaptive Neural Ordinary Differential Equation Based Robust Control of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors
authors: Fobao Zhou, Zhenxiao Yin, Yang Shen, Xueyan Wang, Song Zhou, Boyang Xing, Hang Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: Conventional methods for permanent magnet synchronous motors (PMSMs) often exhibit degraded tracking performance due to their limited capability in handling complex nonlinearities and parameter mismatches. To this end, a projection-constrained adaptive neural ordinary differential equation robust (ANODR) control strategy is proposed to mitigate the adverse effects of unknown external disturbances and model uncertainties. First, a Neural ODE network with hidden state projection constraints is developed to estimate and compensate for composite disturbances induced by model uncertainty and unknown external factors. On this basis, weight update laws for this network are designed by synthesizing Lyapunov stability theory and gradient descent, ensuring both stability and rapid convergence. Additionally, a simplified backstepping method is devised to provide a recursive stability foundation for the speed loop. Furthermore, the bounded stability of the closed-loop PMSM system is strictly proven via the Lyapunov stability method. Finally, experimental results validate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed control strategy. This work provides a promising insight for reliable and real-time neural network deployment in motor control systems.

title: A Sensor-Optimized Data Glove for Taxonomy-Level Human Grasp Recognition
authors: Xin Li, Shengsheng Du, Rong Jiang, Hongrui Sang, Zhipeng Wang, Yanmin Zhou, Bin He
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Embodied AI has advanced rapidly, yet the sim-to-real gap still limits the real-world transfer of skills learned in simulation, especially for fine-grained grasping. Human hand dexterity provides valuable priors for manipulation learning, but capturing and understanding detailed hand-object interactions remain challenging. Data gloves offer a promising solution; however, most prior work focuses on non-grasp gestures or object recognition, and few studies address fine-grained grasp types. We develop GloG, a custom data glove system that captures and recognizes grasp gestures. In the offline stage, we introduce a taxonomy-guided sensor layout strategy that places thin-film pressure arrays and stretch sensors on critical hand regions to maximize discriminability while reducing redundancy. Using this glove, we compile a comprehensive dataset covering 33 typical grasp gestures following the Feix taxonomy. In the online stage, we propose a topology-adaptive graph transformer for static grasp recognition by explicitly modeling inter-sensor coordination on the optimized sensor graph. Experiments show that the system can distinguish multiple grasps per object and generalize across the object shape and size variations. GloG provides a scalable interface for collecting fine-grained human grasp demonstrations and recognition labels, which may support future robotic manipulation learning.

title: Joint ABR and Routing Optimization for 360° Video in SAGIN: Resolving the FoV Prediction-Buffer Dilemma
authors: Zitong Li, Changqiao Xu, Chuxing Fang, Mu Wang, Shujie Yang, Yuang Cai, Lujie Zhong, Gabriel-Miro Muntean
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: 360° video streaming in Space-Air-Ground Integrated Networks (SAGIN) faces a critical dilemma in the context of SAGIN's heterogeneous network topology and its dynamic propagation delays: the trade-off between field of view (FoV) prediction accuracy and buffer stability. Traditional adaptive bitrate (ABR) and routing strategies do not address this trade-off due to their reliance on stable latency assumptions and lack of coordination. Unlike them, we regulate streaming from the perspective of FoV prediction-to-playback (FPTP) delay, which inherently links routing-induced propagation latency with bitrate-driven buffer dynamics. This motivates the proposal of a novel dual-channel temporal-spatial integrated proximal policy optimization (DTS-PPO) which combines a spatial channel that adapts routing to rapid topology and link variations, and a temporal channel that regulates bitrate in response to evolving buffer and prediction trends. DTS-PPO extracts spatio-temporal state features via lightweight neural encoders, fuses them for global representation, and trains a joint routing–ABR policy with proximal policy optimization. This integration enables coordinated decisions that stabilize the FPTP delay under dynamic conditions. Extensive evaluations with real-world FoV traces demonstrate that DTS-PPO improves QoE by 21.3%–34.8% in diverse scenarios, significantly outperforming state-of-the-art ABR and routing baselines.

title: Society Officers & Administrative Committee
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Antennas and Propagation Magazine

title: Biased Federated Learning under Wireless Heterogeneity
authors: Muhammad Faraz Ul Abrar, Nicolò Michelusi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Wireless Communications
abstract: Federated learning (FL) has emerged as a promising framework for distributed learning, enabling collaborative model training without sharing private data. Existing wireless FL works primarily adopt two communication strategies: (1) over-the-air (OTA) computation, which exploits wireless signal superposition for simultaneous gradient aggregation, and (2) digital communication, which allocates orthogonal resources for gradient uploads. Prior work on OTA and digital FL either enforces zero bias (explicitly or via assumed homogeneous path loss) or permits uncontrolled bias, yielding high-variance updates under heterogeneous channels and creating a performance bottleneck due to devices with poor channel conditions. We propose wireless FL updates that admit a structured, time-invariant model bias to achieve low-variance gradient aggregation, and analyze their convergence in a unified framework, in both strongly convex and non-convex settings. The resulting bounds reveal a biasvariance trade-off governed by the design parameters. To optimize this trade-off, we pose a non-convex joint design problem and develop a successive convex approximation framework to tune the parameters. Extensive experiments across heterogeneous wireless settings, covering both strongly convex and non-convex image classification tasks, compare the proposed OTA and digital designs against state-of-the-art baselines. The results demonstrate that optimizing the bias–variance trade-off through a structured bias yields faster FL convergence and improved generalization over existing schemes.

title: MT29 Conference Organization
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity

title: Physicians Scheduling for Online Healthcare Service: Models, Exact and Heuristic Solution Approaches
authors: Haozhou Ma, Ran Liu, Yu Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The rapid development of online healthcare has posed new challenges for hospitals in allocating physician resources across integrated online and offline service systems. This paper investigates the physician scheduling problem of an online healthcare service system while considering offline services. We model the system as a nonstationary processor-sharing queue using continuous-time Markov chains and the uniformization method. Based on this framework, we derive closed-form analytical expressions to compute patient sojourn times, queue lengths, and physician overtime. Then, we formulate the physician scheduling problem as a non-linear model and further linearize this model into a mixed-integer programming (MIP) model. Based on the linearization technique, the problem can be exactly solved by the MIP solver, such as Gurobi. To cope with real-life large-scale problem data, we also propose a two-phase modeling method and a variable neighborhood search (VNS) algorithm. Using empirical data from a partner hospital, we evaluate the accuracy of our modeling and the performance of our solution methods. Numerical experiments demonstrate that our approaches generate high-quality physician schedules that outperform existing practices currently adopted by the hospital. In the non-epidemic scenario, our approach reduces the average patient sojourn time by 12.7% compared with the hospital’s current practice. In the epidemic scenario, our approach reduces the average patient sojourn time by 14.7%.

title: Steady-state optimal filtering for linear and nonlinear systems
authors: A. Astolfi, D. Bhattacharjee, G. Manca, M. Sassano
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: The steady-state optimal filtering problem for linear systems is revisited with the objective of establishing further insights on the structure of the underlying solution. It is shown that, in addition to the invariance property of a suitably defined hyperplane, the optimal filter is related to a triangularizing change of coordinates for certain Hamiltonian dynamics associated to the filtering problem. The implication of the above observation is twofold. First, the novel interpretation admits a conceptually straightforward counterpart in the nonlinear setting in terms of invariant distributions. The latter then permit the design of local steady-state optimal filters for nonlinear systems that only rely upon the solutions of linear partial differential equations, the solution of which is independent from the specific time history of the measured output. The conditions may be further leveraged to determine a polynomial algebraic equation, which characterizes the solution of the filtering problem and which is expressed in the entries of the optimal filter gain alone, hence circumventing the need for solving the underlying Riccati equation.

title: Retraction Notice: Price Prediction of Pre-Owned Automobiles Using Machine Learning-A Comprehensive Survey
authors: Saradha S, K. Vineeth, N. Sreesharan, B. Vigneshwaran, B. Saravanan
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Car sales are at an all-time high. Due of cost, developing nations embrace the practice of leasing new vehicles rather than buying them outright. Which causes the surge in secondhand cars market is raising beyond the expectations. Looking at the demand for goods, automobile vendors may implement this circumstance by proposing exorbitant rates. It is thus necessary for researchers to create a model that is capable of predicting a vehicle's price according to an examination of its highlights and consideration of the retail costs of rival automobiles. In this study, we employ the supervised learning technique Random Forest to forecast used automobile prices. There are various attributes in a car which has different data at different occasions, so only after thoroughly exploring all those data, the model was chosen. To train a Random Forest on the data, 500 Decision Trees were utilized. The model can accurately anticipate the price of cars by using the most linked features.

title: Digital Transformation of Multimedia Education Through Artificial Intelligence and Motion Capture Technologies
authors: Georgi Georgiev, Georgi Hristov, Plamen Zahariev, Diyana Kinaneva
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper examines the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and motion capture (MoCap) technologies into video production education within the “Multimedia” module, which is part of the “Information Technology” class at the high school level of education in Bulgaria. It highlights how traditional production methods are often too complex for students, while AI-powered tools simplify workflows, enhance creativity, and improve accessibility. The study outlines how AI supports all stages of the production process-pre-production, production, post-production, and distribution-while MoCap enables interactive animation creation. The paper also addresses ethical and legal concerns, emphasizing the need for responsible and balanced use of emerging technologies in education. The report concludes with an analysis of students' opinions on their preferences regarding video production education - traditional versus AI approach, and the corresponding conclusions are drawn.

title: Cover Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)

title: Dual-Band CP Metasurface-Array Antenna with Sequential-Phase Feed Network for C-Band Operation
authors: Khwanlada Kakhong, Kittisak Phaebua, Chuwong Phongcharoenpanich, Nathapat Supreeyatitikul
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper presents a four-cluster dual-band circularly polarized (CP) metasurface (MTS)-array antenna integrated with a sequential-phase feed (SPF) network for Cband applications. The antenna is implemented on dual-layer FR-4 substrates, incorporating both big- and small-sized MTSarray elements per cluster, with a tilted square-slot ground plane and microstrip-line feed. The SPF network excites each cluster with equal amplitude and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 0}^{\boldsymbol{\circ}}$</tex> phase shifts, significantly enhancing return loss bandwidth (RLBW), axial ratio bandwidth, and realized gain. The simulated results demonstrate RLBW of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.08-4.51 \text{GHz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.94-8 \text{GHz}$</tex>, and ARBWs of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.08-4.66 \text{GHz}$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5.27-6.42 \text{GHz}$</tex> for the lower and higher operating bands, respectively. The corresponding highest RHCP gains are 6.15 dBic at 3.8 GHz and 4 dBic at 6 GHz. The proposed antenna design achieves bi-directional radiation patterns and shows substantial performance improvements over conventional dual-band CP MTS-array antennas.

title: Integrating Machine Learning into Data-Driven Marketing: A Case Study of Beverage Brand Consumer Segmentation
authors: Phoo Kay Khaing, Thanisorn Kaewlarksee, Pruek Choeiban, Nilubon Kurubanjerdjit, Soontarin Nupap
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: In highly competitive beverage markets, accurately identifying target consumer segments is essential for effective and personalised marketing strategies. This study proposes a data-driven marketing framework that integrates machine learning techniques to analyse and predict customer attraction toward a health-oriented beverage brand (Brand X) in Chiang Rai, Thailand. Survey data capturing demographic information, marketing factors, and consumer personality traits were collected and preprocessed, resulting in a dataset of 219 records after class balancing using the Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE). Five supervised classification algorithms–Random Forest, Gradient Boosted Trees, k-Nearest Neighbours, Deep Learning, and Logistic Regression–were evaluated using accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score metrics. Experimental results demonstrate that the Random Forest model achieved the best overall performance, with an accuracy of 84.52% and an F1-score of 83.9%, indicating strong predictive capability and robustness in handling imbalanced marketing data. Correlation analysis further revealed that factors such as ethical perception, economic value, packaging appeal, and product quality play a significant role in influencing consumer attraction. The findings confirm the effectiveness of machine learning in uncovering actionable consumer insights and support the deployment of targeted, data-driven marketing strategies. This study highlights the practical value of integrating consumer psychology, branding attributes, and machine learning analytics to enhance competitive positioning in crowded beverage markets.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Model Implementation for Context-Aware Robotic Task Execution
authors: Teeratorn Kanjanarajit, Thanapoom Somsak, Wachirawut Suttitanon, Parn Chimsakul, Narong Aphiratsakun Assumption
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: With the expanded use of robot technology in everyday lives, there is a need for a convenient and flexible method to control robots. With this work, we seek to implement a system that uses AI to interpret natural language to issue commands to our mobile robot based on given context and environment. In this work, we have implemented a system using AI to interpret natural language to issue commands to our mobile robot while reflecting the accuracy and practicality issues.

title: Application of the Three-Capacitance Model for Computer Simulation of Partial Discharge Signals under Ripple Voltage Waveforms
authors: Yevhenii Trotsenko, Julia Peretyatko, Olexandr Protsenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper investigates the applicability of the three-capacitance (ABC) model for simulating partial discharge (PD) behavior under pulsating DC or ripple voltage conditions. PDs are localized breakdowns in solid insulation that can lead to deterioration and system failure. While extensively studied under alternating current (AC), PD behavior under non-sinusoidal waveforms, especially pulsating DC, remains less understood. Many real-world high-voltage systems, such as high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission and DC-DC converters, operate under such conditions. This study evaluates the ABC model, originally developed for AC, with modifications to simulate PD under ripple voltage. Ripple-induced PD exhibits time-resolved patterns, typically initiating on the rising slope and extinguishing on the falling slope of the waveform. An extended model incorporating leakage resistances and diodes is implemented in Micro-Cap simulation software. Results show the model effectively captures PD initiation and extinction phases, supporting its relevance for insulation assessment. These findings provide a foundation for further research into PD modeling under diverse voltage waveforms, contributing to improved high-voltage system design.

title: Vivaldi Antennas with Varied Fractal Scaling
authors: Tetiana Bugrova, Volodymyr Onufrienko, Liudmyla Logachova, Mykhailo Chornoborodov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper examines the use of differintegral calculus in analyzing fractal Vivaldi antennas with variable scaling. The mathematical model of the artificial fractal antennas incorporates the electrodynamic similarity principle and accounts for the geometric characteristics of a homogenized medium. A sample calculation is presented for a specific Vivaldi antenna configuration, with emphasis on a model based on the condensation of fractal media. The results demonstrate that fractal boundary configurations strongly influence both the radiation field and the resulting radiation pattern, underscoring the importance of boundary conditions in millimeter-wave antenna design.

title: Effects of Deficit Irrigation Strategies on Energy Consumption and Bacopa Monnieri Yield in Micro-Irrigation Systems
authors: Nattapon Wichan, Nattapon Kabkam, Sutap Boonmabamrung, Kanyaphorn Chaiwong, Ninlawan Chaitanoo, Surachai Imtub
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study aimed to analyze the effects of deficit irrigation strategies on energy efficiency and the yield of Bacopa monnieri cultivated under a drip irrigation system at the Pharma-Agroforestry District, Rajamangala University of Technology Lanna Nan. Five irrigation regimes were evaluated over a total cultivation period of 137 days: 0.5 L per plant per day, 1.0 L per plant per day, 1.0 L per plant every other day, 1.5 L per plant every other day, and 1.5 L per plant every three days. The study assessed plant growth performance, yield, energy efficiency, Energy Productivity Index (EPI), Specific Energy Consumption (SEC), and energy costs. The results indicated that appropriate selection of irrigation regimes can significantly improve production efficiency while reducing energy consumption and costs in Bacopa monnieri cultivation. The treatment of 1.5 L per plant every other day produced the highest yield (6.89 kg fresh weight and 1.0 kg dry weight), with statistical significance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.05)$</tex>. In terms of energy performance, the 0.5 L per plant per day and 1.5 L per plant every other day regimes exhibited the highest energy efficiency, with EPI values of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 7 2}-\mathbf{1. 7 3} \mathbf{~ k g} / \mathbf{k W h}$</tex> and the lowest SEC of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 5 8} \mathbf{k W h} / \mathbf{k g}$</tex>, resulting in the lowest energy costs of 3.06-3.07 THB/kg (fresh weight) and 22.66-22.69 THB/kg (dry weight).

title: Distributed Online Optimization for Energy Management in Microgrid With Battery Storage Under Time-Varying Communication Networks
authors: Wei Chen, Zidong Wang, Jimmy Chih-Hsien Peng, Ziyao Wang, Guo-Ping Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: This article focuses on the energy management problem of microgrids with battery energy storage systems. The primary objective of this work is to develop a distributed algorithm over time-varying networks to real-time regulate the power output of dispatchable generators and energy storage devices, thereby achieving a balance between supply and demand. To accomplish this, the offline time-coupled optimization problem is initially relaxed to a time-average form and subsequently decoupled into a time-independent problem through the use of the Lyapunov optimization technique. An analytical framework is provided to demonstrate that the battery energy constraints can be satisfied by appropriately selecting parameters. Subsequently, a consensus-based distributed algorithm is formulated over time-varying communication topologies, with its linear convergence proven using the small-gain theorem. Finally, case studies are presented to validate the theoretical results.

title: Leader-Following Consensus of Multi-Agent Systems With Energy-Dependent Packet Dropouts
authors: Zhipei Hu, Xueyan Zhao, Daniel W. C. Ho, Bo Shen, Feiqi Deng, Baishu Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automatic Control
abstract: In this article, the leader-following consensus problem of randomly sampled multi-agent systems (MASs) subject to energy-dependent packet dropouts is investigated. Leveraging a hold-input strategy, we first establish a unified discrete-time framework that simultaneously accounts for the impacts of noisy sampling intervals and energy-dependent packet dropouts. Under the centralized energy allocation scheme, we provide a method to calculate the probability of successful sampled-data transmission for all agents' sensors and characterize the statistical property of the energy-dependent consecutive packet dropout process. To facilitate subsequent stability analysis and controller synthesis, we further decouple the mathematical expectation of a stochastic nonlinear coupling term and derive its asymptotic limit. Building upon these theoretical results, we design a stabilizing controller to ensure leader-following consensus in MASs. Finally, a vehicle platoon case study is conducted to validate the effectiveness of the proposed consensus algorithm.

title: TianheWare: Degree-aware Sampling for Large-scale Graph Learning
authors: Xinbiao Gan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: The scalability of graph neural networks (GNNs) is critically dependent on the efficiency of their sampling and feature aggregation steps, which are often bottlenecked by memory access patterns in large-scale graphs. While sampling algorithms like GraphSAGE reduce computational costs, they rely on underlying sparse storage formats such as the Compressed Sparse Row (CSR) format, treat all non-zero vertices equally, and fail to exploit the skewed degree distribution inherent to real-world graphs. To address these challenges, we introduce TianheWare, a degree-aware sparse storage format specifically engineered to optimize sampling during large-scale graph learning. TianheWare groups the numerous low-degree vertices found in real-world graphs, storing only a starting index for each group to minimize the memory footprint. This design not only reduces memory consumption but also, crucially, enables highly efficient batched memory access during the neighbor sampling phase in frameworks like GraphSAGE. A data-driven threshold automatically computed from the graph's degree distribution, adapts this compression to any graph structure. Integrated as a plugin into GraphSAGE, TianheWare demonstrates its impact on the end-to-end learning pipeline by accelerating the underlying sampling operations, achieving up to 3.37× speedup in sampling throughput and over 85% memory reduction compared to stateof- the-art methods, while maintaining full sampling fidelity and model accuracy. Our extensive evaluation, including deployment on a production-scale supercomputer, where it exceeded the topranked Graph500 benchmark performance, confirms that TianheWare serves as a foundational optimization, enabling faster, more scalable graph learning without compromising results.

title: Deep Learning and Recurrent Neural Networks for Intelligent Bandwidth Modeling and Management in Wireless Networks
authors: Sotir Sotirov, Vladimir Poulkov, Agata Manolova
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The exponential growth in wireless network traffic, driven by the proliferation of Internet of Things (IoT) devices, video streaming, and mobile applications, demands advanced solutions for efficient bandwidth allocation. Artificial neural networks (ANNs) emerge as a promising technology to address these challenges by enabling predictive and optimized resource management in real time. This paper investigates the potential of ANNs for bandwidth modeling, emphasizing their ability to forecast traffic patterns, dynamically adapt to network demands, and deliver scalable, secure, and efficient performance in heterogeneous wireless environments. The proposed methodology integrates ANNs with network learning techniques to enhance the responsiveness and adaptability of wireless systems. Unlike traditional static or reactive bandwidth management approaches, ANN-based models leverage historical and real-time data to predict future bandwidth requirements with high accuracy. Utilizing advanced architectures such as Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks the system captures the temporal characteristics of network traffic effectively. A key innovation is the application of federated learning, which enables decentralized training of ANNs using local edge device data while preserving user privacy. This reduces reliance on centralized computing, minimizes latency, and supports real-time applications in 5 G and beyond. MATLAB simulations validate the effectiveness of the proposed system, demonstrating superior performance in high-density urban environments and large-scale IoT networks. The findings underline the scalability, adaptability, and practicality of ANN-driven solutions for next-generation wireless networks, paving the way for intelligent, efficient, and resilient connectivity in an increasingly interconnected world.

title: Retraction Notice: Crime Deterrent with the Framework of Nasal Entity
authors: A. Helen, N. Komal Kumar, M. Jijendira Prasath, B. Avinash, A. K. Agin

title: Retraction Notice: An Exploration of Robotic Process Automation in all Spans of Corporate Considerations
authors: S. Balakrishnan, M.Syed Shahul Hameed, K Venkatesan, G Aswin
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: In advance, the change of digital transformation persistently operates the function of Robotic Process Automation (RPA) and draws out all forms of corporate considerations. The RPA raises a certain kind of business measure mechanization innovation that assists thought the software programming framework which enhances the system robots or man-made artificial intelligence (AI). While RPA is a famous subject in the corporate world and this holds scholarly exploration on research work which does not have a hypothetical and theoretical investigation over RPA. title: Dispersion Characteristics and THz Vortex-Like Modes of the InSb Photonic Crystal
authors: Yevhen Demydenko, Vladyslav Novytskyi, Eugene Odarenko, Yuriy Averkov, Liubov Ivzhenko, Vyacheslav Maslov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: We consider dispersion characteristics and field distributions of TE modes in the two-dimensional photonic crystal based on the square lattice of InSb rods. Finite element calculations are used for theoretical investigation of eigen modes of the semiconductor photonic crystal. Effect of the rod’s radius and external magnetic field on the photonic crystal band diagram has been analyzed. The study of spatial field distributions in the periodic structure made it possible to detect modes with amplitude and phase characteristics inherent to optical vortices.

title: The Effect of a Robotics and IoT-Integrated Constructionist Workshop on Junior High School Students' STEM Learning Achievement
authors: Parichat Cummon, Watchara Thitayanuwat, Chinapat Mongkholsiriwattana, Puthyrom Tep
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Education in the fields of Science, Mathematics, Engineering, and Technology (STEM) is becoming increasingly seen as a significant contributor to sustainable development. However, the integration of robotics, IoT, and Constructionism into STEM learning for junior high school students has not yet been widely explored. This study aims to investigate the effectiveness of integrating robotics and IoT into STEM education through a Constructionist Workshop (CW) for junior high school students. This study adopts a quasi-experimental design with a one-group pretest-posttest format. A total of 48 junior high school students participated in the workshop, comprised 32 male students (66.67 %) and 16 female students <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(33.33 \%)$</tex>, with an average age of 13.64 years. The workshop took place spanning four days and follows three main stages: Inspiring and Engagement, Making and Collaboration, and Competition. The results of the paired-sample t-test revealed a statistically significant difference, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{t}(47)=7.91, \mathrm{p}<.001$</tex>, with a large effect size (Cohen's <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{d}=1.14)$</tex>. The study found that incorporating robotics and IoT technologies into a CW improved the STEM learning outcomes of junior high school students.

title: More on the Size Effects on Photoemission in Plasmonic Solar Cells
authors: Serhii Shylo, Oleksandr Kapliienko, Andrii Korotun
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The genetic relation between such optical parameters of the metallic nanoparticles as the field enhancement factor, the dynamic depolarization factor, the radiation loss factor and the cross-section of electron photoemission from them has been investigated in this work. This allows us to evaluate the possibility of improving the physical and technical characteristics of the solar cells when they are modified with the metallic nanoparticles. The expediency of introducing gold nanoparticles of small radius, which have the highest field enhancement factor, into the absorbing layer of the semiconductor has been established. It is shown that the influence of the classical size effects on the factors of the dynamic depolarization and radiation losses manifests itself in the form of the small-amplitude oscillations on the curves of the size dependences of their imaginary parts.

title: Peculiarities of the Electrophysical Characteristics of SiGe Whiskers under the Influence of Strain
authors: Oleh Shevchenko, Natalia Liakh-Kaguy, Anatoly Druzhinin, Stepan Nichkalo, Igor Ostrovskii
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The results of the study of the magnetoresistance dependency on temperature and strain effect on the magnetoresistance of Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1−x</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> whiskers in a wide range of induction magnetic fields 0 – 14 T and temperatures 4.2 – 300 K are presented. It is established that for the Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1−x</inf>Ge<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</inf> solid-solution whiskers, an increase in temperature and an applied strain contribute to a decrease in magnetoresistance. It is established that even a slight increase in temperature (by a few degrees) causes a noticeable decrease in magnetoresistance, while a sufficiently large compressive strain causes only a slight decrease in magnetoresistance at liquid-helium temperature.

title: Machine Learning Enhanced Zero Trust Architecture for Distributed Information and Communication Systems
authors: Nazarii Balkovskyi, Mykhailo Klymash, Oleksii Assaul, Yuliia Pyrih
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents a Machine Learning Enhanced Zero Trust Architecture (ML-ZTA) that integrates adaptive threat intelligence, context-aware trust scoring, and dynamic policy enforcement into a continuous decision-making loop. The proposed framework introduces a real-time trust computation model based on behavioral analysis and contextual risk assessment, enabling self-optimizing access control with sub-second response latency. The architecture unifies machine learning-driven threat analysis and trust computation to achieve continuous, context-aware security decisions in distributed information and communication systems. Experimental evaluation on a simulated environment with over 1,000 heterogeneous devices demonstrates an 11% improvement in detection accuracy, a 39% reduction in trust convergence time, and a 38% decrease in policy enforcement latency compared to the baseline Zero Trust Architecture. These findings confirm that ML-ZTA provides a scalable, resilient, and adaptive security paradigm that bridges classical Zero Trust principles with intelligent, real-time protection for modern distributed ecosystems.

title: Resilience Synchronization Networks Using Cesium Clock
authors: Vyacheslav Vakas, Valerii Koval, Ihor Blinov, Oleksandr Samkov, Oleh Piskun, Bogdan Samkov
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper examines methods for ensuring redundancy in the phase synchronization network of mobile communication systems and for enhancing the resilience of synchronization signal generation equipment. The study explores the use of cesium clocks with phase synchronization capabilities in scenarios where the availability of GNSS reference signals cannot be guaranteed. Additionally, it presents measurement results of stability parameters for synchronization signals with enhanced resilience, obtained from the operational network under martial law conditions.

title: Aperture-Based Segmentation and Value Approximation for rANS Encoding
authors: Vladimir Barannik, Yevhenii Sidchenko, Dmitry Barannik, Mykola Fomin, Pavlo Pertsev, Rodion Prokopenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of improving both robustness and efficiency of video compression under unstable, noisy, or error-prone communication channels. The study proposes an advanced segmentation and preprocessing methodology aimed at increasing the stability of asymmetric numeral systems (ANS), with a focus on the rANS variant, through optimized structural organization of image fragments prior to entropy coding. The approach introduces an aperture-based segmentation strategy that detects and isolates locally coherent regions with similar structural and textural properties. This is combined with an adaptive intra-aperture value approximation mechanism that reduces alphabet diversity and improves compression efficiency while preserving perceptual quality. Unlike classical uniform 8×8 block segmentation, the proposed model dynamically adapts to image content, enabling more accurate region partitioning and reducing error propagation. The method further incorporates a transition to the perceptually oriented XYB color space, where each channel is independently segmented, approximated, and encoded, improving adaptability and robustness. The novelty lies in the integrated use of aperture coherence detection, adaptive approximation, and color-space transformation within rANS-based compression. Experimental results demonstrate a reduction in encoded size from 406,181 to 228,453 bytes while maintaining structural integrity, with distortions remaining localized under severe channel errors (10⁻¹). The use of the XYB color space further reduced data size to 40,796 bytes, confirming the method’s suitability for real-time and resource-constrained systems.

title: Impact of Inter-core Crosstalk in Unrepeatered Transmission over 2-Core MCF
authors: Hans Bissessur, Alexis Busson, Farana Hedaraly
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: This paper examines the impact of inter-core crosstalk in unrepeatered systems based on two-core multi-core fibers (MCF). Although the fiber cores are nominally uncoupled, there is always some residual coupling between the cores. We first measure one spool of multi-core fiber, deduce the basic fiber parameters and then simulate the crosstalk in an unrepeatered system as a function of its length. We find that the unidirectional and bidirectional crosstalk values cross for 250 km long links, so that the configuration with the lowest crosstalk depends on the link length. Next, we set up a fully equipped multi-core fiber transmission line with high-power boosters and first order Raman pumps. We transmit 42 channels in the C-band with and without crosstalk over distances ranging from 205 km to 359 km, adapting the modulation format to reach the maximum capacity. We investigate crosstalk impairments in unidirectional and bidirectional configurations and conclude about the optimum configuration. Finally, we compare the system crosstalk as estimated from spectral measurements and from the transmission penalty.

title: Prediction-Based Proactive Handover for Reliable Connectivity in mmWave and Terahertz Vehicular Networks
authors: Gwanwoo Na, Gahye Kim, Woori Jeon, Kyubeom Nam, Hyoree Ham, Jun-Sik Kim, Soon-Gi Park, Jungmin So
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) and terahertz (THz) communications are key enablers for high-capacity, low-latency vehicular networks, but their vulnerability to blockage and rapid signal fluctuation poses critical challenges for mobility management. In particular, conventional reactive handover (HO) schemes often fail to respond in time, leading to radio link failures (RLF) or unstable connections. This paper proposes a prediction-based proactive HO framework that forecasts future Reference Signal Received Power (RSRP) values using a Transformer-based model and makes anticipatory HO decisions over a sliding prediction window. The proposed method includes a dual-branch decision logic that either optimizes link quality or prevents imminent degradation. Extensive simulations under both static and vehicle-induced blockage scenarios compare the proposed method against conventional reactive handover and existing learning-based approaches. Results show that the proposed approach achieves a balance across handover failure metrics, reducing Too-Late Handover (TLHO), Too-Early Handover (TEHO), and RLF events, while improving throughput and connection stability. These results highlight the potential of learning-based proactive HO strategies for enhancing reliability in mmWave and THz vehicular networks.

title: Learning-Based Robust Control of Electro-Ribbon Artificial Muscle_supp1-3675438.mp4
authors: Etienne Burdet
publish: N/A
abstract: This article presents a learning-based robust control approach for electro-ribbon artificial muscle systems. The complex and coupled nonlinear electromechanical dynamics of these actuators make accurate modeling difficult. To address this challenge, we employ a neural network to approximate the actuator’s internal dynamics, with weight parameters set during an initial learning phase via gradient descent of a Lyapunov function. Feedback linearization combined with slidingmode control is then used to compensate for model estimation errors and achieve precise trajectory tracking, while an integral action mitigates steadystate uncertainties. A Lyapunov-function-based analysis establishes bounded estimation errors during the learning phase, and uniform ultimate boundedness of the closed-loop tracking errors. Experimental results under various operating conditions validate the novel control scheme, exhibiting improved tracking performance and robustness compared to conventional PID control.

title: Deep Learning-Based Secure Scheduling and Cooperative Artificial Noise Generation in LEO Satellite Networks
authors: Yongjae Lee, Taehoon Kim, Inkyu Bang, Erdal Panayirci, H. Vincent Poor
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: As key enablers of 6G, low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations are expected to offer global and low-latency connectivity. However, the broadcast nature and open-space propagation of satellite communications make it inherently vulnerable to eavesdropping. In dense LEO constellations where multiple satellites simultaneously cover overlapping regions, satellite scheduling can significantly contribute to secrecy performance. In this letter, we investigate a novel deep learning-based secure scheduling problem for physical layer security (PLS) in LEO satellite networks. During the scheduling process, we jointly consider multi-satellite transmission and cooperative artificial noise (AN) generation to defend against eavesdropping. The proposed deep learning-based scheduler, designed using attention-based permutation-invariant neural networks, learns security-oriented scheduling policies based solely on statistical channel information of eavesdroppers. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheduling algorithm significantly enhances secrecy performance in terms of both secrecy rate and secrecy outage probability, outperforming conventional baseline schemes.

title: A 91.4-dB SNDR 2-MS/s 18-bit SAR ADC With Enhanced Optimal-Combination-Algorithm-Based Calibration_supp1-3678017.pdf
authors: Yuanqi Hu
publish: N/A
abstract: This work presents a 2-MS/s 18-bit successive-approximation-register analog-to-digital converter (ADC). Acapacitor digital-to-analog converter (CDAC) and a resistor digital-to-analog converter (RDAC) are cascaded to avoid the linearity limitation caused by parasitic capacitors on the bridge capacitor between the CDAC and the RDAC. An optimal-combination-algorithm (OCA)-based calibration is adopted to improve the linearity of the CDAC and the matching between CDAC and the bridge capacitor, and a least significant bit (LSB)-side dynamic element matching (DEM) strategy is applied to further improve the CDAC linearity. As for the RDAC, this work makes it calibration free by combining the parallel R-unit and R-2R topology. Measurement results show that the proposed ADC can achieve 91.4-dB signal-to-noise and distortion ratio (SNDR), 12.0-parts-per-million (ppm) (3.1-LSB) integral non-linearity (INL), and 174.0-dB FOM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">S,SNDR</sub> (including the power consumed by the calibration logic), and the worst-case INL across 25 dies improves from 72.5ppm (19.0LSB) to 18.8ppm (4.9LSB) after the OCA-based calibration and theLSB-side DEM. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, this is the first high-precision (resolution <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\geq 16$</tex-math> </inline-formula> bit) data converter adopting OCA-based calibration.

title: Lightweight and Efficient Kinetics Estimation with Sparse IMUs via Multi-Modal Sensor Distillation
authors: Md Sanzid Bin Hossain, Dexter Hadley, Zhishan Guo, Hwan Choi
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Gait kinetics, including joint moments and ground reaction forces (GRFs), are derived from optical motion capture and force plate data. However, these systems are costly, time-consuming to process, and location-restricted. Biomechanical and deep learning models using wearables such as inertial measurement units (IMUs) can estimate these parameters. However, numerous sensors complicate positioning, attachment, and calibration while reducing user comfort. Although sparse IMU sets with flexible placement are more practical, they lack critical information available in full sensor configurations, reducing performance. Furthermore, existing deep learning models for kinetics estimation are either computationally expensive or exhibit suboptimal performance due to generic architectures, making them unsuitable for compact, low-cost devices. To address these limitations, we propose Kinetics-MFFM-Net, a lightweight model incorporating two novel components: (i) a gated dual encoder with a multi-modal fusion module, and (ii) a sensor distillation (SD) framework that transfers knowledge from full-sensor supervision (e.g., full set of IMUs with sEMG or joint positions) to sparse-IMU configurations. We validate the proposed approach on two publicly available gait datasets: a lower-limb IMU–sEMG dataset spanning treadmill, stairs, ramps, and level-ground ambulation, and a dataset combining IMUs with dual-camera recordings under diverse treadmill walking conditions. Kinetics-MFFM-Net+ SD achieves up to ∼30% lower NRMSE compared to state-of-the-art models with similar or smaller parameter counts. In contrast, larger baseline models achieve comparable accuracy at higher computational cost, requiring up to ∼130× more parameters. Overall, Kinetics-MFFM-Net+SD demonstrates an effective accuracy–efficiency trade-off for gait kinetics estimation, facilitating reliable performance even with sparse IMU configurations.
