title: Modeling and Simulations on the Streamer Discharge Performance in Transformer Oil with Micro-Bubbles and Paperboard Barriers
authors: Ruiyang Guan, Yifei Xu, Xingyu Cao, Zhipeng Feng, Jiacheng Huang, Hong Tian, Minghe Chi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Oil-paper insulation structure is widely used in the power transformer. The most serious potential threat is the appearances of micro-bubbles in transformer oil, which may trigger a streamer discharge even to lead to insulation breakdown or equipment faults. Here, a streamer discharge simulation model, which can well present the physical processes of streamer discharges in transformer oil with micro-bubbles and paperboard barriers, was established based on both fluid dynamic model and plasma discharge model. The parameters effects on the streamer discharge, including bubble amount, bubble radius, bubble location, as well as different layouts of bubble and barrier, were all studied. The simulation results show that the micro-bubble can trigger a second streamer, which is a major role leading to a rapid breakdown of oil insulation, while the barrier may block the original streamer into a two-head streamer with a lower speed. The presence of micro-bubble in the oil is a crucial factor in facilitating the propagation speed, rather than bubble amount. The smaller the bubble radius is, the more severe the streamer discharge will be. The critical radius of bubble is approximately no larger than 50 μm. The most dangerous bubble location is approximately at 20 % of the whole distance close to the needle electrode. The barrier should be placed in the front of the bubble facing the propagation direction in order to achieve a better shielding function. This article is aimed to find some technical guides for the design of oil-paper insulation in power transformer.

title: Decomposed Transfer Entropy for EEG Brain Networks: Parkinson’s Disease and Dopaminergic Modulation
authors: Shumei Zhu, Wanyi Yi, Yukun Feng, Han Xu, Dengxuan Bai, Weiguo Liu, Jun Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Clarifying the mechanisms of altered brain network communication in Parkinson’s disease is crucial, yet conventional connectivity measures do not distinguish whether directed information flow is driven by phase synchrony or spectral amplitude modulation. To address this, we introduce Decomposed Transfer Entropy (DTE), which represents directed information flow as a mechanistic profile comprising phase, spectral, and interaction components. Simulations indicate that DTE identifies distinct coupling types and remains robust under noise; we further benchmark DTE against representative baseline measures and assess parameter sensitivity. Applied to θ band resting EEG, DTE reveals that the unmedicated (PD-OFF) state is characterized by a phase-dominant reweighting originating from the frontal midline hub Fz; medication shifts this mechanistic composition toward that of healthy reference. Hub-centric composition metrics derived from DTE discriminate PD-OFF from healthy controls, and the normalization of phase dominance relates to motor improvement (ΔUPDRS–III). Overall, DTE offers a mechanism-aware perspective on PD pathophysiology, suggesting that therapeutic benefit aligns with normalization of mechanistic composition rather than simple changes in connection strength, and provides a promising route toward mechanism-oriented biomarkers.

title: MirrorLake: A Digital Twin Platform for Real-Time Synchronization with the Real World
authors: Won Gi Choi, Younghwan Jung, Myungsun Baek, Taemin Hwang, Jinyoung Lee, Sangshin Lee
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the growing interest in digital twin technology, there has been a rise in efforts to apply technology in diverse fields. The digital twin can provide opportunities for humans to benefit in both industrial and daily life. We propose a digital twin platform, MirrorLake, designed to support the effective construction and utilization of digital twins by providing core functionalities aligned with the unique characteristics of digital twins. We proposed the digital twin resource management system to handle the representative components of the digital twin that emerged from our in-depth research analysis. We also implemented a synchronization engine and its ecosystem that supports high-volume and bidirectional synchronization between the real and virtual worlds. The engine also supports synchronization between the components of the digital twin by accommodating heterogeneity and linking real-time data. We demonstrated the utility of MirrorLake through three real-world applications in the city environment, smart office, and public safety, highlighting its potential to facilitate digital transformation in various industries.

title: SLO-Aware Multi-Class Routing for Large-Model AI Services Via Two-Layer Cross-Domain Coordination
authors: Qinding Wang, Weikang Tian, Zihao Zhang, Hongchao Wang, Weiting Zhang, Dong Yang, Hongke Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network Science and Engineering
abstract: The growing scale of Large-Model (LM) Artificial Intelligence (AI) service deployments brings heterogeneous requests with distinct Service-Level Objectives (SLOs), thus posing a challenge to achieve long-term service quality at scale. In this paper, we investigate multi-class routing for LM AI services deployed across multiple autonomous domains interconnected over a wide-area network (WAN). In such WAN-scale settings, privacy and autonomy policies limit sharing of domain-internal states, and heavy cross-domain interfaces hinder their deployment. Under this limited visibility, we formulate joint service-domain selection and in-domain dispatch in a constrained stochastic optimization framework to improve long-term service quality. To address this problem, we propose a Two-Layer coordination framework with Two-Stage execution (TLTS) that separates slow-timescale cross-domain coordination from fast-timescale per-request execution. The Coordination layer compresses policy-compliant aggregated feedback into a per-class pressure signal that summarizes sustained SLO stress, so that exchanging only this pressure enables Two-Layer coordination without sharing domain-internal states. We further develop a Service-Identification-Class based TLTS (SIC-TLTS) algorithm to instantiate TLTS, where the Execution layer performs Two-Stage per-request decisions conditioned on the SIC label and the latest pressure. Simulations on a real-world backbone topology show that SIC-TLTS consistently improves service quality while satisfying per-class SLO constraints, compared with representative baselines.

title: Reasoning-Centric Fake News Detection: A Comprehensive Survey of Architectures, Benchmarks, and Open Challenges
authors: Son Thanh Nguyen, Trong Thua Huynh, Tuan Anh Le
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: The proliferation of misinformation, exacerbated by Generative Artificial Intelligence and Large Language Models, necessitates a paradigm shift from pattern-matching to reasoning-based detection systems. While Transformer-based models and Graph Neural Networks have advanced the field, they often lack interpretability and robustness against adversarial attacks. This survey presents a systematic review of Reasoning-Centric Fake News Detection methodologies for last five years. Unlike conventional surveys that categorize works by architecture, we propose a novel four-phase evolutionary framework that traces the transition from surface-level semantics to multi-view reasoning paradigms. We introduce a multi-axis taxonomy that unifies content semantics, dynamic propagation topologies, and cross-modal consistency. Furthermore, we critically analyze the "performance saturation" phenomenon (reaching 99% on static benchmarks such as FakeNewsNet), contrasting it with the degradation observed in low-resource languages and in the face of evolving events. Finally, we propose an Integrated Multi-View Reasoning Framework, a five-layer pipeline designed to harmonize automated detection with human-in-the-loop verification, and outline a future roadmap focused on neuro-symbolic reasoning and adversarial resilience in the Artificial Intelligence vs Artificial Intelligence era.

title: High-Precision In-situ Inductor Loss Evaluation for DC-DC Converters Based on Gain-Phase Frequency Characteristics Correction in Voltage/Current Measurement Systems
authors: Takaaki Ibuchi, Tsuyoshi Funaki
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: Accurate in-situ evaluation of inductor losses in operating DC–DC converters is hindered by frequency-dependent gain and phase errors in practical voltage and current probes, especially when voltage–current phase approaches 90° in low-loss inductor. This paper presents a frequency‑domain gain–phase correction method based on vector network analyzer characterization of commercially available probes. The proposed approach is based on the same fundamental principle as the cross‑power method and enables high‑accuracy loss evaluation using general‑purpose oscilloscopes and probe systems during actual converter operation. Gain–phase characteristics are measured for high‑voltage differential probe, AC/DC current probe, and optically isolated probes. Applying the derived correction to harmonic components measured in a 100‑kHz, 450‑W boost converter reduces the discrepancy between different probe combinations from approximately 20 W to below 0.2 W. The results demonstrate that frequency‑dependent correction is essential for reliable in-situ magnetic loss evaluation using standard measurement equipment.

title: Agentic AI Empowered Multi-UAV Trajectory Optimization in Low-Altitude Economy Networks
authors: Feibo Jiang, Xitao Pan, Li Dong, Kezhi Wang, Xiaolong Li, Ruidong Li, Changhong Wang, Cunhua Pan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive Communications and Networking
abstract: This study proposes a novel Agentic Retrieval-augmented generation with Mamba-Attention Integrated Transformer (ARMAIT) framework for multi-Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) trajectory optimization. The framework is built upon Large Language Models (LLMs), incorporating Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) empowered by Agentic AI and integrated with a UAV-specific knowledge base. Through the Agentic RAG, the LLM autonomously interprets high-level task requirements and identifies the key components necessary for trajectory optimization, including model inputs and outputs, network architecture, reward functions, and task constraints. To support efficient modeling across different system scales, we introduce the Mamba-Attention Integrated Transformer (MAIT), a hybrid neural architecture that combines the long-range dependency modeling capability of attention mechanisms with the efficient temporal dynamic representation of Mamba. Furthermore, a Trajectory-Group Relative Policy Optimization (T-GRPO) method is proposed to achieve unified policy gradient optimization in both discrete and continuous trajectory spaces for MAIT training. Extensive experimental results validate the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed ARMAIT framework.

title: Multi-step Adaptive Deconvolution via Multi-view for Image Enhancement in Scattering Media
authors: Hu Jiang, Yu-Cheng Wang, Wan-Tong Yin, Xiao-Shu Nie, Hui-Juan Zhang, Bao-Lei Liu, Zhao-Hua Yang, Yuan-Jin Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Imaging
abstract: Imaging through scattering media under natural illumination is a critical challenge in applications such as autonomous driving, underwater detection, and aviation safety. We propose a novel multi-view multi-step deconvolution image enhancement method for imaging in sacttering media, which combines image background noise suppression, detail enhancement, and super-resolution. It integrates the advantages of dark channel prior preprocessing and adaptive deconvolution. A camera array-based common field of view multi-frame imaging model is established to address the challenges of long acquisition time and difficulty in registering moving objects in single-camera systems, thereby enabling multi-frame image acquisition of objects. Then, a multi-step adaptive deconvolution scattering imaging algorithm is designed for image enhancement, background noise suppression, and super-resolution imaging of objects, enabling multi-frame image fusion. The proposed approach tackles the issue of low signal-to-noise ratio in scattering environments, achieving high-quality passive imaging in complex scattering scenes. Finally, the proposed method is validated through simulations, laboratory-equivalent experiments, and real-world experiments, demonstrating its ability to achieve high-quality imaging in complex scattering scenarios with natural illumination, especially in heavy haze environments with limited camera quantities. The results show that when comparing with the existing methods under artificial heavy, moderate, and light haze conditions, the proposed method improves PSNR by 2.24%, 1.83%, and 2.95%, and SSIM by 12.07%, 5.21%, and 5.53%, respectively.

title: BatchRRT: A Fully-Batched Reformulation of RRT with GPU-Accelerated SDF Collision Checking for Real-Time Human-Robot Collision Avoidance
authors: Jie Liu, Meng Li, Zian Liu, Tianmei Sun
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: Industrial human-robot collaboration requires real-time motion planning with accurate collision avoidance in dynamic environments. While recent neural signed distance field (SDF) methods achieve sub-millisecond inference from sparse observations, traditional RRT planners fail to exploit this capability due to serial execution and linear nearest-neighbor search bottlenecks. We present BatchRRT, a fully-batched reformulation of RRT that jointly optimizes batch sampling, KD-tree acceleration, and GPU-parallel SDF collision checking. Through comprehensive experiments across 5,340 planning trials in human-robot interaction scenarios spanning single and dual-person configurations, we demonstrate 4-5× speedup over baseline RRT with consistent 99-100% success rate. Our key finding reveals that with fast neural SDFs, nearest-neighbor search—not collision detection—becomes the dominant bottleneck, with KD-tree optimization providing 15.6× acceleration. Systematic ablation studies show that these three components exhibit strong interdependence: isolated batch sampling provides only modest gains (1.1×), while their combination unlocks substantial improvements. We validate our approach on a 6-DOF industrial manipulator navigating around dynamically posed human obstacles in both reaching and bending motions, including dual-person scenarios.

title: A Semi-Supervised Learning Method Robust to Label Noise for Radar Target Recognition via Label Correction and Sample Selection
authors: Haoqi Mei, Penghui Wang, Jun Ding, Wenchao Chen, Junkun Yan, Hongwei Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Supervised deep learning techniques have been widely applied to radar high-resolution range profile (HRRP) target recognition, achieving significant improvements in classification accuracy. However, these methods generally rely on the assumption of accurate labels, which often does not hold in practice due to the prevalence of label noise. Label noise can cause models to overfit incorrect labels, thereby degrading performance and limiting practical deployment. To mitigate this challenge, we propose UCJS-SSL, a semi-supervised learning approach that combines uncertainty-guided label correction with Jensen–Shannon Divergence (JSD)-based sample selection. The proposed framework consists of three main stages. First, an uncertainty-based label correction module is proposed, which rectifies potentially mislabeled samples by leveraging the model's predictive uncertainty. Next, a JSD-based sample selection mechanism is employed to reliably separate clean samples from noisy ones. Clean samples are used for supervised learning, whereas noisy ones are treated as unlabeled data and incorporated into the unsupervised learning process. Finally, a tailored semi-supervised approach termed Consistency-Enhanced Averaged Match (CEA-Match) is designed for HRRP data, effectively leveraging both labeled and unlabeled samples for robust model training. To verify the proposed approach, experimental results on real-world radar data validate the effectiveness of UCJS-SSL in maintaining high recognition performance across varying label noise levels and practical scenarios.

title: Multi-view Active Vision System for Robust Surgical Instrument Tracking
authors: Yu Cai, Xianzhong Xie, Mingzhu Zhu, Bingwei He, Yan Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Instrument tracking serves as the backbone of sur gical navigation systems, enabling precise real-time localization of surgical tools. However, most visual tracking systems adopt f ixed camera configurations that passively observe the surgical instrument. Such static architectures have limited measurement volumes and cannot fully accommodate complex instrument trajectories during surgery, leading to tracking failures. To avoid this situation, we propose a multi-view active visual tracking system to maintain consistent tracking of moving surgical in struments. Multiple cameras observing from different positions localize the instrument by constructing multi-view multi-point perspective equations. Within this positioning framework, an online viewpoint planning algorithm is developed to actively adjust non-ideal viewpoints by modeling suboptimal-view condi tions and formulating corresponding inequality constraints. The system employs a visual servoing based camera control mecha nism to execute viewpoint adjustments in real time. Following each adjustment, the extrinsic parameters for the camera are recalibrated using an online multi-frame extrinsic calibration method, ensuring consistent spatial accuracy. Both simulation and real-world experiments are conducted, and the proposed system achieves excellent localization performance and exhibits superior robustness against viewpoint limitations compared with f ixed-camera systems.

title: Spatio-Temporal Consistent Soft Sensor Modeling and Monitoring of Thermal Power Plants based on Physical Knowledge
authors: Qianchao Wang, Peng Sha, Leena Heistrene, Yuxuan Ding, Yaping Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Data-driven soft sensors have been widely applied in thermal power plants. However, the consistency of spatio-temporal features and the explicit interpretability in data-driven soft sensors remain challenges. In this light, this work introduces a novel method termed spatio-temporal consistent and interpretable model (STCIM). First, local and far topological spatio-temporal features are captured by a spatio-temporal consistent feature extraction block, in which a local spatio-temporal feature extraction block and a far topological alignment block are designed. Then, the spatio-temporal consistent features are projected into the latent space, which is guided by the first principle and explicitly assigned physical meanings. During the training process, the latent space is integrated into the loss function to predict the state variables and the output and maintain the interpretability of the model. The efficacy of the proposed STCIM is demonstrated through the modeling of two generated datasets and a real-life dataset of coal-fired power plants. The corresponding experiments show: 1) The generalization of STCIM outperforms other methods, especially in different operation situations. 2) The far topological spatio-temporal consistency is vital for feature alignment. 3) The hyper-parameters of physics-informed interpretable latent space loss function decide the performance of state variable estimation, especially the variables whose slight changes will affect the output.

title: Design of a Variable-Stiffness Elbow Rehabilitation Robot and Its Control: A Variable Impedance Strategy Based on LSTM Elbow Torque Prediction
authors: Ao Meng, Zhi Pang, Fengchen Liu, Baogui Qiu, Bingshan Hu, Hongliu Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: In the rehabilitation training of stroke patients, traditional rigid-driven robots struggle to effectively address spasticity, leading to suboptimal training and risks of secondary injury. This paper proposes a dual-motor driven variable stiffness elbow Joint rehabilitation robot based on a cam-spring-roller mechanism, enabling independent control of stiffness and position. We developed an LSTM-based elbow joint torque estimation model trained on a dataset generated using a musculoskeletal model incorporating Hill muscle model, achieving accurate and reliable torque prediction. A stiffness and impedance cooperative control framework is then established based on the estimated torque values: wherein the system provides higher assistive torque with lower mechanical stiffness when lower muscle strength is detected, and reduces assistive torque while increasing mechanical stiffness when higher muscle strength is identified. The framework also includes a spasticity detection model is proposed, incorporating metrics of antagonist muscle abnormal activation, normalized jerk score and velocity-activation quantification, aiming to enhance training safety and demonstrating the potential for improved patient engagement. Finally, experiments on four healthy subjects and a patient with spasticity demonstrated the feasibility and preliminary efficacy of the stiffness-variable impedance control framework and the spasticity detection model in a pilot study.

title: EDTNet: An Edge-Guided Dual-Branch Transformer Network for Mars Layered Ejecta Craters Extraction
authors: Dongchen Di, Shiyang Fu, Zhizhong Kang
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Layered Ejecta Craters (LECs) are distinctive Martian craters with fluidized ejecta whose morphological characteristics correlate with Martian liquid water and the distribution of subsurface water ice. Existing crater extraction methods struggle to effectively extract LECs with complex edges or significantly degraded boundaries and fail to sufficiently characterize weak-texture targets under complex Martian backgrounds. To address these issues, an Edge-Guided Dual-Branch Transformer Network (EDTNet) fusing convolutional neural network (CNN) and Transformer is proposed for automated LEC extraction. The encoder adopts a dual-branch design integrating CNN and Transformer, retaining CNN’s local feature extraction capability while leveraging Transformer’s long-range global information capture. An Edge-Guided Semantic Focusing (EGSF) module is integrated into the CNN branch to improve the extraction accuracy of degraded LEC boundaries. A Fast Fourier Convolution Attention (FFCA) mechanism is introduced in the Transformer branch to mitigate complex Martian background interference and strengthen weak-texture target representation. A Feature Fusion Module (FFM) is incorporated between the two branches to fuse local and global features. Extensive experiments on the self-constructed Mars Layered Ejecta Crater (MLEC Dataset) demonstrate that EDTNet achieves an mIoU of 90.35%, outperforming SOTA methods such as MobileNet, DeepLabv3, ConvNeXt, Swinformer, SegFormer and EASSA.

title: An Area-Efficient, High-Throughput 2T-2MTJ STT-MRAM Architecture for In-Memory Computing Applications
authors: Mohit Gupta, Ravi S. Siddanath, G Prasad, Karnati Paranjai, Manish Goswami, Kavindra Kandpal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Nanotechnology
abstract: This paper presents a novel 2T-2MTJ STT-MRAM bit-cell architecture tailored for In-Memory Computing (IMC) applications, designed to address the limitations of conventional memory architectures. Using 2T-2MTJ bit-cells, our design significantly reduces chip area compared to traditional 8Transistor/10Transistor SRAM bitcell architectures while maintaining high throughput and energy efficiency. A tunable clamp voltage sensing scheme is introduced, enabling parallelism and efficient execution of key bitwise operations such as XNOR, NOR, and NAND without the added complexity of Analog-to-Digital Converters (ADC). Benchmarking using NeuroSim with the VGG-8 neural network, trained on the CIFAR-10 dataset, revealed a 208.52% and 95% increase in throughput (measured in TOPS) compared to 10T SRAM and 1T-1MTJ architectures, respectively, along with improvements in chip area, clock frequency, and overall system performance. This work showcases the potential of STT-MRAM-based IMC architectures to enhance energy efficiency, reduce design complexity, and improve scalability for Artificial Intelligence (AI) / Machine Learning (ML) workloads, making it a promising solution for next-generation computing systems.

title: Research of Network Monitoring Algorithms Based on Artificial Intelligence
authors: Andrij Senyk, Mykhailo Klymash, Bohdan Tsybulyak, Yaroslav Pyrih, Andrii Holdii
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The study analyzes the proposed Contextually Sensitive Cybersecurity Monitoring Algorithm Based on Recurrent Neural Networks (CCM-RNN) for anomaly detection and attack prediction in information systems. CCM-RNN involves the use of a different number of features corresponding to a specific training context, which affects the calculation results. Experiments show that the best results are achieved when using 3–6 features, providing high accuracy and stable training time (average accuracy 53.4% with a training duration of 3.8–4.2 s). Increasing the number of features raises the computational complexity without significantly improving accuracy while reducing it decreases the accuracy of model training. Optimal tuning of the number of features allows for finding a balance between learning speed and accuracy. Additional studies include regularization and optimization of hyperparameters to improve the performance of CCM-RNN in complex conditions. The algorithm shows promising potential for the development of threat forecasting and monitoring systems.

title: Retraction Notice: Framework of cloud well-being tactics intended for guarding data
authors: Srihari Bodapati, A. Akila
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Today, distributed computing is a rising method for registering in software engineering. Distributed computing is an arrangement of assets and administrations that are offered by the system or web. Distributed computing develops different figuring systems like network registering, conveyed processing. Today distributed computing is utilized as a part of both mechanical field and scholastic field. Cloud encourages its clients by giving virtual assets by means of web. As the field of distributed computing is spreading the new procedures are creating. This expansion in distributed computing environment additionally builds security challenges for cloud designers. Clients of cloud spare their information in the cloud henceforth the absence of security in cloud can lose the client's trust. In this paper we will talk about a portion of the cloud security issues in different perspectives like multi-occupancy, flexibility, accessibility and so forth the paper additionally examine existing security systems and methodologies for a safe cloud. This paper will empower analysts and experts to think about various security dangers and models and apparatuses proposed.

title: Red Chili Leaf Disease Identification and Monitoring System: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Narasak Boonthep, Jirabhorn Chaiwongsai, Thana Udomsripaiboon
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Red chili is a crop of substantial economic importance worldwide. However, its productivity is often compromised by a range of foliar diseases. Conventional approaches used to identify leaf diseases in red chili are labor-intensive, subjective, and heavily dependent on expert evaluation, resulting in limited accessibility for small holder farmers. This study proposes an integrated deep learning-based framework for automated detection and monitoring of red chili leaf diseases by leveraging computer vision techniques and convolutional neural networks (CNNs). The system incorporates MobileNetV2 for disease classification and YOLOv8 for real-time object detection. A dataset comprising 2,940 leaf images across six categories-including five disease classes (Leaf Curl, Leaf Spot, Anthracnose, Yellowish) and Healthy-was used for model development. Data augmentation strategies such as rotation, flipping, and color jittering were applied to improve model generalization. Experimental results demonstrated that MobileNetV2 achieved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 6. 5 \%}$</tex> classification accuracy, while YOLOv8 attained a mean average precision of 87.16 % for real-time detection. Furthermore, the system integrates disease identification with harvest prediction, providing actionable insights through a user-friendly mobile application. The proposed framework offers a practical, scalable, and accessible approach to red chili disease management, wherein its integrated detection platform constitutes a significant advancement in precision agriculture

title: Research on the Use of Large Language Models for SQL Query Optimization
authors: Artem Naboka, Oksana Mazurova
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This work addresses the problem of developing high-performance relational databases through the use of large language models. Language model selection strategies for query optimization tasks are analyzed. Potential issues with optimizations generated by language models are described, including syntax errors in queries, inconsistent execution results, and lack of query performance improvement. Methods for addressing these identified problems are also proposed and substantiated, based on providing query execution context to the language model. This work presents experimental results on SQL query optimization using popular large language models. The conducted experiments include optimization without context transmission and with transmission of context of varying size and type in prompts. An evaluation of the impact of query execution context on the effectiveness of resulting optimization is performed, and recommendations for context selection, based on which the most efficient optimizations are generated, are provided.

title: Information Technology for the Selection of Social Service Recipients Based on the Fuzzy Classification Method
authors: Oksana Mulesa, Tamara Radivilova, Larysa Chala, Marian Tokar, Vladyslav Peresoliak, Jolana Golik
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This study is dedicated to the development of decision-making technology for selecting recipients of social services using the fuzzy classification method. The proposed technology automates decision-making in conditions of incomplete information, thereby enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of selecting service recipients. The core of technology comprises a database, an analytical block, and a decision-making block that interact to ensure optimal resource allocation. The fuzzy classification method enables adaptation to various conditions and requirements, ensuring system flexibility and adaptability through the use of production rules.

title: Adaptive Aperture-Based Image Segmentation for Enhanced ANS Compression and Error Resistance
authors: Vladimir Barannik, Yevhenii Sidchenko, Oleksandr Akimov, Mykhailo Babenko, Serhii Fediuk, Pavlo Onypchenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper highlights the relevance of efficient image and video compression under limited computational and communication resources. Traditional methods such as JPEG and ANS lack adaptability to image structure, especially under channel errors, which motivates improving segmentation and compression techniques. The study aims to enhance ANS algorithms by developing an adaptive aperture-based image division method. The feasibility of aperture segmentation is justified through spectral-parametric analysis and value approximation. A transition to the XYB color model is proposed to improve correspondence to human visual perception and support adaptive aperture reference adjustment for better compression and noise robustness. The presented methods significantly reduce compressed data volume while maintaining structural integrity. Effective threshold values for adaptive aperture control are introduced, ensuring optimal trade-offs between speed and quality. Experimental results confirm the efficiency of the approach. The work focuses on improving robustness and efficiency of ANS-based compression via dynamic aperture segmentation that accounts for local structural, spectral, and color features. The novelty includes: (1) a dynamic aperture construction method that stabilizes segmentation and minimizes information loss; (2) adoption of XYB to avoid ARGB packing artifacts and improve channel-wise coherence; (3) adaptive thresholds for updating aperture reference values, reducing computational cost and improving entropy characteristics. Dynamic aperture formation reduces processing time from minutes to seconds while maintaining approximation quality. Compared to fixed 8×8 segmentation, the method shows much higher error resilience: even at error rates of 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> objects remain recognizable. Aperture segmentation with approximation reduces size from 334,952 bytes (8×8) to 59,745 bytes, while inclusion of XYB and adaptive approximation achieves 34,534 bytes.

title: Iot-Based Smart Kitchen Safety System for Lpg Leak Detection With Real-Time Alerts and Safety Cut-Off
authors: Debi Prasad Pradhan, Pritam Nanda, Soumya Ranjan Samal, Shuvabrata Bandopadhaya, Antoni Ivanov, Vladimir Poulkov, Agata Manolova
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: In recent years, the increased usage of Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) in households and commercial kitchens has raised significant safety concerns, particularly in the event of gas leaks. This work presents the design and implementation of an Internet of Things (IoT) based smart kitchen safety system aimed at detecting LPG gas leaks, issuing real-time alerts, and initiating automatic safety measures, such as cutting off the gas supply. The system employs a gas sensor to monitor the concentration of LPG in the environment continuously. Upon detecting a leak beyond a predefined threshold, the system triggers an immediate alert through IoT-based communication. Simultaneously, a solenoid valve is actuated to disable the gas regulator, preventing further leakage and minimizing the risk of fire or explosion. The core of the system is powered by a microcontroller with Wi-Fi capability, enabling data transmission to the cloud. For remote monitoring and control, the Blynk IoT dashboard is used, providing a userfriendly interface that allows real-time visualization of gas levels, system status, and manual override of the gas valve if needed. Through Blynk, users receive instant alerts and can take prompt actions from their smartphones, enhancing the responsiveness and convenience of the system. This system not only ensures prompt response to hazardous conditions, is cost-effective, easy to install, and scalable, making it suitable for widespread adoption in both urban and rural settings.

title: Retraction Notice: DB-Scan Algorithm based Colon Cancer Detection And Stratification Analysis
authors: Gundlapalle Rajesh, Boda Saroja, M. Dhivya, A.B. Gurulakshmi
publish: 2020 Fourth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Histopathological examination of tissue models is basic for the conclusion and reviewing of colon malignancy. In any case, the technique is subjective and prompts imperative intra/bury spectator distinction in the examination as it predominantly relies upon the graphical evaluation of histopathologists. Thus, a tried and true PC supported technique, which can naturally group harmful and ordinary colon tests are required; however, automating this strategy is demanding because of the nearness of exceptions. In this paper, a productive technique for identifying colon disease from biopsy tests which comprise of four imperative stages. DB-SCAN estimation to distinguish colon tumor from biopsy tests is presented in this paper. In the proposed approach, from the outset, the colon biopsy tests are preprocessed using DB-SCAN configuration to make a set of redundant localities in which groups or clusters are formed. At that point, the exceptions inside the bunched areas are created as a tree structure in light of the choice tree in which the anomalies are hubs, and the connection between hubs are delivered based on data about exceptions. At that point, entropy-based exception score calculation will be done on every hub of the tree. The Information picks up technique is utilized to figure the score for the exceptions. At long last, score based grouping is accomplished to order the ordinary or harmful cells. Experimental trials exhibit, the proposed strategy has better outcomes contrasted to existing strategies. It furthermore acclaims that the proposed procedure is adequate for the colon tumor identification process. The proposed strategy is executed on Matlab working platform and the investigations exhibit that the proposed technique has high accomplished high grouping precision contrasted and different strategies.

title: A Large-Scale Constrained Multi-Objective Evolutionary Algorithm with Promising Region Detection and Diversity Enhancement
authors: Xiaoyu Zhong, Xiangjuan Yao, Kangjia Qiao, Dunwei Gong, Yaochu Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: Most existing constrained multi-objective evolutionary algorithms (CMOEAs) suffer from slow convergence and may even fail to find feasible solutions when dealing with problems that involve large-scale decision spaces and complex constraints. To this end, this paper introduces a promising region-guided CMOEA. In the proposed algorithm, a promising region detection and diversity enhancement strategy is designed to avoid entrapment in local optima. Specifically, this strategy first utilizes well-converged feasible non-dominated solutions among all solutions examined so far to detect the promising region where the constrained Pareto front may exist. Then, three non-dominated sorting procedures based on extended Pareto dominance, extended reverse Pareto dominance, and classical Pareto dominance, are sequentially executed to guide the population to evenly search the promising region and approach the constrained Pareto front from diverse search directions. In addition, an accelerated evolutionary search strategy is devised to improve reproduction quality in large-scale search spaces. Comprehensive experiments conducted on four benchmark test suites and thirty coal mine integrated energy system dispatch problems demonstrate the superiority of the proposed algorithm over seven state-of-the-art algorithms.

title: Advancing Quantum State Preparation Using Decision Diagram with Local Invertible Maps
authors: Xin Hong, Aochu Dai, Chenjian Li, Sanjiang Li, Shenggang Ying, Mingsheng Ying
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Quantum state preparation (QSP) is a fundamental task in quantum computing and quantum information processing. It is critical to the execution of many quantum algorithms, including those in quantum machine learning. In this paper, we propose a family of efficient QSP algorithms tailored to different numbers of available ancilla qubits — ranging from no ancilla qubits, to a single ancilla qubit, to a sufficiently large number of ancilla qubits. Our approach exploits the power of Local Invertible Map Tensor Decision Diagrams (LimTDDs) — a highly compact representation of quantum states that combines tensor networks and decision diagrams to reduce quantum circuit complexity. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our methods significantly outperform existing approaches and exhibit better scalability for large-scale quantum states, both in terms of runtime and gate complexity. Furthermore, our method shows exponential improvement in best-case scenarios.

title: Retrieval-Based Deep Difference Modeling for Audio Deepfake Detection
authors: Rui Liu, Jinhua Zhang, Haizhou Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Audio, Speech and Language Processing
abstract: With the rapid advancement of ultra-realistic audio deepfakes, Audio Deepfake Detection (ADD) has raised serious attention. In recent years, retrieval-based augmentation technology has shown great potential in enhancing ADD by providing additional contextual information. However, most existing retrieval-augmented ADD methods construct their reference space primarily from bonafide audio, without explicitly incorporating spoof samples as equally structured retrieval references. This design limits the explicit modeling of fine-grained bonafide–spoof differences at the reference level. To address this, we propose the Retrieval-based Deep Difference Modeling for ADD, termed RDD-ADD, that integrates the following three novel mechanisms: (1) We proposed a new dual-database retrieval strategy, which jointly utilizes both bonafide and spoof databases to provide richer comparative information for the tested audio. (2) We incorporated anchor-based similarity fusion and GRU-based contextual fusion mechanisms to perform targeted dynamic fusion of the retrieved features, enhancing their overall representational power. (3) We introduced a graph-based deep difference feature learning strategy, to model the interactions between the tested audio and the retrieved samples and capture the fine-grained differences successfully. Using the above strategies, we can fully learn the in-depth differences between the audio to be tested and the bonafide audio and the spoof audio, so as to achieve robust ADD performance. Experimental results on three benchmarking datasets demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms eleven advanced baselines, providing a more effective solution for retrieval-based ADD.

title: Region Partitioning-Based Scalable Real-Time Network Verification via Native Distributed Architecture
authors: Bo He, Mingxiao Ma, Yuehan Zhang, Lingqi Guo, Chenyang Zhao, Qi Qi, Zirui Zhuang, Haifeng Sun, Gong Zhang, Jianxin Liao, Cheng Huang, Jingyu Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Networking
abstract: Data plane verification (DPV) is critical in ensuring correct network operation, but practical deployment demands solutions that simultaneously achieve real-time responsiveness and massive scalability. Conventional centralized DPV approaches fundamentally struggle with these requirements due to inherent architectural constraints: the single verification server becomes both a computational bottleneck during rapid verification cycles and a collection bottleneck when gathering real-time data plane updates across large-scale networks. As network scale increases, these limitations manifest as either unacceptable verification latency or incomplete data plane visibility—precisely when robust DPV is essential for preventing packet loss and security violations. This paper introduces Atlas*, a novel distributed data plane verification (DPV) system designed to address fundamental scalability and real-time performance challenges in network verification. Atlas* achieves this through a hierarchical architecture that strategically distributes verification tasks across specialized components, combined with innovative parallel processing techniques to maximize computational efficiency. Simultaneously, Atlas* optimizes communication latency between distributed components through a region partitioning algorithm. Extensive evaluations in simulated environments and production deployments demonstrate Atlas*’s capability to perform fast, scalable verification while maintaining lightweight resource allocation. Empirical results show Atlas* achieves 2-7x speedup compared to existing state-of-the-art solutions, with performance advantages progressively increasing as network scale expands.

title: Co-Optimized Strategy Demonstration of a D-Band TWT Amplifier for High-Data-Rate Wireless Communication Systems
authors: Han Lai, Xiao Yang, Lingling Zhao, Yujiang Liu, Yubin Gong, Zhaoyun Duan, Huarong Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: D-band is a key enabler for next-generation high-speed wireless links. To fully realize its potential, efficient and stable power sources are crucial. This article presents a D-band traveling wave tube (TWT) amplifier, designed to meet the requirements of wireless communication systems and with potential for large-scale production. The device is realized through a co-optimized strategy, including optimizations of the electron optical system (EOS), RF circuit, and assembly process, demonstrating a pathway for reproducible fabrication. The Pierce electron gun features a cylindrical focusing electrode, which provides better operability and enhanced alignment tolerance during assembly. When combined with a periodic permanent magnet (PPM) focusing system, the EOS attains a 99.5% beam transmission rate in a short tube test. The slow wave structure (SWS) utilizes a folded waveguide (FWG) unit, characterized by a well-balanced high interaction impedance and a broad bandwidth. A symmetric circuit with identical input and output sections is designed to compensate for the initial launching loss and maintain high gain. Furthermore, by reducing component variety, this configuration could simplify fabrication and increase yield. It is an important advantage for mass production. Well-matched RF windows and step-shaped attenuators are integrated into the circuit to ensure efficient power transmission and circuit stability. Moreover, a stepwise welding and assembly process is employed to reduce the thermal deformation of precision components, thereby enhancing the reliability and success rate of the prototype. In the final hot-test result, the proposed TWT achieves an output power of 10.5–26.5 W within a 10-GHz operating bandwidth (151–161GHz), with a maximum gain reaching 55.9 dB. The corresponding input drive stays below 1 mW, fulfilling the typical system requirements for communication applications. The development of the final product could provide practical experience in designing and fabricating power sources in the D-band and higher terahertz (THz) frequency ranges.

title: Design Methodology of High-Selectivity Waveguide Filters With Post-Type Coupling Structures
authors: Jun-Jie Yan, Fu-Chang Chen, Kai-Ran Xiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article discusses the post-type coupling structures in the waveguide filter designs. In these filter configurations, a post structure with a resonant frequency (TEM mode) much lower than the center frequency is used to couple the two modes in the waveguide. The redundant TEM mode strongly couples with the other modes and acts as a nonresonating node (NRN). This provides a flexible bypass coupling path for configuring the transmission zero (TZ). Alternatively, the post can be considered a pure coupling structure that competes with other types of coupling to form a mixed-coupling structure that introduces TZs. An interesting conclusion can be drawn: when the redundant mode is far from the passband, a coupling path incorporating an NRN and a parallel bypass coupling path is approximately equivalent to a linear frequency-dependent coupling (FDC). This work tries to combine these two different perspectives to analyze the same structures in waveguide filters. Further exploration of the relationship between the two circuits reveals how to obtain a large slope parameter for the FDC coefficient. This methodology can also be used to analyze nonlinear FDC structures in waveguide filters, which enables their synthesis using linear FDC topologies. Two coupling structures for single-mode filters, including linear and nonlinear FDC structures, are presented to verify the analysis of introducing extremely-close TZs. Additionally, as a specific implementation of the methodology, the design of dual-mode filters with nonlinear FDC structures is presented, illustrating its utility for advanced post-type coupling structures.

title: Spatiotemporal Alignment for EEG-fNIRS Emotion Recognition
authors: Haifeng Li, Xueying Zhang, Guijun Chen, Yaru Zhou, Zelin Wu, Lixia Huang, Ying Sun
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Due to neurovascular coupling, an inherent emotional complementarity is observed between electroencephalography (EEG) and functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS); however, these modalities exhibit significant cross-modal spatiotemporal and individual differences in emotional expression. Furthermore, there is an inherent negative coupling between oxygenated hemoglobin and deoxygenated hemoglobin in fNIRS, which can lead to inconsistent results when models perform emotion prediction based on both. To address these challenges, this paper proposes the EEG-fNIRS spatiotemporal alignment network (ENSTAN), which comprises a dynamic multigraph attention mechanism, temporally aligned cross-attention, and a subspace source-target emotion alignment module. Given the limited availability of publicly accessible EEG-fNIRS emotion datasets, we validated the proposed ENSTAN on the self-constructed ENTER dataset and compared it with various baseline methods. The results demonstrate that ENSTAN achieved average accuracies of 91.97% in random cross-validation and 88.96% in trialunit cross-validation for subject-dependent experiments, with a 73.24% average accuracy in subject-independent experiments, confirming its effectiveness. In addition, the proposed ENSTAN elucidates the salient interaction channels and response latencies between EEG and fNIRS.

title: Motion2VecSets: Non-Rigid Shape Reconstruction and Tracking with 4D Latent Set Diffusion
authors: Jiapeng Tang, Wei Cao, Biao Zhang, Chang Luo, Yaoyao Liu, Matthias Nießner
publish: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
abstract: We introduce Motion2VecSets, a 4D diffusion model for dynamic surface mesh generation from various ambiguous observations, including a sequence of RGB images, sparse and partial point clouds, and low-resolution voxel grids. While recent methods using neural field representations have shown success in modeling non-rigid objects, conventional feed-forward architectures struggle with noisy, partial, or sparse observations due to their deterministic nature. To address the inherent one-to-many mapping problem, we introduce a diffusion model that explicitly learns the shape and motion distribution of non-rigid objects through an iterative denoising process of compressed latent representations. The diffusion-based priors provide more plausible and diverse reconstructions under ambiguous conditions. Instead of relying on global latent codes, we represent 4D dynamics using latent sets. This novel 4D representation captures local shape and deformation patterns, leading to more accurate non-linear motion capture and significantly improving generalization capacity to unseen motions and identities. For temporally coherent tracking, we jointly denoise latent sets across frames and enable cross-frame information exchange. To reduce computational cost, we design an interleaved spatial-temporal attention block that alternately aggregates deformation latents along spatial and temporal dimensions. Extensive experiments on datasets of humans, animals, and articulated objects demonstrate that Motion2VecSets outperforms prior methods in reconstructing and tracking non-rigid deformations from various imperfect observations. Our implementation is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://vveicao.github.io/projects/Motion2VecSets/</uri>.

title: Retraction Notice: An Effective Disease Prediction Algorithms Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Paladugu Sirivanth, S. P. V. Kavya, N V Krishna Rao, M. Tejaswini, Jenvith Manduva, K. Sai Sruthi, J Thirupathi
publish: 2021 Fifth International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Diagnosis is an important factor in every hospital to get patient proposer treatment. The earlier models only concentrate on conventional models like as film-based segmentation, CT image extraction, ultrasound scanning and etc. The following models are only depending on old techniques, so that guanine information is not getting. There for a machine learning (ML) based diagnosis models are required to cross over above limitations. In this work an advanced ML technique are analyzed for disease prediction and clear classification. The performance measures sensitivity 98.45%, accuracy 98.42% and recall 97.987% had been attained which are outperformance the methodology.

title: Retraction Notice: Optimizing Water Usage and Improving Irrigation
authors: Kranthi Madala, Narendra Babu Tatini
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Irrigation is the single biggest client of water and it expends more than 70 percent of universes supplies, through various very much created types of water assets. To have sustenance security in future, extensive speculations are being made by Government of India. The best possible administration of water asset has turned out to be essential, underlining on rising need to assess a current water system framework. Streamlining water use and improving water system on farmland sets aside extra cash, increment yields and ensure the earth in the meantime as making cultivating progressively economical. Water system is the single biggest client of water and it expends more than 70 percent of universes supplies, through various all around created types of water assets. To have nourishment security in future, expansive speculations are being made by Government of India. The correct administration of water asset has turned out to be urgent, underlining on developing need to assess a current water system framework.

title: Retraction Notice: Skew Correction Techniques for Scanned Documents
authors: S Lalitha, K N Madhusudhan, B Harshitha, H H Surendra
publish: 2021 7th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: This work manages viable partition of foreground and background in low quality pictures such as old documents experiencing different kinds of debasements which incorporates effects of aging, uneven foreground and background, noise in scanning, separating the content from the picture, and so forth. The methods of image restoration of these outdated documents are proposed. The process is started by scanning documents page by page using a HD scanner and as an output HD images are obtained. And the different kinds of techniques of processing are acquired which includes skew correction, tilt correction, black hole removal and text extraction. The proposed techniques demonstrate a phenomenal flexibility to handle with these issues of uneven brightening and neighbourhood changes or non-consistency in foreground and background view hues.

title: Retraction Notice: Smart Diagnosis of Disease Using Machine Learning
authors: B Rajapandian, S Premalatha, S Sivakumar, A. Vimonisha, K Abirami
publish: 2022 8th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The impact of the morbidity in the long term leads to chronic disease and high mortality rates. Majority of the people suffering realize the chronic nature at the end stage. Machine learning can be integrated with the physician to deliver better solutions in identifying the nature of disease at early stages. The patients are diagnosed with the help of machine learning techniques and algorithms. Machine learning algorithms like Naïve Bayesian network, decision tree, random forest and support vector machine are integrated with the patient data to diagnose the disease and give the result of the patient's condition as normal or abnormal. After the diagnosis, medications are suggested for the disease. The general methods used for diagnosis are not affordable by the major population and machine learning methods can overcome the above limits.

title: Prism: A Trace-Driven Simulator for Component-Aware Adaptive Streaming of V3C Content
authors: Jérémy Ouellette, Abdelhak Bentaleb
publish: Proceedings of the ACM Multimedia Systems Conference 2026
abstract: None

title: Geographic Information Model for Complex Ecological Analysis in Preselected Geographic Area and Time Dimension
authors: Mykhaylo Melnyk, Yulian Salo, Roman Vavryk, Oleksii Pelekh, Nazariy Dykiy
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The research paper presents a comprehensive geographic information model that could handle analysis of forest lands within a defined geographical area and time boundaries; developed software tools for the further similar research of different geographical areas and time dimensions. The purpose of the research is to analyze forest lands within the targeted geographical combat zone throughout a given time dimension using the methods and means of remote sensing, machine learning, and big data. The input research data consist of Sentinel 2 and Landsat 8 satellite image collections, spectral bands data; NASA FIRMS compiled annual data on the total number of fires in Ukraine.

title: Method for Improving Cache Memory Utilization in Multilayer Edge–Fog–Cloud Infrastructures
authors: Olha Shpur, Marian Seliuchenko, Mykhailo Klymash, Nadia Seliuchenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents a method for improving cache memory utilization in multilayer Edge–Fog–Cloud infrastructures. The proposed method centralizes shared cached content at local Edge node caches and adaptively relocates it to the Fog layer when Edge computational resources become overloaded. Simulation-based evaluation is conducted for two scenarios: cache optimization at the Edge layer and shared cache offloading to the Fog layer under limited Edge resources. The results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly reduces cache data duplication, decreases Edge service memory usage by 36–42%, and lowers the computational load on Edge services. In addition, the approach ensures stable quality of service (QoS) with end-to-end latency below 200 ms. Cache redistribution at the Fog layer enables efficient resource balancing between Edge and Fog, reduces network congestion, and supports shared data reuse across multiple services. Overall, the proposed approach shows practical applicability for streaming microservice applications in multilayer Edge–Fog–Cloud environments, achieving low latency and efficient utilization of constrained resources.

title: A Comparative Forensic Analysis of Unit 26165 and Unit 74455 Cyber Operations on It and OT Environments Using 2S Matrix Framework
authors: Myo Myint Maw, Puttinun Patpituck, Krishna Chimmanee, Naughtakid Phromchan
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative forensic analysis of two Russian state-linked threat groups - Unit 26165 (APT28) and Unit 74455 (Sandworm) - across six publicly documented incidents spanning information-technology (IT) and operational-technology (OT) environments. This research examines and compares IT versus OT escalation pathways of the two entities. MITRE ATT&CK (Enterprise and ICS) is used to normalize technique-level evidence. The 2S Matrix Framework is applied to capture operational tempo (Surface/Spread vs Stealth/Strike) and reconstruct attack pathways. The analysis shows that APT28 operates as an identity-centric espionage actor, emphasizing credential access and mailbox/OAuth governance to sustain low-noise collection, whereas Sandworm coordinates protocol-aware actions (e.g., IEC-104/61850 command messages), scheduled triggers, and recovery inhibitors to produce physical effects in OT. We distill phaseaware detection points for each domain: in enterprise, rule and token governance, outbound SMB controls, and identity telemetry; in OT, protocol allow-listing at master stations, relay/workflow safeguards, and monitoring for timed command sequences. The findings reveal distinct differences in targeting, persistence techniques, and strategic objectives, offering a deeper understanding of Russian gangs' cyber-attacks on IT and OT systems and their impact on critical infrastructures.

title: Enhancing Sport Activity Recognition Based on Wearable Sensors Using Hybrid Deep Neural Networks
authors: Sakorn Mekruksavanich, Narit Hnoohom, Anuchit Jitpattanakul
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) is increasingly important in sports science and fitness for real-time monitoring of athlete performance. This paper proposes a hybrid deep learning architecture for sport activity recognition using wearable sensor data. The model integrates convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for automatic spatial feature extraction with bidirectional gated recurrent units (BiGRUs) to model temporal dynamics, enabling effective learning of complex sport movements. Multisensor fusion of tri-axial accelerometer data from wrist, neck, and thigh placements is employed to capture comprehensive motion patterns. The proposed CNN-BiGRU framework is evaluated on the publicly available IM-Sporting Behaviors dataset, which includes six sport activities performed by 20 subjects. Experimental results demonstrate superior performance over conventional machine learning methods and baseline deep models. The approach achieves accuracies of 99.83 % for wrist-based recognition, 99.83% for neck-based recognition, and 100.00% for thigh-based recognition, with corresponding <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{F 1}$</tex>-scores of 99.78 %, 99.77 %, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0. 0 0 \%}$</tex>, respectively. Low performance variance across all experiments indicates strong robustness and generalization. Ablation studies further confirm that the hybrid architecture significantly outperforms standalone CNN and BiGRU models, validating the effectiveness of jointly learning spatial and temporal representations for sport activity recognition.

title: Triple Band Folded Dipole Antenna for Sub-6 GHz, Radar and Satellite Applications
authors: Ramesh Deshpande, Usha Devi Yalavarthi, Penmetsa Sriya, G. Varshini, Vedanth Deshpande, Satwik Joshi
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The proposed research presents the design and analysis of a compact folded dipole antenna optimized for tripleband operation. The antenna features three vertically aligned radiating arms connected to a central feed line, with integrated slots and rounded tips to enhance impedance matching, efficiency, and current distribution. The dimensions and geometrical details of the design are meticulously optimized to achieve resonance at multiple frequency bands: 3.65–4.51 GHz, 5.56–6.25 GHz, and 7.68–10.32 GHz. These bands make the antenna suitable for applications in sub-6 GHz 5G systems, radar, and satellite communication. The surface current distribution analysis reveals distinct radiation patterns at each resonance, with varying contributions from the radiating arms across frequencies. The antenna demonstrates a peak gain of 5.7 dB at 4.2 GHz and 7.55 dB at 8.3 GHz, with corresponding radiation efficiency ranging from 50 to 89 percent. The E-plane and H-plane radiation patterns exhibit directional and omnidirectional characteristics, respectively, depending on the operating frequency. The proposed antenna design combines compactness, multi-band capability, and reliable performance, making it a promising solution for modern wireless communication and sensing applications.

title: CM-SolarTS: A Chiang Mai Solar Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Dataset for Time Series Analysis
authors: Watcharin Sarachai, Chirawan Ronran
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Forecasting photovoltaic power is essential for ensuring grid reliability and enabling the smooth integration of renewable energy sources. While prior research emphasizes neural network architecture design, this study introduces a dedicated photovoltaic dataset and systematically examines the impact of temporal feature engineering strategies on forecast accuracy across different model families. We evaluate four encoding strate-gies-raw baseline, datetime categorical, cyclical, and lag features-using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Autoregressive LSTM (AR-LSTM). Results reveal a model-dependent relationship: CNN achieves the highest explanatory power (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2} \approx 0.85$</tex>) but with higher MAE, whereas AR-LSTM with cyclical encoding delivers the most accurate predictions (MAE <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\approx 0.0392$</tex>). Interestingly, LSTM and ARLSTM exhibit negative <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> values, highlighting the limitations of this metric for time-series tasks. These findings challenge the assumption that complex feature engineering universally improves performance and provide practical guidelines for selecting encoding strategies based on model architecture and operational objectives.

title: The E-Sports in the Education — Advantages, Disadvantages, Opportunities and Challenges
authors: Plamen Zahariev, Georgi Hristov, Petar Stoilov, Georgi Georgiev, Ivan Beloev, Silvia Beloeva
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: In the last few years, the e-sports have enjoyed a significant rise in popularity. This phenomenon can be explained with the constant technological advancements in the area of the computer and gaming technologies, which are becoming increasingly more realistic, but also more challenging, as well as with the better user access to high-speed Internet and the growing popularity of the online streaming platforms, through which the gamers are promoting their activities, gaming sessions and achievements. In this study, we present the advantages from the utilization of the e-sports in the education and how they can develop the problem-solving abilities and the teamwork capabilities of the students, as well as help improve their communicational skills and the level of their digital literacy. Despite these advantages, the esports also face many challenges, such as the general disapproval and misunderstanding of the ideology behind them, their potential negative impact on the health of the players, etc.

title: Multivariate Intrinsic Time-Scale Decomposition for Characterizing Student Engagement from Physiological Signals
authors: Wanus Srimaharaj, Rupali Dalvi
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Assessing student engagement is necessary for developing adaptive learning environments. This study examines the use of Multivariate Intrinsic Time-Scale Decomposition (MITD) to analyze multimodal physiological data for engagement classification. A dataset containing nine physiological signals, including five electroencephalography (EEG) frequency bands and four eye-tracking metrics, was used. The MITD method was applied to decompose the multivariate signals into shared oscillatory components, from which Multivariate Energy and Multivariate Sample Entropy (mSampEn) were extracted as features. The results indicate that a state of high engagement is characterized by higher Multivariate Energy and lower mSampEn. The differences between low, medium, and high engagement groups were statistically significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(p<0.001)$</tex> for both features. In a comparative analysis, the MITD-derived features achieved a mean classification accuracy of 97.33 %, which was higher than the accuracies obtained using features from Principal Component Analysis (0.8400) and Multivariate Empirical Mode Decomposition (0.8900). The findings show that MITD is an effective method for extracting discriminative features from combined physiological signals to assess student engagement. The approach can be applied to develop systems for monitoring cognitive states in educational settings.

title: Online Trajectory Optimization for Arbitrary-Shaped Mobile Robots via Polynomial Separating Hypersurfaces
authors: Shuoye Li, Zhiyuan Song, Yulin Li, Zhihai Bi, Jun Ma
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: An emerging class of trajectory optimization methods enforces collision avoidance by jointly optimizing the robot's configuration and a separating hyperplane. However, as linear separators only apply to convex sets, these methods require convex approximations of both the robot and obstacles, which becomes an overly conservative assumption in cluttered and narrow environments. In this work, we unequivocally remove this limitation by introducing nonlinear separating hypersurfaces parameterized by polynomial functions. We first generalize the classical separating hyperplane theorem and prove that any two disjoint bounded closed sets in Euclidean space can be separated by a polynomial hypersurface, serving as the theoretical foundation for nonlinear separation of arbitrary geometries. Building on this result, we formulate a nonlinear programming (NLP) problem that jointly optimizes the robot's trajectory and the coefficients of the separating polynomials, enabling geometry-aware collision avoidance without conservative convex simplifications. The optimization remains efficiently solvable using standard NLP solvers. Simulation and real-world experiments with nonconvex robots demonstrate that our method achieves smooth, collision-free, and agile maneuvers in environments where convex-approximation baselines fail.

title: IIR-BinNet: An Ultra-lightweight Neural Network for Document Image Binarization Using IIR Filters
authors: Daria Ershova, Alexander Gayer, Vladimir V. Arlazarov
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Document image binarization task requires neural networks that can effectively analyze large image areas to separate text from complex backgrounds. The traditional approach of expanding the receptive field by increasing the depth of the network results in a significant increase in computational complexity and model size. The latter is most critical for mobile devices and web applications, where speed and size are of paramount importance. To address this problem, we propose IIR-BinNet, a novel ultra-lightweight neural network architecture that leverages multidirectional learnable Infinite Impulse Response (IIR) filters. IIR layers allow efficient multi-scale feature extraction based on different receptive fields provided by each filter within the same IIR layer. The proposed approach was tested in the task of binarization of complex images of historical documents, which requires understanding of the global context. With only 49K parameters, our model is at least 40 times smaller than U-Net-based solutions while maintaining competitive performance on the DIBCO 2017 and H-DIBCO 2018 benchmarks. The results show that IIR-BinNet effectively replaces deep convolutional stacks with a block of learnable multidirectional IIR filters, enabling competitive performance among lightweight approaches on DIBCO 2017 and stronger results on H-DIBCO 2018 within the same class of models. On the H-DIBCO 2018, IIR-BinNet achieves an F-measure of 90.37%, compared to 89.36% for the baseline U-Net. Among lightweight specialized models evaluated on H-DIBCO 2018, it achieves the best reported performance with a 35% reduction in DRD score.
