title: Geometric and Information-Theoretic Evaluation of Multi-View Point Cloud Datasets
authors: Radostina Petkova, Ivaylo Bozhilov, Krasimir Tonchev, Agata Manolova, Vladimir Poulkov
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: As point cloud data become increasingly common in learning-based 3D perception, the quality and structure of the datasets used for training and evaluation play a central role in model performance and generalization. In multi-view point cloud datasets, viewpoint selection, coverage, and redundancy directly affect the amount of information available to learning algorithms. This paper presents a framework for multi-view point cloud dataset evaluation based on geometric and information-theoretic metrics. The proposed framework operates in both point-based and voxel-based representations. Experimental results show that point-based and voxel-based formulations capture complementary aspects of multi-view dataset structure, supporting the use of both for comprehensive evaluation.

title: Two-Element Wideband CP Meta-Surface MIMO Antenna With H-Plane Coupling Suppression
authors: Nathapat Supreeyatitikul, Kittisak Phaebua, Chuwong Phongcharoenpanich, Khwanlada Kakhong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This work presents a novel wideband circularly polarized (CP) multiple-input-multiple-output (MIMO) antenna scheme featuring a two-cluster meta-surface structure with integrated shorting-pin vias, specifically designed for nextgeneration Internet of Things (IoT) applications. The proposed antenna consists of two substrate layers: the upper layer accommodates the 12 corner-truncated square meta-surface elements, while the lower layer implements a dual-element coplanar waveguide (CPW) feeding network. A strategically positioned shorting-pin via, acting as a magnetic wall, is incorporated between clusters to suppress H-plane coupling. The antenna prototype achieves a measured return loss bandwidth (RLBW) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 6. 7 2 \%} \boldsymbol{(} \mathbf{4. 2}-\mathbf{6. 7 7 ~ G H z} \boldsymbol{)}$</tex>, an axial ratio bandwidth (ARBW) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11 \%(5.29-5.9 \text{GHz})$</tex>, and a peak realized gain of 5.82 dBic at 5.5 GHz. Furthermore, the design exhibits excellent isolation (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>25 ~\text{dB}$</tex>), an envelope correlation coefficient (ECC) below 0.002, and a diversity gain (DG) exceeding 9.99 dB. These results affirm the effectiveness and suitability of the proposed antenna scheme for IoT communication systems.

title: Retraction Notice: Faux Reality Detector
authors: Shakthi Kiran R, Sugumar A, Vishnuprabhu R, Sanjeeva Kumar M, Sreemathy J
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: With the advent of a new era in digital content creation, deep learning and generative models have completely changed the way we interact with multimedia. Strong generative adversarial networks (GANs) have been developed, but this has led to grave worries about the potential for malevolent usage. Deep fakes, which are produced by GAN and are hyper-realistic digital forgeries that might be used to spread incorrect information and fool people, pose a serious threat to civilization. As a result, developing reliable and effective deep fake detection algorithms has become an important study area. The major goal of this project is to create and deploy a deep fake detection system that can accurately identify between authentic and false images. Such fake faces can be recognized using a deep learning architecture based on CNNs (Convolutional Neural Networks). Such fake faces can be identified with practically perfect accuracy using a deep learning architecture based on CNNs (Convolutional Neural Networks). based on the fact that the textures of real faces and synthetic ones are very different. The comprehensive research and analysis carried out to understand the fundamental concepts underlying deep fake generation and detection are described in this paper.

title: An Efficient Deep Learning Model With Integrated Inception Module for Elderly People Activity Recognition Using Wearable Sensors
authors: Sakorn Mekruksavanich, Anuchit Jitpattanakul
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The rapid increase in the global elderly population has heightened the need for intelligent monitoring systems that ensure safety and well-being through reliable activity recognition. This paper proposes an efficient deep learning framework that combines Inception modules with a residual network to recognize daily activities of elderly individuals using wearable sensor data. The proposed Inception-ResNet-HAR model exploits multi-scale feature extraction alongside residual learning to capture complex temporal characteristics in raw accelerometer signals, eliminating the need for manual feature engineering. Experimental results on the HAR70+ benchmark dataset demonstrate that the proposed approach consistently outperforms existing deep learning models, achieving accuracies ranging from 93.87 % to 97.41 % across different sensor configurations. The model shows strong robustness across subjects and maintains computational efficiency suitable for real-time deployment, highlighting its potential for practical elderly monitoring and assisted living applications.

title: The Creation of Contemporary Lanna Folklife Art Using Artificial Intelligence (AI)
authors: Nop Kongdee, Somkeit Noamna, Chalermpol Kongjit, Manissaward Jintapotak, Porntida Kaewkamol, Maeva Israel
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study explores the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to conserve and reinterpret Lanna folklife art from three northern Thai communities: Ban Thi, Ban Chaem, and Luang Nuea. Using generative AI tools such as Stable Diffusion and Lora, local visual patterns were digitized, trained, and applied to new contemporary designs while retaining traditional motifs. The research presents a creative process combining qualitative research and technological experimentation. Results reveal that AI enhances the preservation and reinterpretation of local art, creating a sustainable bridge between heritage and innovation.

title: FPGA-Based Hardware and Software Architecture for Accelerated Text Processing
authors: Tetiana Holub, Iryna Zeleneva, Galyna Tabunshchyk, Anzhelika Parkhomenko, Svitlana Hrushko, Carsten Wolff
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: In the modern process of sustainable development of digital infrastructure and smart city concepts, one of the pressing challenges is the use of hardware accelerators for data processing. This particularly applies to the processing of large volumes of natural language text information. An automated system for comparing and classifying such data, for example, may be required for large libraries, archives, product catalogs, etc. The software solution to this problem does not always satisfy the speed requirements to information processing. In this case, the use of accelerated hardware data processing units in addition to the software is a good solution. In this research, a hardware and software system for accelerated text classification using FPGA as an accelerator was developed. The system is designed for use in libraries and archives. Tests were carried out in the city library, and results of the experiments show the possibility to achieve multiple (about ten times on the final step, using FPGA) acceleration of the text classification process.

title: Real-Time LED Defect Detection Using YOLOv7-12 for Smart Manufacturing
authors: Worathep Kongkaew, Orasa Sirasakamol, Samart Yachiangkam, Wichai Sukprasertkul, Narong Mettripun, Surangpimol Chantawong
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study compares the performance of multiple YOLO models YOLOv7, YOLOv8, YOLOv9e, YOLOv10x, YOLOv11i, YOLOv12n, and YOLOv12x for automated defect inspection in LED lamp manufacturing using artificial intelligence (AI) and image processing. A total of 1,196 real-world images were collected from the production line and categorized into three classes: OK (normal), NG (defective), and UNKN (uncertain). The dataset was divided into training, validation, and testing sets in an 80:10:10 ratio and trained using the PyTorch framework. Experimental results show that YOLOv8 achieved the highest overall performance, with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 0.943, F1-score of 0.910, precision of 1, and recall of 0.970, while YOLOv10x provided the best trade-off between accuracy and inference speed. These findings emphasize the importance of selecting an appropriate YOLO architecture based on industrial requirements and highlight the potential of YOLOv8 for real-time LED defect detection in automated manufacturing environments.

title: Detecting Rug Pull Risks in Cryptocurrency Projects on the Binance Smart Chain Using Machine Learning
authors: Tipwadee Leala, Krist Thamniyom, Thawatchai Chomsiri
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Cryptocurrency investments have grown exponentially, but the rapid expansion of decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystems has been accompanied by the rise of sophisticated fraud schemes, particularly Rug Pulls. These scams occur when developers deliberately withdraw liquidity or sell large amounts of tokens, leaving investors with worthless assets. This research presents a machine learning-based framework for detecting rug-pull-prone projectson the Binance Smart Chain (BSC). A comprehensive dataset was constructed by aggregating transactional and smart contract features from reliable sources such as BscScan, TokenSniffer, DEXTools, and PeckShield Alerts. Data preprocessing included handling missing values, removing duplicates, detecting and mitigating outliers, and addressing severe class imbalance using Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE). Seven machine learning algorithms were compared: Decision Tree (DT), Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting (GB), Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP). The top-performing models, Random Forest and XGBoost, were further validated using stratified holdout testing. Results demonstrate that XGBoost achieved the highest overall performance <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{F1} = 0.82,\ \text{ROC-AUC} = 0.90,\ \text{PR-AUC} = 0.994)$</tex> confirming the model's robustness in identifying fraudulent patterns. This approach offers a scalable framework for blockchain fraud detection on BSC, with potential applicability to other networks such as Ethereum and Polygon.

title: Research on Marketing Strategies for Promoting Thai Food Through the Xiaohongshu Platform
authors: Duan Yi, Sirikorn Santirojanakul
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: With the rapid growth of cross-border e-commerce and social media, promoting Thai food in the Chinese market has become increasingly dependent on digital marketing platforms. This study aims to explore effective marketing strategies for promoting Thai food through the Xiaohongshu platform. A mixed-methods research design was employed, combining quantitative surveys of 451 Xiaohongshu users and qualitative interviews with three Xiaohongshu users (from consumer perspective), three Key Opinion Consumers (KOCs), and two Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs). The results indicate that users' familiarity with Thai food, frequency of platform usage, and engagement with influencer content significantly affect purchase intentions. Specifically, short-video UGC (User-Generated Content) and Key Opinion Consumer (KOC) content outperform other formats in building trust and driving conversions. This study provides practical insights for Thai food brands seeking to improve their digital marketing performance and enhance brand visibility in China, while also enriching the theoretical framework of crossborder social commerce marketing. The study concludes that visually appealing short-video UGC and authentic KOC storytelling are the most influential factors driving Chinese consumers' purchase intention toward Thai food. These findings provide practical and theoretical insights for enhancing cross-border social commerce marketing effectiveness.

title: Integrating AI-Generated Content with Collaborative Learning in STEM Education
authors: Khin Kyawt Shinn, Nang Hsu Mon Pyae, Thiri Swe Zin, Yoon Moh Moh Aung
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This case study examines the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into the Software Engineering Program in higher education. It explores how combining AI-generated content with traditional constructivist learning practices influences the students' learning experiences. A blended learning framework was implemented at Mae Fah Luang University, where 108 students were surveyed with a structured questionnaire, designed to measure three key variables: content, collaboration, and adaptability. Quantitative analyses were conducted to examine relationships between these variables. The findings illustrate that while AI-generated content strengthens engagement, offline collaborative activities remain essential for developing professional adaptability skills.

title: Waveform Design With Holistic Utility-Driven Optimization for LEO Satellite Integrated Communication and Navigation
authors: Shihao Wu, Zhaoqi Wang, Wanshun Lan, Yafei Zhao, Xiqing Liu, Yuanwei Liu, Mugen Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: The burgeoning demand for seamless integration of high-rate communication and precise positioning is catalyzing the development of integrated communication and navigation (ICAN) systems, particularly leveraging low Earth orbit satellite constellations. Existing studies predominantly rely on the functional superposition of conventional communication or navigation waveforms and metrics, failing to address the inherent performance trade-off. To bridge this gap, this work proposes an integrated waveform assisted by orthogonal frequency division multiplexing and Gold code. Binary selection and diagonal loading matrices are introduced to enable the joint allocation of subcarriers and power among communication data, pilot signals, and navigation chips, thereby simultaneously ensuring high spectral efficiency and superior ranging resolution. Furthermore, we define a convex and differentiable utility metric, incorporating a preference weighting parameter to flexibly balance the emphasis between communication and navigation. A successive convex Pareto algorithm is developed to obtain the optimal resource allocation scheme and trace the Pareto frontier. Simulation results demonstrate the advantage of our proposal which offers a versatile solution for ICAN services.

title: Nut-Driven Transmission Force Controllable Motion Platform for Suppressing Low-Frequency and Impulsive Base Disturbances
authors: Dasol Cheon, Sehoon Oh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a transmission-force controllable actuator (TFCA) for vibration suppression in linear motion platforms. Unlike conventional screw-driven systems, the nut-driven TFCA integrates a compliant spring and encoder to measure and control the transmission force in real time, thereby reducing reflected inertia, increasing stiffness, and enabling rapid response to external disturbances. Dynamic models were derived, and two control strategies—acceleration-based vibration suppression control (AVSC) and transmission-force and ACSC (TAVSC)—were implemented. Experiments using a base-shaker setup evaluated three scenarios: back-drivability, impulse disturbance, and multisine excitation (0.1–100 Hz). Results show that TAVSC reduced acceleration and jerk by over 50% in the time domain and achieved more than 90% suppression in the 0.5–8 Hz band, while AVSC exhibited only marginal improvement. Under multisine excitation, TAVSC maintained more than 5 dB suppression across the spectrum and 86% reduction in the 0.5–8 Hz band. These findings confirm that transmission-force feedback substantially improves vibration suppression against impulsive and low-frequency base disturbances, demonstrating the potential of TFCA for applications such as ambulance stretchers and human-sensitive transport systems.

title: Smoothed analysis in compressed sensing
authors: Elad Aigner-Horev, Dan Hefetz, Michael Trushkin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Theory
abstract: Arbitrary matrices M ∈ Rm×n, randomly perturbed in an additive manner using a random matrix R ∈ Rm×n, are shown to asymptotically almost surely satisfy the so-called robust null space property. Whilst insisting on an asymptotically optimal order of magnitude for m required to attain unique reconstruction via ℓ1-minimisation algorithms, our results track the level of arbitrariness allowed for the fixed seed matrix M as well as the degree of distributional irregularity allowed for the entries of the perturbing matrix R. Starting with sub-gaussian entries for R, our results culminate with these allowed to have substantially heavier tails than sub-exponential ones. Throughout this trajectory, two measures control the arbitrariness allowed for M; the first is ∥M∥∞ and the second is a localised notion of the Frobenius norm of M (which depends on the sparsity of the signal being reconstructed). A key tool driving our proofs is Mendelson’s small-ball method (Learning without concentration, J. ACM, Vol. 62, 2015).

title: Identity between Bounded Real and Positive Real Characteristic Values through the Scattering Relation
authors: Adriano Scibilia, Giovanni Garraffa, Qiang Lai, Luigi Fortuna
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, new results regarding the identity that occurs between the characteristic values of the scattering matrix S(s) and the characteristic values of the corresponding impedance matrix Z(s) are provided. The results allow us to obtain reduced-order models with similar approximation degree, considering both the approximation of S(s) and of the model Z(s). Even if the computation of the impedance matrix is more immediate, in many cases, the scattering systems S(s) are considered; therefore, in order to avoid different information degradation between the two reduced-order systems, they must be consistent. Numerical examples regarding both electrical networks and multi-port mechanical systems are discussed.

title: The Importance of Key Parameters in Federated Learning for Cross-Device Scenarios: An In-depth Performance Evaluation
authors: Fotios Zantalis, Stylianos Katsoulis, Effie G. Papageorgiou, Grigorios E. Koulouras
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) in cross-device scenarios presents unique challenges due to data heterogeneity and varying hardware capabilities among clients. This study emphasizes the critical role of key parameters, specifically, epoch size, cohort size, and learning rates, in the performance of FL algorithms. A comprehensive evaluation of five prominent FL algorithms, FedAvg, FedProx, FedMimeLite, FedAdam, and FedYogi, was conducted to investigate how these parameters influence convergence speed, communication efficiency, and model accuracy. Experiments on the EMNIST and Shakespeare datasets reveal that careful tuning of epoch and cohort sizes significantly impacts performance, with notable correlations between these parameters and the algorithms’ behavior. The findings highlight that while adaptive optimizers and momentum can enhance algorithmic performance, their effectiveness is highly dependent on the selected parameter settings. This in-depth analysis provides valuable insights for researchers and practitioners, facilitating informed decision-making in the selection and tuning of FL algorithms for cross-device environments.

title: Transformer encoder driven deep learning network for single lead ECG arrhythmia’s across 21,837 records
authors: Sureshkumar Sundaramoorthy, Govardhan Karunanidhi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Cardiovascular disease is often characterised by cardiac arrhythmia, which is a major contributor to global morbidity and mortality statistics. Accurate and timely arrhythmia detection is therefore critical for effective clinical intervention. We developed a transformer based hybrid deep learning model that efficiently classifies arrhythmia’s from single lead ECG data signal by integrating the CNN, transformer encoder, and channel attention strategy. A four layer CNN is employed within the framework for extracting local morphological features, attention strategy for emphasizing clinically salient patterns. In parallel, a multi-head self attention based transformer encoder to capture long range temporal dependencies, that complementing convolutional features. Furthermore, to address class imbalance and enhance predictive performance on minority classes, the SMOTE-Tomek hybrid resampling strategy is employed during training. The proposed network is rigorously evaluated on V6-single lead ECG recordings from PTB-XL dataset. For diagnostic superclasses, the network achieves an accuracy of 97.71%, sensitivity of 93.91%, specificity of 98.51%, and a macro F1-score of 92.69%, resulting in a significant increase of nearly 4.7% in accuracy and 6.9% in macro F1-score compared to conventional CNN-based models, and around 4.8% accuracy over LSTM based and other hybrid models reported in the recent state of the art discussed in this work. For fine grained classification across 22 diagnostic subclasses, the proposed model achieves an accuracy of 98.67%, sensitivity of 83.95%, specificity of 98.89%, and a macro F1-score of 76.43%, outperforming recent baseline CNN and other advanced hybrid models. These results demonstrate that the proposed hybrid model architecture effectively balances local feature extraction and global temporal modeling, resulting in robust classification performance and making it well suited for real time arrhythmia detection in both remote monitoring and clinical settings.

title: A Blockchain-Based Approach to Enhancing Cybersecurity in Operational Technology Systems in the Oil and Gas Industry
authors: Abimbola A. Ogunlade, Kalum P. Udagepola, Akhlaqur Rahman
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Recently, the global oil and gas industry has experienced a sharp increase in cybersecurity-related incidents targeting critical operational technology (OT) infrastructure, with attacks in the last five years demonstrating increased sophistication, causing significant production losses, financial damage, equipment destruction, and fatalities. Traditional information technology security solutions are inadequate in operational technology environments, often exacerbating vulnerabilities by expanding the attack surface instead of mitigating threats. This study addresses this critical gap by designing and implementing a hybrid blockchain framework that integrates public and private blockchain characteristics for seamless integration with the existing OT cybersecurity infrastructure, specifically targeting the prevention of incidents leading to plant upsets, costly shutdowns, explosions, and loss of life. Through rigorous experimental validation across four operational scenarios, the developed prototype utilizing the Quorum Byzantine Fault Tolerance (QBFT) consensus mechanism achieved 92% precision and 86% recall for malicious event classification. Precision and recall quantify the classification accuracy of the evaluated events, whereas the detection rate reported in the scenario analysis represents the detection coverage (detection relative to the total number of write attempts). Under a sustained adversarial load (Scenario 4), the framework improved the detection coverage from 27.35% baseline (Scenario 1) to 57.33%. A chi-square test (x2 = 4709.69, df = 3, N = 38,231, p < 0.001) showed that detection outcomes differed significantly across the four scenarios, indicating that detection performance was strongly dependent on the scenario configurations defined by the proposed framework. The framework achieved process-control-compatible latencies ranging from 24 to 368 ms while using a minimal network bandwidth of 8.15 KB/s. This demonstrates its practical applicability for real-time industrial use and suggests potential annual financial savings of $0.973 billion to $1.99 billion, based on recent analyses of ransomware incidents.

title: Transformer-Based Multi-Modal Fusion for Complex Wall Parameter Distribution Estimation
authors: Xiaopeng Yang, Peng Wang, Xiaolu Zeng, Zixiang Yin, Yuxin Miao
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: Through-wall radar (TWR) plays a pivotal role in non-intrusive detection of buildings because of its excellent penetrability. Many methods have been developed to estimate the parameters of walls and perform wall compensation for accurate through-wall imaging results. However, most existing studies are based on uniform wall models that cannot be generalized to actual building walls with complex structures such as doors, pillars and windows, nor can they quickly generate a parameter distribution map of the entire wall. To solve this problem, this paper proposes an innovative segment-then-estimate framework that first fuses LiDAR point clouds and optical images with simultaneous localization and mapping (SLAM) to reconstruct a 3D exterior wall model, thereby guiding the precise division of radar B-scan data along the slow-time dimension. Then, we introduce a Transformer-based parameter estimation model for the echo data of each segment, leveraging its self-attention mechanism to capture long-range dependencies and deep features to accurately estimate the wall thickness and relative permittivity. Finally, segment-based estimates are stitched using geometric information obtained from multi-modal reconstruction to generate the wall parameter distribution map. Experimental results demonstrate that the wall parameter estimation maps generated by the proposed framework in this study not only achieve highly accurate parameter estimation values but also exhibit distinct door and window boundaries, providing reliable prior information for highly refined compensated imaging.

title: Internet of Drones System for Real-Time Wireless Water Quality Sensing, 2-D Mapping, 3-D Depth Profiling, and Intelligent Sampling
authors: Soheyl Faghir Hagh, Parmida Amngostar, Dylan Burns, Renato Figueiredo, Yun-Jung Ku, Jacob Cianci-Gaskill, Steven E. McMurray, Dryver Huston, Tian Xia
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This article presents an Internet-of-Drones (IoD)-enabled system for real-time, high-resolution, in-situ water quality sensing, sampling, 2-D mapping, and 3-D depth profiling from discrete sensing. Unlike isolated mooring platforms and stationary sensor buoys, our Uncrewed Aerial Vehicle (UAV) platform features a modular IoD architecture with wireless communication for adaptive sampling, parameter mapping, and real-time monitoring. The system integrates pH, temperature, turbidity, total dissolved solids (TDS), and depth sensors within a single-actuator multi-cartridge vessel that collects samples via TDS, depth, and ML-based triggers into four 50mL tubes. The proposed embedded system incorporates LoRa/LoRaWAN for low-power telemetry and LTE for wireless data transmission, with SD card logging, GPS geo-tagging, and Real-time Clock (RTC) synchronization. A high-resolution 3-D interpolation framework is implemented that reconstructs water quality fields from UAV-based missions over a 0.8m × 0.8m × 1m volume and a comprehensive 2-D mapping over a 180m × 180m area. The system incorporates a smart sampling logic based on predefined thresholds (e.g., TDS and depth triggers), along with a machine–learning–assisted mode for adaptive chlorophyll-a detection and real-time decision-making. Comprehensive field tests at the Lake Erie digital test bed validate system performance.

title: ODGNet: Learning Dynamic Variable Association for Online Time Series Forecasting_supp1-3679682.pdf
authors: Kai Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Time series forecasting is integral to diverse fields, significantly influencing both industrial production and human activities. In the real world, time-series data often exists in the form of streaming data and its distribution often changes over time, which is referred to as concept drift. This results in a gradual decline in the performance of traditional deep learning models as time progresses. Current online learning methods attempt to alleviate this issue by employing online adaptation methods in temporal dimension. However, they overlook the changes and drifts in the inter-variable association within time series. To this end, we propose a novel Online Dynamic Graph Network (ODGNet). ODGNet represents variable associations as a matrix polynomial and acquires polynomial coefficients based on online gradients, which models the evolutionary trends of spatial patterns. Furthermore, we emphasize the lossless information mapping between the adjacency matrix and its corresponding polynomial coefficient. Based on this mapping, a graph memory module with low memory consumption is proposed to avoid catastrophic forgetting when graph drift occurs. And a graph drift awareness mechanism is designed to rapidly detect graph drift. Experimental results demonstrate that ODGNet achieves a significant reduction in forecasting error compared to the existing online learning methods on twelve benchmark datasets. The code is available at https://github.com/LiuYasuo/ODGNet.

title: Multi-Stage Prediction CNN: Fast CU Partition for VVC Screen Content Coding
authors: Jing Chen, Jiaxin Zeng, Huanqiang Zeng, Xudong Zhang, Qi Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Multimedia
abstract: Versatile Video Coding Screen Content Coding (VVC-SCC), as the latest screen content coding standard, improves the encoding efficiency significantly while increasing the computational complexity. In VVC-SCC, the exhaustive search for recursive Rate Distortion (RD) optimization, especially the Quad-Tree plus Multi-Type Tree (QTMTT) structure for Coding Unit (CU) partitioning, occupies most of the encoding time. Therefore, this paper proposes a method to reduce the encoding complexity of VVC-SCC from the perspective of CU partitioning. To predict various stages of CU partitioning, a Multi-Stage Prediction Convolutional Neural Network (MSP-CNN) model is designed. A comprehensive dataset containing partition modes and RD costs corresponding to all CU sizes is constructed to train the MSP-CNN model. The model outputs the probabilities of all possible partition modes for the current CU by analyzing encoded frames, thus obtaining the partition result for all CUs in the current frame. Considering that prediction network errors may seriously affect the RD performance of the encoder, two modes are designed to balance encoding complexity and RD performance by utilizing multi-stage thresholds and adaptive thresholds based on screen content edge features. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reduces the intra coding time by 46.86% while maintaining the encoding quality, which outperforms the state-of-the-art fast CU partitioning methods for VVC-SCC.

title: Modulation Design for Integrated SWIPT Receivers with OFDM-based Waveforms
authors: Maria Dimitropoulou, Christos N. Efrem, Constantinos Psomas, Ioannis Krikidis
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel simultaneous wireless information and power transfer (SWIPT) technique that employs orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM)-based waveforms. The proposed scheme jointly designs particular characteristics of the waveform, in such a way as to enable efficient energy harvesting (EH) and low-complexity information transmission. Specifically, information is embedded into two dimensions by modulating the peak-to-average-power-ratio and the position of the power peak. The receiver relies on an integrated circuit architecture that utilizes a low-complexity envelope detector for the information decoding process. We propose two low-complexity suboptimal detection rules and additionally consider maximum likelihood detection. The performance of the proposed technique is approximately evaluated in terms of the symbol error probability and average harvested power. We show that the tradeoff between rate and energy in terms of parameters that arises can be controlled accordingly to achieve diverse requirements in terms of EH and/or information transfer. The presented approach transforms conventional OFDM transmitters to SWIPT devices without costly architectural modifications, thus making it appropriate for practical applications.

title: A Robust 13.56MHz Wireless Power Transmission System For Implantable Cardiac Devices
authors: Shuoliang Ding, Qiang Liu, Mengxi Geng, Zixin Wang, Yifan Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper proposes a novel miniaturized wireless power transmission system designed for deep implantation in human hearts. Utilizing mid-range magnetic resonance coupling, which is more suitable than radiative methods for deep implantation scenarios, the system comprises an external circular transmitter with a radius of 79 mm and four rectangular receivers measuring 7 mm × 20 mm, matching the size of clinical leadless pacemakers such as Micra. This configuration enhances robustness against physiological motions such as respiration, heartbeat, and minor body movements. Stability is achieved through a 4-coupled sub-receivers design validated by theoretical calculations, with performance confirmed via simulations and experimental measurements. Distinct from the existing systems, this design operates efficiently in extremely deep implantation sites, maintains stability against rotation or misalignment, and balances miniaturization with high power transfer efficiency (PTE). Both simulation and experimental results demonstrate the high PTE and robustness of this proposed system.

title: Broadband Self-Diplexing Quarter-Mode Substrate Integrated Waveguide Antenna
authors: Amjad Iqbal, Nazih Khaddaj Mallat, Tayeb A. Denidni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this work, a compact quarter-mode substrate integrated waveguide (QMSIW) antenna is proposed for self-diplexing applications. The antenna operates at 7.5 GHz (C-band) and 9.85 GHz (X-band), providing wide impedance bandwidths of 590 MHz (7.18–7.77 GHz) and 630 MHz (9.54–10.17 GHz), respectively. The broadband response in each band is realized by coupling two QMSIW resonators. In the main resonator, the TM110 mode is excited, while in the parasitic resonator, a perturbed TM110 mode is induced. Proper coupling between the resonators enables wideband performance, with the bandwidth tunable through the adjustment of the gap between them. The antenna features a very compact footprint of 15.5 × 15.5 × 0.78 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, corresponding to 0.009λ<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup><sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub> (where λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</sub> is calculated at 7.5 GHz). This miniaturization is achieved through the use of capacitive slots within the QMSIW resonators. The design ensures high isolation, exceeding 22.6 dB across both operating bands. Moreover, it achieves peak gains of 8.5 dBi at 7.5 GHz and 11.6 dBi at 9.85 GHz. Because of its compact volume, wide bandwidth, and control of resonant frequency and bandwidth, the proposed antenna is well suited for next-generation duplex communication systems. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, this is the most compact and broadband self-diplexing antenna reported so far.

title: Experimental Investigation of CNN-based Voltage Prediction for REBCO Pancake Coil Protection
authors: Riki Sakakibara, Takanobu Mato, Ryota Inoue, Hiroshi Ueda, SeokBeom Kim, So Noguchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Rare-earth barium copper oxide (REBCO) magnets can have a large current, whereas a failure in protection of the REBCO magnets is potentially followed by serious coil damages. It is very difficult to detect the quench events of REBCO magnets because REBCO tapes have the slow normal zone propagation velocity (NZPV) due to its high heat capacity. We have previously built a coil voltage predictor using a convolutional neural network (CNN) to predict a coil voltage rise in the event of normal-state transition. The developed predictor can successfully estimate the future voltage transition for the validation data made with simulation. We have confirmed that the CNN has an excellent potential in protection of REBCO magnets. This paper focuses on the measured data of a turn-insulated REBCO pancake coil to validate the reliability of CNN-based voltage predictor. To detect a sign of normal state transition, three cases with different training data were tested. The experimentally obtained voltage profiles are subject to the input of the voltage predictor, and the prediction performances are investigated. The case with different heater output profiles can successfully predict a voltage rise of REBCO pancake coil ∼1.2 s before the quench event.

title: Predicting LTS Superconducting NbTi Magnet Quench: A 1D-CNN Model for Real-Time Implementations
authors: Aline Y. Machado, Pedro T. Andrade, Amanda C. Martinez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: In superconducting systems, a critical challenge is managing spontaneous quench events, where a part of the superconducting material transitions to a normal resistive state and, if uncontrolled, can cause severe damage. This work presents a machine learning approach for a Quench Detection System (QDS), designed for the Superconducting Wavelength Shifter (SWLS) project at the Sirius particle accelerator, located at the Brazilian Center for Research in Energy and Materials (CNPEM). We developed a light-weight quench prediction system based on a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to forecast voltage increases across the Low-Temperature Superconducting (LTS) coils. We gathered voltage signals from quench training experiments using a vertical cryostat test setup, then we preprocessed the dataset of 43 experiments by characterizing and modeling the setup noise and applied signal processing techniques to prepare the dataset. Furthermore, we trained, tested, and evaluated a 1D CNN classifier to predict quench occurrences. The model met the performance requirements for real-time implementation on the Sirius's SWLS, predicting quench events with a lead time around 10 milliseconds. The network achieved 96% accuracy, 100% precision, 78% recall, and a 0.88 F1 score, a sufficient performance to be used as an early-warning complementary protection layer. It features a minimalist architecture with three layers and 525 parameters, making it suitable for implementation on the project's ZYNQ-based FPGA. This work provides a compact CNN model and generalizable preprocessing steps, offering a solution that is suitable for real-time deployment, enhancing operational safety, and adding a protection layer for the superconducting elements of the Sirius's SWLS.

title: BLAS: A Blockchain-Enabled Efficient and Verifiable Log Audit System with Hybrid Storage
authors: Xuanbo Huang, Mingrui Ai, Lei Ding, Kaiping Xue, Xinyi Luo, Zhuo Xu, Yingjie Xue, Hyundong Shin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: A reliable log audit system is a fundamental tool for efficient security management and attack detection. Blockchain has emerged as a prominent technology for building log audit systems, thanks to its non-repudiation and immutability properties. However, current blockchain-based solutions are computationally intensive and typically rely on coarse-grained queries, making them impractical for real-world systems. Therefore, we propose a blockchain-based verifiable log audit system, BLAS, which adopts three novel techniques. Firstly, we propose a novel data structure called the Index-Object Merkle Forest (IOMF), which combines modified Merkle Tree data structures and keyword-range bitmap indexes to support efficient log auditing. Secondly, we propose a hierarchical extension of IOMF to create an Authenticated Layered Index Structure (ALIS). ALIS enables fine-grained auditing at the entry level. Finally, we propose two optimization techniques in ALIS to reduce computational and communication costs. The performance evaluation confirms that BLAS is consistently faster than the baseline solutions with similar settings in various experiments, achieving speedups of hundreds to over ten thousand times for the most challenging workloads.

title: Design and Control of a Parallel Elastic Actuator With Adjustable Equilibrium Position
authors: Yixi Chen, Evangelos Chatziandreou, Chase W. Mathews, Beau P. Johnson, David J Braun
publish: IEEE Robotics and Automation Letters
abstract: We present an adjustable-equilibrium parallel elastic actuator (AE-PEA) that combines a large direct-drive motor (DDM) and a 3D printed torsional spring with a small motor that can continuously adjust the equilibrium position of the actuator. We demonstrate that the AE-PEA achieves high torque control bandwidth like direct-drive actuators (DDAs), energy storage and shock mitigation like parallel elastic actuators (PEAs), and equilibrium position adjustment similar to series elastic actuators (SEAs). Owing to these features, we foresee the benefit of AE-PEAs in robot joints performing a variety of static load bearing and dynamic oscillatory motion.

title: Computer Vision in Bilateral Integration Evaluation in Children with Developmental Problems
authors: Nan-Ying Yu, Ting-Yuan Fang, Min-Yu Hsieh, Yun-Shi Chen, Shao-Hsia Chang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 14th International Conference on Software and Information Engineering
abstract: None

title: Reverse Vending System for Efficient Seed Dispensation Using Mask R-CNN
authors: Orville B. Salvilla, James Anthony J. Piastro, Christian Louise C. Cuario, Genevieve A. Pilongo, Owen B. Pilongo
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 14th International Conference on Software and Information Engineering
abstract: None

title: DiabetesBuddy: A Smart AI-Powered Diabetes Management Application with Real-Time Big Data Streaming
authors: Fardeen Akeel Shaikh, Eshitha Oyyavuru, H Gautham, Puneeth Sai Siddharth Pallapu, Ruby Dinakar
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 14th International Conference on Software and Information Engineering
abstract: None

title: Organ-Aware Cross-Modality Registration Enables Attenuation Correction Without Repeated CT in Multi-Scan Total-Body PET/CT Imaging
authors: Qianhao Chen, Lianghua Li, Hairong Zheng, Dong Liang, Jianjun Liu, Zhanli Hu
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Multi-Scan Total-Body PET/CT imaging, including dual-time-point and multi-tracer protocols, provides valuable metabolic information for enhanced disease diagnosis. However, the necessity for repeated CT scans due to patient repositioning for attenuation correction (AC) increases radiation exposure, raising safety concerns. To address this, we propose a novel organ-aware cross-modality registration model (OCRM) that enables accurate AC without repeated CT scans. The OCRM predicts a deformation field between the attenuation map of the first scan and the non-attenuation-corrected PET image from the subsequent scan, generating a pseudo-attenuation map that is subsequently used for AC during PET image reconstruction. Furthermore, to enhance registration accuracy and improve generalizability, we introduced a organ-aware mechanism that emphasizes alignment in both key organ regions and the global body contour. In comparison to existing deep learning-based AC methods, the OCRM can be deployed in a zero-shot manner across diverse multi-scan PET/CT imaging scenarios without retraining. We validated the method across four different multi-scan PET/CT scenarios, including dual-time-point and dual-tracer protocols. The proposed method outperformed state-of-the-art CT-free AC techniques, demonstrating superior performance in terms of RMSE, PSNR, and SSIM across both whole-body and specific anatomical regions. Specifically, in all four scenarios, SSIM values for the corrected PET images consistently exceeded 0.995, and the RMSE of standardized uptake values remained below 0.005. These results highlight the practical applicability and generalization capability of the proposed method, enabling its seamless deployment across diverse multi-scan PET/CT imaging scenarios.

title: FedKGC: Federated Knowledge-Grounded Calibration Framework for Hallucination Mitigation in Medical LLMs
authors: Chinmay Chakraborty, Soufiane Ben Othman, Manisha Guduri, Mohamed Amine Frikha
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) into clinical workflows offers transformative potential for decision support and documentation, yet widespread adoption is currently stalled by the prohibitive risks of hallucination and strict privacy regulations preventing data centralization. To bridge this gap, we propose FedKGC (Federated Knowledge-Grounded Calibration), a novel framework that synergizes privacy-preserving Federated Learning (FL) with retrieval-augmented calibration. FedKGC introduces a dual-phase mitigation strategy: employing a Knowledge-Grounded Loss ($\mathcal {L}_{KG}$) at the local level to anchor Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) fine-tuning to private clinical guidelines, and utilizing differentially private metadata during global aggregation to construct a Global Conflict Map that down-weights contradictory concepts. Evaluation on a non-IID partition of the MIMIC-IV dataset and the MedHallu benchmark demonstrates that FedKGC achieves a 28% reduction in hallucination rates compared to standard federated baselines while strictly adhering to $(\epsilon, \delta)$-Differential Privacy guarantees. These results demonstrate that high-fidelity, hallucination-resistant medical AI can be trained collaboratively across siloed healthcare institutions without compromising patient privacy, paving the way for trustworthy distributed medical intelligence.

title: PBox: Cross-Switch Pipeline Orchestration for Accelerated Service Function Chaining in High-Performance Cloud Networks_supp1-3675603.pdf
authors: Deyun Gao
publish: N/A
abstract: The rise of latency-sensitive and bandwidth-intensive services has driven hardware accelerator adoption in cloud networks. Programmable data plane (PDP) switches achieve significant performance gains in high-performance cloud computing but face fixed pipeline constraints that limit flexibility in multi-tenant service function chaining (SFC) with dynamic function compositions. Existing approaches either exhaust resources through redundant embeddings or degrade performance via packet recirculation. This paper presents PBox, a framework enabling flexible SFC orchestration across multiple PDP switches by optimizing network function (NF) execution orders to minimize pipeline traversals-the dominant end-to-end processing delay source. This requires co-designing NF embedding with routing strategies. PBox contributes: (i) an optimized Network Service Header design supporting one-pass matching of multiple NFs through per-bit activation, reducing packet matching overhead by 45-60%; (ii) a nested optimization formulation capturing interdependence between long-term pipeline orchestration and short-term flow routing decisions; and (iii) a sampling-based genetic algorithm with statistical robustness guarantees, achieving fast switch configuration while adapting to dynamic service patterns. Evaluations on BMv2 and Intel Tofino demonstrate 33-79% completion time reduction, 46% capacity increase, and 78% line-rate efficiency, validating cloud-scale deployment potential.

title: A Transformer-based probabilistic deep learning framework for uncertainty-aware long-range flood forecasting using satellite-derived mesoscale inputs
authors: Fatemeh Ghobadi, JungMin Lee, Amir Saman Tayerani Charmchi, Myeong In Kim
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The uneven spatiotemporal distribution of precipitation, exacerbated by climate change, poses significant challenges to sustainable water resource management and flood forecasting. Deterministic models often fail to address the uncertainties inherent in long-term hydrological predictions. To address this gap, this study introduces a novel probabilistic flood forecasting framework that integrates a distribution head into attention-based architectures (Transformer and Informer) to capture long-range spatiotemporal dependencies. A second contribution is the implementation of a cascaded principal component analysis (PCA)-based data fusion approach that incorporates satellite-derived mesoscale inputs for long-horizon forecasting. The framework was evaluated across forecasting horizons of 7, 30, 60, and 180 d in two hydrologically distinct basins in the USA. Experimental results show that the Transformer achieved the best overall performance, with Continuous Ranked Probability Scores (CRPS) up to 35% lower than those of the baseline Bayesian LSTM in extended forecasting scenarios. In addition, the attention-based models maintained high reliability, with Prediction Interval Coverage Probabilities (PICP) exceeding 90% for the 95% prediction interval even at the 180-d lead time. Although the probabilistic LSTM showed greater error accumulation at longer horizons, the Informer provided a computationally efficient alternative, with accuracy remaining within 5% of the Transformer while reducing computational complexity. These findings highlight the potential of attention-based architectures for stable, uncertainty-aware flood forecasting in hydrologically dynamic basins.

title: A Multi-agent Deep Reinforcement Learning Framework for Resource Allocation Optimization in Agricultural Heterogeneous Network
authors: Ke Wu, Yang Li, Jing Nie, Jikai Zhao, Sezai Ercisli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: With the rapid development of smart terminal devices within the consumer electronics sector, optimizing resources across heterogeneous networks has emerged as a critical challenge for enhancing user experience. This paper proposes the multi-agent deep reinforcement learning-based agricultural resource optimization algorithm (MAD3QN), based on multi-agent deep reinforcement learning, to address core issues such as inefficient resource allocation and unstable communication quality within smart device networks. The algorithm integrates a three-layer federated learning framework with a heterogeneous network resource allocation model. It effectively resolves Q-value overestimation through a Dueling Double DQN structure, while employing a multi-agent coordination mechanism to counter interference challenges in complex dynamic environments. Experimental results demonstrate that under a 50-user scenario, the system achieves a capacity of 199.3 Mbps, representing a 3.4% improvement over the next-best algorithm. In high-interference environments, the quality of service (QoS) satisfaction rate reaches 92.7%, with joint energy-spectrum efficiency significantly outperforming existing methods. This provides an efficient and reliable solution for future resource optimization in agricultural IoT environments, offering broad application prospects and practical value.

title: NoctuaXR: Enhancing Video See-Through Perception for XR in Low-Light Environments with Real-Time Ambient-Aware Adaptation
authors: Hongji Li, Dechao Wang, Jialin Wang, Ziming Li, Hai-Ning Liang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: The Video See-Through (VST) technology in Extended Reality Head-Mounted Displays (XR HMDs) allows users to perceive the real-world environment while viewing seamlessly integrated virtual content in pass through mode. This technology has demonstrated significant potential across domains such as industry, healthcare, and intelligent driving. However, the camera of current VST technologies often struggles with visual fidelity, especially in low-light environments. To address this challenge, we introduce NoctuaXR, a lightweight, low-cost, environment-aware enhancement that dynamically adjusts the pixel-level brightness and saturation of the VST view in real time based on ambient light conditions, significantly improving visual quality in low-light scenarios. In a 10 lx environment, which is a very dim level of light, our tests show that NoctuaXR improves the color accuracy of the Baseline by 68.59% and outperforms the current leading, professional XR HMD, the Varjo XR-4 Focal Edition, by 64.26%. Furthermore, a comprehensive evaluation, including a perceptual quality evaluation and a daily task performance evaluation, confirms NoctuaXR's effectiveness. Our method provides users with a significantly improved visual experience, elevating their task efficiency to a level comparable to the Varjo XR-4. We also present compelling real-world use cases—from in-flight entertainment to navigating a dark room without disturbing others—that highlight NoctuaXR's tangible value in making XR technology safer, more convenient, and truly practical for daily life.

title: What Makes a Virtual Celebrity Agent Trustworthy in VR? Exploring the Role of Stylization and Voice
authors: Yang Gao, Yangbin Dai, Guangtao Zhang, Honglei Guo, Fariba Mostajeran, Frank Steinicke, Lin Li, Tao Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Recent advances in generative AI have accelerated the deployment of virtual celebrity agents for commercial endorsements. However, little is known about how their visual style and vocal type, especially when these characteristics are generated via advanced AI reconstruction or voice cloning techniques, affect user trust, perceived realism, familiarity, and social presence. We extracted a representative clip of Sheldon Cooper from The Big Bang Theory as a baseline and generated multiple Sheldon virtual agents that varied in visual style (hand-sculpted, AI-reconstructed, or non-stylized) and vocal type (cloned or synthesized). A 3×2 within-subjects experiment (N = 30) revealed that visual style significantly affected user trust, perceived realism, familiarity, and social presence, while vocal type affected only perceived realism and familiarity. Comparisons between the virtual agents and the video baseline confirmed that even cutting-edge 3D modeling still differs significantly from authentic video representations. Behavioral data indicate that the relationship between interpersonal-distance variations and trust levels is not a simple linear one. These findings provide actionable guidance for designers leveraging generative AI to create trustworthy virtual avatars or agents and delineate new research avenues for virtual celebrity agents.

title: Retraction Notice: Detection of Cyberbullying on Social Media Using Naive Bayes Classifier
authors: Summia Parveen H, Keerthana P, Indhumathi B, Arthi V, Kavyadharshini T
publish: 2024 10th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Cyberbullying has arisen as an inescapable and concerning issue via virtual entertainment stages, influencing the emotional wellness and prosperity of people around the world. To resolve this issue, this study proposes an effective digital harassing discovery framework utilizing the Naive Bayes calculation. This framework starts by gathering and marking a far-reaching dataset containing digital harassing and non-digital tormenting posts, trailed by intensive text pre-handling and element extraction. The system is able to automatically identify instances of cyberbullying in real-time social media content by utilizing the probabilistic framework of Naive Bayes. It is a useful tool for promoting a safer online environment and protecting users' well-being because its effectiveness is ensured by extensive performance evaluation and hyper parameter fine-tuning.

title: Optimal Operation Point Analysis in VVC-Based Compression of Noisy Remote Sensing Images
authors: Mohammed Zouaoui Laidouni, Touati Adli, Boban Bondžulić, Vladimir Lukin, Bogdan Kovalenko, Sergii Kryvenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper investigates the behavior of the Versatile Video Coding (VVC) compression standard applied to noisy still remote sensing images. The study analyzes how compression interacts with noise and identifies the existence of an Optimal Operation Point (OOP) for compression that provides higher quality than the noisy input. Experiments were conducted on a set of remote sensing images under both noise-free and noisy conditions (case of additive white Gaussian noise), with varying noise standard deviation and compression quantization parameter. Results show that in noise-free conditions, the PSNR decreases monotonically with increasing compression ratio, while under noisy conditions, a non-linear dependence appears, leading to a distinct OOP position. It is observed that the OOP position depends mainly on the noise intensity rather than the image content. Based on these findings, a predictive relation between the optimal quantization parameter and the noise standard deviation is derived, providing practical guidance for selecting suitable compression settings.

title: Propagation of a Transient Electromagnetic Wave Excited by a Delta-Like Current
authors: Dmytro Havrylenko, Serhii Berdnyk, Oleksandr Dumin
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The radiation and propagation of a transient electromagnetic H-wave excited by a delta-like surface current on a circular aperture in free space are studied using the Evolutionary Approach to Electromagnetics, adapted to cylindrical geometries. The time-domain problem is reduced to an inhomogeneous generalized telegraph-type equation whose causal solution is obtained through the Riemann-Green method. The full electromagnetic fields are expressed through modal expansions over the transverse cross-section, where the longitudinal-temporal evolution is contained in the modal amplitudes and the radial-azimuthal dependence is represented by the Bessel-type basis functions. For this class of sources, the resulting field expressions reduce to χ-weighted spectral integrals involving products of Bessel functions. Notably, compact closed-form expressions are obtained for the on-axis case, yielding purely transverse fields whose evolution is governed by a causal delta-term. Gaussian regularization is employed for the numerical handling of the delta-function. For the general off-axis configuration, the analysis is focused on retarded-time/axial dependencies. The transverse components are evaluated at zero azimuth, whereas the longitudinal magnetic component is examined at an orthogonal azimuth. Two representative radial positions, corresponding to points inside and outside the aperture region, are considered. It should be emphasized that the results reveal pronounced spatiotemporal localization of the radiated impulse. A brief comparison with Heaviside-type and derivative-of-delta excitations is also included.

title: Latency-Aware Service Endpoint Selection Method for Resilient Distributed Systems
authors: Mykola Beshley, Marian Seliuchenko, Halyna Beshley, Mykhailo Medvetskyi, Vincent Karovič, Serhii Onishchenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Resilient distributed systems play a central role in enabling modern digital services, ranging from critical infrastructure platforms to cyber-physical systems and real-time web applications. For such services, stable and predictable end-to-end (E2E) request latency is a key quality-of-service (QoS) requirement. With the widespread adoption of cloud/edge computing and the evolution toward Software-Defined Networking (SDN)-enabled 5G/6G networks, ensuring low and stable latency becomes increasingly challenging due to user mobility, dynamic network paths, and uneven server load. Existing solutions primarily focus on selecting regional service endpoints based on geographic proximity or aggregated network latency metrics. While effective for global traffic distribution, these approaches do not account for the actual E2E latency experienced by the client, including server-side processing delays, and do not support adaptive service replication, which limits their applicability for latency-sensitive and mission-critical services. In this work, we propose an adaptive latency-aware service endpoint selection method for resilient distributed systems. The proposed method jointly measures E2E service latency between client devices and service instances, as well as network latency between access network (base stations) and candidate data centers. By correlating these measurements, the method identifies the root causes of QoS degradation and enables adaptive service endpoint selection and service replication in resilient distributed systems. Simulation results obtained using a Containernet-based testbed demonstrate that the proposed method effectively stabilizes E2E latency under varying network conditions and dynamic server load, confirming its suitability for resilient distributed systems.
