authors: Bohdan Mytsyk, Nataliya Demyanyshyn, Oleksandr Lishchuk, Kiyoshi Shimamura, Encarnación G. Víllora, Anatoliy Andrushchak, Yaroslav Kost′
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The principal piezo-optic coefficients π<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">im</inf> of the CTAS (Ca<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>TaAl<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>Si<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">14</inf>) crystal were determined using interferometry and quantum-mechanical calculations (used the CRYSTAL17 program). CTAS exhibits the highest π<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">im</inf> coefficients among the langasite group crystals. The values of the maximum elasto-optic coefficient р<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">31</inf> and the corresponding acousto-optic (AO) efficiency were found based on the π<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">im</inf> and the elastic stiffness coefficients. It is concluded that CTAS crystal is suitable for efficient AO modulation of light in the ultraviolet region of the spectrum. In addition, the advantages of the CTAS crystal include its low cost (due to the absence of gallium in the composition), high optical quality, resistance to aggressive environments, and high mechanical strength (≥ 600 kg/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>).

title: Machine Learning-Based Player Performance Prediction and Adaptive Difficulty Control
authors: Amonrat Prasitsupparote, Voravika Wattanasoontorn, Aniwat Bunkhongmak
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study presents a machine learning-based player performance prediction framework designed for a single-player boss fighting game (1-on-1 asymmetric combat). Seventeen gameplay telemetry metrics were collected across 167 sessions from a cohort of 39 players with diverse skill levels. The proposed study used a rigorous feature selection technique utilizing Uni-variate and Tree-based methods representing 24 different feature subsets to develop an efficient prediction model. Four supervised learning classifiers-Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, and Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP)-were trained and evaluated using a 5-fold cross-validation scheme. Experimental results demonstrate that the MLP Classifier consistently outperformed other models, achieving a peak ROC-AUC of 0.9228 and an Average Precision of 0.8906, depending on three consensus features: BlockCount, DamageDealt, and DamageTaken. These findings indicate that defensive capability and damage efficiency are the primary determinants of victory. The proposed framework provides an automatic difficulty adjustment system, which enables game designers to dynamically tune boss attributes to individual player skill levels.

title: From Projects to Permanence: Governing Monetization in Technology-Driven European Excellence Hubs Through a Freemium-to-Premium Transition
authors: Viktoria Dimitrova, Yana Tsankova, Elitsa Gieva, Agata Manolova, Vladimir Poulkov
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: European Excellence Hubs (EIHs) deliver most services as open-access, grant-funded offerings during their project phase. As EU funding concludes, hubs must transition selected advanced services to costrecovery or revenue-generating premium offerings. This freemium-topremium transition is not a commercial strategy decision; it is a governance challenge governed by EU financial regulations, accounting requirements, and the operational constraints of shared digital infrastructures. The mechanisms through which hubs should manage this transition remain under-specified in both research and policy. This paper proposes a Governance Maturity Model (GMM) for EIH monetization comprising four stages and five domains. The model is operationalized through a Monetization Governance Audit Checklist (MGAC) – a 20 -item diagnostic instrument that maps freemium-topremium transition readiness across the five governance domains. The MGAC is applied as a document-grounded assessment to the METACITIES Excellence Hub (Horizon Europe Grant Agreement no. 101087257) using the Business, Investment and Sustainability Plans of the project as the primary evidence source. The paper provides: (i) a GMM for EIH freemium-to-premium transitions calibrated to EU financial regulation; (ii) the MGAC as a reusable 20- item diagnostic instrument; (iii) a documentgrounded application to METACITIES Sustainability plan; and (iv) policy recommendations for integrating freemium-to-premium governance requirements into Horizon Europe WIDERA grant agreements.

title: Development of an Exercise Monitoring Dashboard Using RAD and JAD Methodologies
authors: Orayaphat Jaikhow, Pradorn Sureephong, Suepphong Chernbumroong, Pokin Sakarinkhul, Pongthep Silapawetin
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study presents the development of an exercise-monitoring dashboard to support community-based diabetes-prevention efforts in Lamphun Province. Using the Joint Application Design (JAD) approach within the Rapid Application Development (RAD) framework, three collaborative workshops were conducted with healthcare professionals, village health volunteers, and project stakeholders to identify key monitoring parameters and appropriate visualization methods. Ten essential parameters such as exercise frequency, duration, heart rate, blood pressure, intensity level, and fatigue were selected and mapped to suitable visual formats including line charts, bar charts, pie charts, and icons. The prototype was created using Figma and implemented as a real-time web-based dashboard integrating data from Smart Motion Analysis, smartwatch devices, and the Meta Reverse application. The resulting one-page interface enhances clarity, usability, and alignment with community health workflows. A future pilot deployment will evaluate usability and user experience to support scalable adoption in community diabetes-prevention programs.

title: Content Analysis of Adolescent Feedback on 3D Cultural Heritage Character Designs
authors: Suleeporn Kamchompoo, Jirawat Sookkaew, Tanaka Takamitsu
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This research investigates the emotional responses of adolescents aged 19 to 21 to a 3D character design grounded in the cultural symbolism of the ancient Sun God bead excavated in Krabi, Thailand. The character was carefully crafted within the conceptual framework of protection and sacredness, incorporating key symbolic elements such as a radiant headdress and color schemes that evoke spiritual power and guardianship. Data was collected through an online questionnaire where participants viewed the 3D character and provided written feedback expressing their thoughts, feelings, and interpretations. Using qualitative content analysis, participant responses were systematically coded and categorized into thematic groups reflecting perceptions of sacredness, protection, aesthetic appeal, and emotional resonance. Sentiment analysis further explored the emotional valence embedded in the responses. The findings reveal a strong emotional engagement with the character, highlighting its role as a symbol of cultural heritage that evokes feelings ranging from reverence and strength to warmth and mystery. This study demonstrates that integrating traditional cultural motifs with modern 3D design techniques can effectively communicate complex emotional and symbolic meanings to contemporary youth. The research contributes valuable insights for designers and cultural preservationists seeking to leverage digital media to foster cultural appreciation and emotional connection within younger generations.

title: A Study on the Influence of Information Quality of Xiaohongshu User-Generated Content on Chinese Consumers' Purchase Intention for Thai Mistine Sunscreen Products
authors: Yankun Fan, Raslapat Suteeca
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: With the rapid growth of social commerce in China, User-Generated Content (UGC) on platforms like Xiaohongshu has become increasingly influential in shaping consumer purchase decisions. However, the mechanisms through which different dimensions of UGC information quality affect consumer perceptions and behaviors remain unclear. Drawing on the Stimulus-Organism-Response (S-O-R) framework, this study examines how three dimensions of information quality—accuracy, completeness, and credibility— influence Chinese consumers' perceived credibility, perceived usefulness, and purchase intention regarding Thai Mistine sunscreen products. Data were collected from 412 valid questionnaires and analysed using Partial Least Squares Structural Equation Modelling (PLS-SEM). The results reveal that all three information quality dimensions significantly influence both perceived credibility and perceived usefulness, with credibility demonstrating the most potent effect on perceived credibility and completeness showing the most significant impact on perceived usefulness. Furthermore, perceived credibility significantly promotes purchase intention, while perceived usefulness does not show a significant direct effect. These findings contribute to social commerce literature by clarifying the differential effects of information quality dimensions and provide practical guidance for Thai beauty brands seeking to optimize their content strategies on Chinese social media platforms.

title: A Discrete-Event Simulation Approach to Evaluate Smart Parking Policies for Carbon Reduction: A Case Study of a Thai University
authors: Peeradech Wongkum, Chalermpon Kongjit, Thepparit Sinthamrongruk
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper evaluates smart parking policies using a discrete-event simulation of a campus parking lot at Chiang Mai University (CMU). Four scenarios were modeled: traditional parking (S0), capacity expansion (S1), LED status signs (S2), and a reservation system (S3). The case study used observed arrival patterns, and the key outputs were the parking success rate and total carbon emissions. Independent-sample t-tests were conducted to benchmark each alternative against the baseline (S0). A Weighted Sum Analysis was applied to integrate the Environmental (E: carbon emission) and Social (S: success rate) dimensions. The results show that S2 reduced emissions by 13.1% compared to S0, while S1 increased emissions by 8.5% despite achieving the highest success rate (90%). Differences were statistically significant for S1 and S2, whereas S3 showed no significant change in emissions relative to S0. The weighted evaluation ranked the LED status sign scenario (S2) highest overall, indicating that information-based guidance provides the best sustainability trade-off with low operational complexity.

title: PFLVA: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning With Collusion-Resistant Verification and Fair Arbitration
authors: Jiewang Cai, Wenting Shen, Jiankun Hu, Haining Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Federated learning, as a distributed machine learning framework, enables participants to collaboratively train models by uploading only local gradients instead of exchanging local data. However, the malicious server might infer the participants' private data from the uploaded gradients or return incorrect aggregated results to participants. To tackle the above issues, numerous privacy-preserving and verifiable federated learning schemes have been developed. However, only a few of these schemes address collusion-resistant verification, and none considers the potential disputes between participants and server. In this paper, we put forward a privacy-preserving federated learning scheme with collusion-resistant verification and fair arbitration (PFLVA). In PFLVA, a novel verification method is designed, in which non-colluding participants can verify the correctness of aggregated gradient, even if up to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N-1$</tex-math></inline-formula> participants collude with the server, where <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$N$</tex-math></inline-formula> denotes the total number of online participants. We propose an efficient gradient encryption method to ensure participants' privacy while substantially reducing the computational overhead. We introduce a smart contract to locate the compromised entity when disputes arise and to achieve fair arbitration. Additionally, PFLVA allows participants to go offline without incurring additional computational or communication overheads for the online participants. We provide a comprehensive security analysis to demonstrate the correctness, verifiability, privacy protection, and collusion resistance of PFLVA. Experimental results demonstrate that PFLVA maintains high model accuracy while significantly reducing the computational and communication overhead for participants compared to related schemes.

title: Multi-Modal Transformer-Based Gait Analysis and Deep Learning Model for Slip-Resistant Footwear Evaluation
authors: Shaghayegh Chavoshian, Atena Roshan Fekr
publish: IEEE Transactions on Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Slips and falls remain a leading cause of injury, particularly in icy winter conditions and occupational settings with slippery surfaces. Several laboratory and field-based systems are designed to assess footwear slip resistance, including mechanical testers, tribometers, and human-centred gait-based assessments. While mechanical tests offer standardized slip resistance metrics for footwear, they often fail to reflect real-world variability in human gait, environmental conditions, and anthropometric diversity. On the other hand, although human-centered testing ensures ecological validity, it typically reports a single aggregated score for footwear derived from multiple participants, which obscures individual biomechanical differences and limits the ability to provide personalized assessments of slip resistance performance. This study introduces a transformer-based regression model that integrates (1) temporal biomechanical features, (2) outsole images, (3) footwear metadata, and (4) anthropometric data to predict personalized slip resistance score of 84 footwear across 578 walking trials conducted by 18 human participants on two types of ice: wet and dry. The predictive performance of our multimodal deep learning model varied by surface condition, with substantially better results on dry surfaces (RMSE: 1.34° ± 0.34°, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.45 ± 0.09, MAPE: 10.48% ± 3.58%) than on wet surfaces (RMSE: 2.34° ± 0.43°, R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>: 0.47 ± 0.12, MAPE: 21.45% ± 6.89%). The results demonstrate the potential of fusing biomechanical and outsole characteristics to reliably assess slip risk under varying environmental conditions. Additionally, they emphasize the need for adaptive and context-aware testing protocols and pave the way for the development of intelligent footwear assessment tools aimed at injury prevention.

title: SemDiff: Semantic Guided Diffusion-Based Collaborative Filtering Framework
authors: Xufeng Liang, Zhida Qin, Haoyan Fu, Enjun Du, Haotian He, Chong Zhang, Tianyu Huang, Gangyi Ding
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Diffusion models have demonstrated promising potential in recommender systems owing to their powerful generative ability. However, due to the inherent sparse nature of real-world recommendation data and the inconsistency in the variation of reconstruction and ranking losses during training, existing works suffer from two issues: 1) Randomly sampled Gaussian noise addition tends to obscure original user preferences. 2) Training for generation and preference learning tasks interferes with each other, limiting the generative ability of the model. To address these issues, we propose SemDiff, a semantic guided diffusion-based collaborative filtering framework. For the first issue, instead of using random Gaussian noise, we leverage rich semantic information by incorporating auxiliary signals from text or image modalities to enhance the input data of denoising model. In response to the second issue, based on a comprehensive analysis of the mutual influence between generation and preference learning in diffusion recommender systems, we build a collaborative training objective strategy to transform the interference between them into mutual collaboration, which jointly enhances the effectiveness of model training. Additionally, we employ multiple GCN layers only during inference to incorporate higher-order co-occurrence information while maintaining training efficiency. Extensive experiments on four real-world datasets demonstrate that SemDiff significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Our SemDiff offers an effective solution for enhancing recommendation performance and suggests a novel paradigm for applying diffusion methods in recommender systems. code repository: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Qin-lab-code/SemDiff</uri>

title: Review of High-Step-Up Non-Isolated DC-DC Converters Focusing on the Topology Methodology and Features
authors: Iman Soltani, Gholam Reza Molaeimanesh, Hossein Gholizadeh, Hani Vahedi
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Power electronic converters are essential components in modern electrical systems, with DC-DC converters being particularly crucial for their extensive variety and widespread applications, notably in renewable energy systems and electric vehicles. For applications such as Photovoltaic (PV) arrays, fuel cells, and microgrids, a critical requirement is the need for non-isolated, high step-up DC-DC converters capable of providing a high-voltage gain while mitigating significant practical challenges, including high semi-conductor stress and degraded efficiency at extreme duty cycles. This study proposes a comprehensive, structural-based review of over 100 non-isolated high step-up DC-DC converters, specifically excluding topologies that rely on coupled inductors. Our primary contribution is a novel categorization and comparative analysis framework that delves into the fundamental topological details and features, going beyond the focus of recently suggested reviews. The methodology begins with a systematic analysis of classical non-isolated converters (Boost, Buck-Boost, C´ uk, SEPIC, and Zeta) to establish a baseline and highlight their limitations, such as the semiconductor voltage stress being greater than the output voltage in most cases, which reduces their suitability for high-gain applications. The paper then systematically classifies advanced topologies into 23 distinct groups based on their unique structural characteristics. The comparison is rigorously quantitative and systematic, focusing on structural details and key performance metrics such as voltage gain and density, semiconductor stress, and current continuity and component count. The comprehensive analysis is conducted by deconstructing each topology into its constituent sub-converters to reveal how structural combinations influence key performance metrics. Finally, the findings facilitate a discussion on the practical applications of each topology, matching their inherent characteristics to specific real-world systems like PV arrays, Electric Vehicles (EVs), Vehicle-Integrated PV (VIPV) systems, and electrolyzers .

title: High-temperature sustainable FBG-based Er doped fiber laser for high resolution sensing up to 600°C
authors: Hanmei Zhang, Fengfan Cai, Ruixuan Shi, Yonghang Shen
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: We report an Er doped fiber laser for high resolution temperature sensing over wide temperature range. The laser was constructed in ring cavity structure and was able to operate stably in single mode over wide temperature range up to 600 °C. Narrow spectral bandwidth FBG was fabricated by 515 nm fs laser and utilized as the sensing element through temperature dependent laser wavelength tuning. High temperature sustainable characteristic of the FBG was investigated over temperature range from room temperature to over 1000°C, revealing its excellent operational stability from room temperature to over 600°C and abnormal strengthening at annealing temperature around 1000 °C. Stable power output and wavelength repeatability of the laser were experimentally demonstrated when the FBG was heated and cooled down repeatedly from room temperature to over 600°C. These results demonstrate the suitability of such high temperature sustainable fiber sensor in harsh environment and its great potential in semiconductor industry.

title: Simultaneous Recognition of Hand Gestures, Grasping Force, and Hand Aperture Using a Wearable Displacement Myography System
authors: Enrica Stefanelli, Alireza Mohammadi, Chu Wang, Tianshi Yu, Ying Tan, Peter Choong, Francesca Cordella, Loredana Zollo, Denny Oetomo
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Monitoring forearm muscle activity is essential for estimating human intention in different applications such as virtual reality, rehabilitation, and prosthetics. While prior studies have mainly focused on recognising gestures or simultaneously classifying gestures and force, predominantly using surface electromyography (sEMG), the simultaneous classification of gesture, force, and hand aperture within the same gesture has not yet been investigated. This work extends previous approaches by evaluating a wearable Displacement Myography (DMG)-based system for the combined recognition of hand gestures, four grasping force levels, and multiple hand aperture levels, and by directly benchmarking it against an sEMG system under identical experimental conditions. Data from 13 healthy participants were collected using the MyoLog armband with DMG sensors and sEMG sensors while performing three grasp types (pinch, key, power) with objects of varying size and four force levels, generating 33 configurations. Across several classifiers, the DMG system achieved a peak accuracy of 93 ± 2%, significantly outperforming EMG (64 ± 10%). DMG showed superior discrimination of hand aperture levels (98 ± 3%), while EMG showed relatively better but still lower performance in force-level classification (82 ± 11%). The results are supported by separability analysis using the Fisher Discriminant Ratio, which demonstrated consistently higher class separability for DMG. These findings highlight the suitability of DMG for applications requiring precise recognition of hand aperture and force levels, offering a compact, computationally efficient, and stimulation-compatible solution that overcomes several limitations of EMG-based systems.

title: How AI-Generated Videos Attract Vietnamese Social Media Users: Insights From Engagement and Labelling-Based Content Analysis
authors: Phuong Vinh Thai, Phuong Vy Huynh, Thien Thanh Hoang, Gia Tue Ho, Ngoc Lan Phuong, Le Thi My Danh, Qui Ngoc Nguyen, Pham Ngoc Bao Chau, Trong-Hop Do
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The growing presence of AI-generated content on online platforms in Vietnam, including YouTube, Facebook, and TikTok, has significantly influenced user engagement behaviors. This study utilises a nine-factor content-labeling paradigm to investigate how Vietnamese viewers interact with AI-generated video content. Ordinary Least Squares (OLS) regression was applied to evaluate a dataset of 225 AI-generated videos that were gathered between September and December 2025. With the model explaining up to 27 % of the variance in likes, the findings show that five content factors-Shareability, Trendysocial relevance, Concise pace, Visual appeal, and Audiomusical elements-have the strongest and most consistent impact on views and likes. The findings extend the Stimulus-Response framework by emphasising the role of structural content factors, offer empirical support for multi-platform communication strategies in Vietnam, and suggest the S.T.A.C. model to direct responsible and successful AI-driven content.

title: Towards a More Accurate Determination-Based Correlation Coefficient: Proposals and Empirical Assessment
authors: Vladyslav Holdovanskyi, Vladyslav Alieksieiev
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper explores several methodological enhancements to improve the accuracy and resilience of a determination-based correlation coefficient, particularly for small and noisy datasets. In addition to modifications such as median-based aggregation, percentile binning, distance-weighted averaging, clustering-based, and target-aware binning, we investigate local regression approximators such as linear and poly-ridge models. Furthermore, methods based on overlapping windows are introduced to enhance statistical reliability in smooth discontinuities and low-data regimes. Empirical evaluation on synthetic datasets with sample sizes as small as n = 1000 demonstrates that Poly-Ridge Quantile Regression achieves the lowest mean and median errors (Mean ≈ 0.0086), while Quantile Median Coefficient ef-fectively limits extreme errors (Max ≈ 0.179). Linear Regression Quantile Coefficient performs well in intermediate-sized datasets, and standard Poly-Ridge Regression without quantile adjustment exhibits higher variability and larger errors. Together, these methodological developments provide a comprehensive framework for enhancing correlation estimations, increasing flexibility in handling various data scenarios, and paving the way for more reliable applications in statistical analysis and machine learning.

title: Mentoring Junior Journalists by Community of Practice (CoP)
authors: Cho Mar Lwin, Teeraporn Saeheaw
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Journalism plays an important role in providing access to relevant, reliable, and accurate information to support informed decision-making. As the media industry shifts from traditional platforms to a multimedia environment, skill gaps among journalists have become a significant challenge, particularly for junior journalists. Compared to senior professionals, junior journalists often enter the profession with limited structured training and face difficulties in meeting professional standards, adapting to innovation, and producing high-quality content. This study aims to propose a competency-based process innovation by examining how competency mapping and a Community of Practice (CoP) mentoring approach can enhance the sustainable professional development of junior journalists. Using a mixed-methods design, the study integrates qualitative insights from semi-structured interviews with newsroom management and quantitative competency assessments conducted by editors. The findings from the implementation of the mentoring model demonstrate practical strategies for improving junior journalists' competencies and strengthening newsroom standards, thereby supporting process innovation and sustainable development within media organizations.

title: Integrated Simulation of Detection and Calibration Procedures in Partial Discharge Signal Measurements
authors: Yevhenii Trotsenko, Julia Peretyatko, Yaroslav Haran, Serhii Burian, Hanna Zemlianukhina
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The presence of partial discharges can lead to accelerated aging and failure of power transformers, cables, rotating machines, and other high-voltage equipment. This paper presents the results of further advancements in circuit-based simulation approaches for partial discharge signal measurements, with a specific focus on detection and calibration procedures. The study introduces more sophisticated equivalent circuit models to represent damaged insulation with improved accuracy. It is demonstrated that virtual calibration of the measurement system within the complete test circuit enables effective estimation of the apparent charge of partial discharges in the simulated environment. Overall, the integrated simulation of detection and calibration procedures facilitates verification that the simulation outcomes correspond closely to expected measurement results for real insulation systems. Directions for future research are also outlined.

title: Design and Usability Evaluation of a Gamified Social Reading Platform Based on Self-Determination Theory
authors: Thiranai Paengchai, Panupong Sukbanjong, Adisorn Santongkum, Pruet Putjorn, Thanpahtt Chaichombhoo
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Books remain powerful tools for learning and self-development, yet younger readers increasingly consume literature through digital media. Gen Z readers share and discover books on platforms such as BookTok, Bookstagram and BookTube, and they expect social interaction alongside their reading. Readflow is a prototype web application designed for these digital-first readers. It combines a gamified progress system with a moderated community to encourage sustained reading habits while minimising spoilers and biased reviews. Thirty university students (18–25 years) evaluated two prototypes. The refined design (Prototype A) achieved an average System Usability Scale (SUS) score of 75.17, which is considered good to excellent usability, compared with 69.67 (“acceptable“) for the baseline design. Participants rated Prototype A higher on visual design, ease of use and motivation, and interviews highlighted its modern look and engaging progress visualisation. This paper discusses the design rationale grounded in self-determination theory, the evaluation results and opportunities for further improvement.

title: Functional Generative System Architecture for Natural Language-Driven Control of Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Volodymyr Pastukh, Pavlo Beshley, Serhii Mokhun, Olha Fedchyshyn, Lesia Brych
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Modern cyber-physical systems (CPS) integrate physical processes, sensors, networks, and software services to support safety-critical, real-time, and large-scale applications. However, their control and interaction mechanisms remain largely constrained by predefined user interfaces, rigid command sets, and static control logic, which significantly limit adaptability, scalability, and accessibility. This paper introduces a Functional Generative System (FGS) architecture for natural language-driven control of cyber-physical systems, where a generative artificial intelligence (AI) model acts as an intelligent controller that dynamically translates human intent into executable system commands. The proposed architecture integrates a Natural Language-Driven Controller (NLDC) based on large language models into a clean layered CPS architecture. Instead of relying on fixed voice commands or manually programmed interfaces, the NLDC interprets freeform natural language input, infers user intent, and generates structured command configurations that are directly executed by the CPS control layer. This enables on-the-fly creation of new control functions, adaptive data processing workflows, and flexible system interaction without modifying application code or user interfaces. A functional command abstraction layer is introduced to ensure safe, modular, and extensible execution of AI-generated commands across navigation, external control, and data-processing domains. The architecture is validated through a prototype mobile implementation that demonstrates natural-language-driven control of data handling, navigation, and user interface behavior. The results indicate that FGS enables flexible interaction and runtime extensibility without compromising reliability, providing a practical pathway for integrating generative AI into CPS.

title: Comparison of Phase Synchronization Methods in Systems Using Suppressed Carrier Signals
authors: Ivan Horbatyi, Volodymyr Horbatyi, Bohdan Tsaitler
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper is devoted to the study and compare a number of widely used phase synchronization methods in digital telecommunication systems that use suppressed carrier signals based on the system's noise immunity criterion. The features of the phase synchronization method based on a frequency multiplier by two and the phase synchronization method based on the Costas loop have been experimentally investigated. Recommendations for the application of these methods in modern digital telecommunication systems have been developed.

title: Real-Time Speaker-Sensitive Audio Gating Via Supervised Frame-Level Speaker Recognition
authors: Josemaria Y. Layug, Andrei D. Coronel
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: The study presents a modification to the foundation of a noise gate plug-in to create a more intelligent, context-aware system focused on target-speaker speech identification. A supervised, text-independent, and frame-wise personal voice activity detection model based on [1] is proposed and refined to process audio in real-time with minimal CPU utilization. The model achieved 87.6 % accuracy during batch testing and 72 % accuracy on a 5-minute real-world sample under challenging conditions, outperforming a standard noise gate on the same input. Overall, this work demonstrates the feasibility of applying deep learning models in real-time audio systems and demonstrates the potential of personal voice activity detection as a control mechanism for adaptive audio effects, contributing insights to the interdisciplinary fields of computer science and live audio broadcasting.

title: A Lightweight Filtering Framework for Efficient Edge-Cloud Collaborative Video Semantic Segmentation
authors: Xiyu Cao, Hao Li, Zesong Xu, Shuo Huang, Yangguang Cui, Tongquan Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computer-Aided Design of Integrated Circuits and Systems
abstract: Edge–cloud collaborative video analytics has become essential for intelligent monitoring and semantic segmentation in large-scale IoT systems. However, existing approaches cannot efficiently adapt to new and dynamic environments and often rely on heavy edge-side neural inference, causing high latency and resource waste. In this paper, we propose LEAF, a Lightweight Edge–cloud Analysis Filtering framework for low-latency semantic segmentation. LEAF employs a compressed-domain multi-cue strategy that leverages motion vectors, residuals, and U channel differences to detect scene changes. Unchanged regions are reused while only updated regions are re-inferred, reducing redundant edge computation and uplink transmission. To improve robustness in dynamic scenes, we introduce a stabilization mechanism for fusion weight prediction. By modeling periodic patterns and observation cues, the mechanism produces smooth and reliable weight estimates, enhancing temporal stability and mitigating cold-start instability. Furthermore, we design a periodic edge– cloud prediction–update protocol. The edge performs lightweight per-frame detection and weight prediction, while the cloud periodically conducts full-frame segmentation to update filtering parameters and returns compact updates. This closed-loop update mechanism maintains long-term accuracy under limited bandwidth. Extensive experiments on real-world driving and traffic monitoring datasets demonstrate that LEAF reduces end-to-end per-frame latency by up to 2× while lowering bandwidth usage and edge computational load. LEAF maintains comparable task accuracy and exhibits stable performance across different scenarios, enabling low-latency and resource-efficient semantic segmentation in dynamic edge–cloud environments.

title: ConNet: Complex Orthogonal Network with Low-Rank Regularization for CSI Localization
authors: Zhiyuan He, Shanyi Ke, Desheng Wang, Zhijun Wang, Mahmoud M. Salim
publish: IEEE Communications Letters
abstract: Channel State Information (CSI) fingerprinting can achieve high in-domain accuracy for WiFi localization, yet its performance often degrades severely after deployment in a new environment, hindering rapid transfer. For cross-domain passive localization, we first provide a closed-form prediction error bound (PEB) as an analytical design reference, showing that localization is primarily governed by relative phase structures across antennas and subcarriers, whereas packet-wise global complex scaling acts as a nuisance transformation. Guided by this insight, we propose ConNet, a CSI-specific complex-valued model consisting of a complex-scale-aware backbone and a hard-coupled transfer module that applies Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization before nuclear norm regularization. The resulting design preserves informative complex phase structure, reduces multipathinduced feature redundancy, and promotes a compact domainrobust subspace. Experiments under five-domain source rotation demonstrate improved cross-domain transfer performance and effective location-wise few-shot adaptation.

title: PitVQA++: Vector Matrix-Low-Rank Adaptation for Open-Ended Visual Question Answering in Pituitary Surgery
authors: Runlong He, Danyal Z. Khan, Evangelos B. Mazomenos, Hani J. Marcus, Danail Stoyanov, Matthew J. Clarkson, Mobarak I. Hoque
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) in visual question answering (VQA) offer a unique opportunity to enhance intra-operative decision-making, promote intuitive interactions, and significantly advance surgical education. However, the development of VLMs for surgical VQA is challenging due to limited datasets and the risk of overfitting and catastrophic forgetting during full fine-tuning of pretrained weights. While parameter-efficient techniques like Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) and Matrix of Rank Adaptation (MoRA) address adaptation challenges, their uniform parameter distribution overlooks the feature hierarchy in deep networks, where earlier layers, that learn general features, require more parameters than later ones. This work introduces PitVQA++ with an Open-ended PitVQA dataset and vector matrix-low-rank adaptation (Vector-MoLoRA), an innovative VLM fine-tuning approach for adapting GPT-2 to pituitary surgery. Open-Ended PitVQA comprises 109,173 frames from 25 procedural videos with 795,270 question-answer sentence pairs, covering key surgical elements such as phase and step recognition, context understanding, tool detection, localization, and interactions recognition. Vector-MoLoRA incorporates the principles of LoRA and MoRA to develop a matrix-low-rank adaptation strategy that employs rank vectors to allocate more parameters to earlier layers, gradually reducing them in the later layers. Our approach, validated on the Open-Ended PitVQA and EndoVis18-VQA datasets, effectively mitigates catastrophic forgetting while significantly enhancing performance over recent baselines. Performance-rejection analysis further highlights Vector-MoLoRA’s enhanced reliability and trust-worthiness in handling uncertain predictions. Our source code and dataset is available at https://github.com/ HRL-Mike/PitVQA-Plus.

title: Unleashing the Potential of Large Language Models: A Blueprint for Real-Time, Enterprise-Ready Deployments
authors: Muhammad Faizan Raza, Shuo Luna Yang, Satish Mahadevan Srinivasan, Joanna F. Defranco
publish: Computer
abstract: This article describes a practical “always updating” architecture for large language model pipelines by connecting to streaming data, learning and fact-checking, and automatically improving from errors and human feedback to remain timely, reliable, and fast.

title: Tailoring Frequency Response of Fiber Laser Ultrasound Sensors via Delayed Self-Heterodyne Demodulation for versatile Photoacoustic Imaging
authors: Chaoneng Wu, Peiqian He, Xue Bai, Wei Li, Zhicai Li, Xiaoxuan Zhong, Yizhi Liang, Bai-Ou Guan
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: Optical ultrasound sensors offer distinct advantages for photoacoustic imaging due to their potential for miniaturization, broad bandwidth and high sensitivity; however, unlocking their full performance often requires complex phase demodulation systems. In this work, we propose and demonstrate a tunable delayed self-heterodyne (DSH) demodulation scheme for fiber laser ultrasound sensors that overcomes the limitations of traditional quadrature detection. By introducing the concept of an acoustic “modulation time window”, we establish a theoretical framework showing that the sensor's frequency response can be deterministically shaped by simply adjusting the delay fiber length. We demonstrate two distinct operating modes: a short-delay (4 m) differential mode that optimizes signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) in the 10–40 MHz band for high-contrast micro-vasculature imaging, and a long-delay (4 km) broadband mode that recovers low-frequency components, enabling high-fidelity reconstruction of macroscopic structures without “hollow” artifacts. The proposed method is validated through multi-scale phantom experiments and in vivo human imaging, proving its versatility as a robust, tunable solution for next-generation photoacoustic microscopy and tomography.

title: Retraction Notice: Phishing Attack Detection Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Siva Satya Sreedhar P, Sravani Velpula, Rishwitha Parise, Naidu Krishna Vamsi, Sakhamuri Krishna Chaitanya
publish: 2023 9th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: Phishing attacks are a prevalent form of social engineering that target individuals through emails to obtain confidential and sensitive information. These attacks can lead to larger security breaches in both corporate and government networks. There have been several attempts to counter phishing assaults, but so far none have proven successful. For this reason, improved strategies for identifying phishing attempts are desperately needed. The proposed fix is a deep learning-based strategy for identifying malicious phishing attempts. By analyzing more than 5,000 phishing emails sent at the University of Malaysia’s Department of Computer Science and Information Technology, the authors hoped to create a model that reliably detects phishing assaults to achieve this, they selected relevant features through feature engineering and used the Random Forest models to extract feature importance at different levels. Finally, the model was trained using Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), leading to improved detection and accuracy.

title: An Adaptive Weighting Mechanism for Missing Data Imputation: An Early Evaluation
authors: Mohammad Reza Nosouhi, Keshav Sood, Robin Doss, Gang Li, Zubair Baig
publish: IEEE Networking Letters
abstract: We propose a novel adaptive weighting mechanism for missing data imputation using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) that dynamically adjusts the contribution of each imputed value to the training objective. Our approach integrates three complementary factors into the weighting scheme: (1) the confidence of the generator in each imputed value, (2) the temporal proximity of the missing data to nearby observed data, and (3) feedback from the discriminator, which reflects the realism of the imputation. Experiments on benchmark time series dataset demonstrate that our method significantly improves imputation accuracy and temporal consistency.

title: A Compressed Sensing based Coherent Integration Method with Random Agile Waveform Optimization
authors: Ping Lang, Huizhang Yang, Xiongjun Fu, Jian Dong, Junjun Yin, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Frequency agile radar (FAR) exhibits robustness against active deceptive jamming and effective clutter suppression. However, effective coherent integration of target echoes under random agile waveform transmission remains challenging. This paper proposes a compressed sensing (CS)-based improved sparsity adaptive matching pursuit (ISAMP) method within a joint transmit-receive processing framework. First, a Whale optimization algorithm (WOA)-based optimized random interpulse frequency and pulse repetition time joint agile (RI-FPrtJA) signal model is systematically derived. Then, the ISAMP algorithm achieves coherent integration of RI-FPrtJA echoes through two key enhancements: an adaptive regularization-based mesh mismatch correction (ARMMC) strategy enhances mesh mismatch correction capability, and an adaptive searching extension condition (ASEC) reduces computational cost during sparse reconstruction. Qualitative and quantitative simulations demonstrate that ISAMP outperforms existing CS-based algorithms (e.g., orthogonal match pursuit (OMP), sparsity adaptive matching pursue (SAMP)) in sparse reconstruction under complex scenarios. Furthermore, the WOA-optimized RI-FPrtJA waveform can significantly improve the sparse reconstruction of agile echoes.

title: Hybrid Neural Orbit Propagation Under Sparse Space-Based Observations
authors: Jiaping Xiao, Lu Bai, Mir Feroskhan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Accurate long-term orbit propagation (OP) is a core capability for space situational awareness (SSA), underpinning conjunction assessment, collision avoidance, and catalog maintenance. High-precision orbit propagation (HPOP) models can accurately integrate the motion equations without relying on two-line elements (TLEs), using only initial conditions. In operational space-based SSA, however, initial states are usually obtained from sparse angles-only measurements, and the environment is uncertain, especially in atmospheric density and solar radiation pressure. These factors cause the propagated trajectories to diverge over time, even when using sophisticated numerical propagators. This paper proposes a hybrid physics-based residual learning framework that integrates an HPOP model with a residual-correction module based on long short-term memory (LSTM) networks. The LSTM operates on sliding windows of propagated states and learns to predict systematic residual errors that accumulate over long horizons due to model deficiencies and orbit determination (OD) biases. The corrected state is obtained by adding the learned residuals to the HPOP prediction. We derive the HPOP-LSTM hybrid neural OP formulation, describe the residual-learning architecture, and present numerical experiments in low Earth orbit (LEO), medium Earth orbit (MEO), and geostationary orbit (GEO) scenarios across various observation perspectives. Results show that the proposed hybrid model substantially reduces propagation errors from 400 km to 1 km over 24 hours across various orbit types, particularly in the presence of OD errors typical of sparse angles-only tracking.

title: Reproducibility of Experiments in Networking
authors: Nikolas Wehner, Alexej Grigorjew, Simon Raffeck, Michael Goedicke, Tobias Hoßfeld
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Reproducibility is a cornerstone of good scientific practice, enabling validation of experimental results and fostering progress by building upon prior work. The importance of reproducibility in networking research has gained traction in recent years. Notably, the Dagstuhl beginner’s guide to reproducible experimental networking research has provided valuable best practices for documentation, data handling, and experiment design. However, the identification of specific networking components and aspects that prevent experiment reproducibility has not been investigated yet. This work addresses this gap by systematically identifying such components, constructing a taxonomy based on the findings, and proposing guidelines to increase experiment reproducibility. For this purpose, we discuss and analyze several networking use cases: 5G measurement experiments, Internet measurements, AI/ML-based networking experiments, and subjective QoE studies. Through targeted experiments and a review of existing studies, we identify some key sources of variability and discuss strategies to reduce variability and thus increase reproducibility. Our analysis highlights the importance of clearly defining research questions and expected outcomes as they form the foundation for assessing reproducibility. Additionally, our findings show the need for documenting potential sources of randomness, e.g., platform-specific effects introduced by operating systems, prior to experimental design. Minimizing unnecessary variability, e.g., by abstracting or replacing such components, further enhances reproducibility. While not all variability can be controlled due to the inherent complexity of networked systems, the proposed guidelines offer practical steps toward more reproducible networking experiments.

title: Optimized Stacked Passive Coil Array Design for High Efficiency and Misalignment Tolerance in PCB-Based Wireless Power Transfer Systems
authors: Kaiyuan Wang, Junxiang Yang, Zhen Sun, Yun Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a stacked passive coil array (SPCA) integrated on the transmitter (Tx) side to enhance power transfer efficiency (PTE), power delivered to the load (PDL), and misalignment tolerance in printed circuit board (PCB)-based wireless power transfer (WPT) systems. Power losses in the PCB coils are analyzed, and a modified multi-objective genetic algorithm (GA) is employed to optimize key geometric parameters for simultaneous improvement in PTE and PDL. An equivalent circuit model is developed to quantify the influence of SPCA design factors, including the number of units and their resonant frequencies. A unified parameter design strategy for both the SPCA and compensation network is also proposed to achieve optimal system performance. Experimental validation using a 200 kHz, 160 W prototype demonstrates a 10.5% enhancement in DC–DC PTE at a 120 mm transfer distance, comparable to ferrite-based systems, while achieving a 6.55-fold increase in PDL. The performance surpasses that of both the planar passive coil array (PPCA) and the nanocrystalline ribbon core (NRC) based configurations under identical operating conditions. The SPCA-based system further exhibits superior robustness under lateral misalignment conditions.

title: The Self-Attention IHF-LSTM Network to Model Interactive Hesitant Fuzzy Information and Its Application in UAV Autonomous Navigation
authors: Man Liu, Wei Zhou, Zeshui Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Recently, the uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) autonomous navigation has been widely used in logistics transportation and environmental monitoring. However, the interaction of subjective evaluation information has been the critical challenge for UAVs and its operations. Also, the correlation among different attributes of the evaluation information is unclear, and the interaction between early and late attributes is often overlooked. Then, how to describe this interactive evaluation information and realize classification decisions has become a key issue. To do it, this paper introduces the hesitant fuzzy set (HFS) and develop the temporal and interactive HFS. Further, we propose a self-attention interactive hesitant fuzzy-long short-term memory network to processes and recognizes the defined information. Then, a multi-probability classification algorithm is given to show the calculation process. At last, its feasibility and applicability are verified by an example of UAV autonomous navigation, which provides a new direction for the development of related UAV technologies.

title: How Effective is Mouse Dynamics Against Web Bots? A Study Across Different Sophistication Levels
authors: Oguzhan Salman, Kemal Bicakci
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Web bots pose an increasing threat to online security with mouse dynamics emerging as a key behavioral biometric for detection. This paper critically evaluates a multi-layered defense strategy against a spectrum of bot attacks from synthetic generation to sophisticated replays. We collected a dataset from two custom websites and evaluated three distinct defensive layers: a suite of machine learning models for behavioral analysis, Dynamic TimeWarping (DTW) for historical comparison and click-pattern analysis for contextual validation. Our findings demonstrate that no single technique is a silver-bullet and the optimal defense approach depends on the specific threat model. The supervised Random Forest proved highly effective achieving perfect detection against synthetic attacks and successfully identified the majority of cross-domain replay attacks by leveraging task complexity differences between UI contexts. Furthermore, a simple deterministic click-pattern analysis provided a viable solution against the cross-domain attack highlighting the power of context-aware rules. In contrast, unsupervised models struggled with this context-mismatched replay, while our DTW approach was essential and effective for same-domain replays. This research underscores the necessity of a hybrid, defense-in-depth system that integrates supervised behavioral models, historical analysis and contextual validation to secure mouse-dynamics systems against a comprehensive range of threats.

title: VRGaussianAvatar: Integrating 3D Gaussian Avatars into VR
authors: Hail Song, Boram Yoon, Seokhwan Yang, Seoyoung Kang, Hyunjeong Kim, Henning Metzmacher, Woontack Woo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We present VRGaussianAvatar, an integrated system that enables real-time full-body 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) avatars in virtual reality using only head-mounted display (HMD) tracking signals. The system adopts a parallel pipeline with a VR Frontend and a GABackend. The VR Frontend uses inverse kinematics to estimate full-body pose and streams the resulting pose along with stereo camera parameters to the backend. The GA Backend stereoscopically renders a 3DGS avatar reconstructed from a single image. To improve stereo rendering efficiency, we introduce Binocular Batching, which jointly processes left and right eye views in a single batched pass to reduce redundant computation and support high-resolution VR displays. We evaluate VRGaussianAvatar with quantitative performance tests and a within-subject user study against image- and video-based mesh avatar baselines. Results show that VRGaussianAvatar sustains interactive VR performance and yields higher perceived appearance similarity, embodiment, and plausibility. Project page and source code are available at https://vrgaussianavatar.github.io

title: FootEyePorting: Design and Evaluation of Foot–Eye Teleportation Techniques in Virtual Reality
authors: Jianwei Yang, Tingjie Wan, BoYu Gao, Huawei Tu, Henry Been-Lirn Duh
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Various locomotion techniques, such as teleportation, walking in place, redirected walking, and walking, have been proposed. However, conventional methods overlooked the human ability to coordinate multiple modalities (e.g., eyes and feet) for natural virtual locomotion within a small physical space. Inspired by the natural coordination of the eyes and feet in human walking, as well as insights from prior work, this study investigates how eye–foot coordination can be leveraged more effectively for VR teleportation. We present four novel teleportation techniques based on eye–foot coordination, using users' gaze behavior and 3D foot positions as input modalities. A user study with 20 participants compared our techniques with a state-of-the-art foot-based locomotion method. Results demonstrate the superiority of our approaches: task completion times were significantly reduced, NASA-TLX workload scores and SUS usability scores were markedly improved, and participants expressed a clear preference for our techniques over the baseline. These findings provide a strong foundation for the design and implementation of future eye–foot coordinated teleportation methods in VR.

title: Dynamic Regime Maps of Multilayer Neural Network Training Using the Adam Optimizer
authors: Serhiy Sveleba, Ivan Katerynchuk, Ivan Kuno, Marta Dufanets, Nazar Sveleba
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper presents a study of dynamic regime maps (DRMs) describing the training behavior of multilayer neural networks under the application of the Adam optimizer. The research focuses on the visualization of learning dynamics through DRMs constructed in the hyperparameter space (β<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf>, β<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>, α), which reflect transitions between stable, oscillatory, and chaotic regimes. The obtained results demonstrate the correlation between the optimizer’s hyperparameters and the stability of the training process, revealing regions of underfitting, satisfactory convergence, and overfitting leading to chaotic behavior. The study also provides a methodological approach for constructing DRMs by comparing iterative weight correction values and determining periodicities in the optimization trajectory. The analysis emphasizes the sensitivity of the Adam optimizer to the choice of β<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</inf> and β<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> parameters and illustrates how the learning rate α affects transitions between dynamic regimes.

title: Text Analytics for Identifying the Competitiveness of Northern Thai SMEs' Products
authors: Phusanisa Pengsiri, Chommaphat Malang, Danaitun Pongpatcharatorntep
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This study uses AI-driven text analytics to identify product competitiveness among Northern Thai SMEs. The semi-structured interviews with twelve Chiang Mai SME products yielded qualitative transcripts which conveyed narratives regarding product attributes, pricing, distribution, and promotion. Transcripts were transcribed using Whisper AI while extractive keywords for text were produced using GPT. Text analysis and visualization were carried out using RStudio. The results suggest that AI-assisted text analytics can efficiently support the identification of SME product competitiveness and digital-market readiness in data-limited settings, providing practical insights for marketing and e-commerce strategy development.

title: Impact of ESG Implementation on Organizational Performance in the Air Transport Industry
authors: Noravich Fungkajorn, Pittawat Ueasangkomsate
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Thai airlines play an important role in national transport, and their long-term success depends on responsible and transparent management. As Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) practices become more important in the aviation sector, it is still not fully understood how these practices influence employees and support better performance. This study examines how ESG affects organizational trust, commitment, and performance by using survey data from 429 employees working in full-service, low-cost, and regional airlines. Multiple regression analysis was used to test both direct and indirect relationships among the variables. The results show that social and governance practices directly enhance organizational performance, while environmental practices influence performance indirectly. For all three ESG dimensions, trust and commitment function as mediating variables, and both factors have positive effects on performance. These findings suggest that ESG is not only a requirement but also a practical approach for improving employee attitudes and strengthening daily operations. The study concludes that airlines can achieve better and more sustainable performance by applying ESG practices consistently and clearly across the organization.

title: CI-based Approach for Endowing HCI Subjects Digital Twins with the Property of HCI Object Perception Subjectivization
authors: Andrii Pukach, Vasyl Teslyuk, Anatolii Batiuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The paper is devoted to the development of a specialized CI-based approach for endowing HCI subjects digital twins with the property of HCI object perception subjectivization, which uses at its core an adaptation of a specialized method based on artificial neural networks of a multilayer perceptron. The main purpose of the developed approach is to ensure the possibility of taking into account and researching the aspect of perception subjectivization of HCI object(s) by HCI subjects, as one of the most significant factors of the irrational component of whole HCI (as well as any intersubjective interaction in general), and to endow HCI subjects digital twins with such an extra property. Within a framework of performed research, the following items have been completed, namely: a conceptual model of corresponding component (for endowing HCI subjects digital twins with the property of HCI object perception subjectivization) has been developed, which reflects the key principles of the declared approach; a basic mathematical model of the subjectivization property for HCI objects' perception has been developed for digital twin models of HCI subjects; a specialized algorithm for synthesizing and encapsulating the property of perception subjectivization of HCI objects into the digital twin models of HCI subjects has been developed. Taken together, all of the above-listed implemented components ensure the achievement of the declared research goal for the development of an appropriate approach into the context of solving a relevant scientific and applied problem (of endowing HCI subjects digital twins with the property of HCI object perception subjectivization), which, in turn, is a part of more global scientific and applied problematics for studying the irrational component of HCI, as well as any other varieties, kinds or types of intersubjective interaction in general.

title: AI-Based Proactive Autoscaling Technique for Web Applications in Kubernetes
authors: Halyna Beshley, Sviatoslav Vilhutskyi, Mykola Beshley, Michal Greguš
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Modern web applications deployed in Kubernetes environments are exposed to highly dynamic and unpredictable workloads, which often makes conventional reactive autoscaling mechanisms insufficient to guarantee stable Quality of Service (QoS). This paper presents an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-based proactive autoscaling technique for Kubernetes that leverages time-series forecasting and intelligent model selection to anticipate workload changes and adapt resource allocation before performance degradation occurs. The proposed solution is implemented as a Kubernetes Operator with a Custom Resource Definition (CRD), enabling seamless integration, declarative configuration, and fully automated lifecycle management of predictive models. The system combines a statistical Holt–Winters model and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model within a hybrid forecasting framework. Holt–Winters provides immediate prediction capability during the cold-start phase, while LSTM ensures high long-term accuracy once sufficient historical data become available. A closed-loop Scheduler continuously evaluates model performance using Mean Squared Error (MSE) and dynamically selects the most accurate predictor for each metric. The resulting forecasts are translated into scaling decisions that proactively adjust the number of Kubernetes pods. Experimental validation was conducted on a real Kubernetes cluster using a RESTful web service with stochastic traffic patterns. The results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves service reliability compared to conventional reactive autoscaling. In particular, the proposed proactive autoscaling technique reduced cumulative Service Level Agreement (SLA) violations by approximately 87% compared to the reactive mechanism, leading to a significant improvement in overall service reliability and stability.

title: Theoretical Investigation of Using Deviation for Interpolation of Electron Beam Boundary Trajectories by Root-Polynomial Functions
authors: Igor Melnyk, Mykhailo Skrypka, Olga Demyanchenko
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: This paper examines the use of deviation in the interpolation of stiff numerical datasets with a ravine dependence using root-polynomial functions of varying orders, from second to sixth. It is shown that using deviation, with an appropriate selection of its value, always ensures the convergence of the advanced proposed interpolation method. However, the error in the region of the ravine function minimum is, in any case, quite significant, but average error on the whole interpolation interval is generally small. The proposed interpolation method can be successfully used to mathematically describe the boundary trajectory of a short-focus electron beam propagating in an ionized gas. These physical conditions for the formation and propagation of an electron beam best correspond to the operating conditions of high-voltage glow discharge electron guns. This paper may be of great interest to specialists involved in the physics of electron beams, their modeling, and the industrial implementation of modern electron beam technologies.

title: Integration of Yolov8 Detection Outputs with XGBoost Algorithm for Defect Forecasting in Manufacturing Lines
authors: Surangpimol Chantawong, Orasa Sirasakamol, Anan Wongjan, Samart Yachiangkam, Wichai Sukprasertkul, Worathep Kongkaew
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper presents an integrated framework that combines YOLOv8-based computer vision with XGBoost predictive analytics to enhance defect forecasting and operational reliability in electronic manufacturing. The system targets the Bowl Feeder housing alignment process, where misorientation and jamming frequently cause unplanned downtime. High-resolution images from real production environments were used to train a YOLOv8 deeplearning model, achieving over 95 % detection accuracy with inference latency below 20 ms. YOLOv8 outputs including object counts, alignment ratios, and confidence metrics were aggregated into temporal features and analyzed using XGBoost regression to forecast potential defects and jam events. Experimental results show that the hybrid approach reduced jam occurrences by more than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 0 \%}$</tex> and doubled the mean time between stops, outperforming conventional inspection systems. The findings demonstrate a practical path toward AI-driven smart manufacturing by integrating realtime detection with predictive analytics for continuous process optimization.

title: Retraction Notice: Energy effectiveness practices in WSN over simulation and analysis of S-MAC and leach using the network simulator NS2
authors: Konda Hari Krishna, Tapas Kumar, Y. Suresh Babu
publish: 2017 International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSN) offers one remote medium for transmitting information bundles, and it has a constrained asset with regards to the vitality. Hence, there is a need for vitality proficient convention for getting to the medium. “Sensor-Medium Access Control” (S-MAC) is considered vitality proficient convention since it diminishes the vitality utilizations of the sensors hubs. From these conventions, bunch based directing calculations have accomplished more huge and expanding the life time of the WSN even the hubs are in portable state. title: 3D ultrafast imaging using a 3072-element matrix array_supp1-3680661.docx
authors: Mathieu Pernot
publish: N/A
abstract: 3D ultrafast imaging holds great potential across multiple applications, including blood flow imaging, elastography, brain functional ultrasound, cardiac imaging and microvascular flow imaging. Despite promising preclinical results, extending these results to human-scale organs remains a major challenge due to the limited aperture and channel count of existing fully addressed arrays which constrain depth penetration, field of view and resolution. In this work, we developed a new research platform for 3D ultrafast imaging of human-scale organs using a fully connected 3072-element matrix array. The probe has an aperture of 22.4x15.9mm2, which enables deeper penetration and higher lateral resolution compared to smaller arrays. The platform is driven by two synchronized 512-channel systems, providing a total of 1024 electronic channels, each with a 4:1 multiplexer. We evaluated the system's performance using numerical simulations and experiments on phantoms which showed that the 3072-element array provided a significant increase in lateral resolution— 56% in azimuth and 39% in elevation at a depth of 50 mm—compared to a standard 1024-element matrix array. The larger aperture also allowed for better penetration depth, reaching approximately 110 mm. We investigated different 3D ultrafast imaging strategies with diverging waves. A synthetic aperture imaging approach using sub-apertures in both transmit and receive provided the best imaging performance in terms of lateral resolution, field of view, and contrast. The translational potential of the platform was successfully validated in vivo on swine kidneys and livers, demonstrating the feasibility of 3D ultrafast Doppler and 3D ultrasound localization microscopy (ULM) for visualizing anatomical structures and microcirculation.

title: FCMOC: Fuzzy-C-Means based Optimal Clustering Approach for Load Balanced Energy Efficient Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Ikkurthi Bhanu Prasad, Saumitra Gangwar, Yogita, Satyendra Singh Yadav, Krishna Kumar, Vipin Pal
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Prolonging the life of sensor nodes in deployable wireless sensor network has been a prominent research focus. The literature extensively explores node clustering as a strategy to effectively extend the overall network lifespan. Ensuring even energy consumption among nodes through load balancing to prevent premature node death and extend network lifespan remains a crucial concern. Therefore, a scheme is required to form optimally load-balanced clusters with suitable Cluster Heads (CHs) that are aligned with network operational conditions and enhance network lifetime. This work presents a clustering scheme in which load-balanced tentative cluster creation happens by employing the fuzzy c-means technique, where nodes’ location and residual energy are considered. The Silhouette coefficient technique helps to determine the clustering quality index that facilitates optimal cluster formation in the network, aligned with network operational conditions. The best possible nodes for the role of cluster heads are selected based on the Topsis technique. The experimental results reveal the proposed scheme has superior load-balancing capability and ameliorates energy-efficient operation when compared to other existing methods.
