title: Markerless Pose Estimation and Tracking on Facial Regions for in vivo Robot-assisted Terahertz Sensing
authors: Shruti Chakraborty, Arturo I. Hernandez-Serrano, Anubhav Dogra, Dominic Jones, Agrima Agarwal, Pietro Valdastri, Emma Pickwell-MacPherson
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Terahertz (THz) light’s sensitivity to water, nonionizing nature, and sub-millimeter resolution make it ideal for medical imaging, particularly for diagnosing and managing skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and skin cancer. Traditional handheld THz probes suffer from positional errors, tremors, and inconsistent contact pressure, which worsen with prolonged use. To address this, robotic THz acquisition systems can be leveraged to segment regions of interest on the skin surface and compute the appropriate probe pose for scanning. This paper proposes a markerless method for automatic identification and estimation of the pose for the placement of the THz probe over high-curvature facial regions. An RGB-D camera is utilized to select, recognize, and track the scanning targets on the patient using an end-to-end network-based model for 3D human face mesh prediction. We then solve for the landing pose to position the probe at normal incidence to the tissue surface at the target scan regions. We evaluated scanning area localization accuracy, motion execution accuracy, and THz acquisition capability using a mannequin head and human volunteers. Results demonstrated target registration error of 1.71 mm, scan target localization accuracy of less than 3 mm and probe landing pose estimation accuracy of 2.769 ±3.02 mm in X, 6.62 ± 0.6 mm in Y and 4.792 ± 2.18 in Z and orientation error of 1.6±0.8 degrees.

title: A Dual-Arm Robotic System with Virtual Fixture-Guided Light Tracking for Fundus Surgery
authors: Zhaodong Wang, Chenhan Guang, Yu Zheng, Hao Li, Yang Yang, Chuang Lin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Medical Robotics and Bionics
abstract: Retinal surgery demands submillimeter precision in constrained intraocular spaces, with subretinal injections requiring accurate bimanual coordination between surgical tools and illumination devices. This paper presents the Dual-Arm Robotic System for Subretinal Injection (DASSI), featuring a motion-decoupled Remote Center of Motion (RCM) mechanism and an initial placement strategy to optimize dual-arm workspace overlap. An Automatic Light Pipe Tracking algorithm with Guided Virtual Fixtures (ATLP-GVF) is developed to eliminate redundant light pipe motion when illumination needs are met. Integrated with its hardware design (motion-decoupled RCM mechanism) and ATLP-GVF, DASSI achieves precise performance: orientation tracking error (0.65° ± 0.28°), depth error (1.86 mm ± 0.93 mm), and stable illumination (tracking offset ratio 26% ± 14%). Notably, in the ex-vivo experiment, ATLP-GVF reduces light pipe movement duration to less than 10% of that with continuous tracking methods, representing a reduction of more than 90%. DASSI demonstrates potential to address bimanual coordination challenges in retinal microsurgery, offering a precise platform for complex procedures such as subretinal gene-therapy delivery.

title: System-Level Efficiency Study of Modular DC/DC and Fuel Cell Stack Series–Parallel Configurations
authors: Erdem Asa, Vivek A. Sujan, Omer C. Onar
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: This paper presents a system-level efficiency study of modular DC/DC converter and fuel cell stack configurations under series, parallel, and series–parallel connections. The investigation considers selected DC/DC converter topologies, including isolated and non-isolated architectures, boost, non-inverting buck-boost, and resonant converters, integrated with commercially available fuel cell stacks, including the Accelera FCE150, Ballard FCgen-HPS, and Toyota TFCM2. DC/DC converter modules are evaluated in modular configurations rated at 60 kW and 90 kW and two distinct output voltage ranges, specifically 580–730V and 780–930V, examining how different interconnection schemes impact overall system efficiency. The converters are evaluated under full load (100%), partial load (66%), and light load (33%) conditions, providing a comprehensive assessment of efficiency and operational characteristics across varying power demands. Approximately two-thousand efficiency data points are obtained from laboratory prototype–level component measurements and validated design evaluations across multiple converter topologies, modular configurations, voltage ranges, and load conditions, providing a robust dataset for comparative system-level efficiency analysis. The results highlight the effects of modularity and topology selection on system-level efficiency, offering a “playbook” framework for designers to select appropriate DC/DC converter arrangements and fuel cell stack connections for series, parallel, or hybrid configurations based on efficiency considerations.

title: Tri-Mode Hybrid Dielectric Resonator Antenna Fabricated via Laser-Based Selective Material Processing for WLAN Applications
authors: Zhenyi Shou, Nianyao Chai, Xiao Yang, Lin Li, Kewen Pan
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this paper, a novel tri-mode hybrid dielectric resonator antenna (DRA) designed for wireless local area network (WLAN) applications is presented. Through a special DRA design, the antenna profile is compressed to 0.05 λ<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub>. TE<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sup> <sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1+δ11</sub> and TE<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">y</sup> <sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2+δ11</sub> modes of a complex, irregular dielectric resonator are merged to provide the coverage of WLAN 5.2/5.8-GHz bands, while a quarter-wavelength monopole mode of an F-shaped metal strip is excited for the WLAN 2.4-GHz band. Meanwhile, a hybrid fabrication method has been developed including stereolithography (SLA)-based ceramic 3D printing for irregular dielectric resonator fabrication and laser-based selective metal patterning to achieve high-precision integration of metal strip lines (< 20 μm accuracy). Measured and simulated results of reflection coefficients along with radiation patterns show good agreement. 6.87% and 23.16% bandwidth coverage have been achieved for WLAN 2.4-GHz and WLAN 5.2/5.8-GHz bands respectively.

title: ECLAS: Efficient Certificateless Aggregate Signature with Minimal Latency in VANETs
authors: Zhiyuan Sui, Yaxing Zha, Yujiao Sun, Maoning Wang, Youwei Wang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: VANETs are a critical component of modern transportation systems, offering significant benefits in terms of safety, efficiency, and user experience. However, addressing challenges related to security, privacy, and scalability is essential for their successful implementation and widespread adoption. The verification of digital signatures in VANETs is crucial for security and privacy issues, but it can generate significant communication overhead due to certificate management, signature transmissions, and verification computations. This can lead to scalability issues, where the system becomes less efficient and more prone to delays. In this paper, we propose an efficient certificateless aggregate signature scheme (ECLAS) to address the above three challenges. The ECLAS scheme is proven to be a secure certificateless signature scheme against chosen-message attacks in the random oracle model. Therefore, the ECLAS scheme eliminates certificate management in the system. Further, the performance comparison shows that the ECLAS scheme achieves the shortest signature size and the most efficient verification cost for traffic-related message transmissions. Consequently, the ECLAS scheme achieves minimal communication latency in VANETs.

authors: Chuanping Lyu, Zhe Li, Zhen Yu

title: 140–400-mV Input Switched-Capacitor Converter for Miniaturized Photovoltaic Power Transfer
authors: Reyhan Ramadhan, Masato Saito, Yasufumi Yokoshiki, Takashi Tokuda
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This article presents a wide operating range switched-capacitor (SC) converter designed for miniaturized photovoltaic (PV) energy sources. In millimeter-scale devices, PV power transfer is limited by lowgenerated voltage and available power, posing significant challenge for power conversion. To address this constraint, the proposed SC converter employs an input-powered 3× differential switching signal generation and boosting path, which enhances performance in the subthreshold operating region while maintaining sub-microwatt operation and cold-start capability across the operating range. The converter also incorporates an exponential reconfigurable charge pump topology that achieves high voltage multiplication gain with a reduced number of stages and capacitor footprint. To maintain compatibility with the most efficient monolithic PV implementation, the converter is designed to operate in the negative supply domain. The proposed circuit was implemented in 180 nm CMOS process and experimentally verified using both a controlled voltage source and a discrete 2.1-mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> silicon photodiode. Measurement results demonstrate robust operation over an input range of 140–400mV with a peak power conversion efficiency of 56.6% and up to 9.89× voltage gain, enabling energy harvesting from incident light intensities as low as 20 nW/mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>.

title: Improving crude oil price prediction using LLM-based hierarchical semantic extraction and heterogeneous graph neural networks
authors: Shang Cao, Ziqi Qu, Caichun Cen, Zeling Xu, Yuntao Zou
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Crude oil price forecasting serves energy policy formulation and financial risk management. Existing deep temporal models rely on the assumption of historical pattern continuity and respond insufficiently to exogenous events such as geopolitical conflicts. Mainstream large language model approaches encode news as single vectors, and fine-grained semantic information is compressed and lost in this process. Causal propagation relationships among events and heterogeneous temporal lag characteristics have not yet been incorporated into modeling. To address these limitations, this paper proposes three improvements building upon multimodal graph neural network and Temporal Fusion Transformer architectures. At the semantic extraction level, a four-layer pyramid architecture based on large language models is designed to parse news texts into structured representations covering entity stances, event types, causal chains, and market sentiment. At the graph modeling level, a heterogeneous information graph containing multiple node types and edge types is constructed, introducing causal and temporal edges beyond existing static entity associations to characterize cumulative, propagation, and synergistic effects among events. At the feature fusion level, a semantic-driven market state identification module is designed to replace numerical-statisticsbased lagging detection, dynamically adjusting feature weights according to the identified state. Using Brent crude oil price forecasting as a validation case, the proposed method achieves a 17.7% RMSE improvement over the Temporal Fusion Transformer baseline, with improvement reaching 34.7% during event-intensive periods and the coefficient of determination R2 reaching 0.903.

title: Adaptive Feature Selection With Hierarchical Learning for Drug-Target Interaction Prediction_supp1-3678266.pdf
authors: Mingliang Xu
publish: N/A
abstract: Accurate prediction of drug–target interactions (DTIs) is essential for drug discovery and repurposing. Although deep learning has driven substantial progress, critical limitations remain: a singular focus on intermolecular associations results in suboptimal representation learning, and the failure to leverage key features during interactions constrains further performance gains. Here, we propose ASHL-DTI, a novel framework that integrates hierarchical learning with adaptive feature selection to significantly boost both feature quality and model generalizability. Specifically, the hierarchical learning component captures multi-level intramolecular associations to learn more discriminative representations. Simultaneously, we incorporate an adaptive Top-k selection mechanism to retain the most predictive features, facilitating effective interaction between drugs and targets. Experimental results across multiple public benchmark datasets demonstrate that ASHL-DTI achieves superior performance compared with state-of-the-art approaches. Moreover, ASHL-DTI exhibits strong generalization ability in predicting novel drug–target pairs, underscoring its potential in drug discovery. The complete source code of ASHL-DTI is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/Miwkwh/ASHL-DTI</uri>.

title: A Stacked Decoupling Coupler Structure With Real-Time 2D Positioning for Short-Rail DWPT
authors: Weida Xu, Qingxin Yang, Xian Zhang, Ming Xue, Fei Xu, Shengyan Qiu
publish: IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Power Electronics
abstract: This paper proposes a layered decoupling segmented transmitter coil structure designed to mitigate the detrimental effects of cross-coupling in short-rail dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) systems, particularly the reduction in transmission efficiency and output fluctuations caused by resonance point shifts. By stacking bipolar decoupling coils, the structure achieves decoupling between adjacent transmitter coils, effectively suppressing mutual inductance fluctuations and enhancing system stability. Additionally, an H-type receiver coil structure and a four-coil positioning method are proposed. Real-time monitoring of voltage variations across the four receiver-side positioning coils enables precise dynamic detection of Autonomous Vehicle (AV) positions in a two-dimensional (2D) plane. This capability supports autonomous path correction for AV and significantly improves system robustness against lateral misalignment. To meet real-time switching requirements for moving loads, the voltage feedback characteristics of the bipolar decoupling coils are utilized to detect the position of moving receiver coils in advance, ensuring reliable and timely transmitter coil switching. Finite element simulations are employed to optimize the dimensions and number of turns of the bipolar coils, reducing the cross-coupling mutual inductance between adjacent transmitting coils from 21.05 μH to 0.08 μH. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method reduces the overall output power fluctuation coefficient by 23.34%, achieves a peak transmission efficiency of 89.03%, and maintains a DC-DC efficiency fluctuation coefficient of only 2.03%.

title: Task-Difficulty-Guided Multi-Gate Mixture-of-Experts for Robust Uncertainty Quantification in Solar Power Forecasting
authors: Heng-Yi Su, Guan-Zhang Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: Reliable solar power interval forecasting, critical for grid integration and operational planning, is challenged by quantile heterogeneity and non-Gaussian error distributions. This paper introduces a task-difficulty-guided multi-gate mixture-of-experts (TD-M²oE) framework within paired-quantile multi-task learning. TD-M²oE is characterized by a novel masking gate that adaptively allocates experts across varied quantile tasks, yielding coherent prediction intervals across multiple coverage levels. A composite loss enforces quantile monotonicity and promotes expert diversity, empirically enhancing robustness under non-Gaussian and heteroscedastic conditions. Experiments on real-world solar power datasets demonstrate consistent improvements in interval reliability and sharpness over state-of-the-art baselines.

title: The Influence of Environmental Fidelity on Virtual Presence, Intrinsic Motivation, Cognitive Load and Learning Outcomes in Medical VR
authors: Danny Schott, Matthias Kunz, Claudia Schrader, Elias Ringler, Alexander Schwadtke, Jonas Mandel, Patrick Müller, Constantin Kleinbeck, Daniel Roth, Anne Albrecht, Rüdiger C. Braun-Dullaeus, Christian Hansen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Immersive virtual reality learning environments (IVRLEs) are increasingly used in medical education, yet the role of environmental fidelity—particularly scene design—remains underexplored. This study examines how varying levels of fidelity and contextualization affect motivational and cognitive outcomes. Eighty-seven medical students were randomly assigned to one of three scene conditions: a minimalistic “Blank Scene,” a “Reconstructed Classroom Scene”, or an “Inside-Human Scene”. All students used a custom-developed application to learn about embryonic heart development. We measured virtual presence, intrinsic motivation, cognitive load, learning outcomes, and usability. Results showed that scene design influenced virtual presence, selected aspects of intrinsic motivation, cognitive load, and learning outcomes. The Inside-Human Scene elicited higher physical and self-presence as well as higher comprehension scores compared to the Reconstructed Classroom Scene. The Reconstructed Classroom Scene was associated with higher extraneous cognitive load. Intrinsic cognitive load was rated higher in the Inside-Human Scene, while germane cognitive load did not differ between conditions. No significant differences were found for task performance or factual recall. Overall, the findings indicate that scene design in IVRLEs affects how learners engage with complex content and may support deeper understanding when perceptual and contextual properties are coherent, while visually detailed environments may increase extraneous cognitive demands without improving learning.

title: The Impact of Antenna Polarization on Mirrored Aperture Synthesis Radiometry
authors: Liangbing Chen, Yuhang Tian, Leshi Zhao, Qingxia Li, Zhenyu Lei, Rong Jin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: High-spatial-resolution and real-time sounding of atmospheric temperature and humidity in geostationary orbit is urgent for the accurate forecasting of extreme climate events, and mirrored aperture synthesis (MAS) has been proposed as a potential means to provide high spatial resolution with relatively low system complexity. However, a key issue regarding the impact of antenna polarization on MAS was neglected, which is comprehensively investigated in this paper. The impacts on the theoretical expression of the received signal and the cross-correlation output are analyzed, and numerical simulations and indoor fringe pattern experiments of 1-D and 2-D MAS are conducted to demonstrate the correctness of the theoretical analysis. Then, imaging experiments based on a 2-D MAS prototype are designed, and the results indicate that the match between the transformation matrix and the antenna polarization is crucial.

title: Fluid Antenna-aided Semi-Full-Duplex System with Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access
authors: Yibo Shan, Lin Bai, Jiahao Ma, Rui Han, Jiaxing Wang, Xin Xie, Jinho Choi
publish: IEEE Wireless Communications Letters
abstract: Rate balancing becomes challenging with a relatively high downlink signal-to-noise ratio, particularly in achieving symmetric downlink and uplink rates. The semi-full-duplex (SFD) approach, which borrows downlink rate, provides improved rate balancing. However, existing work has mainly investigated SFD with a single uplink user. In practice, multiple uplink users should be supported, requiring advanced techniques to enhance transmission rates. This paper introduces a non-orthogonal multiple access (NOMA) system with the fluid antenna (FA) to improve channel gain in SFD, and investigates the optimization of total uplink rate through FA port selection and time allocation. The results demonstrate that our proposed system achieves better rate balancing.

title: Development of Organic Photodetectors Based on Benzothiadiazole and Fluorene Derivatives
authors: Nataliia Kuzyk, Ihor Semkiv, Khrystyna Ivaniuk, Pavlo Stakhira, Iryna Yaremchuk, Mohamed Abdella, Jurate Simokaitiene, Dmytro Volyniuk, Juozas Vidas Grazulevicius
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: In this study, we investigated the potential of benzothiadiazole and fluorene derivatives serving as functional materials for organic photodetectors. Within the fabricated structure, the new synthesised donor material, characterised by an optical bandgap energy of 3.43 eV, acts in conjunction with the C<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">70</inf> acceptor (E<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">g</inf> = 1.77 eV) as UV-sensitive components that efficiently absorb near-ultraviolet light. The developed bulk heterojunction device designed for ultraviolet detection exhibits a low dark current (~0.2 nA) and high photosensitivity while operating at a low bias voltage. Furthermore, the photocurrent-to-dark current ratio achieves a maximum value of approximately one order of magnitude at an applied voltage of -0.5 V.

title: Performance Degradation of Higher-Order APSK in LEO Links Under Phase Noise and HPA Nonlinearity
authors: Marin Nedelchev, Daniel Iliev
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This paper investigates the error performance of higher-order APSK modulation in Low Earth Orbit satellite links under the combined impact of oscillator phase noise and nonlinear high-power amplifiers (HPAs). A detailed baseband system model is built for 16APSK and 32APSK over an AWGN channel, including a realistic PLL synthesizer phase-noise process and a nonlinear HPA described by the modified Rapp model. On this basis, closed-form approximations for the symbol error probability are derived by decomposing errors into dominant angular and radial components, averaging over the Gaussian-approximated phase-noise distribution, and applying nearest-neighbour bounds per ring. MATLAB/Simulink simulations validate the analysis and show that phase noise and HPA nonlinearity jointly reduce inter-ring distance, increase angular dispersion on outer rings, and introduce a pronounced error floor, especially for 32APSK at low input back-off. The results quantify the effective SNR penalty due to RF hardware impairments and provide practical guidelines for selecting APSK order and HPA back-off in cost-constrained LEO user terminals.

title: Method of Cluster-Driven Spectral-Parametric Transformant Coding for Adaptive Compression of Segmented Video Data
authors: Vladimir Barannik, Evgeniy Eliseev, Valeriy Barannik, Anatolii Berchanov, Volodymyr Lenets, Oleksandr Fedorovskiy
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: High-complexity video data produced by modern intelligent systems and transmitted through wireless, bandwidth-restricted, and cyber-threat-exposed infrastructures increasingly exhibit intensified information flows, pronounced structural variability, and growing pixel volumes. These conditions sharpen the contradiction between the demand for high-quality intelligent video services and the constraints imposed by limited reliability, security, and communication resources. As segmented video streams become more heterogeneous, traditional JPEG-compatible, statistical, and redundancy-reduction approaches fail to ensure stable coding performance, particularly when transformants differ in spectral composition and segmentation-driven structure. To address this limitation, the article introduces a compression method operating in a clustered spectral-parametric space, enabling coordinated encoding of heterogeneous transformant sequences. The method integrates formation of a spectral-parametric description (SPD) via sub-band parametrization, transformant clustering based on SPD homogeneity, differentiated normalization in a normed space, locally monotonic binary block coding with adaptive bit-length assignment, and a generalized code representation designed to support robust reconstruction. In addition, the method systematically accounts for inter-transformant dependencies, ensuring consistent parameter behavior across clustered segments. Clustering in spectral-parametric space enhances adaptability by aligning structurally similar transformants and reducing inter-segment redundancy, thereby stabilizing compression across variable content. The resulting pipeline improves compression efficiency while maintaining controlled-distortion behavior, making the method suitable for secure, resource-constrained intelligent video services operating under dynamic network conditions and elevated cyber-risk.

title: Application of Quasioptical Transmissing Line for Transformation of the EM Fields
authors: Mykhaylo Andriychuk, Victor Tkachuk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The wireless transmitting power system consisting of a set of the confocal lenses is considered. The geometry of system satisfies the quasioptical conditions that allow to transfer the energy in the framework of the Gaussian beam. The criterion of the field transmission is functional presenting the specific relation of the prescribed and resulted fields in the system. In order to maximize the values of functional, the iterative method for determination of the phase characteristics in lenses is developed. The proposed iterative procedure increases the value of functional at each iteration. The presented numerical results demonstrate the quick convergence of iterations and the effectiveness of line even with small quantity of lenses in line.

title: Propagation Dynamics of Radially Polarized Terahertz Laser Beams with a Phase Singularity
authors: Andrey Degtyarev, Mykola Dubinin, Vyacheslav Maslov, Konstantin Muntean, Vladislav Senyuta
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: Analytical expressions are obtained for describing nonparaxial diffraction of vortex beams during their propagation in free space. The studied beams are excited by radially polarized modes TM0m (m = 1, 2, 3) of the dielectric waveguide resonator of the terahertz laser, interacting with a spiral phase plate with an arbitrary topological charge n, installed at the output of the resonator. The Rayleigh-Sommerfeld vector theory is used for modeling. It is shown that the use of a spiral phase plate with topological charges n = 1, 2 leads to the appearance of the azimuthal field component in the field of the studied beams. In this case the radial and longitudinal components already present in the field of the original beam are preserved. At the same time the contribution of the longitudinal component to the total beam power for all topological charges of the phase plate is insignificant. For a topological charge n = 1 the field maximum is observed on the axis, whereas for charges n = 0 and n = 2 the transverse profiles of the radiation beam have a pronounced ring structure. The maximum values of the field intensity during the propagation of beams in free space correspond to a topological charge n = 1 and for a charge n = 2 the magnitude of these values decreases significantly. The phase distributions of the transverse components of the vortex beam field were also calculated and visualized. For a topological charge n = 1, the phase distributions have a one-lobed helical structure. For charge n = 2 the phase profile changes from one to two lobes. In this case, a greater number of spiral twists is observed for the azimuthal component.

In the field of image processing, object recognition is a harsh but useful dilemma. This paper has presented a computer vision-based scheme for detecting temples to consider a lot of effort had been put in on animals, people, bikes, cars, and so on. title: Research on the Influence of Branch Length on the Transmission Parameters of a Home Electrical Wiring Network
authors: Vasyl Oreshkov, Oleksandr Yanevych, Kseniia Tryfonova
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: In this work, a fragment of the home electrical wiring network is considered, consisting of segments of ShVVP 2×1.5 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> wires that form the main transmission line and one branch. An analytical model of the home electrical wiring network fragment has been developed using the decomposition method based on the ABCD matrix. The frequency responce of the home electrical wiring network fragment have been investigated for different types of loads − equal to the characteristic impedance of the network wires, open circuit, and short circuit. Special attention is paid to studying the influence of the branch length on the network's frequency responce. It has been determined that the branch length significantly affects both the amplitude-frequency response and the phase-frequency response of the home electrical wiring network.

title: Minimum inertia requirement assessment method for renewable energy grid-connected systems with multiple virtual inertia control units
authors: Guirong Hu, Wei Wu, Tan Li, Lili Cheng
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: With the high proportion of renewable energy sources being integrated into power systems, the level of system inertia continues to decline, posing severe challenges to frequency stability. Existing methods either ignore governor deadband effects or require iterative simulations, leading to excessive prediction errors or excessively long computation time. This paper proposes a method for evaluating the minimum inertia requirement of grid-connected systems with multiple virtual inertia control units. First, a multi-machine frequency response model is established, incorporating multiple virtual inertia control units such as synchronous generators, wind turbines, and photovoltaic-energy storage systems. A linear-exponential hybrid function explicitly captures deadband-induced nonlinear dynamics. Second, with minimum system inertia as the optimization objective, a minimum inertia assessment model is established that incorporates multiple constraints including system inertia level, rate of change of frequency, available adjustable capacity, and frequency extreme points. Simultaneously, the frequency response is analytically partitioned into pre- and post-primary regulation stages, enabling direct solution without iteration. Particle swarm optimization efficiently solves the model. Finally, simulation case studies are conducted to verify the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed method, achieving a frequency nadir prediction error below 2% and an 85% faster computation (<15 s).

title: Range-Ambiguous Clutter Suppression for Spaceborne Radar via Joint Elevation-Coherent FDA and Quadratic Phase Coding
authors: Fuhai Wan, Jingwei Xu, Weiwei Wang, Yanhong Xu, Guisheng Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems
abstract: Spaceborne radar (SBR) suffers from pronounced range ambiguity due to its ultra-long detection range, while Earth's rotation further induces significant range dependence in clutter, effects that collectively render traditional space-time adaptive processing (STAP) potentially ineffective. This paper investigates an SBR framework that integrates elevation-coherent frequency diverse array (FDA) and quadratic phase coding (QPC) to mitigate range ambiguity in long-range target detection, where frequency diversity is implemented along the elevation dimension of the planar array and quadratic coding phase is imposed across the pulse dimension. The elevation-coherent FDA adopts coherent waveforms across transmit channels, circumventing the complexity of orthogonal waveform design in multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) system, while its transmit beampattern frequency scanning feature enables wide space coverage within a single pulse. Capitalizing on this merit, a range-ambiguous clutter suppression approach is devised, which comprises range frequency-elevation angle matched filter (RF-EA MF) followed by QPC demodulation. The coupling property between elevation angle and range frequency in coherent FDA is leveraged to design the RF-EA MFs that extract far-range mainlobe clutter. Nevertheless, the mainlobe width of these filters is constrained by the finite elevation degrees of freedom (DOF), leaving residual mainlobe ambiguous clutter that contaminates the filter output. Subsequently, QPC demodulation introduces Doppler shifts to achieve approximate spectral alignment among residual components. Post this cascade processing, the extracted far-range mainlobe clutter becomes nearly range-independent, permitting effective suppression via reduced-dimension STAP. Simulation experiments validate the efficacy of the proposed framework in mitigating severe range ambiguity.

title: Adaptive Model Slimming for Communication and Computation Efficient Federated Edge Learning Under Non-IID Data Distribution
authors: Qimei Chen, Zhang Wang, Xinyao Zhang, Dingzhu Wen, Guangxu Zhu, Muhammad Kaleem Awan
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: Federated Edge Learning (FEL) enables privacy-preserving collaborative model training across distributed edge devices, yet its efficacy is critically challenged by the dual constraints of Non-IID data distributions and limited on-device resources. While existing methods often address communication, computation, or data heterogeneity in isolation, a holistic solution remains underexplored. To bridge this gap, we propose LightFEL, a novel FEL framework that incorporates adaptive model slimming via dynamic layer freezing. By selectively freezing convolutional layers during local training based on their sensitivity to data heterogeneity, LightFEL eliminates unnecessary backward propagation, thereby significantly reducing computational load and communication overhead, while inherently enhancing robustness to Non-IID data. We formulate a joint optimization problem that co-designs layer freezing policies with communication and computation resource allocation to minimize training latency. A theoretical convergence analysis is provided, establishing the interplay between sparsification strategies, resource decisions, and learning performance under Non-IID settings. The resulting mixed-integer nonlinear program is efficiently solved using a Q-learning and Lagrange Multiplier approach. Extensive experiments demonstrate that LightFEL substantially reduces computation costs and cuts communication overhead, while achieving higher accuracy and faster convergence under challenging Non-IID scenarios compared with benchmarks, which validate its dual capability in resource efficiency and heterogeneity robustness.

title: Three-Patterns-Protected Searchable Encryption Supporting Disjunctive Keyword Search
authors: Jiguo Li, Licheng Ji, Wuwei Weng, Yicheng Zhang, Yang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Searchable encryption (SE) enables the client to execute keyword searches in encrypted data stored on the untrusted server and has been widely studied in cloud storage. To achieve higher efficiency and more functionalities, most SE schemes allowed the client to leak some information to the server. These leaked information are commonly referred to as leakage patterns. There are three important leakage patterns: search pattern, access pattern and volume pattern. Recent research has exploited at least one of these three patterns to attack SE schemes, resulting in the compromise of the confidentiality of encrypted data and queried keywords. Although existing SE schemes support conjunctive keyword search and protect these three patterns, these schemes do not support disjunctive keyword search and have a higher computational cost. In this paper, we use a private set union protocol based on additively symmetric homomorphic encryption to construct an SE scheme, which not only protects three patterns but also supports disjunctive keyword search. Specifically, we design an efficient token generation algorithm to protect the search pattern and a non-naive padding method to protect the volume pattern. Furthermore, we prove the correctness of our scheme through theoretical analysis and strictly prove the security under the leakage function. Finally, performance evaluation demonstrates that our scheme supports disjunctive keyword search while achieving a favorable trade-off between leakage protection and efficiency. Moreover, for components that exhibit relatively higher overhead during evaluation, we introduce optimization strategies that effectively enhance search efficiency and scalability.

title: Dual-Error Transformation Approach to Prescribed Performance Control for Unknown Euler–Lagrange Systems with Actuator Faults
authors: Chenglong Hu, Dongming Li, Chaoxu Mu, Anguo Zhang, Yongduan Song
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper addresses the intricate control challenges posed by unknown Euler–Lagrange systems operating under actuator faults and subject to asymmetric error constraints. A novel prescribed performance control (PPC) strategy is proposed, leveraging a dual-error transformation technique. The first transformation is designed to decouple the initial tracking errors from the predefined performance functions, thereby significantly relaxing the stringent initial condition dependencies typical of conventional PPC schemes and ensuring errors remain confined within adjustable asymmetric boundaries. The second transformation introduces exponential decay constraint functions to dynamically regulate the convergence rate and steady-state accuracy of the tracking errors. Theoretical analysis rigorously demonstrates that the proposed strategy, without requiring prior knowledge of the system’s complex nonlinearities, guarantees global boundedness of all closed-loop signals. Furthermore, it ensures that tracking errors converge to prespecified asymmetric residual zones at a preset rate, even in the presence of actuator faults. The efficacy and superiority of the proposed strategy are validated through comparative simulations conducted on a two-link robotic manipulator. The experimental source code is available at https:// github.com/hclll22/Dual-Error-Transformation-Approach-PPC.

title: Model–Hardware Co-Design of Depthwise Separable Convolution With Interlayer Pipelining for Stereo Matching
authors: Zetao Guo, Yifan Wang, Wendi Sun, Jiaheng Ruan, Yukang Han, Song Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Stereo matching is fundamental to depth perception in computer vision. While deep-learning-based stereo matching methods have significantly improved disparity map accuracy, their substantial computational demands pose challenges for deployment on resource- and power-constrained edge devices. To enable high-accuracy real-time stereo matching on such platforms, this article presents a co-design approach integrating algorithmic optimizations and hardware acceleration. First, inspired by depthwise separable convolution (DSC), a computation-driven decomposition block selection strategy is proposed to construct a lightweight stereo matching (LSM) neural network, which substantially reduces computational cost with negligible accuracy loss. Second, an optimization method for the interlayer pipelining paradigm is introduced, leveraging a DRAM access analysis model to derive optimal scheduling via dynamic programming, thereby maximizing its potential for optimizing off-chip memory access. Finally, we design a reconfigurable accelerator for interlayer pipelining that supports both single-layer and pipelined execution, achieving fine-grained load balancing via dynamic resource allocation. On the KITTI2015 dataset, the proposed LSM model yields an error rate of 2.67%. Synthesized in a 28-nm process, the accelerator processes <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$960{\,}\times {\,}544$</tex-math> </inline-formula> resolution images at 35.7 f/s. Compared with the baseline design, it delivers <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$5.24\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10.93\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> speedups, with energy reductions of 46% and 60%, respectively. Compared with state-of-the-art (SOTA) accelerators, it delivers <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1.44\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2.00\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> speedups under comparable accuracy.

title: Improving Portfolio Stability with Shrinkage Estimation in Mean-Variance Optimization
authors: Kevin Zhang, Aditya Kirubakaran
publish: 2025 IEEE Integrated STEM Education Conference (ISEC)
abstract: This study explores the application of Mean-Variance Optimization (MVO) to maximize returns while minimizing risk for a portfolio of 20 diverse stocks. Utilizing a decade of historical data from 2013 to 2023, the research calculates daily returns and estimates covariance matrices to construct an efficient frontier. Key findings demonstrate that higher risk levels correlate with increased expected returns, albeit with diminishing marginal gains. The analysis reveals limitations in the MVO model, particularly its sensitivity to input parameters and reliance on historical data. To address these challenges, the study implements shrinkage estimation techniques to enhance expected return estimates. Surprisingly, the shrinkage model defaults to sample means, suggesting robust initial estimates. This research contributes to the ongoing refinement of portfolio optimization strategies, offering insights into the practical application and limitations of MVO in contemporary financial markets, while highlighting the relationship between risk and portfolio performance. The study also demonstrates how shrinkage techniques, initially used to improve expected returns, prove valuable for portfolio diversification, expanding their utility in optimization models.

title: Retraction Notice: Content Based Remote-Sensing Image Retrieval with Bag of Visual Words Representation
authors: Amruta Rudrawar
publish: 2018 2nd International Conference on I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC)I-SMAC (IoT in Social, Mobile, Analytics and Cloud) (I-SMAC), 2018 2nd International Conference on
abstract: Retrieval of images assumes a noteworthy part in various areas including therapeutic determination, biometrics, geological data satellite frameworks, web searching and authentic research etc. At the point, when size of the database increases constantly, the applications including images confront new diculties and signicant issues in indexing, learning and retrieving. We require a productive retrieval system to retrieve images from the vision or audio database. CBIR-Content-based image retrieval is a image retrieval procedure used for retrieving images productively by utilizing low level image features texture, shape and color. In CBIR framework, a query image is described by features within the database. In this report, there are three steps. First, images from dataset are split into training and validation sets. Second, SURF features are extracted of the images and they are represented as bag of visual words using clustering and image indexing. Third, retrieval using cosine similarity. All these steps are carried out on remote rensing images. This technique does not require any relevance feedback for retrieval and it also reduces annotation work with similar results to query.

title: Setup-Independent Full Projector Compensation_supp1-3680736.pdf
authors: Haibo Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Projector compensation seeks to correct geometric and photometric distortions that occur when images are projected onto nonplanar or textured surfaces. However, most existing methods are highly setup-dependent, requiring fine-tuning or retraining whenever the surface, lighting, or projector-camera pose changes. Progress has been limited by two key challenges: (1) the absence of large, diverse training datasets and (2) existing geometric correction models are typically constrained by specific spatial setups; without further retraining or fine-tuning, they often fail to generalize directly to novel geometric configurations. We introduce SIComp, the first Setup-Independent framework for full projector Compensation, capable of generalizing to unseen setups without fine-tuning or retraining. To enable this, we construct a large-scale real-world dataset spanning 277 distinct projector-camera setups. SIComp adopts a co-adaptive design that decouples geometry and photometry: A carefully tailored optical flow module performs online geometric correction, while a novel photometric network handles photometric compensation. To further enhance robustness under varying illumination, we integrate intensity-varying surface priors into the network design. Extensive experiments demonstrate that SIComp consistently produces high-quality compensation across diverse unseen setups, substantially outperforming existing methods in terms of generalization ability and establishing the first generalizable solution to projector compensation.

title:
LaO
                    <sub>x</sub>
                    Atomic Flip Dipole Technique With Single Dipole Shifter in CMOS GAA NSFETs
authors: Yu Wang, Jiaxin Yao, Yanzhao Wei, Qingzhu Zhang, Feixiong Wang, Shuang Liu, Lei Cao, Yuzhe Yu, Wenjuan Xiong, Shuai Yang, Junfeng Li, Huaxiang Yin, Xiaolei Wang, Jun Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this article, for the first time, we proposed one-novel atomic LaO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> flip technique to achieve WFM-free multi-V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>s modulation by a single dipole shifter in CMOS GAA NSFETs. By mitigating the atomic LaO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">x</sub> dipole layer from the down-side HfO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> surface to the up-side, named in the atomic flip dipole technique (AFDT), the dipole force direction is successfully inverted, causing a band-edge impact on p-type work function metal (WFM) modulation, resulting in a linear 4-level V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub>s modulation within a ~250mV range of NSFETs. Furthermore, by flip/interfacial hybrid CMOS integration strategy, the 6N+ 6P multi-V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> results were demonstrated, exhibiting precise V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> control. The V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> tuning range is ~330mV for GAA nFETs, and ~400mV for GAA pFETs, with an average of ~66mV/level for nFETs and ~80mV/level for pFETs and V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> distribution have been achieved on for WFM-free GAA n/pFETs with less carrier mobility penalty, which provides a promising method to lessen limitations in p-type dipole modulation technology and one lower cost dipole V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">T</sub> modulation solution for CMOS NSFET integration with higher transistor performance.

title: DoA-Guided GNSS Anti-Jamming via Satellite Selection and Weighting
authors: Ivan Horbatyi, Oleksandr Usatyi
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: A lightweight method for protecting Global Navigation Satellite Systems against jamming is introduced. The approach combines estimation of the direction of arrival of interference with satellite geometry obtained from broadcast ephemerides. Bearings of jammers are estimated using subspace algorithms and then compared with the real-time positions of satellites. A geometry-aware weighting is applied to ensure that at least four high-quality satellites are selected while satellites aligned with jammers are down-weighted. An optional beamspace or null-steering stage can be added, but the baseline operates with scalar satellite weights, enabling implementation on modest hardware. The idea is evaluated under narrowband, wideband, and chirp jamming scenarios, demonstrating improved availability of positioning fixes, lower dilution of precision, and reduced positioning error compared with conventional carrier-to-noise-based selection.

title: Data Management Framework for the Process Digital Twin
authors: Hesam Rezaee Ahvanouee, Raghuveer Rajesh Dani, Aditi Shirke, Galyna Tabunshchyk
publish: 2026 IEEE 18th International Conference on Advanced Trends in Radioelectronics, Telecommunications and Computer Engineering (TCSET)
abstract: The architecture of a Process Digital Twin (PDT) merges both structural and behavioral representations of Cyber–Physical System(CPS), and its overall performance is strongly influenced by the chosen strategies for data exchange and by the supporting implementation toolchain. This work presents an integrated framework that unifies the Modular Digital Twin methodology based on Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) with established manufacturing standards for digital twins. In particular, it incorporates the Asset Administration Shell(AAS) to provide a standardized, static snapshot of system assets, and OPC UA as a robust industrial communication protocol for dynamic data interaction. Furthermore, the paper demonstrates the feasibility of the approach through simulation and verification, illustrating how the combined use of MBSE principles and industry standards enhances interoperability in digital twin implementations.

title: Comparison of Decision Tree and Random Forest Techniques for Sleep Posture Classification
authors: Waruna Saowapark, Pattharakorn Satharalai, Pirawit Tepimonrat, Pinyapat Sattamwilai, Chacharin Lertyosbordin
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: Sleep posture is a critical factor influencing sleep quality and musculoskeletal health. This study compares the performance of Decision Tree (DT) and Random Forest (RF) algorithms for classifying six distinct sleep postures: supine, fetal, freefall, lateral, starfish, and prone. The system utilizes data from 33 force sensors embedded in a conventional mattress to capture pressure distribution patterns. Data were collected from 20 participants and evaluated using a 10 -fold crossvalidation technique to assess generalization capability. The results indicate that the Random Forest model achieved a superior accuracy of 88.18 % and an F1-score of 0.88, significantly outperforming the Decision Tree model, which attained an accuracy of 73.33 % and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex>-score of 0.73. While the Decision Tree model proved highly efficient with a training time of 0.005 seconds, the Random Forest model required approximately 1.06 seconds due to its ensemble complexity. However, Random Forest demonstrated robust performance in distinguishing between geometrically similar postures, effectively reducing misclassifications found in the single-tree approach. These findings suggest that despite higher computational demands, Random Forest offers a viable balance between accuracy and feasibility for non-intrusive, embedded sleep monitoring systems.

title: Design and Implementation of a 70 Mhz Fifth-Order Elliptic Low-Pass Filter for FPGA-Based SDR Platforms
authors: Nattawat Thuanboon, Pisit Janpangngern, Pattawin Changnam, Chanchai Thongsopa, Nuchanart Santalunai, Samran Santalunai
publish: 2026 Joint International Conference on Digital Arts, Media and Technology with ECTI Northern Section Conference on Electrical, Electronics, Computer and Telecommunication Engineering (ECTI DAMT &amp; NCON)
abstract: This work presents the systematic design, simulation, fabrication, and experimental validation of a fifthorder elliptic low-pass filter with 70 MHz cutoff frequency for FPGA-based software- defined radio (SDR) platforms. A comprehensive comparative analysis of Butterworth, Chebyshev Type I, and Elliptic topologies was conducted across multiple filter orders to identify the optimal configuration balancing spectral efficiency and component complexity. The comparison revealed that the fifth-order Elliptic topology provides the sharpest transition band characteristics while meeting stopband attenuation requirements with minimum component count, where equivalent-order Butterworth and Chebyshev implementations require higher orders to achieve comparable performance. The design methodology integrates circuit-level simulation with full-wave electromagnetic analysis to predict parasitic effects and validate PCB implementation. The fabricated prototype, implemented with high-Q surfacemount inductors and capacitors on FR4 substrate, was characterized using a Keysight FieldFox microwave analyzer. Measured results demonstrate insertion loss |S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">21</inf>| below 0.6 dB, return loss |S<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">11</inf>| better than -10 dB, and stopband attenuation exceeding 20 dB, confirming the Elliptic topology as optimal for sharp transition band requirements in compact SDR implementations.

title: Retraction Notice: Opinion Mining on Food Services using Topic Modeling and Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: R. Akila, S. Revathi, G. Shreedevi
publish: 2020 6th International Conference on Advanced Computing and Communication Systems (ICACCS)
abstract: The development in technologies and the use of social media on a wide scale, has created opportunities for growth in analytics in obtaining useful insights from data with no proper schema. The use of opinion mining in the field of big data is used for categorizing the opinions of people or customers into different sentiments and to evaluate the mood of the customers. Opinion mining has gained significant importance over time due to the steep increase in amount of opinions available online. People share their opinions on products, services, movies, restaurants, schools, hospitals, holiday destinations etc. Social media has made it simple for users to share their opinion and make it available for any person on the Internet. The companies improve their strategy, products, marketing and services in order to detect latest trends and opportunities or to gauge how effective their marketing is. Several research attempts are being made at sentiment analysis to detect sentiment polarity and to perform predictive analytic. It is a research field which uses computer science and psychology. Natural Language Processing studies how machines can be used to comprehend a language and make it do meaningful things. The process of collecting public opinion and analyzing huge amount of social data has drawn great importance because of its real time behavior. A real time McDonald's dataset has been used for this work to determine the positive, negative and neutral reviews about the policies followed or violated by the restaurant. The people have posted their reviews as feedback of the service provided at McDonalds. McDonald's gets an enormous amount of customer comments on their website daily. Their employees don't have time to read every single comment, but they do want to read a subset of comments that they are most i
